En: Fuck it. Do it. It can't be worse than what you've already felt.
Zh: 去他的吧。动手吧。总不可能比现在的感觉更糟糕了吧。

En: Read ten more pages...
Zh: 再多看十页。

En: No. I won't subject myself to Graadian realism.
Zh: 不。我不会向格拉德的现实主义屈服的。

En: You've made the right life choices today, sir.
Zh: 先生，今天你做出了正确的人生抉择。

En: Their happiness is fleeting. A cruel jest. The absurdity of the state totally crushes them and they end up betraying each other. She becomes an official for "the regime" and he, a lens-grinder in Ferdydyrke. The end.
Zh: 他们的幸福转瞬即逝。这是一个残酷的玩笑。国家的荒谬彻底击垮了他们，他们最终背叛了彼此。她变成一名“管理体制”的官员，而他成为了弗迪德莱客的一个镜片研磨工。剧终。

En: Feels heavy.
Zh: 感觉很沉重。

En: What does she look like?
Zh: 她看起来什么样？

En: It would physically hurt you to keep reading. Are you sure?
Zh: 继续看下来的话，你的身体会受到伤害的。你确定吗？

En: Good stuff. You fucking *loved* this.
Zh: 好东西。我他妈就*喜欢*这个。

En: As ethnic tensions run wild, a pair of young lovers meet each other on the street. Somehow, in the middle of all the chaos, they manage to lock arms and look into each other's eyes.
Zh: 随着种族关系愈发紧张，一对年轻的恋人在街上相遇。不知何故，在一片混乱之中，他们紧挽手臂，看着彼此的眼睛。

En: That doesn't sound too bad...
Zh: 听上去还可以……

En: What happens next?
Zh: 接下来会怎么样？

En: At the beginning of the book, ethnic unrest has taken Yugo-Graad by storm. Windows and mailboxes are smashed, motor carriages turned over and set on fire...
Zh: 在书的开篇，一场民族骚乱席卷了南格拉德。窗户和邮箱被砸坏，汽車被掀翻点燃…

En: Black smoke hangs above cobblestones speckled with droplets of reddish-grey blood. Militant guardsmen, decked in heavy armour, have been called in to control the riots...
Zh: 黑烟笼罩在鹅卵石上——上面点缀着红灰色的血滴。好战的卫兵身披重甲，受命镇压暴动…

En: They go through a brief and somewhat awkward love affair, and in the end they betray each other and succumb to the absurdity of Graadian life. The man becomes a lens-grinder, completely abandoning his former existence...
Zh: 他们经历了一段短暂而又有些尴尬的风流韵事，最后他们背叛了彼此，屈服于荒谬的格拉德式生活。男人变成了一个镜片研磨工，彻底抛弃了之前的生活…

En: She spends the next several decades standing at a conveyor in a Sosnovor fish processing plant. The smell of fish guts slowly seeps into her hair and skin... as every single one of her dreams dies, one by one.
Zh: 接下来的十几年，她会一直站在索诺梵鱼品加工厂的传送带旁。鱼内脏的气味会逐渐渗入她的发丝和皮肤……她所有的梦想都将破灭，一个接着一个。

En: As ethnic tensions run wild, a pair of young lovers meet each other on the street. Somehow, in the middle of all the chaos, they manage to lock arms and look into each other's eyes.
Zh: 随着种族矛盾的加剧，一对年轻恋人在街头相遇了。不知为什么，在这场混乱中间，他们还是能紧紧相拥，深情凝望对方的双眼。

En: That doesn't sound too bad...
Zh: 听起来好像也不太糟…

En: What happens next?
Zh: 那接下来呢？

En: The memory of their short time spent together tortures the former lovers ceaselessly, until the end of their days... Years pass in solitude, their bodies growing ever more decrepit.
Zh: 那段短暂的记忆不断折磨着昔日的恋人，直到彼此生命的尽头…岁月在孤独中流逝，他们的身体变得越来越衰老。

En: He toils through the daily drudgery at the Lenka Polyfabricate. Happiness and fulfilment have eluded him his whole life, and in the end... he has nothing to do but dedicate himself to the craft.
Zh: 他在兰卡聚乙烯制造厂每日做着苦差。一生都没能得到幸福和成就，最后…别无选择的他只好全身心地投身于工艺之中。

En: And the woman?
Zh: 那个女人呢？

En: As life leaves their remains between the soiled sheets, their final thoughts are filled with regret.
Zh: 当生活把他们的残躯遗留在肮脏的床单上时，他们最后的念头里充满了悔恨。

En: This is terrible...
Zh: 真是太可怕了…

En: Why can't I put this book away?!
Zh: 为什么我不能把这本书放在一边呢？！

En: In your hands you hold 'Sixteen Days of Coldest April', by Yekartina Dahl. The cover image shows a row of concrete apartments, above which span a black-and-white rainbow.
Zh: 你手中握着的是*四月最冷的那16天*，作者是叶卡蒂娜·达尔。封面上画着一排混凝土公寓，在那上面是一道黑白彩虹。

En: You stare at the now-familiar black-and-white rainbow. The book feels heavy in your hands.
Zh: 你盯着这道已经熟悉的黑白彩虹。手里的这本书感觉很沉重。

En: Indeed. The book is *unusually* heavy in your hands, as though the cover were lined with lead...
Zh: 确实。手中的这本书感觉*异常*沉重，仿佛封面里内衬了铅……

En: They *would* do that in Graad.
Zh: 他们在格拉德*的确*会这么做。

En: Look at the back cover.
Zh: 看看封底。

En: How long is this book, anyway?
Zh: 话说这本书有多长？

En: [Put the book away.]
Zh: 把书放在一边。

En: The back cover is dominated by a black-and-white photograph of the author...
Zh: 封底主要是作者的一张黑白相片……

En: She can't be much older than her mid-30s in this photograph, and yet from this cover the eyes of a sad, old woman stare back at you.
Zh: 从相片来看，她最多不超过30来岁，不过封底上盯着你的，却是一双悲伤的老妇人的眼睛。

En: You flip through the book. The pages are thinner than you realized, and the type quite small and tightly set. It's nearly 600 pages long.
Zh: 褴褛飞旋你草草翻阅着这本书。书页比你想象的要薄，字体很小，排版紧凑。一共接近600页。褴褛飞旋

En: Real art is dense and difficult. If it didn't feel like you had to wrestle a suicidal bear to get through it, you weren't really reading.
Zh: 真正的艺术是密集而困难的。如果你觉得不需要跟一头有自杀性倾向的巨熊搏斗就能读完的话，那就不是真正的阅读。

En: Start reading.
Zh: 开始阅读。

En: In cold, detached prose, the author describes a scene from one of the Yugo-Graad Riots in the Twenties. Youths overturn motor carriages and set trash cans ablaze while heavily armoured guardsmen dash in and disperse them in a flurry of baton blows...
Zh: 作者以冷酷无情的口吻描述了二十年代南格拉德暴乱中的一幕。年轻人推翻机动車，点燃垃圾桶，与此同时，全副武装的卫兵冲入人群，胡乱挥舞警棍驱赶他们……

En: In excruciating detail, the author describes the socio-economic conditions that have brought the people of Yugo-Graad into the streets -- centuries of humiliations and despair. And then, during these riots, two young people meet...
Zh: 作者用折磨人的细节描绘了把南格拉德人民引上街头的社会经济状况……那是耻辱和绝望的世纪。然后，在这些暴动中，两个年轻人相遇了……

En: The Yugo-Graad Riots took place from '27 to '29, fuelled by ethnic unrest and the state's repressive tactics. These events are often seen as marking the end of a brief period of liberalization known as the Yugo-Graad Spring. Like all such periods, it is frequently memorialised in art and literature.
Zh: 南格拉德暴乱发生于27年至29年，种族骚乱及国家的镇压战术导致了冲突加剧。这些事件通常被视为南格拉德之春这段短暂自由化时期的终结。和所有类似的时期相同，它成为了频繁在艺术与文学作品中登场的纪念对象。

En: Don't be fooled. The bad times have already begun.
Zh: 别被骗了。艰难的时期已经开始了。

En: The lack of alcohol swelling is a bonus. Though there's a *pinch* of that in your cheeks already.
Zh: 没有因为酒精而肿胀也是一个加成。不过，你的脸上已经出现*一点*迹象了。

En: A name like armour.
Zh: 像是盔甲的名字。

En: The badge in your hands shines as you rotate it, catching light. You see lines of information:
Zh: 你转动手中的警徽，光线底下的它闪闪发亮。你在上面看见一行信息：

En: He trusts you -- for now. Try not to spoil it.
Zh: 他相信你——暂时而已。尽量别破坏它。

En: Still -- he doesn't trust you completely.
Zh: 不过——他还没有完全相信你。

En: Such a small yet precious thing. Expensive paper caught between thick plastic like a fly in amber. It reads:
Zh: 真是个小巧却又珍贵的东西。昂贵的纸张夹在厚厚的塑料中间，就像是困在琥珀里的苍蝇。上面写着：

En: The long and short of it is: you're his superior.
Zh: 长话短说：你是他的上级。

En: A new badge usually comes with a new rank. You seem to have been... doing well then.
Zh: “新警徽一般意味着新警衔。你似乎……表现地挺好的。”

En: But the badge is new. You used an old photo for a new badge.
Zh: 但是这枚警徽是新的。你的新警徽上用的是旧相片。

En: Good choice. A newer photo would look... different.
Zh: 明智的选择。新相片的话，看起来会……不太一样。

En:
Four months ago? Seems like a new document was recently made. One is handed to you as part of a promotion, or... if you lose the old one.	“四个月之前？这看起来像是最近才签发的。可能是在你晋升之后发给你的，也可能……是在你把之前的弄丢之后。”
0x010000060001C3EC	Dialogue Text	INVENTORY / BADGE	Logic	A new document is given to you as part of a promotion. Or when you lose the old one.	一份给你的新文件，属于晋升的部分资料。或者，是你把旧的弄丢了。
0x010000060001C3F5	Dialogue Text	INVENTORY / BADGE	Endurance	The pain in your chest tells you you were working yourself to death to earn that rank.	胸口的那阵疼痛告诉你，为了赢到那个警衔，你都快把自己给累死了。
0x010000060001C3FB	Dialogue Text	INVENTORY / BADGE	Electrochemistry	The case created a lot of *edge* you have to *take off*. The death march really gets us going.	这起案件出现了很多你必须*缓解*的*状况*。这次死亡之旅真的让我们热血沸腾。
0x010000060001C400	Dialogue Text	INVENTORY / BADGE	Logic	You're pretty sure you weren't doing *well* -- but better? Probably yes.	你很肯定自己表现的并*不好*——不过更好呢？也许吧。
0x010000060001C410	Dialogue Text	INVENTORY / BADGE	Horrific Necktie	I remember that time... the tie around your neck feels tight suddenly. That was a good time. We had a good work/drink balance going. What happened, man? Pump it up.	我还记得那段时间……你脖子上的领带突然收紧了。那是一段美好的时光。我们在工作和饮酒之间保持了很好的平衡。出什么事了，老兄？加油啊。
0x010000060001C41B	Dialogue Text	INVENTORY / BADGE	You	What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: It's a *tough* station to work in. You have all of Jamrock to cover -- that district should have *three* precincts, but money is what it is.
Zh: “在那个分局可*不太容易*。你们要负责整个加姆洛克——那个地区应该有*3*片管辖区，不过都是钱的问题。”

En: It's no wonder you are like you are, he thinks. But then again...
Zh: 也难怪你是这副模样，他想到。不过话又说回来……

En: But then again... A faint smile. "It's a legendary district. And a hell of a station too. It must be an honour and a curse to work with people like Pryce, McCoy, Berdyayeva..."
Zh: “话说回来……”警督露出一丝微笑。“那可是个传说般的辖区。你们的警局也名声远扬。能与普莱斯，麦考伊，别尔嘉耶娃等人共事，这一定是种荣耀，也是一种诅咒……”

En: And you? Is it an honour to work with you? Don't ask him, ask yourself.
Zh: 那你呢？跟你一起工作是一种荣耀吗？别问他，问问你自己。

En: He knew all those people although they're not from his station. They must be big.
Zh: 他知道这些并非来自他的警局的人。他们一定相当有名。

En: ...Roberts, Feuerbach, De Mettrie... suddenly names from your décomptage flash in your forebrain.
Zh: ……罗伯茨，费尔巴哈，德梅特里……突然间，这些名字如数家珍般闪现于前脑之中。

En: A police badge on which you see the photo of a man -- you. Some seaweed is stuck to the back.
Zh: 一枚警徽，你在上面看到一个男人的照片——是你。背后卡着一些海藻。

En: I found my badge!
Zh: “我找到我的警徽了！”

En: At least *something* good came out of all this. The lieutenant glances at the badge in your hands, frowning slightly. "You may have lied to me before about it missing, but... yes. At least you have it now."
Zh: “至少还是*有些*好结果的。”警督瞥了一眼你手中的警徽，微微皱了皱眉。“你之前也许是跟我撒谎说它不见了，不过……好吧。至少现在物归原主了。”

En: At least *something* good came out of all this. The lieutenant glances at the badge in your hands.
Zh: “至少还是*有些*好结果的。”警督瞥了一眼你手中的警徽。

En: The man in the photo looks at you solemnly.
Zh: 相片里的男人严肃地看着你。

En: Study the badge.
Zh: 研究警徽。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: You see: a photo, a name, a rank, a document number, the date of issue, and -- in the lower right corner -- your precinct.
Zh: 你看见：一张相片，一个名字，一个警衔，一串证件编号，签发日期，还有——在右下角——你所属的分局。

En: How old?
Zh: 多久以前的？

En: Eight, maybe ten years. The guy in the picture is rather good looking. He's got a nice haircut and is distinctly lacking in massive sideburns.
Zh: 八年，也许十年。相片上的男人真的很好看。发型精致，显然是少了那一大把连鬓胡子。

En: And he's winking? Why?
Zh: 他在眨眼？为什么？

En: Why do you think? His face is already contorted by the Expression. Although it looks less grotesque on him than it did on you.
Zh: 你为什么会这么想？他的脸已经被表情扭曲了。虽然在他那张脸上没那么诡异，不像你现在这张脸。

En: Why do you think? His face is already contorted by the Expression. Although it looks less grotesque on him than it does on you now.
Zh: 你为什么会这么想？他的脸已经被表情扭曲了。虽然出现在他那张脸上没那么诡异，不像你现在这张脸。

En: It looks better on him because he isn't in as much *pain* while producing it as you are now. Although there's already a hint of that pain, certainly.
Zh: 在他脸上看起来更好，是因为他不像你现在这么*痛苦*。不过，肯定是一点点痛苦的痕迹了。

En: Don't be fooled. The bad times have already begun.
Zh: 别被骗了。艰难的时期已经开始了。

En: Wake up the past and tell it to stay awake -- the bad times are here to stay.
Zh: 唤醒过去，让它保持清醒——艰难的时期就在这里。

En: No, he's not. He's going keep calling you "officer" when he's angry with you -- and "detective" when he's not.
Zh: 不，他不会。当他生你气的时候，他会继续叫你“警官”——不生气的时候叫“警探”。

En: It's meant to keep you safe.
Zh: 这是为了保护你的安全。

En: ...from bullets, hunger, shrapnel, kidnappings, and worse.
Zh: ……子弹，饥饿，榴弹，绑架，还有更糟糕的。

En: He's not going to call you Harrier. He'll keep calling you 'officer' when he's angry with you and 'detective' when he's not.
Zh: 他不会叫你哈里尔的。生气的时候他会叫你‘警官’，不生气的时候叫‘警探’。

En: What is a 'lieutenant double-yefreitor?'
Zh: “‘双重荣誉警督’是什么意思？”

En: The lieutenant is a rank above sergeant and below captain. It's the highest rank in the RCM that still does field work. I am a lieutenant.
Zh: “警督的警衔在队长之上，警长之下。对于仍然参与现场工作的警察来说，这是最高警衔。我就是一名警督。”

En: And double-yefreitor?
Zh: “那双重荣誉呢？”

En: The title of yefreitor is added to your rank when you decline a promotion to a higher rank -- in your case, captain, the lieutenant explains. "You have declined twice. Thus you're double-yefreitor."
Zh: “当你拒绝升迁的时候，警衔里就会添加荣誉称号——以你的情况来看，是升到警长，”警督解释到。“你拒绝过两次。所以你是双重荣誉。”

En: Declined?
Zh: “拒绝？”

En: There are many reasons one would do this. The rank above you in your precinct's *décomptage* might be taken. Or sometimes promoted officers do not *want* to replace their superiors -- out of respect.
Zh: “这样做的原因有很多种。也许在你们分局的*倒装体系*里，在你之上的警衔已经有人了。或者有时候，获得升迁的警官*不想*取代他们的上级——出于尊重。”

En: And sometimes, he continues, "they just prefer the type of police work available to their current rank -- in your case, lieutenant."
Zh: “还有些时候，”他继续说到，“他们只是更喜欢现有警衔的工作——你的情况就是这样，警督。”

En: Heavy-duty case-solving machine.
Zh: 重型破案机器。

En: The countdown is modelled after the dual-leadership system employed by the Left during the Revolution. Which in turn was developed by last century experimental psychologists in the University of Koenigstein.
Zh: 倒计时系统效仿的是大革命时期左翼采用的双重领导体系。在上个世纪，专门由柯尼希施泰因大学的实验心理学家研发出来的。

En: The lowest rank is junior officer -- usually teenagers. Then there are the patrol officers. Then sergeants, lieutenants, and then a captain. That's basically it, except for a few kinks.
Zh: “最低的警衔是初级警员——通常是青少年。然后是巡警。接下来是队长，警督和警长。基本上就是这个样子的，除了一些特例。”

En: What kinks?
Zh: “什么特例？”

En: Kinks like satellite-officers and the additional yefreitor rank I already explained.
Zh: “比如说随迁警官和额外添加的荣誉警衔，我已经解释过了。”

En: Wait -- satellite-officers?
Zh: “等等——随迁警官？”

En: Thanks for explaining all this. (Don't mention it.)
Zh: “感谢你的解释。”（别提了。）

En: My pleasure.
Zh: “不客气。”

En: You are given the title of 'satellite-officer' if your partner is quickly promoted through the ranks and you rise with him. You don't seem to be a satellite...
Zh: “如果你的搭档警衔提升很快，而且你也跟着一起提升，那你就会获得‘随迁警官’的称号。你看起来不像是随迁的……”

En: So you've been putting up with all my bullshit because I'm your superior?
Zh: “所以你一直容忍我这些胡话，是因为我是你的上级？”

En: So you've been putting up with me because we're of the same rank?
Zh: “所以你一直容忍我这些胡话，是因为我们是平级？”

En: I'm not putting up with anything, the lieutenant says with a little smile. "You put your life on the line and we came out on top. That matters."
Zh: “我没容忍任何东西，”警督笑着说到。“你让自己命悬一线，而我们最后也获得了成功。这一点很重要。”

En: No. I've been putting up with you because, despite an unconventional approach, you're doing good police work.
Zh: “不是。我一直容忍你，是因为不管你的方法到底有多不常规，但是警察的本职工作做的很好。”

En: More than driving your motor carriage into the sea. The upside of that is, of course, you finding your badge.
Zh: “比你开着汽車冲进大海更重要。那样做的好处，当然就是找到了你的警徽。”

En: It matters more than driving your motor carriage into the sea. He smiles encouragingly. "And now we've even found your badge."
Zh: “这比你开着汽車冲进大海更重要。”他朝你露出一个鼓励的笑容。“而且甚至还找到了你的警徽。”

En: The numbers are not there for 'good measure'. They have an administrative purpose. One that's unfortunately been erased from your memory.
Zh: 这些数字可不是什么表现出色的‘衡量标准’。它们的目的是为了行政管理。很不幸，这一点也从你的记忆力里清除掉了。

En: Encased between two durable plastic sheets is a bluish card with lines of information -- and a watermark in the shape of the street grid of Revachol West.
Zh: 在两块耐用塑料布之间包裹着一张黛青色卡片，上面有一行行信息——以及瑞瓦肖西部街道网格形状的水印。

En: Look at the photo.
Zh: 查看相片。

En: Rank: LTN-2JFR?
Zh: “警衔：LTN-2JFR？”

En: Name: Harrier Du Bois...
Zh: “姓名：哈里尔·杜博阿……”

En: Date of issue: 7th of November, '50...
Precinct: 41?
The man keeps winking at you with his green-grey eyes. The photo is old, no doubt about that.
Zh: 灰绿色眼睛的男人一直不停地朝你使眼色。相片很老，这一点毫无疑问。

En: Put the badge away. [Leave.]
Zh: 把警徽放在一边。[离开。]

En: It looks like you had quite the charming face -- way back then. Your hair is brown and slightly curly, your eyes seem more clear somehow.
Zh: 看来你的脸应该挺迷人的——不过是很久以前的事了。你的头发是棕色的，微卷，不知道为什么，眼睛好像要更清澈一些。

En: 'Harrier,' that's long for 'Harry'. So you *are* Harry... He thinks. "Evrart was half right. Probably not a lot of people know your full name. Whoever told him you're Harry Du Bois didn't."
Zh: “‘哈里尔，’那是‘哈里’的长名称。所以你*是*哈里……”他想到。“艾弗拉特对了一半。知道你全名的人可能并不多。不管哈里·杜博阿这个名字是谁告诉他的，肯定不知道这个。”

En: Harrier Du Bois... He looks you up and down. "It suits you. I'm going to call you 'Harry,' since it's short for 'Harrier'."
Zh: “哈里尔·杜博阿……”他上下打量着你。“很称你。我就叫你‘哈里’吧，毕竟是‘哈里尔’的短名。”

En: This document confirms it -- Harrier Du Bois, i.e. Harry, is really your name.
Zh: 这份文件证实了这一点——哈里尔·杜博阿，也就是，哈里，的确是你的真名。

En: This is your name. You are Harrier Du Bois. Harry.
Zh: 这是你的名字。你叫哈里尔·杜博阿。哈里。

En: Wait, what kind of name is 'Harrier'?
Zh: “等等，‘哈里尔’到底是什么名字？”

En: Strange. It doesn't say Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau.
Zh: “奇怪。上面显示的不是拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托。”

En: Not strange at all -- your name is Harrier Du Bois. Like it says on your police badge.
Zh: “一点也不奇怪——你的名字叫哈里尔·杜博阿。警徽上就是这么说的。”

En: It's a wartime name. Revolutionary. The kind mothers give their sons during troubled times. Like 'Undying' or 'Boxer' or 'Ironhide'.
Zh: “这是一个战时的名字。革命者的名字。就是那种母亲会在乱世给儿子起的名字。比如‘安代因’（不朽）或者‘博克瑟’（拳击手）或者‘艾恩海德’（铁皮）。”

En: That's what it says on the badge. No more information is provided.
Zh: 警徽上就是这些说的。没有其他信息了。

En: The badge in your hands shines as you rotate it, catching light. You see lines of information on it. And a shining watermark.
Zh: 你转动手中的警徽，光线底下的它闪闪发亮。你在上面看见一行信息。还有一个闪亮的水印。

En: Lieutenant double-yefreitor.
Zh: “双重荣誉警督。”

En: You should look at the badge when Lieutenant Kitsuragi is around -- he could help you make sense of all this.
Zh: 你应该等曷城警督在身边的时候再查看警徽——他可以帮你理解这一切。

En: The number shows that this badge belongs to an officer of the 41st precinct.
Zh: 号码显示这枚警徽属于一个41分局的警官。

En: Yes. It's the designation of your Precinct. Forty-one. Like mine says Fifty-seven. The 57th is mostly Industrial Harbour. A lot of asphalt. The 41st is... He stops.
Zh: “是的。这是你警局的名称。41。我的上面写着57。57主要是工业港口。有很多飙車狂。41……”他停了下来。

En: The badge was issued roughly four months ago.
Zh: 警徽大约是4个月以前签发的。

En: Four months ago -- I'm guessing that's when you were promoted to the rank of lieutenant double-yefreitor.
Zh: “4个月之前——我猜你就是在那个时候升为双重荣誉警督的。”

En: A lot can happen in four months. Especially in winter. The winters are never easy on you, of that you are sure.
Zh: 4个月可能发生很多事情。特别是在冬季。冬季对你来说从来都不好过，这一点你可以肯定。

En: Serial: REV12-62-05-JAM41...
That's just the serial number. Revachol, Jamrock, Precinct 41, with some numbers thrown in there for good measure.
Zh: “那只是编号。瑞瓦肖，加姆洛克，41分局，另外还有一些数字。”

En: Revachol, Jamrock, Precinct 41. You don't remember what the numbers in the middle mean.
Zh: 瑞瓦肖，加姆洛克，41分局。你不记得中间的数字是什么意思。

En: What's a 'décomptage'?
Zh: “倒装是什么？”

En: Turn back to the document.
Zh: 回到证件上去。

En: I thought my rank was *drunk*.
Zh: “我以为自己的警衔是*醉鬼*。”

En: Yes, apparently you've had a rather successful career in the past and, -- he pauses to examine you -- "this leads me to believe maybe your current *situation* is only temporary."
Zh: “是的，显然你过去的职业生涯非常成功，而且……”他停下来打量着你。“……这也让我相信，也许你目前遇到的*状况*只是临时的。”

En: I won't forsake the drink -- it makes me happy.
Zh: “我不会断了喝酒的念想——它能让我开心。”

En: Thanks. That gives me hope.
Zh: “谢谢。这给了我希望。”

En: I'm afraid there are no ex-alcoholics.
Zh: “恐怕并没有什么*前*酒鬼。”

En: I don't want to get better -- I want to get worse.
Zh: “我不想变好——我想变得更糟糕。”

En: Good, he says with a quick smile.
Zh: “很好，”他笑着说到。

En: People have been through worse and gotten back on their feet, lieutenant double-yefreitor, he says with a reassuring little smile.
Zh: “经历过更糟糕事情的人都能重新站稳脚跟，双重荣誉警督，”他一边说着，一边露出了一个安慰的微笑。

En: I... uh, don't really know how to respond to that, lieutenant double-yefreitor.
Zh: “我……呃，真的不知道该怎么回应，双重荣誉警督。”

En: It does seem to be a rather *special* relationship the two of you have forged, yes, lieutenant double-yefreitor.
Zh: “看起来你们俩之前的关系是很*特别*，没错，双重荣誉警督。”

En: Décomptage is the hierarchical system employed by the Revachol Citizens Militia. It means counting down to twos.
Zh: “倒装是瑞瓦肖公民武装采用的等级体系。它的意思是倒数到二。”

En: But... I don't *want* to be Harrier Du Bois. (Don't accept it.)
Zh: “但是……我*不想*当哈里尔·杜博阿。”（不要接受。）

En: Why? It's a cool name. I like it. He shrugs. "Besides, you're Harry anyway. No one's ever called *Harrier*."
Zh: “为什么？这名字挺酷的啊。我喜欢。”他耸耸肩。“而且，不管怎么说，你都是哈里。没人叫过你*哈里尔*。”

En: Harrier Du Bois it is, then. (Accept it.)
Zh: “那就哈里尔·杜博阿吧。”（接受它。）

En: Pleased to make your acquaintance, Harrier Du Bois.
Zh: “很高兴认识你，哈里尔·杜博阿。”

En: These boots were probably left on the corpse because they were impossible to pull off while it was hanging. They're not exactly pleasant to look at right now, much less smell.
Zh: 靴子留在尸体上很可能是因为悬挂状态下没办法脱下来。现在看着都让人觉得不太舒服，更不用说那股味道了。

En: Even less pleasant now that you know the name of the man pieces of whose flesh are still stuck to the insides of the boots.
Zh: 更令人不舒服的是，现在你已经知道了这个男人的名字，而他的血肉还困在鞋子里面。

En: You missed your chance to learn more about them by telling Alice the wrong serial number.
Zh: 你把错误的序列号告诉了爱丽丝，这让你失去了进一步了解它们的机会。

En: Hopefully you will know who they belonged to very soon.
Zh: 希望你很快能知道它们属于谁。

En: Hopefully soon you'll know more about them.
Zh: 希望很快你会更了解它们一些。

En: Fairweather T-500 / VE is imprinted under each heel.
Zh: 每只脚底上都印着“费尔韦瑟T-500/VE”。

En: You'll know more about them once you run the serial number you found.
Zh: 一旦查阅过你发现的序列号之后，你就能更了解它们了。

En: Look at the soles of the boots.
Zh: 看看靴子底部。

En: Look inside the boots.
Zh: 看看靴子内部。

En: [Put the boots away.]
Zh: [把靴子放在一边。]

En: Look the boots over carefully.
Zh: 仔细地检查靴子。

En: Still just pieces of sock and rotting cadaver.
Zh: 还是一些袜子和腐坏的尸体。

En: Graceful whorls cover the boots' glossy surface. These will be gorgeous once you get them clean.
Zh: 优雅的螺纹盖住了靴子光滑的表面。只要把它们擦干净，它们又会变回华丽灿烂的样子。

En: All those graceful whorls again, and the serial number hidden among them.
Zh: 又是那些优雅的螺纹，中间还隐藏着序列号。

En: Will I ever be able to wear them?
Zh: 我还能有机会穿上它们吗？

En: Isn't there a saying about walking in another man's shoes?
Zh: 不是有种说法叫穿着别人的鞋子走路吗（站在别人的角度思考问题）？

En: The size seems about right. Once you get the foul-smelling flesh-gunk out.
Zh: 大小似乎是合适的。只要你能把那些臭烘烘的烂肉给弄出来。

En: You've seen too much to be that naive, even if you don't remember it. You'll never know what it was like to be him.
Zh: 就算你已经记不得，不过你看过这么多东西，总不会那么天真吧。你永远也不会知道成为他那样的人是什么感觉。

En: Okay. What more?
Zh: 好吧。还有什么？

En: Cool.
Zh: 酷毙了。

En: It must be glorious to behold. Dark pink and snow white.
Zh: 看上去肯定很壮观。深粉加雪白。

En: The *bang* is for all the scary things in the world... that may happen at any time.
Zh: 世界上所有可怕的东西都跟*砰的一声*有关……任何时候都可能会发生。

En: Performing? Yesss, birdie!
Zh: 表演？哦耶，小鸟不错啊！

En: [Put the book away.]
Zh: [把书放在一边。]

En: Read about the funeral cockatoo.
Zh: 了解葬礼凤头鹦鹉。

En: Read about the Major Majestic cockatoo.
Zh: 了解威严凤头鹦鹉。

En: Read about the most common bang-bang cockatoo.
Zh: 了解最普通的砰砰凤头鹦鹉。

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: This is a yellow-tailed black cockatoo. Its specific name *Psittacus funereus* relates to its dark and sombre plumage. This bird looks as if dressed for a funeral, 24/7. There is something indisputably ominous about it.
Zh: 这是一种长着黄色尾巴的黑凤头鹦鹉，它的专用名*非洲灰鹦鹉属黑凤头鹦鹉*跟它那身漆黑又阴郁的羽毛有关。这种鸟看起来像是要去参加葬礼一样，24小时/7天。毫无疑问，它身上有种不吉利的感觉。

En: Yes, but there has to be something *great* about cockatoos as well.
Zh: 是的，不过凤头鹦鹉身上肯定也有一些*很棒*的地方。

En: Alright, what problems do these birds have?
Zh: 好吧，这些鸟有什么问题？

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: Where to even begin? All cockatoos are known for their needy natures, requiring attention for at least two full hours a day. They love to talk, and have been described as 'loveable clowns who just don't know how to wrap up'.
Zh: 该从哪里说起呢？所有凤头鹦鹉都是以性情乖僻而闻名的，每天至少需要整整两个小时的关注。它们喜欢说话，甚至被说成是‘不知道该如何收场的可爱小丑’。

En: Honestly, this bird does sound a bit like me...
Zh: 说实在的，这只鸟听起来确实跟我很像……

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: You're right, cockatoos are magnificent creatures. They love to perform, cuddle and show off, and will even scream for *fun* -- often as loud as up to *135* decibels!
Zh: 你说得对，凤头鹦鹉的确是一种非常华丽的生物。它们喜欢表演，拥抱和炫耀，甚至会因为*好玩*而大声尖叫——通常会达到*135*分贝！

En: Ouch. That must hurt.
Zh: 哎哟。那可就有些伤人了。

En: I could handle that... It would be nice to have someone to talk to when feeling lonely.
Zh: 我能承受的住……每当感到孤独的时候，有人能说说话肯定挺好的。

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: It is not recommended to get a cockatoo if you're not able to cook them food every day. And give them the full care that they need. These birds will never understand that you have a life of your own.
Zh: 如果你不能每天为它们烹饪食物，给它们一切所需的全心照顾，那就不推荐你饲养凤头鹦鹉。这种鸟永远也不会理解你也有自己的生活。

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: Yes, but all those cockatoo species are so different! Which one are you?
Zh: 是的，不过凤头鹦鹉那些种类都很不一样！你是哪一种？

En: The grey and common bang-bang, that's me.
Zh: 普通的灰色砰砰凤头鹦鹉，那就是我。

En: Only the funeral cockatoo in its darkness can truly grasp the depths of my doom-laden soul.
Zh: 只有漆黑的葬礼凤头鹦鹉才能真正触及到我充满厄运的灵魂深处。

En: Isn't that obvious?! Major Majestic, here I come!
Zh: 难道还不够明显吗？威严凤头鹦鹉，我来啦。

En: But what if I am just a *fuckupatoo* -- a fuck-up *and* a cockatoo?!
Zh: 不过如果我只是一只*操蛋凤头鹦鹉*呢——操蛋的人加凤头鹦鹉？！

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: You're right, you're a fuckatoo embodied. This sorry-ass bird belongs on your heraldry! It probably doesn't even have feathers and only screams.
Zh: 你说得对，你就是操蛋凤头鹦鹉的具现。这只可怜的小鸟属于你的纹章！它甚至都没有羽毛，只会尖叫。

En: I have to take some time to think about it.
Zh: 我必须花时间考虑一下。

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: Understandable. Choosing your heraldic bird isn't a trivial question.
Zh: 能理解。选择纹章鸟类可不是个微不足道的小问题。

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: You're right, you're majesty embodied. This big beautiful bird belongs on your heraldry!
Zh: 你说得对，你就是威严的具现。这只美丽的大鸟就是属于你的纹章！

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: You're right, you're *Psittacus funereus* embodied. This ominous bird belongs on your heraldry!
Zh: 你说得对，你就是*非洲灰鹦鹉属黑凤头鹦鹉*的具现。这只不祥的鸟儿就是属于你的纹章！

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: You're right, you're the mediocrity embodied. This unsung bird belongs on your heraldry!
Zh: 你说得对，你就是平庸的具现。这只被埋没的鸟儿就是属于你的纹章！

En: Who would ever want a chatty companion?! I want someone who knows how to *listen*.
Zh: 谁会想要一个爱说话的同伴啊？！我想要个懂得如何*倾听*的人。

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: Pet owners also report moodiness, loudness and hostility as reoccurring issues. If left unsatisfied cockatoos may scream non-stop, pluck their feathers or become aggressive.
Zh: 宠物主人还报告说喜怒无常，吵闹，敌对情绪的状况会反复出现。如果凤头鹦鹉不满意，它们可能会无休无止的尖叫，拔自己的羽毛，或者变得很有攻击性。

En: Right. That's pretty bad. Anything else?
Zh: 没错。真的挺糟糕的。还有别的吗？

En: Not great for the neighbours.
Zh: 对邻居来说不太好吧。

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: Perhaps the most *impressive* of all the species, the endangered Major Majestic cockatoo is often described as the most flamboyant bird in the jungle, its pink-coloured wings and flowing crest embellishing its proud and *bumptious* nature.
Zh: 濒临灭绝的威严凤头鹦鹉也许是其中最让人*印象深刻*的一种，它们通常被称作是丛林里最艳丽的鸟类，那对粉色的翅膀，还有流动的羽冠修饰着它骄傲又*自大*的本性。

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: In the words of poet-explorer Sir James Fournier: "Few birds more enliven the monotonous hues of the verdant forest than this big, bold and beautiful species."
Zh: 用诗人探险家詹姆斯·福尼尔爵士的话说：“很少有鸟儿能像这个硕大、大胆又美丽的物种一样，为单调青翠的森林带来勃勃生机。”

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: Despite its *banging* name, the bang-bang cockatoo is actually the shyest of the species. Common in almost all Semenese forests, as well as zoos and homes all over the world, its plumage is mainly grey and white. The Semenese name *bang-bang* is thought to be of onomatopoeic origins.
Zh: 尽管名字很*吵闹*，其实砰砰凤头鹦鹉是其中最害羞的一种。它们在塞美尼的所有丛林都很常见，同样还有全世界的动物园和私人家里，它的羽毛主要是灰色和白色的。塞美尼名字*砰砰*被认为是拟声的起源。

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: A cockatoo is a parrot with an erectile crest, found on the Semenine islands and in southern Face-à-la-Mer. Known for their intelligence and general precociousness, cockatoos are popular birds in aviculture; however, they often exhibit various behavioural issues.
Zh: 凤头鹦鹉是一种长着直立羽冠的鹦鹉，是在塞美尼群岛和法斯拉美南部被发现的。以才智和普遍早熟而闻名的凤头鹦鹉在鸟类饲养界很受欢迎；然而，它们经常会表现出各种各样的行为问题。

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: This book talks about the delicate nature of 'toos, as well as introducing some of the most popular species among the bird enthusiasts: the funeral cockatoo, the Major Majestic cockatoo, and the most common bang-bang cockatoo. It's colourfully illustrated.
Zh: 这本书讲述了凤头鹦鹉的微妙本质，同时为鸟类爱好者介绍了一些最受欢迎的物种：葬礼凤头鹦鹉，威严凤头鹦鹉，还有最普通的砰砰凤头鹦鹉。里面配有很鲜艳的插图。

En: Close your eyes. Reflect upon your core essence -- *really* bore into the depths of your soul and reach a hand toward the book stand.
Zh: 闭上眼睛。仔细回想你的核心本质——*真正*钻进你的灵魂深处，把手伸向书架。

En: You see an old, out-of-shape drunkard of a cop incapable of solving a simple case. Imaginary worlds can't save you.
Zh: 你看见一个身材走样、酒气冲天的年迈警察，连一个简单的案子都破获不了。想象中的世界是无法拯救你的。

En: I can feel it.
Zh: 我能感觉到。

En: What the hell?
Zh: 什么鬼？

En: What does this mean?
Zh: 这是什么意思？

En: Standard Wirrâl die.
Zh: 标准威勒尔骰子。

En: I feel profound kinship with this man.
Zh: 我能感觉到跟这个人有着深厚的感情。

En: A stocky man on the cover art of the codex looks straight at you. He is horrendously hairy with a bulbous nose. He wields a large battle axe and grins like a maniac.
Zh: 古抄本封面图片上那个矮胖的男人直直地盯着你。他毛发多得简直是骇人听闻，还长着一个圆球状的大鼻子。他挥舞着一把巨大的战斧，像疯子一样龇牙咧嘴地笑着。

En: Is this really me?
Zh: 这真是我吗？

En: What do I find?
Zh: 我发现了什么？

En: The normal dweorgr are a breed intelligent yet greedy who drink hard and do battle even harder. Yet behind the brutish exterior, they also enjoy braiding their hair and beard, and going on heroic quests. This latter activity is most common for shield dweorgr.
Zh: 普通的矮人拥有智能，却贪婪成性，嗜酒如命，打起仗来更不要命。在粗犷的外表之下，他们其实热衷于把头发和胡子编成辫子，投身于英勇的使命。持盾矮人更倾向于后者。

En: Dweorgr, huh? The lieutenant shrugs. "I was always more of a sidhe or huldur guy myself."
Zh: “矮人，是吗？”警督耸耸肩。“我自己一般更偏向仙族或者丛林仙女。”

En: The title reads: "Codex Urik: the Industrious Dweorgr of Wirrâl". It's an introductory race creation and dweorg cultural backstory manual.
Zh: 标题是：“尤里克法典：威勒尔的勤勉矮人。”这是一本介绍种族创作和矮人文化背景故事的手册。

En: I'm not sure this is the correct choice.
Zh: 我不确定这是正确的选择。

En: Embrace your inner dweorg. You're a tall dweorg. Become the Dweorg King.
Zh: 拥抱你内心深处的矮人。你是一个高个矮人。成为了矮人国王。

En: Like a tiny homunculus, the dweorg lives within your blue soul.
Zh: 矮人就像小妖精一样生活在你的蓝色灵魂里。

En: Very funny.
Zh: 很有趣。

En: [Put the book away.]
Zh: [把书放在一边。]

En: The super fancy topping pie is waiting for you to dig in.
Zh: 超级美味的馅饼正在等待你的品尝。

En: You should eat it slowly, really savour the chef's concept.
Zh: 你应该慢点吃，真正领会厨师的理念。

En: Eating this pie will almost certainly make you less of a man. What were you thinking, agreeing to take a wheat-free, meat-free pie from that sleazy bastard?
Zh: 吃掉这个馅饼会让你变得不太爷们，这一点几乎是可以肯定的。你到底在想什么，居然会从那个低级的杂种那里拿走这个不含小麦，还不含肉的馅饼？

En: Consume the pie contemplatively.
Zh: 一边吃馅饼，一边沉思。

En: Hey, Kim, share this topping pie with me?
Zh: “喂，金，要不要跟我一起分享这个馅饼？”

En: Leave it for later. [Put the topping pie away.]
Zh: 之后再说吧。[把馅饼放在一边。]

En: Throw the topping pie on the ground. That seems to be the thing you do.
Zh: 把馅饼扔到地上。那才比较像是你会做的事。

En: Throw the topping pie on the ground. You're not taking any bribes from questionable persons.
Zh: 把馅饼扔到地上。你不会从可疑人员那里收受任何贿赂。

En: You can barely taste what you're eating as you shove the slices in your mouth, but you can tell: it's doing your body good. Soon it's all gone -- just some wheat-free flour left on your hands.
Zh: 当你把切片塞进嘴里咀嚼的时候，几乎没尝到什么味道，不过你可以看出来：它对你的身体很有好处。很快它就全没了——你的手上只剩下一些不含小麦的面粉。

En: I'm hungover and *famished*! Who cares what's on it? Devour that topping pie as quickly as you can! It's what you do with food.
Zh: 我宿醉未醒，而且都快*饿死*了！谁在乎上面有什么？用你最快的速度吞掉这个馅饼！你都是这么对待食物的。

En: I'm hungover and *famished*! Who cares what's on it? Devour that topping pie!
Zh: 我宿醉未醒，而且都快*饿死*了！谁在乎上面有什么？狼吞虎咽地吃掉这个馅饼吧！

En: It's an unexpectedly pleasing combination of flavours and textures -- salty crunch of meatless bacon, butteriness of avocado, sweet bursts of pineapple and fig tempered by the pungency of basil, with a kick from the pepper, paprika, and lime... You have to admit: even the wheat-free crust is pretty good.
Zh: 口感和风味组合在一起，意外地让人愉悦——咸脆的无肉培根，奶油状的牛油果，香甜的菠萝，被罗勒的辛辣中和过的无花果，再加上一些胡椒，辣椒和酸橙……你必须承认：就连不含小麦的外壳也很不错。

En: Even such a profound experience of topping pie has to come to an end, though. Soon, you have nothing but flour on your hands.
Zh: 不过，即使是如此意义深远的馅饼体验之旅也会走到尽头。很快，你手上就只剩下一些面粉。

En: The pie lies sadly face-down on the ground, a waste of culinary creativity, some of its colourful toppings scattered around it.
Zh: 馅饼悲伤地躺在地面上，正面朝下，这是对烹饪创意的一种浪费。有些彩色的馅料散落在周围。

En: The lieutenant looks at you wearily. "Please let's not go through this again."
Zh: 警督疲惫地看着你。“请不要再做这种事情了。”

En: No, thank you.
Zh: “不用了，谢谢。”

En: Don't you ever eat?
Zh: “难道你都不吃东西的吗？”

En: Please, Kim. At least take one slice. It's really too big for me, and it's going to just grow colder and soggier if I keep any part of it...
Zh: “拜托，金。至少来一片吧。对我来说有点太大了，如果一直放着的话，只会变得更冷更软。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: The pie is still in your hands, its round face untouched, unbroken.
Zh: 馅饼还在你手上，它那张圆圆的脸仍然没有改变，完好无损。

En: He sighs. "Don't take it personally."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“别往心里去。”

En: The lieutenant crosses his arms. "Food of my choice, on my own schedule."
Zh: 警督抱起双臂。“我会自己选择食物，按点进食。”

En: He thinks about it for a moment, then gingerly picks up a slice and starts chewing on it thoughtfully.
Zh: 他考虑了一会儿，然后小心翼翼地拿起一片，若有所思地咀嚼了起来。

En: You ate that one man's salami. Why won't you eat my topping pie?
Zh: “你都吃过那个人的萨拉米香肠了。为什么不愿意吃的馅饼呢？”

En: I've seen you share the food of other people! Can't help but wonder if you consider me an enemy.
Zh: “我见过你跟其他人一起分享食物！我忍不住会想你是不是把我当敌人了。”

En: I just don't want any. Please, enjoy, and then let's move on.
Zh: “我只是不想吃而已。请自己好好享受，然后我们继续前进。”

En: That's a ridiculous notion. We are not living in a highly ritualistic society where not sharing food with someone indicates animosity. We are in Revachol, where people ought to be free not to eat topping pie when they are not hungry.
Zh: “这个想法太荒谬了。我们并没有生活在一个高度仪式化的社会，不跟某人分享食物并不代表敌意。我们生活在瑞瓦肖，当你没有感觉到饥饿的时候，应该可有选择不吃馅饼的自由。”

En: No, but really! You must..! (Shove the topping pie into Kim's hands.)
Zh: “不，不过说真的！你必须来一片……！”（把一片馅饼塞到金的手里。）

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: The lieutenant takes the pie from you and calmly lets it fall to the ground, face down. "All done. Shall we?"
Zh: 警督从你手中接过馅饼，面不改色地让它掉到地上，正面朝下。“搞定。就这样吧？”

En: Thank you, partner. (Eat the rest of the pie.)
Zh: “谢谢你，搭档。”（吃掉剩下的馅饼。）

En: He finishes his slice and brushes the wheat-free flour off his hands. "That wasn't terrible. Well, shall we?"
Zh: 他吃完了自己的那一片，拍掉了手上不含小麦的面粉。“其实也没那么可怕。好吧，可以走了吗？”

En: His tone is almost stern beneath the politeness.
Zh: 在那份礼貌之下，他的语气几乎可以说是严厉的。

En: He sighs. "Haven't I already proven that we are comrades or whatever by splitting that sandwich with you?"
Zh: 他叹了一口气。“我跟你分享那个三明治的时候，不是已经证明我们是同伴或者什么之类的了吗？”

En: But that was just a sandwich! This is a topping pie!
Zh: “但那只是个三明治啊！这个是馅饼！”

En: I was just asking out of politeness.
Zh: “我这么问只是出于礼貌。”

En: Every meal we share strengthens our bond!
Zh: “我们分享的每一顿饭都会加强我们之间的团结！”

En: Why won't you *ever* share my food with me, Kim?
Zh: “你为什么*从来*没有跟我分享过食物呢，金？”

En: Why won't you share my food with me, Kim?
Zh: “你为什么不跟我分享食物呢，金？”

En: He raises his eyebrows. "You know, you really should consider doing to liquor what you do to food." He pauses thoughtfully. "And not doing what you do to food... to food."
Zh: 他扬起眉毛。“你知道吗，你应该考虑像对待食物一样对待酒。”他停下来，若有所思。“而不是像对待食物那样……对待食物。”

En: He inclines his head. "Why...?" he starts, then thinks better of it.
Zh: 他歪起头。“为什么……？”他有些吃惊，然后又改变了注意。

En: I thought it was the symbolism that was important, not the type of... He takes off his glasses and rubs his forehead. "Please, just... enjoy the pie."
Zh: “我觉得这是一种重要的象征，而不是那种类型……”他取下眼镜，摸了摸额头。“拜托，你还是……好好享受你的馅饼吧。”

En: Well, then: no, thank you.
Zh: “那好吧：不用了，谢谢。”

En: That depends on one's perspective. He takes off his glasses and rubs his forehead. "Please, just... enjoy the pie."
Zh: “这取决于某人的观点。”他取下眼镜，摸了摸额头。“拜托，你还是……好好享受你的馅饼吧。”

En: You can barely taste what you're eating as you shove the slices in your mouth, but you can tell: it's doing your body good. Soon it's all gone -- just some wheat-free flour left on your hands.
Zh: 当你把切片塞进嘴里咀嚼的时候，几乎没尝到什么味道，不过你可以看出来：它对你的身体很有好处。很快它就全没了——你的手上只剩下一些不含小麦的面粉。

En: Even such a profound experience of topping pie has to come to an end, though. Soon, you have nothing but flour on your hands.
Zh: 不过，即使是如此意义深远的馅饼体验之旅也会走到尽头。很快，你手上就只剩下一些面粉。

En: Thank you, partner. (Eat the rest of the pie.)
Zh: “谢谢你，搭档。”（吃掉剩下的馅饼。）

En: The pie is still in your hands, its round face untouched, unbroken.
Zh: 馅饼还在你手上，它那张圆圆的脸仍然没有改变，完好无损。

En: There is no loophole. The simple truth is the current residents are going to lose their street access and for the next 12-40 months their lives will be dominated by constant construction noise right next door.
Zh: 里面没有漏洞。事实很简单：目前这些居民将会失去街道通道，在接下来的12-40个月里，他们的生活将会被隔壁连续不断的建筑噪音所支配。

En: Wait, what are the ramifications of this?
Zh: 等等，这里有什么影响呢？

En: Look, Kim... (Point to the photocopy.) "These people are going to have to move away because of the noise."
Zh: “看啦，金……”（指着复印件。）“这些人会因为噪音被迫搬走的。”

En: This is terrible. Poor people. Can't I do something?
Zh: 这也太可怕了。那群可怜人。我就不能做点什么吗？

En: Once the construction starts it'll probably take a few months -- a year maybe -- for even the most stubborn occupants to get tired of living like this. After that they'll sell their property for cheap and move out.
Zh: 一旦施工开始，可能会花费几个月——也许是一年——即使是最顽固的钉子户也会厌倦这种生活。在那之后，他们会便宜卖掉自己的财产，然后搬出去。

En: Of course... I should have seen it. The lieutenant frowns as he reads over the document again.
Zh: “当然……我早该发现的。”警督又看了一遍文件，皱起了眉头。

En: How very rational of you. Rational of Evrart, too -- he will, no doubt, benefit from the village being abandoned.
Zh: 你还真的是很有理性呢。艾弗拉特也一样——毫无疑问，废弃的村子能让他从中获利。

En: Evrart probably has eyes on us, but... He pauses to think. "If the second signature were to be somehow *wrong*..."
Zh: “艾弗拉特可能在监视我们，不过……”他停下来想了想。“如果第二份签名出了点*差错*的话……”

En: He won't say it outright, but he's suggesting forgery.
Zh: 他没有明说，不过他在暗示你造假。

En: You're suggesting...
Zh: “你在暗示……”

En: That's illegal. I expected more from you, Kim.
Zh: “这不合法吧。我对你的期望比这个要高多了，金。”

En: He purses his lips. "Forgery, yes. It would render the document invalid."
Zh: 他撅起嘴唇。“没错，伪造。这样文件就无效了。”

En: I am not sure whether working with Evrart is particularly ethical either... But I understand and respect your position. He looks off to the side, deep in thought.
Zh: “我不确定跟艾弗拉特一起合作是不是就特别道德……不过我能理解，也很尊重你的立场。”他看向一边，陷入沉思。

En: I was actually thinking of forging Isobel Sadie's signature...
Zh: “其实我在想伪造伊泽贝尔·莎蒂的签名……”

En: I thought of forging Isobel Sadies signature, but then decided against it. But now I see it may be the right thing to do.
Zh: “我想过伪造伊泽贝尔·莎蒂的签名，之后决定放弃。不过现在，我觉得这么做也许是对的。”

En: I considered forgery and ruled it out. It's illegal and unethical.
Zh: “我考虑过伪造，然后又排除了这种想法。这不合法，也不道德。”

En: He nods approvingly. "Not a decision I would normally condone, but, under the circumstances..."
Zh: 他赞许地点点头。“一般这种决定我是无法容忍的，不过，在这种情况下……”

En: I am not sure whether working with Evrart is particularly ethical either... But I understand and respect your position. He looks off to the side, deep in thought.
Zh: “我不确定跟艾弗拉特一起合作是不是就特别道德……不过我能理解，也很尊重你的立场。”他看向一边，陷入沉思。

En: He nods. "Not the sort of act I would normally condone, but, under the circumstances, if done *discreetly*... it may be the only way to save what's left of the village."
Zh: 他点点头。“一般这种行为我是无法容忍的，不过，在这种情况下，如果*谨慎*处理的话……可能是唯一拯救那个残破村子的方式。”

En: The noise will be tough on the villagers, but I guess that's just the cost of progress.
Zh: “噪音对村民来说可能难以忍受，不过我想这就是发展的代价。”

En: I guess that's just the cost of progress.
Zh: 我想这就是发展的代价。

En: I should have seen it. He frowns as he reads over the document again. "People will move away on their own accord. Yes, I suppose change always has a price."
Zh: “我本该料到的。”他又看了一遍文件，边看边皱起眉头。“人们会自愿搬走。毕竟改变不可能毫无代价。”

En: How very rational of you. Rational of Evrart, too -- he will, no doubt, benefit from the village being abandoned.
Zh: 你还真的是很有理性呢。艾弗拉特也一样——毫无疑问，废弃的村子能让他从中获利。

En: He is not altogether comfortable with your contribution to progress in this instance.
Zh: 在这种情况下，他对你为发展做出的贡献并不完全满意。

En: The noise will be tough on the villagers, but I guess that's just the cost of progress.
Zh: “噪音对村民来说可能难以忍受，不过我想这就是发展的代价。”

En: I guess that's just the cost of progress.
Zh: 我想这就是发展的代价。

En: You probably don't have a pen. He takes one out of his pocket. "Here, you can keep this. I have another one."
Zh: “你可能没有笔。”他从口袋里掏出一支。“给你，留着吧。我还有一支。”

En: Oh yeah, let's do this.
Zh: “哦耶，我们就这么干！”

En: It's not the sort of act I would normally encourage, but, under the circumstances, if done *discreetly*... it may be the only way to save what's left of the village.
Zh: “一般这种行为我是无法容忍的，不过，在这种情况下，如果*谨慎*处理的话……可能是唯一拯救那个残破村子的方式。”

En: He feels guilty about failing to protect the inhabitants of the fishing village from a greedy mob boss.
Zh: 他对未能保护渔村居民不受贪婪的帮派老大伤害而感到愧疚。

En: Now all we have to do is mail these. I think I saw a mailbox on the plaza.
Zh: “现在我们要做的就是把东西寄出去。我好像在广场看见过一个邮筒。”

En: All you need to do now is mail the signatures to Evrart's accountant in La Delta.
Zh: 现在你要做的就是把东西寄给艾弗拉特在德尔塔的会计师。

En: You take the legal documents out of the envelope: a 12-40 month construction period and the zoning plan in the addendum. Underneath all this -- on the signature lines -- your little forgery.
Zh: 你把法律文件从信封中取了出来：附录里有一份建设周期为12-40个月的分区规划图。所有内容下方——在签名栏上——都有你的小伪造品。

En: You take the legal documents out of the envelope: a 12-40 month construction period and the zoning plan in the addendum. It's signed by 'Idiot Doom' Spiral and someone called 'Don't Call Abigail', apparently.
Zh: 你把法律文件从信封中取了出来：附录里有一份建设周期为12-40个月的分区规划图。很明显，签名者是*厄运漩涡*和某个叫*别打给阿比盖尔*的人。

En: You take the legal documents out of the envelope: a 12-40 month construction period and the zoning plan in the addendum. It's signed by 'Idiot Doom Spiral' and someone called 'Domwtclall Abheqoeel', apparently.
Zh: 你把法律文件从信封中取了出来：附录里有一份建设周期为12-40个月的分区规划图。很明显，签名者是*厄运漩涡*和某个叫*尔盖比阿给打要不*的人。

En: You take the legal documents out of the envelope: a 12-40 month construction period and the zoning plan in the addendum. It's signed by Isobel Sadie and Lilienne Carter.
Zh: 你把法律文件从信封中取了出来：附录里有一份建设周期为12-40个月的分区规划图。很明显，签名者是*伊泽贝尔·莎蒂*和*莉莉恩·卡特*。

En: You take the legal documents out of the envelope: a 12-40 month construction period and the zoning plan in the addendum. It's signed by Lilienne Carter and 'Idiot Doom Spiral'.
Zh: 你把法律文件从信封中取了出来：附录里有一份建设周期为12-40个月的分区规划图。很明显，签名者是*伊泽贝尔·莎蒂*和*厄运漩涡*。

En: You take the legal documents out of the envelope: a 12-40 month construction period and the zoning plan in the addendum. Underneath all this -- Lilienne's signature and your little forgery.
Zh: 你把法律文件从信封中取了出来：附录里有一份建设周期为12-40个月的分区规划图。所有内容下方——在签名栏上——是莉莉恩和你的小伪造品。

En: You take the legal documents out of the envelope: a 12-40 month construction period and the zoning plan in the addendum. Underneath all this -- Lilienne's signature and your little forgery.
Zh: 你把法律文件从信封中取了出来：附录里有一份建设周期为12-40个月的分区规划图。所有内容下方——在签名栏上——是莉莉恩和你的小伪造品。

En: You take the legal documents out of the envelope: a 12-40 month construction period and the zoning plan in the addendum.
Zh: 你把法律文件从信封中取了出来：附录里有一份建设周期为12-40个月的分区规划图。

En: You take the legal documents out of the envelope: a 12-40 month construction period and the zoning plan in the addendum. Underneath all this -- Lilienne's signature and an empty line, waiting for a signee.
Zh: 你把法律文件从信封中取了出来：附录里有一份建设周期为12-40个月的分区规划图。所有内容下方——是莉莉恩的签名和一条空白的细线，等待着一名签名人。

En: You take the legal documents out of the envelope: a 12-40 month construction period and the zoning plan in the addendum. Underneath all this -- your failed forgery.
Zh: 你把法律文件从信封中取了出来：附录里有一份建设周期为12-40个月的分区规划图。所有内容下方——是你失败的伪造签名。

En: No, it's just a little line. Let's get an actual signature on top of it and it'll be fine.
Zh: “不，只是一条线而已。我们在它上面弄个真正的签名，那样就没问题了。”

En: No, it's just a line. Forgery obviously isn't your strong suit -- sorry for suggesting it. If you get someone to *actually* sign this, it should be fine.
Zh: 不，只是一条线而已。伪造显然不是你的强项——很抱歉，我不该提这种建议的。如果你能找人*真正*在上面签字的话，应该就没问题了。

En: Well, you *could* trick Evrart. Get someone random to sign the document. By the time the Union boss finds out, your business here will already be concluded.
Zh: 呃，你*可以*欺骗艾弗拉特。随便找些人签署这些文件。等到工会老大发现的时候，你在这里的工作早就已经结束了。

En: He is not altogether comfortable with your contribution to progress in this instance.
Zh: 在这种情况下，他对你为发展做出的贡献并不完全满意。

En: Of course... I should have seen it. He frowns as he reads over the document again. "People will move away on their own accord. I'm not sure they should..."
Zh: “真是的……我本该料到的。”他又看了一遍文件，边看边皱起眉头。“人们会自愿搬走。可我不确定他们该不该这么做……”

En: Or! You *could* try to trick Evrart. Get someone random to sign the document. By the time the Union boss finds out, your business here will already be concluded.
Zh: 或者！你*可以*欺骗艾弗拉特。随便找些人签署这些文件。等到工会老大发现的时候，你在这里的工作早就已经结束了。

En: Okay, let's do this! (Commence the forgery.)
Zh: 好吧，就这么干！（开始伪造。）

En: Evrart's people could be watching you here.
Zh: 艾弗拉特的人可能在这里监视你。

En: Indirect Modes of Taxation.
Zh: 间接征税。

En: Dresscode: Interisolar business class.
Zh: 着装要求：跨岛商务舱。

En: Dresscode: Paper-jockey.
Zh: 着装要求：资料师。

En: The name: "Isobel Sadie."
Zh: 名字：“伊泽贝尔·莎蒂。”

En: The pen is shaking in your hand as you draw a pathetic little line.
Zh: 你颤颤巍巍地画出一条可怜的线，笔在你手中抖个不停。

En: With a confident flourish you complete your forgery. What do you see on the signature line?
Zh: 你自信地大笔一挥，伪造工作就完成了。你在签名栏上看见了什么？

En: Kim, what do you think of this?
Zh: “金，你对这个有什么看法？”

En: Look at the zoning plan.
Zh: 看着分区规划图。

En: Put the documents back in the envelope. [Leave.]
Zh: 把文件放回信封里。[离开。]

En: I'm no property lawyer, but it looks fine, the lieutenant replies, flipping through the documents. "I like the print size. They're not selling or leasing anything. It's not a perfect solution, but..." He shrugs.
Zh: “我不是产权律师，不过看起来挺不错的，”警督一边翻阅文件，一边回复到。“我喜欢这种打印尺寸。他们没有出售或者出租任何东西。这不是个完美的解决方案，不过……”他耸耸肩。

En: Try to find a loophole in the deal.
Zh: 设法找出交易里的漏洞。

En: Centre's very close to houses.
Zh: 中心离房屋非常近。

En: Ominous shape.
Zh: 不祥的形状。

En: Cool cubic pyrite.
Zh: 酷炫的立体黄铁矿。

En: The youth centre cuts into the ocean like the bow of some great modern ship. Apparently it's going to cover most -- if not all -- of the street and the square between the existing houses. It's three storeys tall.
Zh: 青少年活动中心像某种大型现代战舰一样向海洋进犯。很明显，它会覆盖大多数——即便不是全部——现存房屋之间的街道和广场。这是一栋三层楼高的建筑。

En: It's going to be awfully close to the already existing buildings. Almost wall to wall, practically integrating them into the youth centre.
Zh: 它与这些现存房屋之间的距离近的可怕。基本上是墙贴墙，实际上是把它们集成到青少年活动中心里面去了。

En: There is no loophole. The simple truth is the current residents are going to lose their street access and for the next 12-40 months their lives will be dominated by constant construction noise right next door.
Zh: 里面没有漏洞。事实就是现在的居民将会无法在街道上通行。在接下来的12-40个月里，他们的生活将会被隔壁连续不断的建筑噪音所支配。

En: There doesn't seem to be anything of interest here. No fine print. You're smart. You can read.
Zh: 这里似乎没什么有趣的东西。没有难懂的条文。你很聪明。你能阅读。

En: What are the ramifications here?
Zh: 那这里有什么影响呢？

En: Good thing I got those drunks to sign this.
Zh: “幸好我让那些醉鬼签了这个。”

En: Good thing I got those drunks to sign this.
Zh: 幸好我让那些醉鬼签了这个。

En: There are none. The signatures of the drunks are worthless and once Evrart is ready to start the construction, he'll need to actually get Lilienne and Isobel to sign.
Zh: 什么也没有。醉鬼的签名毫无价值，等到艾弗拉特准备好开始施工的时候，他还是需要拿到莉莉恩和伊泽贝尔签名。

En: That was a nice move, yes.
Zh: 这一步走的很漂亮，没错。

En: Yes. The lieutenant nods. "Too bad we can't see Evrart's face when he finds out."
Zh: “是的。”警督点点头。“很可惜，我们看不到艾弗拉特脸上是什么表情了——等到他发现的时候。”

En: Again, the document doesn't seem to have any hidden clause. Maybe, just maybe, what you see is what you get with Mr. Evrart?
Zh: 再次声明，这份文件似乎没有任何隐藏条款。也许，只是也许，你看到的是你跟艾弗拉特先生之间是什么关系？

En: Hmmmm, the lieutenant hums, watching you examine the document again.
Zh: “嗯……”警督看着再次检查文件的你，小声嘟囔着。

En: There is no loophole. The simple truth is the current residents are going to lose their street access and for the next 12-40 months their lives will be dominated by constant construction noise right next door.
Zh: 里面没有漏洞。事实很简单：目前这些居民将会失去街道通道，在接下来的12-40个月里，他们的生活将会被隔壁连续不断的建筑噪音所支配。

En: Wait, what are the ramifications of this?
Zh: 等等，这里有什么影响呢？

En: Look, Kim... (Point to the photocopy.) "These people are going to have to move away. Can we do something about it?"
Zh: “看看，金……”（指着复印件。）“这些人会被迫搬走的。我们不能做点什么吗？”

En: This is terrible. Poor people. Can't I do something?
Zh: 这也太可怕了。那群可怜人。我就不能做点什么吗？

En: Once the construction starts it'll probably take a few months -- a year maybe -- for even the most stubborn occupants to get tired of living like this. After that they'll sell their property for cheap and move out.
Zh: 一旦施工开始，可能会花费几个月——也许是一年——即使是最顽固的钉子户也会厌倦这种生活。在那之后，他们会便宜卖掉自己的财产，然后搬出去。

En: Evrart probably has eyes on us, but... He pauses to think. "We could try to get other people to sign this instead of those listed. *Or* you could forge their signatures yourself. By the time he finds out, we'll already be gone."
Zh: “艾弗拉特可能在监视我们，不过……”他停下来想了想。“我们可以试着找其他人代替清单上那些人来签字。*或者*你可以自己伪造他们的签名。等他发现的时候，我们已经走了。”

En: I should have seen it. The lieutenant frowns as he reads over the document again.
Zh: “我早该发现的。”警督又看了一遍文件，皱起了眉头。

En: Well, you *could* trick Evrart. Get some random people to sign the document. By the time the Union boss finds out, your business here will already be concluded.
Zh: 呃，你*可以*欺骗艾弗拉特。随便找些人签署这些文件。等到工会老大发现的时候，你在这里的工作早就已经结束了。

En: There is no loophole. The simple truth is the current residents are going to lose their street access and for the next 12-40 months their lives will be dominated by constant construction noise right next door.
Zh: 里面没有漏洞。事实很简单：目前这些居民将会失去街道通道，在接下来的12-40个月里，他们的生活将会被隔壁连续不断的建筑噪音所支配。

En: What are the ramifications here?
Zh: 那这里有什么影响呢？

En: Good thing I forged the signatures.
Zh: “还好我会伪造签名。”

En: Good thing I forged the signatures.
Zh: 还好我会伪造签名。

En: Yes. The lieutenant nods. "Too bad we won't see Evrart's face when he finds out."
Zh: “是的。”警督点点头。“很可惜，我们看不到艾弗拉特脸上是什么表情了——等到他发现的时候。”

En: There are none. Your forged signatures are worthless and once Evrart is ready to start the construction, he'll need to actually get Lilienne and Isobel to sign.
Zh: 什么也没有。你伪造的签名毫无价值，等到艾弗拉特准备好开始施工的时候，他还是需要拿到莉莉恩和伊泽贝尔签名。

En: That was a nice move, yes.
Zh: 这一步走的很漂亮，没错。

En: I should have seen it, the lieutenant says with regret. "The people in the village are going to move out on their own accord sooner or later."
Zh: “我本该料到的。”警督的语气充满悔意。“村里的人迟早会自愿搬出去。”

En: What are the ramifications here?
Zh: 那这里有什么影响呢？

En: Once the construction starts it'll probably take a few months -- a year maybe -- for even the most stubborn occupants to get tired of living like this. After that they'll sell their property cheap and move out.
Zh: 一旦施工开始，可能会花费几个月——也许是一年——即使是最顽固的钉子户也会厌倦这种生活。在那之后，他们会便宜卖掉自己的财产，然后搬出去。

En: It's hard to imagine anyone enduring these conditions. The stubborn ones might last for a year, but before 40 months are through, they'll sell their property for pennies and move on.
Zh: 很难想象有人能忍耐这种状况。顽固的那些也许能坚持一年，不过在40个月过完之前，他们就会便宜卖掉自己的财产，然后搬出去。

En: There is no loophole. The simple truth is the current residents are going to lose their street access and for the next 12-40 months their lives will be dominated by constant construction noise right next door.
Zh: 里面没有漏洞。事实很简单：目前这些居民将会失去街道通道，在接下来的12-40个月里，他们的生活将会被隔壁连续不断的建筑噪音所支配。

En: Isobel and Lilienne have a rough 12-40 months ahead of them.
Zh: “伊泽贝尔和莉莉恩即将面对12-40个艰苦的岁月。”

En: Isobel and Lilienne have a rough 12 to 40 months ahead of them.
Zh: 伊泽贝尔和莉莉恩即将面对12-40个艰苦的岁月。

En: All we can do now is *not* mail these to Evrart's accountant. He pauses. "Your gun would probably stay missing though."
Zh: “我们现在能做的就是*不要*把这些寄给艾弗拉特的会计师。”他停顿了一下。“不过你的枪也许就找不到了。”

En: You could always *not* mail the signatures to Evrart's accountant. But then you'll never see your gun again.
Zh: 你可以*不*把这些寄给艾弗拉特的会计师。不过那样的话，你永远也见不到自己的枪了。

En: I don't need my gun. I'm not going to mail it. (Cancel task.)
Zh: “我的枪不要了。我不会寄出去的。”（取消任务。）

En: Damn it... I really need my gun -- and to see where this is going.
Zh: “该死……我真的需要我的枪——而且我想看看事情的走向到底会怎么样。”

En: Damn it... I really need my gun.
Zh: 该死……我真的需要我的枪。

En: I don't need my gun. I'm not going to mail it.
Zh: 我的枪不要了。我不会寄出去的。

En: The lieutenant gives you a long look before speaking. "For what it's worth, I think you're doing the right thing. Firearms can be replaced. People not so much."
Zh: 警督看了你很久，然后才开了口。“无论如何，我觉得你在做一件正确的事情。武器是可以替换的。人不行。”

En: Let's go get it then.
Zh: 警督点点头。“那我们就这么做吧。”

En: Putting the good of others before your own... You're a good man, Harry. A good and honourable man. And guns can be replaced -- people can't.
Zh: 把他人的利益放在你个人之前……你是个好人，哈里。一个善良又正直的人。而且，武器是可以替换的——人不行。

En: No one would judge you. After all, what is a cop without his gun? Is he really even a cop anymore?
Zh: 没人会评判你。毕竟，没了枪的警察那算什么？还能算得上是警察吗？

En: Indeed. To be honest, it does look very convincing. The calligraphy is close to superb. It might as well be her actual signature.
Zh: 确实。老实说，看起来确实很有说服力。笔法近乎精湛。很可能就是她本人的签名。

En: Good. Evrart will have to start over with his scheming. The lieutenant nods. "When she denies that this is her signature the document will be rendered null and void."
Zh: “很好。艾弗拉特要重新盘算他的阴谋了。”警督点点头。“当她绝不承认这是她本人签名的时候，这份文件就会被视为无效。”

En: But it's not, and the document will be nullified if she disputes it. That means Evrart will have to start over.
Zh: 但是并不是，如果她提出异议，这份文件就会作废。那就意味着艾弗拉特要重新开始了。

En: This isn't going to work, the lieutenant says, looking over your shoulder. "It doesn't even look like a name. Just a scratch mark."
Zh: “这样不行的，”警督回头看了看你，说到。“看起来甚至都不像是个名字。这就是一道刮痕。”

En: Keep drawing.
Zh: 继续画。

En: Did I just ruin it?
Zh: “我是不是刚把它毁掉了？”

En: No, it's just a little line. Let's get an actual signature on top of it and it'll be fine.
Zh: “不，只是一条线而已。我们在它上面弄个真正的签名，那样就没问题了。”

En: What have I done?
Zh: 我都做了什么？

En: It appears you have drawn a line instead of a signature. A puny line. Looks like the late stages of dementia.
Zh: 似乎你只是画了一条线，而不是写了个签名。一条微不足道的线。看起来像是痴呆症晚期。

En: Have I ruined the document?
Zh: 我毁掉这份文件了吗？

En: The line gets even shakier and starts to curve towards the bottom of the page.
Zh: 这条线变得更加不平稳了，开始朝着页面底部弯曲。

En: No, it's just a line. Forgery obviously isn't your strong suit -- sorry for suggesting it. If you get someone to *actually* sign this, it should be fine.
Zh: 不，只是一条线而已。伪造显然不是你的强项——很抱歉，我不该提这种建议的。如果你能找人*真正*在上面签字的话，应该就没问题了。

En: Fuck these people. What have they ever done for me? We move on.
Zh: “谁他妈管这群人。他们有为我做过什么吗？我们继续吧。“

En: Yes -- being an *asshole* makes this much easier. Let's go.
Zh: 警督点点头。“是啊——当个*混账*要容易地多。我们走吧。”

En: We'll need to find you a new weapon -- and we'll need to hope to finish the case without Evrart's help. He sighs.
Zh: “我们需要给你找一支新武器——而且，希望我们能不在艾弗拉特的帮助下解决这起案件。”他叹了一口气。

En: (Sigh.) "Okay, damn it... it sounds like too much. Let's fucking mail it."
Zh: （叹气。）“好吧，该死……听起来太麻烦了。妈的，我们直接寄出去吧。”

En: Alright, I'm ready to take the damage for the sake of my conscience.
Zh: “好吧，看在我的良心的份上，我已经准备承受伤害了。”

En: You made the right choice. The lieutenant smiles approvingly. "Now let's move out and solve this case."
Zh: “你的选择是正确的。”警督赞许地笑了。“现在我们开始行动，解决这起案件吧。”

En: Yes... He reluctantly nods. "It's probably the smart choice."
Zh: “是啊……”他懊恼地点了点头。“这可能是明智的选择。”

En: ...how else are you going to build something? It's always inconvenient to build things, and citizens inevitably have disagreements over such construction projects, but there's no other way.
Zh: ……要想建造什么东西的话，你还能怎么做呢？建东西一直都很不方便，市民必然会对这些建设工程产生分歧意见，不过也没有别的办法了。

En: This is either an ominous or cool architectural choice -- hard to say.
Zh: 这是个不吉利或者很酷的建筑选择——很难说清楚。

En: My money is on cool. Looks like a cubic pyrite.
Zh: 我押注酷炫。它看起来像个立体黄铁矿。

En: You are of course going to have to find a new weapon and hope you can finish this case without Evrart's help.
Zh: 你当然会找到一支新武器，而且，希望你能在没有艾弗拉特帮助的情况下破获这起案件。

En: Alright, I'm ready to take the damage for the sake of my conscience.
Zh: 好吧，看在我的良心的份上，我已经准备承受伤害了。

En: Damn it... it sounds like too much. I'm going to fucking mail.
Zh: 该死……听起来太麻烦了。妈的，我还是直接寄出去吧。

En: This is probably the smart choice. Now go mail the document and don't think about it anymore.
Zh: 这可能是个聪明的选择。现在就把这份文件寄出去，以后别想了。

En: Okay. Cool. Honourable too. Just walk away with your head held high.
Zh: 好吧。很酷。还很荣耀。昂首阔步的走开吧。

En: Any halfway competent notary should be able to handle this. *Not* getting her signature was probably the best thing to do.
Zh: “任何半调子的公证人应该都能处理好这个。*没有*让她签字可能是最好的结果吧。”

En: Two names: *Isobel Sadie* and *Lilienne Carter*.
Zh: 两个名字：*伊泽贝尔·莎蒂*和*莉莉恩·卡特*。

En: Indeed. They look distinctly different and very convincing. These might as well be their actual signatures.
Zh: 确实。它们看起来明显不一样，非常有说服力。很可能就是她们本人的签名。

En: All you need to do now is mail the signatures to Evrart's accountant in La Delta.
Zh: 现在你要做的就是把签名寄给艾弗拉特在德尔塔的会计师。

En: Now all we have to do is mail these. I think I saw a mailbox on the plaza.
Zh: “现在我们要做的就是把东西寄出去。我好像在广场看见过一个邮筒。”

En: Any halfway competent notary should be able to handle this. *Not* getting their actual signatures was probably the best thing to do.
Zh: “任何半调子的公证人应该都能处理好这个。*没有*让她签字可能是最好的结果吧。”

En: Good. Evrart will have to start over with his scheming. The lieutenant nods. "When Isobel and Lilienne dispute the signatures the document will be rendered null and void."
Zh: “很好。艾弗拉特要重新盘算他的阴谋了。”警督点点头。“当伊泽贝尔和莉莉恩对签名提出争议的时候，这份文件就会被视为无效。”

En: But they're not, and the document will be nullified if they dispute it. That means Evrart will have to start over.
Zh: 但是并不是，如果她提出异议，这份文件就会作废。那就意味着艾弗拉特要重新开始了。

En: There was a mail delivery box in the plaza, near the corner of the bookstore.
Zh: 广场有一个邮筒，靠近书店转角。

En: However... We'll need access to the coast before we do anything. Evrart won't believe you got villagers' signatures if you can't even get to the village. He shrugs. "You can try a forgery as soon as we can cross the water lock."
Zh: “然而……在我们采取任何行动之前，应该先去一趟海岸。如果你都没去过村子的话，艾弗拉特是不会相信你拿到村民签名的。”他耸耸肩。“等我们走过水闸之后，你就可以试着伪造签名了。”

En: Indeed. Your forgery looks very convincing right next to Lilienne's actual signature. It might as well be real.
Zh: 确实。你伪造的签名就在莉莉恩本人签名的旁边，看起来很让人信服，就像真的一样。

En: Good. Evrart will have to start over with his scheming. The lieutenant nods. "When Isobel disputes her signature the document will be rendered null and void."
Zh: “很好。艾弗拉特要重新盘算他的阴谋了。”警督点点头。“当伊泽贝尔质疑签名的时候，这份文件就会被视为无效了。”

En: But it is not, and the document will be nullified if Isobel disputes it. That means Evrart will have to start over.
Zh: 但是没有，如果伊泽贝尔提出质疑，这份文件就会视作无效。那就意味着艾弗拉特必须重新开始。

En: Any halfway competent notary should be able to handle this. *Not* getting Isobel's actual signature was probably the best thing to do.
Zh: “任何半调子的公证人应该都能处理好这个。*没有*让伊泽贝尔签字可能是最好的结果吧。”

En: René! That old man in the uniform. Maybe you should show it to him when you get the chance.
Zh: 雷内！那个穿着制服的老人。等你有机会，不妨拿给他看看吧。

En: Wait, have you noticed there's a *war veteran* playing a ball game -- on the plaza? You should ask him about the flowers.
Zh: 等等，你有没有注意到有个*老兵*在玩球类游戏——就在广场上？你应该找他打听花的事。

En: This is the Insulindian Lily. The lieutenant corrects his glasses. "Called 'May bells' or 'Lucille's tears' during the Revolution. Girls used to pin these on soldiers before sending them off to battle."
Zh: “这是伊苏林迪百合。”警督调整了一下自己的眼镜。“叫做‘铃兰’，或者大革命期间也叫‘露西尔的眼泪’。女孩们曾经会把这种花别在士兵们的胸口，然后目送他们走向战场。”

En: What is the Revolution?
Zh: “大革命是什么？”

En: Very well. (Put the flower back in your pocket.)
Zh: “很好。”（把花放回你的口袋。）

En: Who pinned them? Which side?
Zh: “谁会别着这些？哪边的？”

En: Blow on your fingers.
Zh: 朝你的手指吹口气。

En: Does this flower blossom in early spring?
Zh: “这些花是在早春开放的吗？”

En: Water flows under the channel bridge -- dark water. You rub your sides for warmth but there is none.
Zh: 运河大桥下，水潺潺流过——是黑水。你摩擦着身体寻求温暖，然而并没有效果。

En: ...the wrinkled fingers of an old man crush flower petals. Then sprinkle them in the stream, like white salt.
Zh: ……老人用皱巴巴的手指碾碎花瓣。然后把它们洒在小溪里，就像白色的盐。

En: A big political and military conflict. Ask around, or buy a book. He seems annoyed by the question.
Zh: “一场大型的政治和军事冲突。在附近问问，或者买本书看。”他似乎被这个问题惹恼了。

En: The revolutionaries: so the communards and the anarchists. White's their colour. But the custom started in the Suzerain's army so it held meaning for the kingsmen too.
Zh: “革命派：公社社员和无政府主义者。他们的代表色是白色。不过这个传统是从宗主国的军队兴起的，所以对军队的人来说也有意义。”

En: It's about girls and boys more than *sides*. Girls sending off boys who are going to their deaths -- then also dying themselves in the ruins, from dysentery and consumption. It's a symbol of the Civil War.
Zh: “相比*哪一边*，更多是跟女孩和男孩相关的。女孩把男孩们送上死亡的道路——然后自己也因为痢疾和肺痨在废墟中死去。它是内战的一种标志。”

En: Yes, but not *this* early -- not to my knowledge... He observes the petals: "It looks dried. Preserved."
Zh: “是的，不过并没有*这么*早——据我所知并不是这样……”他观察着花瓣：“看起来脱过水，是保藏起来的。”

En: Inland, above the Martinaise distributary, the channel that brought waste water from the silk mills of Jamrock; and then dead bodies during the War...
Zh: 在内陆，马丁内斯支流的上游，这条运河输送着加姆洛克丝绸厂的废水；还有战时的尸体……

En: Stand still.
Zh: 站着不动。

En: The feeling recedes.
Zh: 这种感觉逝去了。

En: The petals feel dry and fragile in your hand.
Zh: 你手中的花瓣感觉干枯又脆弱。

En: Six crumbling petals rest on your palm. They're white, a bell-shaped crown.
Zh: 六片破碎的花瓣躺在你的掌心。它们是白色的，铃铛形状的王冠。

En: Somewhere in Central Jamrock, Lieutenant Kitsuragi steps into the public library, rows of shelves pass on either side... you feel he should be here for in-depth flower analysis.
Zh: 在加姆洛克中央区的某处，曷城警督走进了公共图书馆，走过两旁一排排的书架……你感觉他是为了做花卉的深入分析才来这里的。

En: What is this, Kim?
Zh: “这是什么，金？”

En: This flower is a spring flower, but it's a bit early for that, isn't it?
Zh: 这是一朵春天的花，不过现在有些太早了，不是吗？

En: Is it a coincidence -- it being on the roof?
Zh: “它出现在屋顶——是一种巧合吗？”

En: Maybe -- maybe not. He makes a note in his little note book. "We'll see."
Zh: “也许是——也许不是。”他在小笔记本里做了点记录。“再说吧。”

En: Never mind [Leave.]
Zh: 没事了。[离开。]

En: As I said -- an Insulindian Lily, or a May bell. Girls pinned them on their boys during the Revolution.
Zh: “我说过了——这是伊苏林迪百合，或者铃兰。大革命期间，女孩会把它们别在男孩的身上。”

En: Why did I fondle the shit jacket again?
Zh: 我为什么又开始摸这件脏夹克了？

En: There is no why.
Zh: 没有为什么。

En: Let's just put it away then.
Zh: 那我们就把它放在一边吧。

En: As you hold it in your hands, it makes an uncomfortable crunching sound...
Zh: 当你把它拿在手里的时候，它发出了一阵不安的嘎吱声……

En: Some secrets are better left uncovered. Don't even try, seriously.
Zh: 有些秘密还是不被发现的好。说真的，甚至都别去尝试。

En: [Put the filthy jacket away.]
Zh: [把脏夹克放在一边。]

En: Think about it.
Zh: 想一想。

En: Don't think about it.
Zh: 别想了。

En: It occurs to you that you're not even *holding* the jacket itself, but rather the thick crust of jetsam and seagull shit that ensconces it.
Zh: 你突然想到自己手里*拿*的甚至都不是夹克本身，而是一层厚厚的沉料和海鸥粪便结成的外壳。

En: Good choice. The less you think about the jacket, the better.
Zh: 明智的选择。你越不去想这件夹克，结果就越好。

En: On second thought, let's *do* think about it.
Zh: 转念一想，我们*还是*思考一下吧。

En: It smells like a dead sea creature, tangled in grey strands of seaweed. It must have spent quite some time in the water before the tide deposited it ashore...
Zh: 它闻起来像是死亡海洋生物的味道，纠缠在灰色的海藻之间。在海浪把它推到岸边之前，它肯定已经在水里待了好一阵子了。

En: The faint impressions of many footprints are also present, though it's impossible to tell what kind or how many. Suffice to say, the jacket spent some time on the ground before someone draped it over the railing...
Zh: 周围有很多模糊的脚印，虽然不太可能说清是什么种类，或者到底有多少。可以说，有人把这件夹克搁在栏杆上之前，它在地上躺过一段时间。

En: Gross.
Zh: 恶心。

En: Okay, but what's the crust *made* of?
Zh: 好吧，但是这个外壳是什么*形成*的呢？

En: Why, why did you think about it? Look at your hands -- They're covered in muck!
Zh: 为什么，你为什么要去想它？看看你的手——上面沾满了脏东西。

En: Maybe if I wipe my hands on my pants?
Zh: 也许我可以用裤子把手擦干净？

En: What? No! Then it'll just be on your *pants*. This is a disaster. You'll never get the smell out.
Zh: 什么？不行！那不就粘在你的*裤子*上了吗。这是个灾难。你永远也没办法清除这股味道了。

En: Somehow it was carried or dragged to the boardwalk, if not by human hands than perhaps the feral dogs that prowl the beaches at night...
Zh: 不知怎么的，它被拖上了木板道，如果用的不是人手，那就有可能是晚上在海滩边游荡的野狗……

En: Okay, that's pretty disgusting. I've had enough.
Zh: 好吧，确实挺恶心的。我已经受够了。

En: What happened once someone put it on the railing?
Zh: 有人把它放在栏杆上，之后怎么样了？

En: The crust is hard. This jacket spent at least a day baking in the sun. Who knows what happened to it then?
Zh: 外壳很坚硬。这件夹克至少在太阳底下晒了一天。谁知道后来发生了什么事？

En: They are like the bombardiers of the aerostatic brigades, gliding above a *target-rich environment*...
Zh: 它们就像是静风舰队的投弹手，在*放眼全是目标的环境*里翱翔……

En: Somewhere, high above the city, a pair of seagulls trace loops through the air...
Zh: 城市上空的某个地方，一队海鸥在空中盘旋……

En: All of a sudden, the bomb bay doors open, and their white glittering payload rushes to the ground...
Zh: 突然间，炸弹舱门打开了，闪烁着耀眼白光的炸弹直冲向大地……

En: *SPLAT*. An explosion of white on a man's shoe. A curse goes up, but the birds do not hear.
Zh: *啪嗒*。一枚白色炸弹在男人的鞋子上爆炸。一阵咒骂声响起，但是鸟儿并没有听到。

En: Ew, ew, ew! (Flick your hands.)
Zh: 呕！（甩动你的手。）

En: Now you're just flicking that shit everywhere! This is a disaster. You'll never get the smell out.
Zh: 现在你把那些脏东西甩的到处都是！这是个灾难。你永远也没办法清除这股味道了。

En: I'm just going to pretend I didn't think about any of this.
Zh: 我要假装自己没想过这些。

En: I feel like I really understand this jacket now. [Put the jacket away.]
Zh: 我感觉自己现在已经理解这件夹克了。[把夹克放在一边。]

En: Why did I just spend all this time thinking about seagull shit?
Zh: 我为什么要花这么多时间思考海鸥粪便呢？

En: It's probably better that way.
Zh: 那样可能更好吧。

En: It's too late! You've already thought about it. And now your hands are covered in muck!
Zh: 太迟了！你已经开始想了。现在你的手上沾满了脏东西。

En: Man, how did this jacket get so disgusting?
Zh: 天啦，这件夹克怎么会变成这么恶心的样子？

En: Let's not think about that right now. [Put the filthy jacket away.]
Zh: 现在还是别想了。[把脏夹克放在一边。]

En: It's a sordid, filthy tale, not for the weak. Are you sure you can stomach it?
Zh: 这是一个肮脏又污秽的传说，不适合那些弱鸡。你确定能够忍受吗？

En: Something tells you that won't be any time soon. This'll have to be one of those epic tasks that's open for a while...
Zh: 有什么东西告诉你还不会那么快找到。这会是那种持续很长时间的史诗级任务……

En: Make?
Zh: 那制造商呢？

En: Antiques enthusiasts, guerrilla fighters in distant countries, a few lucky Jamrock bangers. You're looking for a well-preserved antique, most likely.
Zh: 古董爱好者，遥远国家的游击队队员，还有一些幸运的加姆洛克黑帮分子。你的目标大概是一把存良好的古董枪吧。

En: I think I know where this came from. (Dangle the bag thoughtfully.)
Zh: “我想我知道它是从哪里来的了。”（若有所思地晃晃袋子。）

En: Okay. And?
Zh: “好吧，所以呢？”

En: Prohibiting peacetime law-enforcement to front loaded rifles is a policy enforced by the Moralist International in all the nations of the Reál Belt.
Zh: 禁止和平时期的执法人员使用后膛枪是国际道德伦理委员会在所有雷亚尔货币体系国家执行的一项政策。

En: Very interesting. You have an unusual, military-grade bullet in your hand. Now all you need is to find a gun that could fire it.
Zh: 很有意思。你手上有一只不同寻常的军用级子弹。现在你只需要找到能发射这种子弹的枪支就可以了。

En: This is a real killer. Not some garbage street ammo. You can *feel* it.
Zh: 这是一个真正的杀器。不是街头那些垃圾子弹。你能*感觉*到。

En: Wow, I actually *know* this...
Zh: 哇哦，我竟然*知道*这个……

En: Yes. It's as if you've seen bullets before, officer.
Zh: 是的。就好像你之前见过这种子弹，警官。

En: Yes. It's as if you've seen bullets before, officer.
Zh: 是的。就好像你之前见过这种子弹，警官。

En: Wow, I actually *know* this...
Zh: 哇哦，我竟然*知道*这个……

En: We were right. This came from a serious weapon.
Zh: 我们是对的。它来自一支很有杀伤力的武器。

En: Highly unusual. The people of Revachol haven't carried breech-loading weapons like this for nearly half a century.
Zh: 很不寻常。瑞瓦肖人已经有近半个世纪未能持有这种后膛装填的武器了。

En: The bullet is safely sealed away in a plastic bag bearing the RCM stamp. Kim has filled out the label on the bag with the item number, case number, and date and location the bullet was found.
Zh: 子弹安全地密封在一个有RCM印章的塑料袋里。金填好了袋子上的标签，包括物品编号，案件编号，日期，还有发现子弹的地点。

En: Feel the bullet through the bag.
Zh: 隔着袋子抚摸子弹。

En: Inspect the bullet closer.
Zh: 仔细检查子弹。

En: The bullet has nothing more to say. [Put it away.]
Zh: 子弹没什么可以说的了。[把它放在一边。]

En: Try to determine what type of weapon shot this.
Zh: 尝试确定这是什么武器射出的子弹。

En: Have a similar rifle on hand.
Zh: 你手上有一只类似的步枪。

En: Aware of the name of the antique rifle you found.
Zh: 之前发现的那支步枪，你已经知道它的名字了。

En: Have the full bullet specs.
Zh: 你拥有这枚子弹的完整参数。

En: Encyclopedia said this came from a breechloader.
Zh: 博学多闻说这是一枚后膛枪的子弹。

En: Cursed Iilmaraan die.
Zh: 伊尔玛诅咒骰子。

En: The squashed bullet has some sharp edges where the jacket has split open. It feels cold, even through the bag.
Zh: 被压扁子弹的边缘有些锋利，那周围的外壳已经裂开了。就算是透过袋子，它依然感觉很冰冷。

En: The jacket of the bullet is made of yellowish metal. It has blossomed out to reveal a dark grey core. The base of the bullet is close to 5 mm in diameter.
Zh: 子弹外壳是淡黄色金属制成的。它已经绽放出了深灰色的核心。子弹底部的直径接近5毫米。

En: You can't remember what happened last week -- what makes you think you're going to remember arcane firearm models?
Zh: 你记不起上周发生了什么事情——是什么让你觉得自己会记得那支神秘武器的型号呢？

En: A rifle. Revolutionary-period. Your bullet looks to be an old 4.46 mm -- from the surplus left over from the turn of the century. Probably an antique or a retrofitted antique.
Zh: 是一支步枪，大革命时期的。这枚子弹看起来像是老式的4.46毫米的——是世纪之交残留下来的东西了。可能是一个古董，或者一个翻新的古董。

En: The 4.46 calibre was widely used with the *Belle-Magrave* rifle, a Revacholian manufacturer. The B-M dominated the battlefields of the Insulindian theatre of the Antecentennial Revolution, 50 years ago.
Zh: 4.46口径被广泛应用于*贝莱-马格雷*步枪，一家瑞瓦肖制造商。50年前，B-M在世纪前革命的伊苏林迪剧院战场上占据了主要地位。

En: Is anyone still making these rifles?
Zh: 还有人在生产这种步枪吗？

En: Who uses Belle-Magrave rifles these days?
Zh: 现在谁会使用贝莱-马格雷步枪呢？

En: Antiques enthusiasts, guerrilla fighters in distant countries, a few lucky Jamrock bangers. You're looking for the same thing you found in that hidden weapons cache -- only in working order.
Zh: 古董爱好者，遥远国家的游击队队员，还有一些幸运的加姆洛克黑帮分子。你寻找的东西跟隐藏武器库里的那些是一样的——只不过它还能正常使用。

En: No, but Zieleger, a major firearm manufacturer, ended up with a surplus after the war, so there are still a lot of these old military rifles floating around, usually broken. The quality was appalling.
Zh: 没有了。不过一家名为齐格勒的大型军火制造商，在战后还剩有不少库存。所以还有很多旧军事步枪广为流传，一般都是坏的，质量实在太糟糕了。

En: Back at the station, the lieutenant is standing at a counter, diligently filling out paperwork to hand over to a tired records clerk.
Zh: 回到警局，警督正站在一个柜台前面，勤奋地填写文件，准备交给一个疲惫的档案管理员。

En: You should share your findings with him as soon as possible.
Zh: 你应该尽快跟他分享你的发现。

En: You wouldn't ordinarily have cause to handle jacketed bullets. The Citizens Militia uses cast bullets only -- little pebbles of metal loaded from the muzzle, usually in a cartridge.
Zh: 通常你没有接触包壳子弹的理由。公民武装只会使用无壳弹——从枪口装填的小金属弹，一般是装在管壳里的。

En: The core is lead. The brass jacket probably didn't cover the entire bullet, otherwise the softer metal wouldn't have expanded quite like that.
Zh: 核心是铅制的。黄铜外壳可能无法包裹住整颗子弹，否则软金属是不会像那样膨胀的。

En: Look at the jacket.
Zh: 看看外壳。

En: And the core?
Zh: 那核心呢？

En: You can just about make out a few striations near the base of the bullet. Little hairlines, linear. It feels standard.
Zh: 你只能辨认出子弹底部附近的一些小条纹。极其细小，呈线状。感觉没什么特别的。

En: It's quite destroyed. Some of the fragments are still lodged in the wound.
Zh: 它损毁的很严重。有些碎片还卡在伤口里。

En: The shot probably came from a Belle-Magrave rifle.
Zh: “子弹很可能是从一支贝莱-马格雷步枪里射出来的。”

En: Hmm... The lieutenant jots something down in his notebook. "What are you thinking? Bullet?"
Zh: “嗯……”警督在笔记本里记了些什么。“你在想什么？子弹？”

En: I mean... have some ideas, but I can't be sure...
Zh: “我的意思是……有点想法，不过不太确定……”

En: Our stiff was killed by a jungle gun.
Zh: “我们这具尸体是被一支丛林枪击杀的。”

En: Belle-Magrave rifle. The ammo points to it.
Zh: “贝莱-马格雷步枪。子弹指向了它。”

En: What's your best guess?
Zh: “你的最佳猜测是什么？”

En: You mean some turn-of-the-century surplus firearm?
Zh: “你是说世纪之交盈余的枪支？”

En: An antique. That makes sense. There can't be many breech-loading rifles floating around in Martinaise, or anywhere in Revachol, really...
Zh: “一个古董。是有点道理。不可能有很多后膛枪在马丁内斯广泛流传，或者说真的，瑞瓦肖的任何地方都不太可能……”

En: Why not?
Zh: “为什么不呢？”

En: That's probably a good thing.
Zh: “那可能是件好事。”

En: He nods. "I have to hand it to the monarchs -- it's quite admirable that they took the advice of criminologists last century and banned the use of breechloaders in peacetime."
Zh: 他点点头。“我必须赞扬一下君主——他们听从了上世纪犯罪学家的建议，禁止在和平时期使用后膛枪，这是一件挺让人钦佩的事。”

En: Sure, there's some arms trafficking. But the laws prohibiting the use of breechloaders we inherited from the monarchy have been effective, from what I've seen.
Zh: “肯定会有一些军火交易。不过法律禁止我们使用从君主制继承的后膛枪这一点，据我观察，是颇有成效的。”

En: Some new RCM recruits get impatient with their muzzleloaders once they've trained with military-grade weapons, but they realize it's worth it, in the end.
Zh: “RCM有些新成员，一旦接受过军用级武器训练之后，就会对他们的前膛枪感到不耐烦，不过最后他们会意识到这是值得的。”

En: Imagine if everyone -- cops, citizens -- had access to firearms that could shoot multiple rounds without pausing to reload. After the first shot, the second, third, and so on -- come much easier.
Zh: “想象一下，如果所有人——警察，市民——都能接触到不用停下来重新装填就能发射好几轮子弹的枪支。第一枪过后，就会有第二枪，第三枪，以此类推——那样要来的容易得多。”

En: Worth what? Getting shot?
Zh: “值得什么？中枪吗？”

En: I think we should have more *powerful* guns. We're the law!
Zh: “我觉得我们应该拥有更*强大的*枪械。我们可是法律！”

En: Makes you consider every shot. I like it.
Zh: “让你考虑自己开出的每一枪。我喜欢这样。”

En: But back to the investigation.
Zh: “不过，还是继续说回调查。”

En: Seems we're looking for an antiques enthusiast.
Zh: “看来我们在寻找一个古董爱好者。”

En: Could the victim have been mixed up with some foreign guerrilla fighters?
Zh: “难道凶手把受害人和某些外国的游击队战士弄混了吗？”

En: Have well-armed Jamrock bangers started crossing over into Martinaise?
Zh: “难道是全副武装的加姆洛克黑帮分子开始进军马丁内斯了？”

En: Doesn't seem *that* likely, but we'll check out all possible leads. Next step -- finding the gun itself.
Zh: “*那个*看起来不太可能，不过我们需要查证所有可能的线索。下一步——找到那支凶枪。”

En: Let's find out. Next step -- finding the gun itself.
Zh: “这就要由我们去发现了。下一步——找到那支凶枪。”

En: Incidentally, you have just such a rifle with you. The dusty old thing you found hidden in the basement below the commercial area. It's unusable, sadly. If it were, the bullet would *probably* fit the chamber.
Zh: 顺便说一句，你身上就带着一支类似的步枪。就是商业区地下室里藏着的那个旧玩意。可悲的是，它不能用了。如果可以的话，子弹*可能*跟枪膛是符合的。

En: What can you say about the bullet so far?
Zh: “到目前为止，你对子弹有什么看法？”

En: Even the RCM uses ordinary unjacketed conical bullets. This is... strange. Very strange. I like this, officer. Strange means unique. Unique means incriminating. We need to find the gun that shot it.
Zh: “就连RCM使用的都是普通的无壳圆锥弹。这有点……奇怪。很奇怪。我喜欢这样，警官。奇怪就意味着独一无二。独一无二就意味着有罪。我们需要找到射出这枚子弹的枪。”

En: It's a soft-point bullet with a lead core and a brass jacket close to 8 mm in diameter.
Zh: “这是一枚软尖弹，拥有铅质核心和直径大约8毫米的黄铜外壳。”

En: It's a jacketed bullet, close to 5 mm in diameter.
Zh: “这是一枚包壳子弹，直径接近5毫米。”

En: Okay... Jacketed means it would have been shot from a military-grade breech-loading rifle. And not from a muzzleloader like those typically found on the streets of Revachol West.
Zh: “好吧……包壳就意味着它可能出自一支军用级后膛步枪，而不是瑞瓦肖西部街道上那些常见的前膛枪。”

En: A jacketed bullet. Okay... It would have been shot from a military-grade breech-loading rifle, not from a muzzleloader like those typically found on the streets of Martinaise.
Zh: “一枚包壳子弹。好吧……那可能出自一支军用级后膛步枪，而不是马丁内斯街道上那些常见的前膛枪。”

En: Far away, Lieutenant Kitsuragi is flipping through stations in his Kineema. He is not here examining the bullet with you. He should be... he would find this very intriguing. Mention it to him, later when he's here.
Zh: 警督身在远方，他正坐在锐影里，来回切换着无线电台。他没有在此时此地和你一起检查这颗子弹。他应该在这里的……他肯定会对此兴致盎然。等他回来，再和他谈谈这件事吧。

En: What's interesting about the bullet thus far?
Zh: 目前为止，这枚子弹有什么有意思的地方吗？

En: It's a soft-point bullet with a lead core and a brass jacket close to 8 mm in diameter.
Zh: 这是一枚软尖弹，拥有铅质核心和直径大约8毫米的黄铜外壳。

En: It's a jacketed bullet close to 5 mm in diameter.
Zh: 这是一枚包壳子弹，直径接近5毫米。

En: Exactly. A very *unusual* jacketed bullet. One that would have been shot from a military-grade breech-loading rifle, not from a muzzleloader like those typically found on the streets of Martinaise.
Zh: 没错。一枚*不寻常*的包壳子弹。可能出自一支军用级后膛步枪，而不是马丁内斯街道上那些常见的前膛枪。

En: A jacketed bullet... which would have been shot from a military-grade breech-loading rifle, not from a muzzleloader like those typically found on the streets of Martinaise.
Zh: 一枚包壳子弹……可能出自一支军用级后膛步枪，而不是马丁内斯街道上那些常见的前膛枪。

En: Kim, take a look at this. It's a soft-point bullet with a lead core and a brass jacket close to 8 mm in diameter.
Zh: “金，看看这个。这是一枚软尖弹，拥有铅质核心和直径大约8毫米的黄铜外壳。”

En: Kim, take a look at this. It's a jacketed bullet close to 5 mm in diameter.
Zh: “金，看看这个。这是一枚包壳子弹，直径接近5毫米。”

En: Beside his orderly handwriting, the bullet looks especially sad. Like a tiny, shrivelled head of cauliflower.
Zh: 子弹靠在他工整的笔迹旁边，看起来特别悲伤。就像是一个小小的，枯萎的菜花头。

En: The bullet is still safely sealed away in a plastic bag bearing the RCM stamp.
Zh: 子弹还是安全地密封在一个有RCM印章的塑料袋里。

En: What do I do with you, bullet?
Zh: “我该拿你怎么办呢，子弹？”

En: The squished little thing has no idea.
Zh: 这个被压扁的小东西也不知道。

En: What? The lieutenant steps closer.
Zh: “什么？”警督靠近了一些。

En: I said: What do I do with you, bullet?
Zh: “我说：我该拿你怎么办，子弹？”

En: Well, if I was the bullet -- which I'm not -- I would say: find the weapon that shot me.
Zh: “呃，如果我是子弹——当然我不是——我会说：找到把我发射出来的那支武器。”

En: Good idea.
Zh: “好主意。”

En: I don't know...
Zh: “我不知道……”

En: If we find who owns it, we will have likely found who *used* it -- possibly to kill our victim.
Zh: “如果我们找出它的所有者，就很有可能发现到底是谁*使用*过它——可能用它杀死了我们的受害人。”

En: In conclusion: the more we know about this bullet of yours, the better.
Zh: “总之：我们对这枚子弹了解的越多，结果就越好。”

En: I know! You should find the gun that shot this bullet.
Zh: 我知道！你应该找出这枚子弹是什么武器射出来的。

En: Eat it!
Zh: 吃掉它！

En: Find the gun that shot it!
Zh: 找出发射这枚子弹的武器！

En: Sell it for profit!
Zh: 把它卖掉赚钱！

En: But the bullet is not edible, sadly.
Zh: 但是很悲哀，子弹是不可食用的。

En: Who would want this *used* bullet?
Zh: 谁会想要这枚*用过*的子弹呢？

En: It's my friend, I'm gonna start talking to it. It will keep me company.
Zh: 它是我的朋友。我要开始跟它说话。它会陪伴我的。

En: But bullets don't talk.
Zh: 但是子弹不会说话。

En: Yeah, what are you, *crazy*? Bullets can't talk. They don't have emotions. A bullet can't keep you company.
Zh: 是啊，你怎么了，*疯了*？子弹不会说话的。它们没有感情。子弹也不能陪伴你。

En: That sounds like something a police detective would do. First you should to learn all you can about this little guy!
Zh: 没毛病，像是一个警探会做的。首先，你需要尽你所能地去了解这个小家伙！

En: Then find the gun that shot it, and the person who that gun belongs to -- he used it to kill your victim. Neato!
Zh: 然后找出发射它的那支枪，还有枪的所有者——他用它杀害了你的受害人。不赖吧！

En: Yes. Something *mysterious* is afoot with this antique bullet type.
Zh: “是的。某些*神秘的*东西正在跟这种古老的子弹类型一起发酵。”

En: Mysterious? He shrugs. "Okay. Either way, we'll know when we find the gun."
Zh: “神秘？”他耸耸肩。“好吧。不管怎么说，等找到那支枪的时候，我们就会知道了。”

En: You mean semen, right?
Zh: 你的意思是精液，对吗？

En: Oh, okay then.
Zh: “哦，那好吧。”

En: The wrapper -- soaked in tomato juice and mayo -- is not gonna hold out forever, but for now the sandwich retains it's impressive composure.
Zh: 包装纸——浸满了番茄汁和蛋黄酱——是没办法永远保存的，不过现在三明治还是保持着让人印象深刻的沉着和镇静。

En: Ouch... he did *not* like that.
Zh: 哎哟……他*不*喜欢那样。

En: The lieutenant nods and adds, "But you go right ahead."
Zh: 警督点点头，补充到：“但是你继续吧。”

En: No, you're right. Let's just drop it.
Zh: “不，你说得对。我们还是把它扔掉吧。”

En: Go through all that and then just dump it? The lieutenant smirks. "You better just eat it, no need for dramatic gestures."
Zh: “经历了那么多，然后就把它扔掉？”警督假笑了一下。“你最好吃掉它，没必要搞什么夸张的动作。”

En: It's a little joke the lieutenant made. He knows what you meant.
Zh: 这是警督跟你开的一个小玩笑。他知道你是什么意思。

En: This is good shit. (Eat your half of the sandwich.)
Zh: “这破玩意不错。”（吃掉你那半三明治。）

En: Friends forever! (Eat your half of the sandwich.)
Zh: “友谊万岁！”（吃掉你那半三明治。）

En: Eat your half in silence.
Zh: 默默地吃掉你那半。

En: Good. The lieutenant nods. "Very nourishing. Let's go solve crimes now."
Zh: “很好。”警督点点头。“很有营养。现在我们去破案吧。”

En: Look what you did...
Zh: “看看你做了什么……”

En: Great, that's 2000 kilo-calories wasted right there.
Zh: “太好了，刚才浪费了2000卡路里。”

En: Congratulations, Kim. It was a test and you passed.
Zh: “恭喜，金。这是一个测试，你通过了。”

En: Step back everyone, it's gonna get messy. (Eat the sandwich.)
Zh: “所有人都退后，场面肯定会很乱的。”（吃掉三明治。）

En: You jam your teeth into the sandwich and immediately feel a series of taste explosions go off in you mouth. It's like someone's carpet-bombing your tongue with ambrosia.
Zh: 你的牙齿咬在三明治上，立即感觉到一连串味道在嘴巴里面爆炸。就好像有人在用珍馐美馔对你的舌头进行地毯式轰炸。

En: I'm just not hungry and therefore have little reason to take it.\n
Zh: “我只是不太饿，所以没什么吃它的理由。”

En: He shrugs. "I mean I'm not hungry and therefore am not gonna take it. Thanks though."
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“我的意思是我不饿，所以我不想吃。不过还是谢谢你。”

En: No.
It really is every bit as good as it looked and smelled. The different components all chip in and compliment each other, forming a flavour that is unique to this particular masterpiece.
Zh: 它的美味跟它的外观和香味一模一样。不同的组合彼此配合，相互协调，让这件特别的杰作呈现出一股独一无二的风味。
