En: When you hold something up long enough, some people are compelled to take it. Try it on the lieutenant.
Zh: 当你把某种东西拿的足够久，大家就会被迫接过它。在警督身上试试。

En: I'll pass, the lieutenant replies with a polite nod. "Thanks."
Zh: “我不用了，”警督礼貌地点头回应。“谢了。”

En: Slow down, feel the texture of the components and subtle nuances in taste.
Zh: 慢一点，感觉材料的口感层次，还有味道里微妙的差别。

En: Here, Kim! Let's share. (Hold out the sandwich.)
Zh: “来吧，金！我们分着吃。”（拿出三明治。）

En: The bread, as the foundation of the sandwich, is slightly moist due to tomato juice and mayo, but holds together nicely. Almost nothing falls off the sandwich. You feel like you really got everything out of this experience.
Zh: 面包，作为三明治的基底，在番茄酱和蛋黄酱的影响下略微有些潮湿，不过确实一种很好的组合。几乎什么都没有从三明治里掉出来。你觉得自己真的从这次经历中得到了一切。

En: You really ram into the sandwich, *deep*. The tomato slides off, mayo drips everywhere (including your moustache and clothes), bits of ham fall onto the ground...
Zh: 你真的很用力去啃咬那个三明治，一口下去*又大又深*。番茄掉了下来，蛋黄酱滴的到处都是（包括你的胡子和衣服），咬了一口的火腿掉到了地面上……

En: Camaraderie?
Zh: “兄弟情？”

En: Why won't you take the sandwich?
Zh: “你为什么不拿三明治呢？”

En: What do you mean 'you'll pass'?
Zh: “你说‘不用了’是什么意思？”

En: Keep holding the sandwich.
Zh: 继续握着三明治。

En: Take giant bites and swallow them half-chewed.
Zh: 大咬一口，嚼了一半就吞下去。

En: Get into there and do some real damage!
Zh: 来吧，搞点真正的破坏！

En: This is what angels must taste like. Your saliva production increases tenfold. You can barely contain it all.
Zh: 这一定就是天使的味道。你的唾液分泌增加了十倍。几乎有些控制不住了。

En: This isn't about hunger, Kim, it's about camaraderie.
Zh: “这跟饥饿无关，金，这跟兄弟情有关。”

En: Hold the sandwich up for the lieutenant to take it.
Zh: 握着三明治，等着警督来拿。

En: Thank you, but no, I'm not hungry.
Zh: “谢谢你，不过不用了，我不饿。”

En: Okay, I guess I'll keep it for later.
Zh: “好吧，那我先留着，以后再说。”

En: Yes! By sharing this sandwich you and I solidify the bond between us.
Zh: “没错！分享这个三明治之后，我们之间的关系就更加团结了。”

En: Hold the sandwich up, moving it closer to the his chin.
Zh: 拿着三明治，凑到他的下巴附近。

En: It *really* is.
Zh: “*真的*就是这样。”

En: The lieutenant nods.
Zh: 警督点点头。

En: Is this necessary?! Is friendship necessary? Is brotherhood?
Zh: “有这个必要吗？！友谊有必要吗？兄弟情呢？”

En: No.
Zh: “不会。”

En: I just thought it would be a nice gesture. I'm sorry...
Zh: “我只是觉得这是一个善意的举动。我很抱歉……”

En: Then sure. Let us 'strengthen our bond'. The lieutenant breaks the sandwich in two and quickly eats his half.
Zh: “那是当然。让我们‘加强这种团结关系’吧。”警督把三明治掰成两半，然后快速把自己那份咽了下去。

En: Is it really necessary?
Zh: “真有这个必要吗？”

En: Lower the sandwich.
Zh: 放下三明治。

En: Do not lower the sandwich.
Zh: 不要放下三明治。

En: Okay-okay. They are all necessary. Let's share the sandwich, if it matters that much to you. The lieutenant breaks the sandwich in two and quickly eats his half.
Zh: “好吧。这样很有必要。如果对你来说真的这么重要，那我们就把这个三明治分了吧。”警督把三明治掰成两半，然后快速把自己那份咽了下去。

En: You can barely feel the taste, but it seems decent enough. At least it gets your stomach nice and full. Good sandwich -- durable!
Zh: 你几乎没怎么尝到味道，不过好像挺不错的。至少它很好地填满了你的胃。很好的三明治——够持久！

En: For the brötherhööd! (Eat your half of the sandwich.)
Zh: “为了兄弟情！”（吃掉你那半三明治。）

En: Stop that! He doesn't sound amused.
Zh: “住手！”他听起来不像是开玩笑。

En: ...some people would feel compelled to take it. Yes, I know.
Zh: “……有些人会觉得不得不接过来。是的，我知道。”

En: Have the sandwich-interceptor circle in front of the lieutenant's face and then gently nosedive against his lips.
Zh: 让三明治拦截机在警督的面前盘旋，然后轻轻地俯冲到他的嘴边。

En: You know, Kim, if I were to hold this sandwich up long enough...
Zh: “你知道的，金，如果我拿着这个三明治足够久的话……”

En: Sure, sure. The lieutenant nods impatiently. "Time to go solve a murder investigation."
Zh: “当然，当然。”警督不耐烦地点点头。“是时候去破获一起凶杀案了。”

En: This man does not crack under pressure. Stars would die before he'd succumb to any kind of manipulation. With this realization comes a sudden flash of pride.
Zh: 男人在压力之下也没有屈服。等他向任何形势的操纵屈服的时候，恐怕天上的星星都死绝了。意识到这一点，你突然感觉到有一点骄傲。

En: The lieutenant's eyes express an absolute lack of interest.
Zh: 警督的眼睛流露出毫无兴趣的神情。

En: Move the sandwich around in front of the lieutenant's mouth and make aircraft noises.
Zh: 在警督的嘴巴前面移动三明治，发出飞机的噪音。

En: The lieutenant stands next to you, but he might as well exist in a parallel realm.
Zh: 警督就站在你身边，但也有可能存在于平行世界。

En: No, it's... it's okay. Let's share it, okay? The lieutenant breaks the sandwich in two and quickly eats his half.
Zh: “不，这……好吧。我们分了它，行吧？”警督把三明治掰成两半，然后快速把自己那份咽了下去。

En: No.
Zh: “不要。”

En: Officer. We have serious matters to attend to. I suggest we get to them right now. He seems completely unfazed by the situation.
Zh: “警官。我们还有很重要的事要做。我建议我们最好马上动身。”他似乎完全没有为眼下的情况感到苦恼。

En: Hey!
The lieutenant slaps the sandwich out of your hand faster than you could ever hope to react in your sorry state. It lands in the dirt and falls apart.
Zh: 警督啪的一声把三明治从你手中打落在地，速度快的你都没来得及在难过状态下做出反应。它掉落在泥土里，四分五裂。

En: Proceed with the mission.
Zh: 继续任务。

En: One look at the lieutenant immediately tells you he is ready for your Assault-Sandwich.
Zh: 稍微看一眼警督，你立即就能看出他已经准备好迎接你的三明治袭击了。

En: A bad idea. Men like the lieutenant are not won over by such childish manoeuvres.
Zh: 这是个坏主意。像警督这种男人是不会被这种孩子气的手段说服的。

En: I'm just not hungry.
Zh: “我只是不太饿。”

En: Send the HAM fighter-craft for another mission.
Zh: 派出火腿战斗机去执行另一个任务。

En: Okay, but *why* don't you want it?
Zh: “好吧，但是你*为什么*不想要呢？”

En: Not you, though?
Zh: “你不会吗？”

En: [Put the sandwich away.]
Zh: [把三明治放在一边。]

En: Fuck comeraderie, fuck friendship, FUCK THIS SANDWICH! (Slam it in the ground.)
Zh: “去他妈的兄弟情，去他妈的友谊，去他妈的三明治！”（把摔在地上。）

En: I cannot be bought! (Throw the sandwich on the ground.)
Zh: “我不能被收买！”（把三明治扔在地上。）

En: The ham-sandwich looks fresh and nutritious in you hand, begging to be eaten.
Zh: 你手中的这个火腿三明治看起来新鲜又营养，正祈求着被人吃掉。

En: Great. The lieutenant shakes his head. "Wonderful. Good job. I mean why *wouldn't* you throw it... wait, turns out I don't actually care. Leave it and let's go."
Zh: “很好。”警督摇摇头。“太棒了。做得好。我的意思是你为什么*没有*扔掉它……等等，结果其实我并不在乎。别管它了，我们走吧。”

En: So worth it -- can you feel how liberating that was? De-compression completed.
Zh: 真是值了——你能感觉到这有多自由吗？解压完成。

En: The taste-assault in your mouth abates for a moment... wolfing it down is efficient, but lacking in appreciation.
Zh: 这场美味袭击在你嘴里停留了一会儿……狼吞虎咽是很高效，不过少了一点欣赏。

En: A treat of this magnitude should not be enjoyed alone.
Zh: 这种规模的盛宴不能独自分享。

En: The bonds between officers -- partners -- are *paramount*. That's how trust is built. Trust saves lives.
Zh: 警官之间的团结关系——搭档——是*至高无上*的。信任就是这么建立起来的。信任能拯救生命。

En: The sandwich falls to the ground with a splat, tomatoes and lettuce flying everywhere, mayo spilling out like blood on a crime scene.
Zh: 三明治啪的一声掉在地上，番茄和生菜飞的到处都是，蛋黄酱像犯罪现场的血迹一样喷溅出来。

En: The sandwich falls to the floor with a loud splat, tomatoes and lettuce flying everywhere, mayo spilling out like blood on a crime scene.
Zh: 三明治啪的一声掉在地上，番茄和生菜飞的到处都是，蛋黄酱像犯罪现场的血迹一样喷溅出来。

En: You can do it. It'll be worth it. There's something here.
Zh: 你能做到的。这是值得的。这里一定有些什么。

En: Thanks for nothing.
Zh: 别瞎起劲

En: Steer clear of this one. There's something terrible about this one.
Zh: 避开这玩意，它有点可怕。

En: What?
Zh: 什么？

En: Not thinking about it. Not thinking about it. Back to quiz.
Zh: 别想它。别想它。回到测试。

En: A strange sensation of loss. When she left the Earth, the dust and the ice and the humans... That is unimportant to the quiz. Stop thinking about this.
Zh: 一种奇怪的失落感。当她离开地球，灰尘，坚冰和人类……这些对测试都不重要。别再想了。

En: Yes. The quiz is impersonal. No need to rouse *sensations* in yourself at the mention of Dolores Dei. Who was the *first* innocence -- it wasn't Dolores Dei...
Zh: 是的。这个测试是客观的。没必要一提到德洛莉丝·黛就唤起你心里的*感觉*。谁是*第一任*无罪之王——并不是德洛莉丝·黛……

En: Incorrect again. While she originated many modern institutions, launched several successful expeditions, and was even critical of the innocentic system itself -- and *somehow* keeps popping up in your mind! -- she is not often considered the *strongest*.
Zh: 又错了。虽然她一手创办了很多现代机构，发起了数次成功的远航，甚至还对无罪系统本身持批评态度——而且*不知为何*总会浮现在你的脑海之中！——但她通常并不被认为是*最强大的*。

En: Dolores Dei! Dolores...
Zh: 德洛莉丝·黛！德洛莉丝……

En: Dolores Dei!
Zh: 德洛莉丝·黛！

En: Dolores...
Zh: 德洛莉丝……

En: Dolores...
Zh: 德洛莉丝……

En: Please. Relax.
Zh: 拜托。放松一点。

En: Just steer clear of this one, okay. There's something terrible about this one.
Zh: 只要避开这玩意就好，它有点可怕。

En: Not thinking about it. Not thinking about it. Back to quiz.
Zh: 别想它。别想它。回到测试。

En: What?
Zh: 什么？

En: Who knows? You feel like you *lost* her somehow. When she left the Earth, the dust and the ice and the humans... This is unimportant to the quiz. Stop thinking about this.
Zh: 谁知道？不知怎么的，你感觉自己*失去*了她。当她离开地球，灰尘，坚冰和人类……这些对测试都不重要。别再想了。

En: Yes. The quiz is impersonal. No need to rouse *sensations* in yourself at the mention of Dolores Dei. Who was the *first* innocence -- it wasn't her.
Zh: 是的。这个测试是客观的。没必要一提到德洛莉丝·黛就唤起你心里的*感觉*。谁是*第一任*无罪之王——不是她。

En: Okay. It wasn't Dolores Dei. She's not false.
Zh: 好吧。不是德洛莉丝·黛。她不是伪造的。

En: Dolores...
Zh: 德洛莉丝……

En: No. Stop thinking it. I said it wasn't her. She was true.
Zh: 不。别想了。我说过不是她。她是真的。

En: At least it clearly wasn't Dolores Dei. She wouldn't be *false*. She's beautiful.
Zh: 至少很清楚不是德洛莉丝·黛。她也不是*伪造*的。她很美。

En: While it was actually her...
Zh: 实际上就是她……

En: I don't even have to say it anymore.
Zh: 我甚至都不用再说了。

En: You would have done better if you'd just left Dolores Dei for the end. Dial the Dolores Dei down *a bit*.
Zh: 如果把德洛莉丝·黛放到最后，你肯定会表现地更好一些。稍微少提*一点*德洛莉丝·黛。

En: You would have done better if you'd just dial the Dolores Dei down a bit.
Zh: 如果能少提一点德洛莉丝·黛，你的表现肯定会好一些。

En: Close the book. [Leave.]
Zh: 合上书。[离开。]

En: And more beautiful, and *more* beautiful...
Zh: 而且更加、*更加*美丽……

En: You really navigated some treacherous waters here. Well done.
Zh: 你真的安然渡过了一片危险的水域。做得好。

En: Yes. Immensely.
Zh: 是的。非常。

En: Great!
Zh: 太棒了！

En: Oh yes, militarism and nationalism -- my bad.
Zh: 哦，是的，军国主义和民族主义——是我的错。

En: Yes. Obviously.
Zh: 是的。很明显。

En: You can't. The idea of coming face to face with the *Greatest Innocence* fills you with immense sadness and dread now.
Zh: 你做不到。一想到要面对*最伟大的无罪者*，无尽的悲伤和恐惧就充溢了你的内心。

En: Dora...
Zh: 朵拉……

En: The Greatest Innocence by João Paolo Salomao Lopez de Fuego. The book is large and heavy.
Zh: 《最伟大的无罪者》，作者约翰·保罗·萨洛芒·洛佩斯·富埃戈。这本书又大又重。褴褛飞旋

En: Your educational survey is done. Did you catch any of that? No? Oh well, it's pop quiz time! Let's see what you've learned. This might take a few minutes. You ready?
Zh: 你的教育调查已经完成。你明白了吗？没有？好吧，突击考试时间到！我们来看看你学到了什么。可能需要花上几分钟时间。准备好了吗？

En: Aced it once, no trouble acing it again.
Zh: 已经得过一次优秀了，再来一次也没什么麻烦的。

En: Okay, I can definitely do better this time.
Zh: 好吧，这次我肯定能做得更好。

En: Sure, why not?
Zh: 当然，为什么不呢？

En: Nah, one triumph is good enough for me.
Zh: 不用了，一次胜利对我来说已经足够。

En: Fuck no, I'm not falling for your trickery again.
Zh: 妈的，当然不要了，我再也不会上你当了。

En: Oh god no, let me out of here.
Zh: 天啦，不要，放过我吧。

En: Smarmy jerk. Bet you just got lucky that first time around anyway. Or you cheated off someone smarter.
Zh: 虚情假意的蠢货。反正我赌你第一次不过是碰巧走运罢了。要不就是你去欺骗了一个更聪明的人。

En: Probably for the best. It wasn't looking too good for you, anyway.
Zh: 也许这样最好。反正看起来也不太适合你。

En: Disappointing. You'll never get anywhere with that attitude.
Zh: 失望。你这种态度只会一事无成。

En: Imagine that, it's quite easy when you already know the answers, isn't it? But okay, if this makes you feel better. Here we go again. Question 1: Who was the first innocence?
Zh: 试想一下，当你已经知道答案的时候，这根本就不成问题了嘛，对吧？但无所谓了。如果这能让你感觉良好的话，咱们就再来一遍吧。第一个问题：谁是第一位无罪者？

En: It seems rather unfair to try again after you already know the answers, but sure, if this makes you feel better. Here we go again. Question 1: Who was the first innocence?
Zh: 既然你都已经知道答案，再试一次就显得不太公平了，但无所谓，如果这样能让你感觉良好的话，咱们就再来一遍吧。第一个问题：谁是第一位无罪者？

En: That's the spirit. Here we go! Question 1: Who was the first innocence?
Zh: 好样的。那就开始吧！第一个问题：谁是第一位无罪者？

En: Easy! Everybody knows the answer to this. You, me, anybody.
Zh: 简单！所有人都知道这个答案。你，我，任何人。

En: Oh yeah, this is what I was made for.
Zh: 哦耶，我就是为此而生的。

En: That so? Alright, go on, give me all the hints you got.
Zh: 这样吗？好吧，继续，把所有提示都告诉我。

En: Sola?
Zh: 索拉？

En: Dolores Dei?
Zh: 德洛莉丝·黛？

En: The Perikarnassian?
Zh: 褴褛飞旋佩里卡纳西斯？褴褛飞旋

En: Incorrect. Sola was anointed during the previous century and even lived to see the current one.  She was an urban planner who spoke her mind and largely left history to its own devices, encouraging people to excel on their own rather than prescribing to a deified model of history. She is often called an *anti-innocence*.
Zh: 不对。索拉在上个世纪就被选定，甚至还活着见到了现在这一任。她是一名城市规划师，对历史基本是顺其自然的态度，鼓励人们超越自己，而不是顺从一个神化的历史模范。她一般被称为是*反无罪者*。

En: Incorrect. Dolores Dei was the innocence of humanism, internationalism, and the welfare state. She codified parliamentary democracy and created modern institutions, among these the Moralintern. She was powerful. And beautiful, on all her icons...
Zh: 不对。德洛莉丝·黛是人道主义，国际主义和福利国家的无罪女王。她编制了议会民主制法典，创建了很多现代机构，国际道德伦理委员会就是其中之一。她很强大。她的所有肖像都美丽动人……

En: Correct! Nothing much is known about him. It's not even clear that he was a *he*, but Franconegro presumed as such and called him Pius. He's depicted as a young man with molten gold pouring out of his mouth -- all he spoke was gold. It's said he *invented* god; and equality of men before god. He also introduced the gold standard as a way for measuring people's love for aurum.
Zh: 正确！关于他，我们所知甚少。甚至无法确定他是*男性*，但弗朗哥尼格罗推测是如此，并称之为派厄斯。他被描述为口吐熔化黄金的青年——正所谓金口玉言。据说他*创造*了神，并声称在神面前人人平等。他还制订了金本位制度，用于衡量人们对黄金的爱。

En: A pop quiz is a short examination designed to test your knowledge without any prior warning or announcement. Such exams allow the teacher to assess how thoroughly the students have retained the material at hand -- voila! Now blast that first innocence!
Zh: 突击考试是一种简短的测试，目的是在没有任何事先通知或警告的情况下，测试你的知识水平。这类考试能够让老师评估学生们对手头材料的掌握程度——来啦！现在请说出第一任无罪之王吧！

En: Question 2: Who was the strongest innocence?
Zh: “第二个问题：谁是最强大的无罪者？”

En: Yes, yes, so interesting, but I thought you'd give me the answer to the question.
Zh: 是的，是的，太有意思了，不过我还以为你会直接把问题的答案告诉我。

En: Okay, very interesting, but that doesn't help me answer the question.
Zh: 好吧，很有意思，不过这样也不能帮我回答问题。

En: Sure, try again. I trust you've got my back.
Zh: 当然可以，再试一次。我相信你会支持我的。

En: Doesn't sound too familiar to me, shoot me a hint.
Zh: 听起来不太熟悉，给我个提示。

En: Dolores Dei?
Zh: 德洛莉丝·黛？

En: Vesper-Messina?
Zh: 维斯珀-墨西拿？

En: Franconegro?
Zh: 弗兰考格斯？

En: Incorrect. While she originated many modern institutions, launched several successful expeditions, and was even critical of the innocentic system itself -- not to mention *beautiful* -- she is not often considered the *strongest*.
Zh: 错误。虽然她一手创办了很多现代机构，发起了数次成功的远航，甚至还对无罪系统本身持批评态度——她的*美貌*更是无需再提——但她通常并不被认为是*最强大的*。

En: Incorrect. Vesper-Messina is not a person, but a defunct state on the southeastern coast of the Occident. It used to take up most of the peninsula, before separating into the Republics of Vesper and Messina. Care to try again?
Zh: 不对。维斯珀-墨西拿不是一个人，而是奥西登海岸东南部的一个失落国度。它曾经占据了大部分半岛，之后分裂成了维斯珀共和国和墨西拿。要不要再试一次？

En: Um... I can do better! Okay, so commonly an innocence does not enforce his or her power through military power. This is seen as unnecessary. The innocence wins because an innocence can't help but win, for their deeds are inevitabilities. Did this help?
Zh: 呃……我可以再接再厉！这么说吧，一般来说，无罪者不会通过军事实力来行使权力。因为没有这么做的必要。无罪者所向披靡，是因为无罪者战无不胜，他们的意志必将实现。你觉得这些有用吗？

En: Correct! Named the innocence of militarism, he codified hereditary rule -- but at the same time ended serfdom and established the interisolary reál as the global reserve currency. He also established the concept of the Nation.
Zh: 正确！他被称为军国主义的无罪者，他将世袭制度编为法典——但与此同时，还终结了农奴制度，并建立了以跨洲雷亚尔充当世界储备货币的制度。此外，他还亲手设立了国家的概念。

En: Question 3: Who was the false innocence?
Zh: 第三个问题：谁是虚假的无罪者？

En: An innocence is the highest category of historical personage in the world, a literal personification of History. Traditionally, an innocence, when anointed, assumes supreme rule over the Occident, or the known world in general -- at least the parts that matter.
Zh: 无罪者是世界上最高类别的历史人物，亦是字面意思上的历史化身。依照传统，无罪者会在受膏之时登基，成为奥西登的最高统治者——统治人类所认知的整个世界——或至少是世界上事关紧要的那部分。

En: Got it under control. No problem. Solid on this one. It's wide-spread historical information.
Zh: 一切尽在掌握。没问题。这个绝对可靠。这是一个广而周知的历史信息。

En: Sigh heavily, out loud.
Zh: 重重地叹口气，很大声。

En: Sigh internally.
Zh: 在内心叹了口气。

En: You going to be helpful this time? I could use a hint.
Zh: 这次你能帮上忙吗？我需要提示。

En: All news to me, so maybe a few hints?
Zh: 对我来说都是新知识，也许可以来点提示？

En: Stepan the Despicable?
Zh: 卑鄙者斯特潘？

En: Kedra?
Zh: 肯德拉？

En: Ernö Pasternak?
Zh: 艾尔诺·帕斯捷尔纳克？

En: Incorrect. Stepan the Despicable, regent of Kedra, was a ruler who conquered the known world during the Kedreatic Conquest -- *instead* of the despotic Ernö Pasternak. Would you try again?
Zh: 不对。卑鄙者斯特潘，肯德拉的摄政王，在肯德拉征战期间征服了整个已知世界的统治者——*取代*了艾尔诺·帕斯捷尔纳克。要再试一次吗？

En: Incorrect. Kedra is a large nation in the mid-northern area of the Mundi isola, north of the Occident, between the Irmalan mountain range and the Pisantic *mare interregnum*. Care to try again?
Zh: 不对。肯德拉是蒙迪洲中北部地区的一个大国，位于奥西登北部，厄马兰山脉和*阴暗过渡区*皮桑缇克海中央。要再试一次吗？

En: There seems to be a mix-up with the sources. It's not my fault.
Zh: 信息来源似乎有点混淆了。这不是我的错。

En: Correct! There have been a number of counter- or false-innocences -- some assumed to have innocentic qualities, some who just thought so themselves. Occasionally they have the support of a faction inside the ecclesiastic organization, and accusations of foul play have arisen...
Zh: 正确！曾有数位反无罪者——或是伪无罪者——有些人曾被认为具备成为无罪者的素质，还有些人自认为如此。有些时候，这些人能获得教会组织中某些派别的支持，而违规的指控接踵而至……

En: Final stretch, you've come so far and learned so much. This is the most important one. Question 4: Who was the *greatest* innocence? The most *important* of them all. The most precious to human kind...
Zh: 最终阶段，你已一路披荆斩棘，受益良多，即将面对最关键的问题。第四个问题：谁是*最伟大的*无罪者？无罪者中*至关重要*的存在。对人类而言空前绝后的那位……

En: Yes. There exists a group called the Founding Party. Known as the Holy Party during the time of the Perikarnassian. This, the world's oldest international organization, spends its time in search of either the re-emergence of the innocence, or new members.
Zh: 是的。曾有一个名为奠基党的组织。在佩里卡纳西斯时期被称为圣党。这是史上最古老的世界性组织，致力于寻找重现世间的无罪者，顺便发展新的成员。

En: I've got it! Honest.
Zh: 我明白了！真的。

En: Okay, are you sure *this* time? I'll bite. Hint me.
Zh: 好吧，*这次*你能确定吗？我会咬钩的。给我点提示吧。

En: You sound confident, go right ahead -- a hint would come in handy.
Zh: 你听起来好像很自信，那就来吧——来点提示会有用的。

En: You're *absolutely* certain of this?
Zh: 这次你*绝对*能肯定吗？

En: Ernö Pasternak.
Zh: 艾尔诺·帕斯捷尔纳克。

En: Franconegro.
Zh: 弗兰考格斯。

En: Dolores Dei.
Zh: 德洛莉丝·黛。

En: Incorrect. He was a violent bastard out to torture, maim and kill whoever stood in his way. He was also a *false* innocence, disqualifying him from being the greatest and most important -- and most precious to human kind. Would you like to try again?
Zh: 错误。他是个暴戾的恶棍，沉溺于折磨，残害并杀戮所有妨碍他的人。此外，他还是*虚假的*无罪者，意味着他没有资格成为最伟大的，至关重要的——对人类而言空前绝后的存在。想再试一次吗？

En: Incorrect. While Franconegro built concrete churches and cathedrals, and while many people, especially the Mesque, consider him a father figure due to his militarism and nationalism, João Paolo Salomao Lopez de Fuego argues differently. Another choice, perhaps?
Zh: 不对。弗兰考格斯建造了混凝土教堂和大教堂，而且因为他的军国主义和民族主义思想，有很多人，特别是梅斯克人，会认为他是一个父亲般的角色，但是约翰·保罗·萨洛芒·洛佩斯·富埃戈的意见有些不同。要不，再选一次？

En: Zero doubts.
Zh: 毫不怀疑。

En: Correct! The Mesque might see Franconegro as the father of nations, but as of this century there's been a great shift in attitude -- Dolores Dei has become widely regarded as the greatest innocence. A most radical change to the whole fabric of the world.
Zh: 正确！梅斯克人也许将弗朗哥尼格罗视为世间诸国度之父，但自本世纪以来，这种态度发生了巨大的转变——德洛莉丝·黛被普遍认为是最伟大的无罪者。她为整个世界的结构带来了最彻底的改变。

En: ... an F! For *Failure*. Ha ha ha.
Zh: ……F级。代表*失败*。哈哈哈。

En: ... a D. Not particularly remarkable, but technically a passing grade. Not that anyone really expected better.
Zh: ……D级。不算特别出色，不过从技术上讲，也算过了及格线。其实也没谁期待更好的结果。

En: ... a C. Vaguely passable. A solid effort with nothing much else to show. Nice going, mediocrity-man.
Zh: ……C级。勉强及格。扎实的努力成果，没什么别的可以炫耀。干得好，庸才。

En: ... a B. Good enough. Everyone misses a few from time to time.
Zh: ……B级。已经很不错了。所有人偶尔都有一点失误。

En: ... an A. All correct. Impressive, *oddly* so.
Zh: ……A级。全部回答正确。令人钦佩。虽然有些*古怪*。

En: Of course! This is my *thing*, the reason I exist in this world. The correct answer is Franconegro.
Zh: 当然！这可是我的*专长*，我存在在这个世界上的理由。正确答案是弗兰考格斯。

En: Damn.
Zh: 该死。

En: No.
Zh: 没有。

En: You know, I didn't really absorb anything from what I just read.
Zh: 你知道的，我并没有领会刚才读到的东西。

En: Then go with your best guess! Isn't that what students are trained to do when they don't know the answer?
Zh: 那就尽力去猜吧！学生不知道答案的时候，不是接受过这样做的训练吗？

En: Sola resigned after an assassination attempt by a Yugo nationalist, who blamed her for not taking the side of the left during the turn of the century revolutions. (Innocences don't usually resign). Care to try again?
Zh: 一个尤古民族主义者意图行刺索拉之后，她就退位了，对方指责她在世纪之交的大革命期间，没有站在左翼这边。（无罪之王一般是不会退位的。）要不要再试一次？

En: Her colours are silver, white, and apricot. And when you think her name, Dolores, stomach acid rises to the back of your throat and it hurts -- you see a flash silver, a wreath, an airport bag. And blonde hair, you don't know why. Another choice, perhaps?
Zh: 她的颜色是银色、白色和杏色。当你想起她的名字，德洛莉丝，胃酸就一路攀升到你的喉咙后部，疼痛难耐——你看见一道银色的闪光，一个花冠，还有一个旅行箱。还有一头金发。你也不知道这是为什么。也许，再试一次？

En: As the first innocence, he declared that there should be more of those like him. It is presumed his disciples were the beginning of the Holy Party (the Founding Party).
Zh: 身为首位无罪者，他曾宣称应有更多同类存在于世。人们推测他的门徒即是圣党（奠基党）的起源。

En: Even though the words most associated with her rule are *l'amour, la compassion, l'autodiscipline* -- love, compassion, self-control -- which could be seen as facets of strength. Would you like to try again?
Zh: 尽管跟她的统治关联最密切的词语是*爱*，*怜悯*，*自律*——这些可以被视为是力量的特征。你要不要再试一次？

En: Franconegro attempted to solve the rising tensions between the aristocracy and bourgeoisie by building a unified society, in which every man has a place and a mission, but at the same time may rise to nobility provided on the strength of his virtue.
Zh: 弗兰考格斯试图建立一个统一的社会，以此来化解贵族和资产阶级之间不断升级的紧张局势。在那个社会里，每个人都有自己的位置和使命，不过同时他们也能凭借自己的美德而升为贵族。

En: The most famous -- and important -- of these  was Ernö Pasternak. He was into torture, despotism, hymns, cannons, and world conquest, but got defeated and humiliated by Stepan the Despicable of Kedra.
Zh: 他们之中最著名——而且最重要的——就是艾尔诺·帕斯捷尔纳克。他沉迷于酷刑、独裁、赞美诗、大炮和征服世界，但是却被肯德拉的卑鄙者斯特潘击败和羞辱了。

En: Everything from interisolary travel, to the connected world, to three consecutive scientific revolutions can be traced back to her. Every decade that passes she seems less human somehow -- and more beautiful...
Zh: 从跨洲旅行，到互联世界，再到三次连续的科学革命，这一切都是她的功绩。不知为何，每过十年，她都会变得越来越不像人类——但同时还变得愈加美丽动人……

En: You are really just talking to yourself. It is quite silly.
Zh: 你真的只是在自言自语。看起来挺傻的。

En: The lieutenant is jolted to attention by your sudden outburst. "Huh? Take what?"
Zh: 你突然的爆发把警督一下给惊醒了。“嗯？拿什么？”

En: I put this book in it's place, that's what. I'm so goddamn smart.
Zh: “我把这本书放回原处了，就是这么回事。我他妈太聪明了。”

En: Never mind. Thinking aloud.
Zh: “没什么。只是自言自语。”

En: A-ha. The lieutenant looks at you with his usual lithic mask of a face.
Zh: “啊哈。”警督带着寻常那张扑克脸看着你。

En: Damn you, you arrogant book.
Zh: “该死，你这本傲慢自大的书。”

En: The book is unfair.
Zh: “这本书不公平。”

En: Thinks it's so smart, god damn...
Zh: “我还以为它很聪明，该死……”

En: The lieutenant is jolted awake by your furious cursing. "What's going on with you?"
Zh: 你愤怒的咒骂声一下把警督惊醒了。“你这是怎么了？”

En: Oh-kay. The lieutenant stares at you, his visage unflinching.
Zh: “哦，好吧。”警督盯着你，脸上还是云淡风轻。

En: You are shouting at an inanimate object like a real weirdo. No wonder you seem to have trouble with the right answers.
Zh: 你像个真正的怪人一样朝着一个没有生命的物体大喊大叫。难怪你似乎很难找出正确答案了。

En: Got you this time! Not so smart anymore, are you?
Zh: “这次抓到你了吧！看来你也不比其他人聪明，不是吗？”

En: Aced again! You're no match for me, book.
Zh: “又得分了！你根本比不上我，破书。”

En: Still got it, book!
Zh: “还有两把刷子嘛，破书！”

En: The lieutenant is doing his best to ignore your behaviour.
Zh: 警督尽力让自己忽略你的行为。

En: How is it possible that you're messing this up? Are you doing it on purpose?
Zh: 你怎么可能会把这个搞糟呢？你是有意这么做的吗？

En: Yeah, sure. It's so much easier once you know the answers, right?
Zh: 是啊，当然了。一旦知道答案之后，就变得容易多了，不是吗？

En: The lieutenant is tapping his foot, but you can't tell if it's a habitual tic or impatience.
Zh: 警督拍打着自己的脚，但你也分辨不出是习惯性的抽搐，或者是不耐烦。

En: Fuck you again! You damned condescending prick. I swear, I'll rip you apart!
Zh: “去你妈的！你这个该死的居高临下的蠢蛋。我发誓，一定要你撕碎！”

En: Oh damn it, what went wrong this time.
Zh: “见鬼，这次哪里出错了呢。”

En: But endless tries aren't really fair, don't you think?
Zh: 但是无休止的尝试真的不太公平，你不觉得吗？

En: *Congratulations* on finishing the test again. The results and your subsequent grade have been calculated. You get...
Zh: *恭喜*你再次完成测试。结果和随后的评级已经计算完毕。你拿到了……

En: Congratulations on finishing the test. The results and your subsequent grade have been calculated. You get...
Zh: 恭喜你完成测试。结果和随后的评级已经计算完毕。你拿到了……

En: Man, something's weird about this book. You should do something fun instead. Rock out, forget about it.
Zh: 老兄，这本书有点古怪。你应该做点有趣的事。摇滚起来吧，忘了它。

En: Aced that. Take that, you book.
Zh: “又得分了。接招吧，破书。”

En: Good enough! Take that, you book.
Zh: “够好了！接招吧，破书。”

En: [Put the book away.]
Zh: [放下这本书。]

En: Browsing through the book again, you still have trouble understanding the torrent of information. It only seems to add to the confused jumble of facts inside your brain.
Zh: 再次浏览这本书的时候，你依然很难理解那些滔滔不绝的信息。它似乎只会增加你大脑里困惑杂乱的事实。

En: Browsing through the various chapters, you try your best to understand the ceaseless overflow -- the sprawl -- of names, dates, places, events historical. Most of it ends up as a twisted mass of facts inside your brain.
Zh: 你浏览着各个章节，努力想要理解那些没完没了——杂乱无章——的名字、日期和历史世界。大多数最后都会变成你大脑中一团扭曲的事实。

En: Crack open this immense tome.
Zh: 翻开这本大书。

En: You carefully fold the wrapper into a square, before putting it back into your pocket. Lovingly even -- despite the strange feeling of regret.
Zh: 你小心翼翼地把包装纸折成方形，放回口袋。整个过程甚至显得一往情深——虽然还有一种挥之不去的悔恨。

En: Ancient temple?
Zh: 古代寺庙？

En: Yes. From the height of antiquity. A long, long time ago. Millennia ago. On an island of time you can never return to.
Zh: 是的。站在古代的高度。很久、很久以前。几千年前。在一座时间的孤岛，你再也回不去的那座孤岛。

En: A crinkled chewing gum wrapper. The worn label reads: 'Tutti Frutti.' It smells faintly of apricots, with a barely noticeable twinge of cinnamon.
Zh: 一张皱巴巴的口香糖包装纸。磨损的标牌上写着：‘百果蜜饯’。上面有些微微的杏子香，还有一丝让人难以察觉的肉桂味。”

En: There it is again -- the scent of apricots, with a touch of cinnamon. Smells like the end of some distant summer. The surface of another planet, or some ancient temple.
Zh: 又来了——杏子的香味，再加上一丝肉桂。闻起来像是某个遥远夏日的尾巴。在另一个星球的表面，或者是某座古代寺庙。

En: Why do I even care about this?
Zh: 我为什么要在乎这个？

En: I'll hang on to it, just in case.
Zh: 我会留着它，以防万一。

En: Take a deep, *deep* breath.
Zh: 深深，*深深*地呼吸一口。

En: [Put the wrapper away.]
Zh: [把包装纸放在一边。]

En: Good question, There is no answer or secret phrase written on the inside of the wrapper. Yet you're drawn to it.
Zh: 这是个好问题。包装纸内部没有任何答案或者暗语。不过你还是被它吸引了。

En: Drawn by regret. Stop, before you *hurt* yourself with the Tutti Frutti wrapper. Throw it away, please.
Zh: 被悔恨吸引了。停下来，在你被那张百果蜜饯的包装纸‘伤害’之前，把它扔掉吧，求你了。

En: Bitter, citrus, sweet, it seems to grow stronger, like a glow, with every breath you take...
Zh: 苦涩，柑橘，甜蜜，它似乎变得越来越强烈，就像一阵辉光，你每每呼吸一次……

En: Feels so, so familiar.
Zh: 感觉是如此，如此的熟悉。

En: Feels very, very familiar. Harry. Please. You were supposed to discard it.
Zh: 感觉非常，非常熟悉。哈里，求你了。你应该丢掉它的。

En: Your heart working overtime, trying to keep up with the panicked synapses firing all over your brain. Moving litres of blood through you, panicking.
Zh: 你的心脏正在超负荷运转，努力跟上你大脑里那些恐慌灼热的神经突触。它朝你的身体输送着大量血液，惴惴不安。

En: Cinnamon's not a fruit.
Zh: 肉桂不是水果。

En: You feel compelled to point out that there's nothing fruity about cinnamon -- It's a spice derived from tree bark.
Zh: 你觉得有必要说明一点：肉桂完全没有任何水果的味道——它是一种源于树皮的香料。

En: Can a used wrapper shrug? It seems like that's what the wrapper is doing, somehow. This is not about flowers, or bark? What *is* this about? Why are you looking at this wrapper?
Zh: 一张用过的包装纸会耸肩吗？不知道为什么，它似乎正在做这种事情。这跟花或者树皮无关，对吗？那*这是*关于什么的呢？你为什么要看着这张包装纸？

En: End of summer...
Zh: 夏日的尾巴……

En: Whatever petrochemical by-products they used to create this artificial flavour have bonded tightly to the wrapper. Or is that just your memory, filling in the gaps?
Zh: 不管他们用了什么石油化工副产品创造出这种人造味道，这种紧紧附着在包装纸上的味道。或者那只是你的记忆，在填补空白？

En: The sun sets into the sea, but the water does not boil. Instead it turns to liquid gold. For a moment, the world's store of precious metals seems to increase dramatically, and you are rich...
Zh: 太阳落在海面上，但海水并没有沸腾，反而变成一汪金色的海洋。有那么一瞬间，世界上的贵重金属储量似乎在急剧增加，而你也变得富有起来……

En: There is a movement next to you. The shuffle of a small coat. Warm, like the evening. But when you turn toward it there's nothing there...
Zh: 你旁边有什么东西在活动。是一件小外套在扭捏不安地动作着。暖洋洋的，有如傍晚。然而，当你转向它的时候，那里什么也没有……

En: Where did it go?
Zh: 它去哪了？

En: Wait, come back!
Zh: 等等，快回来！

En: Why are you talking to a gum wrapper?
Zh: 你为什么在跟一张口香糖包装纸说话？

En: You neatly fold the wrapper into a square before you put it back into your pocket. Carefully. Lovingly even...
Zh: 你把包装纸整齐地折成了一个四方形，然后放回自己的口袋。小心翼翼。甚至有些含情脉脉……

En: ... until a blossom of skin and flower petals erupts behind your closed eyes. Made of toffee, cream, and distance. You just *had* to take a dive.
Zh: ……直到你紧闭双眼，由皮肤与花瓣构成的花朵在脑海中绽放。那里传来太妃糖，奶油和疏远的味道。你*不得不*深陷其中。

En: Oh! This seems interesting...
Zh: 哦！这个好像挺有意思的……

En: What better place to escape than past?
Zh: 还有比逃避到过去更好的地方吗？

En: It is physically impossible for a human being to effectively dual-wield *zweihänder* swords in any kind of real-life combat situation.
Zh: 在任何现实战斗中，用两只手有效地挥舞*双手剑*，从身体上讲是不可能的。

En: Enough! You're ruining the pacing of the story. Now where did you leave off? Ah, yes...
Zh: 够了！故事节奏都被你破坏了。我们刚才看到哪里了？啊，是的……

En: That's enough of Man from Hjelmdall for me. (Close the book.)
Zh: 来自赫姆达尔的男人就看到这里吧。（合上书。）

En: No, beautiful naked people fucking in the bushes is what you came for. Sweat, cocks and and titties please!
Zh: 不，你就是冲着漂亮的裸体在灌木丛中野合来的。拜托，来点香汗伶俐的场面吧！

En: Nah, that's just too silly for my taste. (Close the book.)
Zh: 不了，以我的品味来说有点太愚蠢了。（合上书。）

En: He probably is, but you can't be sure, cause this one ends in a cliffhanger and a footnote, saying: To be continued in 'Hjelmdallermann at the Gates of Tomorrow'. Coming soon to your local bookstore.
Zh: 很可能是吧，但你无法确定，因为这本书在悬念中结束了，只留下一条脚注：未完待续，敬请期待“赫姆达尔人在明日之门”。本地书店即将有售。

En: An excellent question. One easily answered by purchasing the next thrilling chapter in the Hjelmdallerman saga: 'Hjelmdallermann at the Gates of Tomorrow.' The last page of this one merely reads: 'To be continued...'
Zh: 真是绝妙的问题。只需购买赫姆达尔人传奇的下一部扣人心弦的篇章：“赫姆达尔人在明日之门”，难题就会迎刃而解。这本书的最后一页只有一句：“未完待续……”

En: Considering the sheer amount of different Hjelmdallermann books out there it's very unlikely you're ever gonna find the sequel.
Zh: 考虑到市面上有那么多不同的赫姆达尔英雄传丛书，你是不太可能找到续作的。

En: You know the story by heart at this point. Nothing new or remarkable catches your eye.
Zh: 此时此刻，你已经把这个故事背下来了。没有什么新的或者值得注意的东西。

En: Considering the sheer amount of different Hjelmdallermann books out there it's very unlikely you're ever gonna find the sequel.
Zh: 考虑到市面上有那么多不同的赫姆达尔英雄传丛书，你是不太可能找到续作的。

En: Considering the sheer amount of different Hjelmdallermann books out there it's very unlikely you're ever gonna find the sequel.
Zh: 考虑到市面上有那么多不同的赫姆达尔英雄传丛书，你是不太可能找到续作的。

En: Considering the sheer amount of different Hjelmdallermann books out there it's very unlikely you're ever gonna find the sequel.
Zh: 考虑到市面上有那么多不同的赫姆达尔英雄传丛书，你是不太可能找到续作的。

En: The cover of this tattered paperback features the Man from Hjelmdall standing atop a pile of bloody corpses. His two zweihänders are crossed over his massive chest in a dramatic 'x', their blades dripping with gore...
Zh: 这本残破的平装书封面上画着来自赫姆达尔的男人，他正站在一堆血淋淋的尸体顶端。他的两把双手剑交叉成一个戏剧性的‘X’形，摆放在他魁梧的胸膛前方，还有鲜血正从剑刃上滴落……

En: Read the first couple of pages.
Zh: 阅读前几页。

En: The story opens *in media res*. The Man from Hjelmdall and his band of northern *reavers* are in the bowels of an ancient temple, surrounded by an army of gibbering *wights* led by the deathless *necromancer*. One by one the brave northmen fall to the relentless crush of undead, only to join their number as reanimated corpses...
Zh: 故事是从*中段*开始的。来自赫姆达尔的男人和他的北方*掠夺者*兄弟正身处一座古老神庙的腹地，被不死*巫师*指挥的一支口齿不清的*生物*大军团团包围。忠诚的北方战士一个又一个被残忍的不死族杀害，转而又加入了复苏的亡灵大军……

En: [Put the book away.]
Zh: 把书放在一边。

En: Look at the back cover.
Zh: 看看封底。

En: The back blurb reads: "His latest adventure serves as the perfect introduction to the savage *Hjelmdall Saga*! Meet Man from Hjelmdall, his faithful blood-brother Tyrbald, the noble Lord Wrôthgär, and some of their most fearsome foes yet. For the Northlands! For HJELMDALL!"
Zh: 背后简介：“他最新的冒险故事可以作为残酷的*赫姆达尔英雄传*的完美介绍！认识一下来自赫姆达尔的男人，他忠诚的结拜兄弟提尔巴德，贵族领主沃斯戈，还有他们最可怕的对手。为了北国的荣耀！为了赫姆达尔！”

En: The publishers must have a dim view of their readership.
Zh: 出版商肯定对他们的读者人数抱持着悲观态度。

En: Where there's a demand, there's always the supply. Nothing wrong with that.
Zh: 哪里有需求，哪里就有供应。这一点没什么错。

En: Their main concern is the continuation of the franchise, not making literary history.
Zh: 他们最主要的顾虑是特许经营权的延续，而不是创造文学历史。

En: People find comfort in familiarity, remembering old experiences, looking at old photos, reading old stories.
Zh: 大家会在熟悉中得到安慰，回忆过去的经历，翻看旧相片，阅读古老的故事。

En: As the vile ceremony ends, the sound of steel unsheathing draws the Nachtherrers' attention to a pair of cloaked figures standing in the doorway...
Zh: 邪恶仪式结束了，钢铁脱鞘的声音把纳赫特尔巫师的注意力吸引到门口那两个身披斗篷的人影身上……

En: Behind him rise the harsh snowcapped peaks of his native Northlands. In bold, red letters, the title "Hjelmdallermann: The Man from Hjelmdall" unfurls like a banner. In the foreground, a nearly nude woman lies spread in supplication, her privates strategically covered by her flowing black hair.
Zh: 在他后面，耸立着北国家乡那白雪皑皑的山峰。红色粗体标题写着“赫姆达尔传说：来自赫姆达尔的男人”，就像一面旗帜迎风招展。前景是一个近乎全裸的女人躺在地上祈祷，私处很有策略性地被自己的黑发遮掩住了。

En: Hell yeah.
Zh: 哦耶。

En: Seems... over the top?
Zh: 好像……有点过分了？

En: By nightfall only the Man from Hjelmdall and his blood-brother, noble Tyrbald are left standing against the dead horde. "It is an honour to die by your side, brother!" Tyrbald cries as his battle-axe beheads one of their reanimated comrades.
Zh: 黄昏时分，唯一的幸存者只剩下来自赫姆达尔的男人和他忠诚的结拜兄弟提尔巴德。“能战死在你身边是一种荣耀，兄弟！”提尔巴德一边挥舞着战斧大叫，一边斩掉了复生同伴的头颅。

En: *Nein*, Tyrbald! the Man from Hjelmdall bellows over the army of flesh and bone. "Have courage! For the Northlands... for HJELMDALL!!!" He leaps to the mezzanine to face the black-eyed figure and like a mad ice bear whirling twin *zweihänders* -- *Sturm* and *Drang* -- he ploughs through the ranks of the deathless, surrounding the necromancer...
Zh: “*不行*，提尔巴德！”来自赫姆达尔的男人在血肉和骨头的大军中怒吼。“勇敢一些！为了北国……为了赫姆达尔！！！”他瞬间跳上夹层，直面黑眼人，像一头发疯的冰熊，挥舞着手中的一对*双手剑*——*风暴*和*怒涛*——他费力地从包围着巫师的不死族士兵中穿过……

En: ... and with deadly finesse his twin blades scissor the necromancer's head from its body. The undead legions collapse like rag dolls as the dark magic leaves them and the two northmen stand alone in the accursed temple, where the bodies of their lost brothers litter the floors.
Zh: ……他用一对双手剑使出致命绝技，巫师立即身首分离。他的不死奴仆像布娃娃一样崩溃倒地，只剩下两个北国男儿孤独地站在诅咒神庙里，周围的地板上躺着他们逝去的兄弟。

En: Skip ahead.
Zh: 跳着看。

En: I've read enough. (Close the book.)
Zh: 我看够了。（合上书。）

En: The Man from Hjelmdall stares at you on the cover, his face fixed in grim resolve.
Zh: 封面上，来自赫姆达尔的男人紧盯着你，满脸尽是残酷的决绝。

En: A few chapters later the Man from Hjelmdall and Tyrbald are in a completely different setting, high above an arid desert, riding a pair of gryffins that always seem to appear at narratively convenient moments...
Zh: 几章之后，来自赫姆达尔的男人和提尔巴德就来到了一个完全不同的地方，他们骑着格里芬在一片荒芜沙漠的高空翱翔，它们似乎总是在为了方便叙述的时刻出现……

En: The northmen find themselves in the court of a powerful southern king -- *Lord Wrôthgär*. The old king beseeches the Man from Hjelmdall to rid his kingdom of the *Nachtherrer*, a subterranean clan of blind wizards who traded their souls for demonic powers.
Zh: 北国男儿发现他们来到了一个庭院，那里的主人是强大的南方国王——*沃斯戈领主*。老国王恳求来自赫姆达尔的男人除掉王国境内的*纳赫特尔*，一个盲眼巫师的地下部落，他们因为出卖自己的灵魂获得了恶魔的力量。

En: The Man from Hjelmdall extracts a high price from the desperate king. He asks land for the homeless desert-pygmies living exiled in the kingdom's borderlands. Wrôthgär offers the hand of his eldest daughter instead, but the son of winter does not budge...
Zh: 来自赫姆达尔的男人向绝望的国王开出了一个高价。他为流亡到王国边境那些无家可归的沙漠侏儒请求一片生存之地。沃斯戈试图以自己的长女作为交换，然而冬之子没有改变心意……

En: Hold on, why is the Man from Hjelmdall doing any of this?
Zh: 等一下，来自赫姆达尔的男人为什么要这么做？

En: What do you mean?
Zh: 你是什么意思？

En: Why is he doing all of this?
Zh: 他为什么要这么做？

En: Nevermind. (Keep reading.)
Zh: 没关系。（继续看。）

En: Because the flame of justice burns bright within his chest. Because above all he's driven to bring honour to the name of his beloved Hjelmdall. Because his noble Katlan blood lusts for glory and conquest. It cannot be denied.
Zh: 因为正义之火在他胸中熊熊燃烧。因为他首先想到的是为自己心爱的赫姆达尔赢得荣耀。因为他高贵的卡特拉血脉渴望着征服和荣耀。这是无法抗拒的。

En: Is that all?
Zh: 就这些？

En: Fair enough. Let's get back in there.
Zh: 有道理。还是回到正题吧。

En: What do you mean 'is that all'?
Zh: 你说‘就这些’是什么意思？

En: Is there anything else to his character?
Zh: 他还有什么性格特点吗？

En: He is the Man from Hjelmdall! Forged in the fury of battle, death was his sire and blood his dam. He had but fourteen winters when he left his frigid homelands in Katla to seek glory and honour. *That* is his character.
Zh: 他可是来自赫姆达尔的男人！在激烈的战斗中出生，死亡是他的先父，鲜血是他的先母。当他离开卡特拉的寒冷北国，寻求骄傲和荣耀的时候，不过才刚刚度过了14个冬季。*那*就是他的性格。

En: Exactly. Where's the tension *inside* him?\n
Zh: 没错。他*内心*深处的压力在哪里？

En: Okay, but does he have any *internal* tension?
Zh: 好吧，不过他有什么*内在*的压力吗？

En: Muscle and blood is all I need. Let's get back to that.
Zh: 我只需要肌肉和鲜血。还是继续说回那个吧。

En: Nevermind, just get to the good part.
Zh: 没关系，还是直接进入好的部分吧。

En: He is the wildest man alive, the only mortal strong enough to wield the twin-*zweihänders*, *Sturm* and *Drang*, whose names mean 'Storm' and --
Zh: 他是现存人类中最狂放的男人，唯一强壮到能挥舞那对*双手剑*，*风暴*和*怒涛*的男人……

En: Yes, I get that. He's strong and has big swords. But why should I *care* about his character?
Zh: 是啊，我明白了。他很强壮，还有一对大剑。但是我为什么要*在乎*他的性格呢？

En: What are you? A book critic?
Zh: 你是什么人？书籍评论家吗？

En: No, you're right, let's give this a chance. (Keep reading.)
Zh: 不，你说得对，我们给它个机会吧。（继续看。）

En: I don't need sass from a discount paperback. (Close the book.)
Zh: 我才不要跟打折平装书顶嘴呢。（合上书。）

En: You skip past a section detailing an elaborate banquet with *many* toasts, as well as the long and perilous journey from Wrôthgär's keep to the ancient forest of *Yezzdor*, where our heroes infiltrate the Nachtherrer's dire-hall...
Zh: 你跳过了一个章节，里面详细描述了一场豪华宴会，里面有*很多*祝酒辞，还有从沃斯戈领主城堡前往*叶泽多尔*古代森林那段冗长而又乏味的旅程，我们的英雄就是从那里潜入了纳赫特尔部落的恐怖礼堂……

En: In the centre of the great hall stands a blood altar on which a pair of Nachtherrer are disembowelling a shrieking peasant -- an unholy rite that empowers the Nachtherrer forbidden magic...
Zh: 大礼堂中央站矗立着一个鲜血祭坛，一对纳赫特尔巫师正剖开一个尖叫农民的胸膛——这是一种罪恶的仪式，能赋予纳赫特尔巫师禁忌魔法的力量……

En: Swiftly the visitors toss off their cloaks and unsheathe glimmering northern steel -- it's the Man from Hjelmdall and his valiant blood-brother Tyrbald!
Zh: 来访者瞬间扯下了他们的斗篷，拔出闪闪发光的北国钢铁——是来自赫姆达尔的男人和他勇敢的结拜兄弟提尔巴德！

En: Well, yeah, who else would it be?
Zh: 呃，是啊，还有可能是谁呢？

En: Whoa. I did *not* see that coming!
Zh: 哇哦。我确实*没有*猜到这种走向！

En: In an instant the Nachtherrer summon a horde of gibbering orkenoids who rush our heroes. The hall fills with the clang of blades and the screams of the dying as the floor goes slick with steaming orkenoid viscera...
Zh: 电光火石之间，纳赫特尔巫师召唤了一支口齿不清的鬼精大军朝我们的英雄冲了过去。礼堂里充满剑刃的叮当声和死亡的叫喊声，地面因为热腾腾的鬼精内脏变得光滑起来……

En: When the din of battle settles, only the Man from Hjelmdall and Tyrbald remain standing. Exhausted and dripping with the entrails of their foes, the two northmen confront the foul Nachtherrer. The Man from Hjelmdall's sodalite-blue eyes fill with indescribable fury. "For the Northlands!" he cries, rushing headlong into a desperate *mêlée*...
Zh: 当战斗的喧嚣平息之时，唯有来自赫姆达尔的男人和提尔巴德屹立不倒。两条北方汉子筋疲力尽，浑身沾满敌人的内脏，与邪恶的纳赫特尔对峙的时刻已至。来自赫姆达尔的男人那如同方纳石般湛蓝的双眼充满了无法描述的愤怒。“为了北地！”他怒吼着冲上前去，投身于孤注一掷的*肉搏战*……

En: You gotta be kidding me...
Zh: 你是在跟我开玩笑吧……

En: Cut 'em up, Hjelmdallermann!
Zh: 把它们全砍倒，来自赫姆达尔的男人！

En: What?
Zh: 什么？

En: This is almost exactly the same as the first scene! Even the word *gibbering* is used twice.
Zh: 这几乎跟第一幕一模一样嘛！甚至*口齿不清*这个词还用了两遍！

En: In the first scene the Man from Hjelmdall fought a powerful necromancer and his army of the dead. Now he's confronting a pair of Nachtherrer and their horde of orkenoid thralls. The two couldn't be further apart.
Zh: 在第一幕里，来自赫姆达尔的男人是在跟一个强大的巫师和他的亡灵大军战斗。现在他面对的是一对纳赫特尔巫师，还有他们的鬼精奴仆。这两简直有天壤之别好吗。

En: Okay, fine. What happens next?
Zh: 好吧。那接下来呢？

En: Wow... they're in trouble now.
Zh: 哇哦……现在他们遇到麻烦了。

En: I'm too old for this...
Zh: 我太老了，不适合这个……

En: Actually yes, I'm a *goddamn* art cop.
Zh: 其实是的，我是个*该死*的艺术警探。

En: I might be.
Zh: 我也许是。

En: All aboard for slaughter and glory. (Keep reading.)
Zh: 来不及了，快上車，屠杀和荣耀等着我们呢。（继续看。）

En: These images speak to something burbling deep within you: Quick! Find someone or something that needs to be gutted, beheaded, impaled on a spike!
Zh: 这些画面对你内心深处的某些心声说到：快点！找个人，或者找个东西，把它开肠破肚，斩首，刺死在尖刺上！

En: Naked women and giant swords -- is that really the best they could come up with? Feels formulaic and... derivative.
Zh: 裸女和大剑——他们就只能想出这些东西了？感觉很公式化，而且……像派生物。

En: These images speak to something burbling deep within you: The primal struggle of a bunch of violent apes just dukin' it out.
Zh: 这些画面对你内心深处的某些心声说到：一群暴力类人猿的原始战争就这么结束了。

En: That is what you came for, isn't it? A climactic bloodletting, where men are reduced to muscle and fury?
Zh: 你就是为这个来的，不是吗？一场高潮迭起的杀戮，人类在那里都退化成了肌肉和愤怒？

En: He doesn't even have a real name. What is inside this Man? Or is muscle and bone all he is?
Zh: 他甚至连一个真正的名字都没有。这个男人心里在想什么？或者除了一堆肌肉和骨头之外，他什么也不是？

En: Hey! Nothing wrong with muscle and bone.
Zh: 喂！肌肉和骨头可没什么问题。

En: The point is: Why should you *care* about this character? Does he have any inner conflicts? Unspoken motivations? A tragic flaw, perhaps?
Zh: 问题在于：你为什么要*关心*这个角色？他有什么内在冲突吗？难以言喻的动机？也许，是一个悲剧性的缺陷？

En: What pacing?!
Zh: 什么节奏？！

En: Oh, for fuck's sake...
Zh: 哦，去他妈的吧……

En: The writer has *never* held a sword in their life, let alone two two-handed swords at the same time.
Zh: 作者这辈子肯定*从来没有*拿过剑，更不用说同时拿起两只双手剑了。

En: This book makes a mockery of the very idea of good plotting, though something tells you coherence was never the point.
Zh: 这本书是对优秀情节的嘲讽，不过有什么东西告诉你，连贯性从来都不是重点。

En: The Man from Hjelmdall stares out at you from the cover, his face fixed in grim resolve.
Zh: 封面上，来自赫姆达尔的男人紧盯着你，满脸尽是残酷的决绝。

En: Below, in smaller font, you see the parenthetical: "(Adapted from: 'Man from Hjelmdall and the Necromancer's Treasure,' 'Man from Hjelmdall: Lord of Wrôthgär,' and 'Man from Hjelmdall: The Curse of Nachtherrer.')"
Zh: 下面用更小的字号附加了一段说明：“（改编自：‘来自赫姆达尔的男人与亡灵巫师的宝藏’，‘来自赫姆达尔的男人：沃斯戈之王’，以及‘来自赫姆达尔的男人：纳赫特尔的诅咒’。”

En: A brazen attempt to bilk fans for more money by splicing together old stories under a new title.
Zh: 一个厚颜无耻的企图，把老故事冠上新标题，然后从粉丝那里骗取更多钱财。

En: Behind him rise the harsh snowcapped peaks of his native Northlands. In bold, red letters, the title "Hjelmdallermann: The Man from Hjelmdall" unfurls like a banner. In the foreground, a nearly nude woman lies spread in supplication, her privates strategically covered by her flowing black hair.
Zh: 在他后面，耸立着北国家乡那白雪皑皑的山峰。粗体红色的标题写着“赫姆达尔传说：来自赫姆达尔的男人”，就像一面旗帜迎风招展。前景是一个近乎全裸的女人躺在地上祈祷，私处很有策略性地被自己的黑发所遮掩住了。

En: *This* is beyond primitive.
Zh: *这个*已经超出原始了。

En: Put book away.
Zh: 把书放在一边。

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: You open the book to a random page. Man from Hjelmdall, wielding his two zweihänders, is carving through a sea of savages, his visage fixed in grim determination.
Zh: 你随便翻开了这本书的某一页。来自赫姆达尔的男人正挥舞着两把双手大剑，在蛮族的人山人海中浴血奋战，他的表情坚定，满是不屈的决心。

En: Time to flip through the book.
Zh: 是时候翻阅这本书了。

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: The jacket copy proclaims: "Man from Hjelmdall returns in his most exciting adventure yet! After crashing into a strange jungle, cannibalistic natives abduct his only surviving comrade, noble Tyrbald. Before Man from Hjemdall can mount a rescue..."
Zh: 封皮上写着：“来自赫姆达尔的男人，带着最激动人心的冒险回归了！闯入一片怪异的丛林后，食人土著绑架了他唯一幸存的伙伴，忠诚的提尔巴德。来自赫姆达尔的男人还没来得及展开救援……”

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: ...he is ambushed by a tribe of female warriors and taken to the ancient citadel of Cloud City, where a mysterious and wicked queen rules supreme. Will Man from Hjelmdall be able to escape his dire situation and find his missing friend?
Zh: “……他就被一个女战士部落伏击，带回了云之城的远古城堡，那里的统治者是一个神秘又邪恶的女王。来自赫姆达尔的男人究竟能否逃离这个可怕的处境，找到他走失的好友呢？”

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: The edges of the pages are worn and smudged. A lot of people have read about the Devil Woman's altercations with the Hjelmdall Man.
Zh: 书页的边缘残破又肮脏。很多人看过魔女和来自赫姆达尔的男人之间的争执片段。

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: Berserker rage burns in his azure-hued eyes as he brings glory and honour to his long lost Hjelmdaller tribe from the village of Hjelmdall. The ivory giant roars like an ice bear, and the winds of Katla howl out his name.
Zh: 狂战士的怒火在他天蓝色的眼睛里熊熊燃烧，他给已经逝去许久的赫姆达尔村庄部落带去了光辉与荣耀。乳白色的巨人像冰熊一样咆哮，卡特拉的狂风大声呼喊出它的名字。

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: A passage reads: "The Man from Hjelmdall looks up, his eyes blue as the mountain lakes of his homeland. He rarely speaks, but now his voice booms in the darkened throne room: 'Do not try to sap my masculine essence, wicked temptress! Son of Hjelmdall will never succumb to your seductive wiles'."
Zh: 有一个段落是这么写的：“来自赫姆达尔的男人抬起头，蓝色的眼睛宛如故土山中的湖泊。他很少说话，但是此时此刻，他的声音在黑暗的王宫大殿里轰鸣：‘别想吸干我的阳气，你这个邪恶的妖妇！赫姆达尔的子孙永远不会屈服于你诱人的诡计。’”

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: 'Thine spells are no match for purity and strength of will. Brothers of Hjelmdall stand above the vices of flesh for it is weak and corruptible, yet mine is forged in gore and strife.' Queen Lydiaana just laughs, a sultry and salacious sound, then says...
Zh: “‘你的魔咒根本无法与纯洁和意志的力量相抗衡。赫姆达尔的弟兄们将会站立在肉体的罪恶之上，因为它是脆弱而腐败的，然而，我的肉体确是用鲜血和冲突铸造的。’莉迪亚拉女王笑了，嗓音风骚而淫荡，然后她说到……”

En: I kept hoping it would get better but the writing is terrible and repetitive.
Zh: 我一直希望后面能变好一点，不过写作实在太糟糕，而且还是重复的。

En: Gimme more!
Zh: 再接着看看！

En: I've had enough. (Close the book.)
Zh: 我受够了。（合上书。）

En: Okay, got it. No need to read further.
Zh: 好吧，了解。没必要继续看下去了。

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: 'I have grand plans for you, Man from Hjelmdall.' She gestures her diabolical hand toward an array of potions and unguents. 'First you shall please me, then lead my armies against the vicious cannibals.' Not a muscle moves in the face of Man from Hjelmdall, yet inside there is turmoil -- this goes against all he holds sacrosanct.
Zh: “‘我为你准备了一个宏伟的计划，来自赫姆达尔的男人。’她伸出恶魔之手，朝着一大堆药剂和油膏比了个手势。‘首先你要取悦我，然后带领我的军队去跟邪恶的食人族战斗。’来自赫姆达尔的男人的面部毫无波澜，不过内心却在骚动——这违反了他奉若神明的一切。”

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: What do you even know about literature, have you ever read a book in your life?
Zh: 你对文学到底有多少了解，你这辈子到底看过书没有？

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: Man from Hjelmdall rides on a gilded gryffin, his golden mane billowing in the breeze. Both zweihänders sheathed on his back, he is off to war. Will he conquer his enemies? Will he conquer himself?
Zh: 来自赫姆达尔的男人骑上一头镀金的格里芬，金色的鬓毛在微风中飘扬。两把双手剑都挂在他的背后，他要奔赴战场。他能否征服敌人？他能否战胜自己？

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: The steel muscles of Man from Hjelmdall gleam in the humid jungle air, yet the man does not sweat. In meditation his soul drifts in the frigid Northlands he calls home.
Zh: 来自赫姆达尔的男人那有如钢铁一般的肌肉在潮湿的丛林空气中闪闪发亮，但他并没有流汗。在冥想之中，他的灵魂依然在寒冷的北国，那里是他的家乡。

En: Of course I have.
Zh: 我当然有了。

En: Maybe, I don't know.
Zh: 也许吧，我不知道。

En: Excuse me, I know a thing or two about critical analysis. And art.
Zh: 不好意思，我对批判性分析还是略知一二的。还有艺术。

En: Oh shit, gritty and heroic.
Zh: 该死，真是坚忍不拔，英勇无畏。

En: Ugh, vulgar. (Close the book.)
Zh: 呃，庸俗。（合上书。）

En: It calls out to you. This is the spirit of Revachol.
Zh: 它在呼唤着你。这就是瑞瓦肖精神。

En: Let's give this a chance...
Zh: 我们给它个机会……

En: Holy shit, this is so *me*! (Really get into the book.)
Zh: “真他么绝了，这不就是*我*吗！”（真正投入书中。）

En: Wow... this is epic!
Zh: 哇哦……这真是一部史诗！

En: Onward!
Zh: 勇往直前！

En: Uh-huh. I'm done. (Close the book.)
Zh: 呃。我够了。（合上书。）

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: In the final pages, Man from Hjelmdall mounts Galawaryn -- his mighty gryffin -- and turns his gaze to the horizon. Queen Lydiaana is dead, but an army of cannibals is storming the gates and still there's no word of trusty Tyrbald...
Zh: 在这本书的最后几页，来自赫姆达尔的男人骑上了加拉沃恩——他那头威风的格里芬——并将目光投向远方。莉迪亚女王已逝，但食人族的大军仍在冲击城门，还是没有任何关于提尔巴德的消息……

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: This is not just another book. It's Man from Hjelmdall. And it's done with you.
Zh: 这不是普通的书。这是来自赫姆达尔的男人。而且它跟你已经没什么好说了。

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: Write a thesis. Man from Hjelmdall is done with you.
Zh: 那就写篇论文去。来自赫姆达尔的男人跟你之间已经结束了。

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: Man from Hjelmdall has no time for illiterates. The book is done with you.
Zh: 来自赫姆达尔的男人没空搭理文盲。这本书跟你之间已经结束了。

En: Look at the back cover.
Zh: 看看封底。

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: His arms, whirling like windmills, are soaked with the blood of his enemies. Mangled corpses litter the battlefield.
Zh: 他的胳膊像风車一样挥舞着，上面沾满了敌人的鲜血。严重损毁的尸体散落在战场上。

En: Good god this is some retrograde stuff. When was this shit written?
Zh: 天啦，这玩意简直是文学的退化。这些鬼东西是什么时候写出来的？

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: You flip to the copyright page. This book was written in '38.
Zh: 你翻到了版权页。书是38年写的。

Actor: Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman
En: To find out what happens next, you'll have to pick up "Man from Hjelmdall and the Three-Eyed Skull", available in fine bookstores everywhere, spring '39.
Zh: 欲知后事如何，请购买“来自赫姆达尔的男人与三眼骷髅”，39年春季，所有精品书店正式发售。

En: You spend a moment contemplating the cover's slightly unnerving swirl of orange, yellow, and green.
Zh: 你凝视着封面上略微令人心慌的漩涡，其中卷入了橙色，黄色和绿色……

En: The cover of this pocket-sized volume features a swirl of orange, yellow, and green. The title, "A Brief Look at Infra-Materialism," is set in an authoritative yet approachable serif font.
Zh: 这本袖珍书籍的封面上画着橙黄绿三色相间的漩涡。标题“亚唯物主义简论”采用了充满威严，却又平易近人的衬线字体。

En: What an interesting colour palette. It's vibrant, yet somehow leaves you ever so slightly nauseated.
Zh: 多有意思的颜色搭配啊。它充满生机，却又让你略感恶心。

En: No, no. These are *terrible* colours. They're like... like...
Zh: 不，不。这些颜色非常*糟糕*。这就像……就像……

En: Apricots.
Zh: 杏子。

En: On the inside jacket flap, you find a brief summary: "What is infra-materialism? A highly theoretical branch of Mazovian communism? A collection of mystical ramblings by a discredited revolutionary? Or possibly both?"
Zh: 你在勒口上读到了这样一段提要：“什么是亚唯物主义？是马佐夫康米主义的高度理论化分支？还是一位声名狼藉的革命家令人费解的胡话合集？或者两者皆是？”

En: This Brief Look(TM) introduces readers to one of this century's most fascinating and misunderstood theories in a concise, jargon-free manner.
Zh: “这本简论（TM）以简洁明快，毫无术语的方式，向读者介绍本世纪最迷人，最遭人误解的理论之一。”

En: So far, so good.
Zh: 到目前为止，一切还好。

En: This feels like kid stuff. Is there no Slightly Longer Glance(TM) series?
Zh: 感觉像是哄小孩的。就没有稍微深入了解（TM）系列吗？

En: Let's return to it later. [Leave.]
Zh: 以后再说吧。[离开。]

En: You turn to the table of contents. The guide itself is divided into several sections with seemingly esoteric titles like 'Effects of Plasm on Root Vegetables' and 'Mental Projection and Transference'. There's also a brief introduction about the life of Ignus Nilsen.
Zh: 你翻到目录页。这本指南被划为数个版块，每个都挂着高深莫测的命名，例如“原质对根茎类蔬菜的效用”，“精神投射和移情”。还附有一篇对伊格纳斯·尼尔森生平的简介。

En: The back of the book contains four or five pages of primary and secondary sources, but you'd need access to a university library to find most of them.
Zh: 书后附有四五页一手及二手资料，但其中绝大多数恐怕只能在大学的图书馆里找到。

En: In other words, this guide is the only source you're likely to find.
Zh: 换句话说，想了解这个的话，这本指南就是你唯一的信息来源了。

En: The book fits quite snugly into your palm. It would also fit comfortably into a jacket pocket.
Zh: 这本书的尺寸紧贴你的掌心。刚好适合塞进夹克口袋里。

En: Start reading the first chapter.
Zh: 开始阅读第一章。

En: Keep reading.
Zh: 继续阅读。

En: [Put the book away.]
Zh: [把书放在一边。]

En: Read the introduction on Ignus Nilsen.
Zh: 阅读伊格纳斯·尼尔森的生平简介。

En: Finish the book.
Zh: 看完这本书。

En: Known to his numerous admirers as the 'Evangelist of the Revolution' and to his even-more-numerous enemies as the 'Apocalyptic Shrike', Ignus Nilsen remains one of the most controversial and fascinating figures to emerge in the years of the Antecentennial Revolution, second only to Kras Mazov himself.
Zh: 为数众多的仰慕者将其视为*革命的福音战士*，而为数更多的敌人则将其称为*末日的伯劳鸟*。世纪大革命的年代已逝，而伊格纳斯·尼尔森仍是当年涌现出的最具争议，最迷人的历史人物之一，仅次于卡拉斯·马佐夫。

En: During his unparalleled life, he helped guide a revolution in one country and found a new state in another. Along the way, he committed some of the most notorious war crimes in an era famed for its atrocities.
Zh: 在其空前绝后的一生中，他为一个国家的革命指引了方向，还在另一个国家建立了全新的政权。在这一过程中，他还犯下了一些臭名昭著的战争罪，即便是在那个以残暴著称的年代，他的罪行也显得耸人听闻。

En: He is your brother, a fellow *doom prophet*.
Zh: 他是你的兄弟，你们同为*末日先知*。

En: And yet, his most *fascinating* contribution to history may be the most overlooked: His theory of ideological plasm, from which his followers and successors developed the school of communism known as *infra-materialism*.
Zh: 然而，他对历史所做出的最*迷人*的贡献，反而可能是最被世人忽视的：他的意识形态原质理论。他的追随者和继承者从中发展出了一个康米主义学派，名为*亚唯物主义*。

En: If you're like most people, you probably believe that your thoughts reside in your brain, right?
Zh: 如果你与大多数人意见一致，你很可能认为思维活动要通过大脑进行，对吧？

En: Yes, you have a little cabinet for organizing and storing them away.
Zh: 是的，你有一个小小的置物阁，用于整理和存放思维。

En: That's not right at all. Thoughts don't exist in the brain, they float through the *air*! Your brain is but a fish swimming through them...
Zh: 根本不是那样。思维并不存在于大脑里，它们漂浮在*空气*中！你的大脑不过是游荡在其中的一条小鱼……

En: Completely backwards. You think with your hands, always have.
Zh: 根本就是与事实背道而驰。你一直是靠双手思考的。

En: Maybe if you're some kind of binoclard. *You* on the other hand, are a gut-thinker, through and through.
Zh: 只有四眼才那样。而*你*完全是靠直觉思考的。

En: Uh, right?
Zh: 呃，是这样吗？

En: Nah, I always listen to my gut.
Zh: 不，我向来只听从直觉。

En: I'm more of a tactile guy. I let my fingers guide me.
Zh: 我更讲究手感。十指指引我前行。

En: I always thought I *swam* through them.
Zh: 我一直以为是我在思维之中*畅游*。

En: Honestly, I have no idea where some of these thoughts come from.
Zh: 说实在的，我也不知道某些想法到底是怎么来的。

En: Wrong! At least according to Ignus Nilsen...
Zh: 大错特错！至少是不符合伊格纳斯·尼尔森的理论……

En: There is no evidence that the human digestive system is the seat of cognition. But let's get back to Ignus Nilsen, shall we?
Zh: 直觉？没有证据表明直肠具备知觉。但咱们还是回到伊格纳斯·尼尔森的正题上吧，好吗？

En: It's common for some developmentally delayed children to confuse sites of sensory input with the site of cognition. But let's get back to Ignus Nilsen, shall we?
Zh: 某些发育迟缓的儿童经常会分不清感觉输入的位置与认知的位置。但咱们还是回到伊格纳斯·尼尔森的正题上吧，好吗？

En: *Very* interesting. And not so far removed from Ignus Nilsen's own theories...
Zh: *真*有意思。而且与伊格纳斯·尼尔森的理论相差不多……

En: This sounds more like a question for a psychiatrist. (See also: 'A Brief Look at Psychiatry'.) But let's stick with Ignus Nilsen a moment...
Zh: 这听起来更像是留给精神科医生的问题。（另见：“精神病学简论”。）但咱们还是继续谈伊格纳斯·尼尔森的事吧……

En: As Mazov's devoted comrade and leading theoretician, Nilsen was responsible for developing much of the intellectual foundation of communism. But his interests and speculations were famously wide-ranging.
Zh: 身为马佐夫的忠实战友和首席理论家，尼尔森负责发展康米主义的大部分学术基础。但他以兴趣广泛著称，在广博的领域留下了深刻的见解。

En: During his final years in exile, he produced, among other things, an early guide to homebrewing, instructions for raising revolutionary children, plans for a universal pictographic language, and a detailed materialist critique of Dolores Dei's chess strategy.
Zh: 他的晚年是在流放中度过的，在此期间留下了诸多作品，包括自酿啤酒指南，培养革命小将的方法，创造世界通用象形文字的计划，还有一部细致入微的，针对德洛莉丝·黛棋艺的唯物主义批判。

En: We may yet discover, he wrote in his notebooks, "that under certain, exceptional circumstances, the proletariat's embrace of historical materialism may be *so fervent* that their beliefs take form in the world of matter as a kind of revolutionary 'plasm'."
Zh: 他在笔记中写道：“我们可能已经发现，在某些特殊情况下，普罗阶级对历史唯物主义的拥护是*如此热烈*，甚至足以让他们的信念在物质世界中化为实体，成为一种革命的‘原质’。”

En: Certainly. In essence, Nilsen is arguing that thoughts don’t just reside *inside* the brain, they radiate outward *from* it. According to this idea, the brain is an ideological transponder, constantly emitting waves of highly politicised energy, which Nilsen called 'plasm'.
Zh: 当然。究其本质，尼尔森声称思想并不仅仅存在于大脑*内部*，思想还*从中*放射至外界。根据这一观点，大脑是意识形态的转发器，持续发射着高度政治化的能量，尼尔森称之为“原质”。

En: Whoa is right! What's more, Nilsen speculated that this plasm, when it becomes powerful enough, might begin to *influence* the material reality surrounding it. Hence the name: *infra-materialism*.
Zh: 哇得好啊！此外，尼尔森曾推测，只要这种原质积累足够的能量，就有可能*影响到*周遭的物质实体。*亚唯物主义*由此得名。

En: Stay with us a moment longer. What's more, Nilsen speculated that this plasm, when it becomes powerful enough, might begin to *influence* the material reality surrounding it. Hence the name: *infra-materialism*.
Zh: 再读一会儿吧。此外，尼尔森曾推测，只要这种原质积累足够的能量，就有可能*影响到*周遭的物质实体。*亚唯物主义*由此得名。

En: Unfortunately, Nilsen passed away before he was able to develop these initial ideas into a full-fledged theory. *That* work was left to subsequent generations of communist theorists...
Zh: 遗憾的是，尼尔森还没来得及将这些初步构思发展为成熟的理论，就撒手人寰。*这项*工作留给了后世的康米主义理论家们……

En: Building on Nilsen's basic insight, these theorists reached a startling conclusion: that a sufficiently revolutionary state might begin to exhibit certain *extra-physical* effects based on the amount of plasm generated by its citizens.
Zh: 基于尼尔森的基本理解，这些理论家得出了一个惊人的结论：一个足够革命的国家，基于其人民生成的大量原质，有可能会展现出某种*超物理*的效果。

En: Correct. Though certain particulars of the theory are commonly attributed to Nilsen himself, the *evidentiary* basis of those attributions has always been a point of contention between infra-materialists and their critics.
Zh: 正确。虽然这一理论的特定细节通常被归功于尼尔森，但这些来源的证明是否*证据确凿*，一直是亚唯物主义者及其批判者争论的焦点。

En: In fact, many of infra-materialism's critics both inside and outside the communist movement have said the same thing.
Zh: 事实上，很多亚唯物主义的批判者都表示过相同的观点，无论他们是否是康米主义运动的参与者。

En: Please, just stick with it. Otherwise, may we recommend 'A Brief Look at Words and Concepts'?
Zh: 拜托，别跑题。不然的话，你对我们推荐的“词汇与概念简论”感兴趣吗？

En: The actual theory is highly technical, but for the purposes of this Brief Look(TM), that's a fine *working definition* of the concept.
Zh: 实际上，这是一套相当高深的理论，但考虑到本书仅为简论（TM），对这一思想的定义已经*足够明确*了。

En: Hold on, can you put that in slightly *more* basic terms?
Zh: 等等，你能用稍微*更*基础一点的语言解释这个概念吗？

En: Whoa.
Zh: 哇哦。

En: Huh. Okay...
Zh: 哈。好吧……

En: Wait, so this isn't even *Nilsen's* theory -- it's his followers'?
Zh: 稍等，原来这甚至都不是*尼尔森的*理论——而是他的追随者的？

En: This... doesn't make any sense.
Zh: 这……根本没道理嘛。

En: I'm completely lost.
Zh: 我根本看不懂了。

En: So these people are saying that if enough people just *believe* in communism, it will... come true?
Zh: 也就是说，这些人的主张是，只要有足够多的人，哪怕仅仅是*相信*康米主义，它就会……实现？

En: That's because this is absolute idiocy. Not even worth engaging with.
Zh: 因为这根本就是一派胡言。甚至都不值一驳。

En: No-no-no. There's *something* here, you can feel it!
Zh: 不——不是这样。这一定*言之有理*，你能感觉到的！

En: There's probably something wrong with this theory, but you're the kind of person who believes anything they read in a book, so why don't you just roll with it?
Zh: 这套理论很可能有问题，但你可是无论在书上读到什么都会信以为真的人。你不妨就信了吧？

En: Alright, let's hear about this extra-physical stuff!
Zh: 好耶，咱们来深入了解一下这个超物理是怎么回事！

En: I'll just give it a few more pages...
Zh: 那我就再翻几页……

En: Infra-materialists divide the extra-physical effects of the revolutionary state by the level of plasm required to achieve them.
Zh: 关于革命国家能通过原质实现的超物理效果，亚唯物主义者将其分为几个等级。

En: At the lowest, or *first level*, revolutionary plasm is believed to stimulate or invigorate matter without altering its essential properties.
Zh: 革命原质最低级的效果，或*第一级*效果是刺激或活化物质，但不会改变物质的本质特性。

En: This sounds like that business with the turnips...
Zh: 听起来好像和那些萝卜有关……

En: How exactly does the plasm 'stimulate' matter?
Zh: 原质怎么才能*刺激*物质？

En: To take one example: During the Revolutionary period, many communard farmers in Graad reported extraordinary yields of certain root vegetables, most notably turnips.
Zh: 举个例子：在革命年代，格拉德有很多公社农民都曾报告过某些根茎类蔬菜产量惊人，其中最为突出的就是萝卜。

En: The difference in these yields was simply too great to be accounted for by mere climate or soil conditions. The only reasonable explanation, infra-materialists argue, is the high level of revolutionary plasm emanating from the commune itself.
Zh: 这些作物的产量差距是如此之大，甚至都无法通过气候或土壤条件来解释。亚唯物主义者们声称：唯一合理的解释就是公社放射出了高强度的原质。

En: Even today, in parts of the SRV there are collective farms whose root vegetable yields exceed those thought possible by capitalist agronomists.
Zh: 时至今日，SRV某些地区的集体农场取得的根茎类蔬菜产量仍然远超资本主义农学家认定的极限。

En: Who's to say the farmers aren't just cooking the books? Sounds like obvious SRV propaganda.
Zh: 谁知道那些农民是不是在做假账？这明摆着就是SRV的政治宣传。

En: It's also been postulated that plasm may account for the remarkably full and manly facial hair observed on *some* communist males.
Zh: 此外，*某些*男性康米主义者长着举世瞩目的胡须，浓密且充满男性气概。这可能也是原质的功效。

En: It's also been postulated that plasm may account for the remarkably full and manly facial hair observed on many communist males.
Zh: 此外，很多男性康米主义者长着举世瞩目的胡须，浓密且充满男性气概。这可能也是原质的功效。

En: Your hand feels clammy against your bare chin. You wipe it along the back of the book and keep reading...
Zh: 你摸了摸光秃秃的下巴，感觉掌心一阵湿冷。你在书背上蹭了蹭手，继续埋头苦读……

En: Of course, infra-materialists argue that revolutionary plasm may stimulate human physiology in other ways as well.
Zh: 当然，亚唯物主义者声称革命原质还有可能以其他方式激发人体生理机能。

En: Stroke your bare chin.
Zh: 摸一把自己光秃秃的下巴。

En: Don't, it's embarrassing.
Zh: 得了吧，太尴尬了。

En: Stroke your beard proudly.
Zh: 骄傲地摸一把自己的胡子。

En: Stroke it, but modestly.
Zh: 摸摸自己的胡子，但注意保持谦虚。

En: Oh yeah, we're talking about white-hot *communist sex*, baby.
Zh: 哦耶，咱们正在讨论的可是白热化的*康米主义者性交*，宝贝儿。

En: That's correct! Reports from the Revolutionary period claim that the most radically devoted communards were able to engage in vigorous intercourse for up to *eight hours* at a time.
Zh: 正是如此！革命年代曾有报道宣称，最为激进，最具献身精神的公社成员能够精力充沛地一次连干*八个小时*。

En: In fact, reports from the Revolutionary period claim that the most radically devoted communards were able to engage in vigorous sexual intercourse for up to *eight hours* at a time.
Zh: 事实上，革命年代曾有报道宣称，最为激进，最具献身精神的公社成员能够精力充沛地一次连干*八个小时*。

En: Eight hours, huh? Seems doable. Your machinery could go the distance if called upon...
Zh: 才八小时，嗯？不算难嘛。如果有必要，你的钢铁之躯也能坚持那么久……

En: Stop, stop! Banish the thought. This isn't what you came for.
Zh: 打住，打住！别再琢磨这种事了。你可不是冲着这种事读书的。

En: Eight hours? There's no way. Your equipment would be mashed to jelly.
Zh: 八个小时？不可能。你的精密仪器会被捣成浆糊的。

En: No wonder the communards couldn't shoot straight -- they were too shagged out!
Zh: 难怪公社社员总是打不准——他们都精疲力竭了！

En: Hyper-productive vegetables and ultra-horny communards are fine, but this theory hasn't quite gotten strange enough for you.
Zh: 超高产量的蔬菜和无比饥渴的公社成员听起来还不错，可这套理论对你而言还不够奇特。

En: Sexual euphoria... increased sensitivity to stimulation... if you didn't know better, you'd say this plasm stuff sounds a lot like being on amphetamines.
Zh: 肉体欢愉……提升敏感度……要不是对此了然于胸，你会觉得这原质的效果很像是吸了安非他明。

En: And on that note, you feel like you've gotten the general idea of Infra-Materialism. Enough to carry on a basic conversation, at least. But if you'd like to go even deeper into some of the more speculative aspects of the theory, you could always read further...
Zh: 关于这一点，你感觉自己已经掌握了亚唯物主义的笼统概念。这种程度至少足以应对基本的对话。但如果你还想深入了解这套理论更为思辨的某些层面，那还要继续埋头苦读……

En: Of course you want to go deeper. What else are you here for?
Zh: 你当然想深入了解了。不然的话你为什么会捧着这本书？

En: Precisely, though plasm does more than increase vegetable yields. It may also influence the *physiology* of revolutionaries themselves.
Zh: 确切地说，虽然原质对于蔬菜产量的促进作用更为明显，但它对于革命者自身的*生理机能*也有着不可忽视的影响。

En: You flip forward a few pages until you come upon a chapter titled 'Mental Projection and Transference'...
Zh: 你跳过了几页，一直翻到了名为“精神投射和移情”的新篇章……

En: When a community has achieved a sufficiently high degree of revolutionary fervour, infra-materialists believe that second-level effects may be observed.
Zh: 亚唯物主义者认为，当一个社群的革命热情高涨到某种程度之后，就能从中观测到原质的第二级效果。

En: At this second level, certain hyper-revolutionary individuals may even develop the ability to extend their thoughts into material space and vice versa.
Zh: 在第二级效果的作用下，某些超革命斗士甚至可能会培养出将思想延伸至物质空间的能力，反之亦然。

En: Hey, Kim. You'd say that we share a pretty special connection, right?
Zh: “嘿，金。对你而言，咱们的关系还挺特殊的吧，是吧？”

En: Wait, does that mean communists can *read minds*?
Zh: 等等，意思是说康米主义者能够*读心*？

En: So why do these effects only work for hyper-revolutionary communists? (Continue.)
Zh: 为什么只有超革命斗士能享受到这种效果？（继续。）

En: If by 'special' you mean 'marred by extreme dysfunction,' then yes, I suppose it is pretty special.
Zh: “如果你说的‘特殊’指的是‘因极端的功能失调而受损’，我可以认同你的说法，我咱们的关系确实相当特殊。”

En: I would say we share a perfectly average connection for two law officials working a case.
Zh: “我认为，世间任意两位执法人员在合作办案时都会有你我这般的关系。”

En: I suppose we do work quite well together.
Zh: “我觉得咱们确实合作默契。”

En: That's exactly what I mean. It's probably why we're always finishing each other's...
Zh: “我就是这个意思。很可能是因为咱们总在为对方……”

En: It's way more than average. Haven't you noticed that we're always finishing each other's...
Zh: “咱们的关系比那个强多了。你还没注意到吗，咱们总在为对方……”

En: You and me, we're on the same wavelength. It's why we're always finishing each other's...
Zh: “你与我，我们的波长一致。所以咱们总在为对方……”

En: Your words hang in the air as the lieutenant scribbles something in his notebook. At some point he realizes you're waiting for him and looks up with a tightly knit brow.
Zh: 警督在笔记本上信手记下什么，没有理会你说到一半的句子。过了一会儿，他意识到你在等他接话，于是抬起头，微皱着眉看向你。

En: ... sentences.
... sandwiches.
Zh: “……吃三明治。”

En: ... cigarettes.
Zh: “……吸烟屁股。”

En: It's true that you and I do share a similar addiction, but that's hardly remarkable among officers of the RCM.
Zh: “你我确实有相似的嗜好，但在RCM的诸多警官中，这算不上什么值得注意的事。”

En: That happened exactly once, and only under extreme duress.
Zh: “那只发生过一次，而且实在是迫不得已。”

En: We have never shared a sandwich, detective. Despite your heroic efforts, I might add.
Zh: “我要补充一下，虽然有你的英雄壮举在前，但咱们从未享用过同一个三明治，警探。”

En: The lieutenant looks at you evenly for a moment, then returns to his notebook without a word.
Zh: 警督淡定地看了你一会儿，接着就一言不发地继续埋头看他的笔记本了。

En: Did I? My bad, detective. Won't happen again. He doesn't look up from his notebook.
Zh: “我还做过这种事吗？是我不好，警探。不会再有这种事了。”警督仍然盯着笔记本，连头都没抬。

En: My apologies, I thought leaving your partner hanging was simply the style at the 41st.
Zh: “抱歉。我还以为不搭理自己的搭档是41分局的办事风格呢。”

En: The lieutenant remains engrossed in his notebook.
Zh: 警督仍在埋头盯着他的笔记本。

En: As we were saying, it's generally believed that these effects are only exhibited by certain *hyper-revolutionary* individuals, generally less than .01% of the revolutionary population...
Zh: 正如之前所说，通常认为这些效果只会出现在某些*超革命*斗士身上，一般来说只占全部革命者的0.01%……

En: Kim, you left me hanging there.
Zh: “金，你别不搭理我啊。”

En: Forget it.
Zh: 算了吧。

En: According to infra-materialist theory, yes! Under suitably revolutionary conditions, that is...
Zh: 根据亚唯物主义理论，正是如此！前提是在合适的革命环境之中……

En: Of course, one doesn't need to be a communist to be attuned to the thoughts of your brothers and sisters.
Zh: 当然，无需成为康米主义者，人们也能理解兄弟姐妹们的想法。

En: It's become something of a folk legend that during their final meeting, Nilsen and Mazov didn't speak a single word, preferring to sit in silence with their chamomile tea, reading one another's thoughts.
Zh: 尼尔森与马佐夫的最后一次会面已经成为了民间传说：两人相向而坐，一言不发，在沉默中品尝着甘菊茶，通过读心交流。

En: Because plasm has never been directly observed, the exact mechanism behind these effects remains entirely speculative.
Zh: 因为还没有人直接观测到原质，这些功效的确切机制姑且只是推测。

En: In other words, there's no evidence that any of this mind-reading stuff is real.
Zh: 换句话说，没有证据表明真存在读心这种事。

En: Why are you getting hung up on mechanisms... Open your mind's aperture just a little wider! You're so close to true understanding!
Zh: 纠结于机制又是何苦……把心灵之窗再稍微开大一点！你马上就能了然于胸了！

En: Pssst! You know what *else* lets you feel like you can read minds? Spooky ol' pyrholidon...
Zh: 嘿！你猜*还有*什么能让你产生能读心的感觉？阴森恐怖的吡嚯浣酮……

En: Wait, if there's no evidence plasm exists, how can the theory be true?
Zh: 等等，如果无法证明原质的存在，这套理论是怎么成立的？

En: Let it go.
Zh: 别想了。

En: Most infra-materialists would argue that the inability of skeptics to detect plasm is simply evidence of their own insufficient revolutionary enthusiasm.
Zh: 绝大多数亚唯物主义者会这样反驳：质疑者之所以无法感知到原质，只是因为他们缺乏革命热情。

En: There you have it. A perfectly unfalsifiable loop. The theory is impossible to disprove on its own grounds...
Zh: 果然是这样。不可证伪的循环论证。这套理论以自身为基础，根本无懈可击……

En: Meaning it's hardly a theory at all.
Zh: 意味着这根本就算不上是一套理论。

En: This should be more than enough for a stimulating discussion. That said, if you're still yearning for *more*....
Zh: 我们已经完成了内容详实的兴趣讨论。虽说如此，如果你还想*继续深入*的话……

En: You breeze through the next several sections until you arrive at the final chapter, titled 'A Communism Above Reality'...
Zh: 你草草翻过之后数个版块，一直翻到了最后一章，标题是“超越现实的康米主义”……

En: When a society's revolutionary fervour reaches the third and highest level, infra-materialist theoreticians have postulated that the laws of physics cease to be laws.
Zh: 根据亚唯物主义理论家们的推测，当社会的革命热情到达最高的第三级时，物理法则便不再是法了。

En: SAY IT. You know you want to.
Zh: 说吧。你已经迫不及待了。

En: That's because *I'm* the law. The law-jaw.
Zh: 那是因为*我*才是法。连我的下巴都是法。

En: Cease to be laws... What do they become then?
Zh: “不再是法了……”那是变成什么了？

En: Under suitably revolutionary conditions, you might be! Though infra-materialists would argue that it's more like the community is *collectively* the law.
Zh: 在合适的革命环境中，你真有可能是这块料！不过亚唯物主义者会声称那种情况更像是整个社群*集体*成为法。

En: More like suggestions, according to some of the SRV's leading infra-materialists.
Zh: 据SRV的一流亚唯物主义学家所说，会变成类似于建议的东西。

En: At last, a book gives you the respect and validation you're entitled to.
Zh: 这本书总算是给了你应得的尊重与肯定。

En: Of course, it's impossible to say what exactly happens under these conditions. No known society has ever achieved the levels of revolutionary enthusiasm the theory seems to require.
Zh: 当然，没人能断定在这种环境中究竟会发生什么。有史以来，人类社会的革命热情尚未达到过这套理论所需的高度。

En: Some infra-materialists have even argued that it might require more plasm than *humanity alone* may be capable of producing.
Zh: 甚至还曾有亚唯物主义者宣称，达到第三级所需的原质远超*仅凭人类*所能产生的总和。

En: Wait, are there non-human sources of plasm?
Zh: 等等，难道非人类也能产生原质？

En: Has anyone ever gotten close to reaching the third level?
Zh: 有人曾经接近过第三级吗？

En: What would a third-level society even look like? (Conclude.)
Zh: 第三级功效下的社会会变成什么样？（总结。）

En: In the SRV, there have been attempts to organise certain species of aquatic mammals, as well as a few of the higher corvids...
Zh: SRV曾试图组织过某些水生哺乳动物和高智商的鸦科鸟类……

En: But as of this writing only human beings have demonstrated the intellectual capacity for revolutionary communism.
Zh: 但直到本书完稿之时，只有人类展示出了足以投身康米主义革命的智力水平。

En: It concerns an interesting series of events that took place during the invasion of Revachol. As Coalition forces made landfall, a cadre of Nilsen's most fervent acolytes *attempted to compress a Coalition aerostatic* with their collective will...
Zh: 在瑞瓦肖遭受侵略期间，曾发生过一系列与此相关的事件。在联盟国军队着陆之时，尼尔森麾下最热忱的干部曾试图通过集体的意念*压制一艘联盟国静风舰*……

En: According to communard lore, these acolytes positioned themselves at the top of a redoubt just over the Bay of Revachol. From that vantage, they proceeded to visualise *pinching* Coalition Warship Debutante between their fingers, a gesture believed to assist in the extra-physical materialisation of their thoughts.
Zh: 根据公社成员的口口相传，这些干部站到位于瑞瓦肖海湾的防御工事顶端，借助天时地利，摆出将联盟国战舰名媛号*捏*在指尖的姿势。他们认为这种姿势有助于显现出思想的超物理功效。

En: Why didn't they just get some high-powered merc gear? (Tap your armour.)
Zh: 他们怎么不搞点战斗力更强的雇佣兵装备呢？（拍拍你的装甲。）

En: Should've used their finger pistols instead. (Aim your finger pistols toward the bay.)
Zh: 他们应该用上手指枪的。（用你的手指枪瞄向海湾。）

En: Did it work?
Zh: 生效了吗？

En: The communards were notoriously under-equipped against the much better-funded Coalition. In any event, it's doubtful even ceramic armour would have altered the outcome.
Zh: 与联盟国的坚船利炮相比，公社成员武装的简陋程度堪称惨绝人寰。无论如何，就算是陶瓷装甲恐怕也难以为他们挽回败局。

En: There's no evidence the communards were equipped with finger pistols, though it's unlikely they would have decisively altered the outcome.
Zh: 没有证据表明公社成员配备了手指枪，就算有了手指枪，也未必能为他们挽回败局。

En: The acolytes (along with the redoubt) were vapourised in an artillery strike before the process could be completed...
Zh: 这些干部（连带着整座防御工事）在大功告成之前，就被一阵炮火炸成飞灰了……

En: It's been said, though, that in the weeks following the battle, the captain of the Debutante noted an increase in incidents of crewmen striking their heads on unexpectedly low bulkheads.
Zh: 不过，据说在那场大战之后的数周之间，名媛号舰长发现船员在低得离谱的舱壁上撞到头的次数有了明显增长。

En: It's a matter of fierce debate what form society would assume under the highest levels of revolutionary plasm, also known as 'Full Communism' or, as Nilsen termed it, 'Communism Above Reality'.
Zh: 在最高级的革命原质作用下，社会将变成何种形态。这个话题总会引起激烈的争论。这种形态被称为“完整的康米主义”，尼尔森的说法是“超越现实的康米主义”。

En: In some of his later writings, Nilsen himself speculated about the potential for an *extra-physical architecture* that disregards the laws of 'bourgeois physics' and instead relies on the revolutionary faith of the people for structural integrity.
Zh: 在晚年留下的作品中，尼尔森曾构思过一种*超物理建筑学*：无视*资产阶级的物理法则*，通过人民的革命信念维持建筑结构的完整性。

En: Meaning the buildings stay up because people *believe* they'll stay up?
Zh: 他的意思是，只要人们*相信*这种建筑屹立不倒，它们就真不会倒？

En: That... seems like a bad theory to design buildings based on.
Zh: 那……可算不上是靠谱的建筑学。

En: Precisely. Nilsen observed that the financial system operates on the same principle of faith, so why not an architectural system?
Zh: 确切地说，尼尔森是洞察到金融系统正是基于同样的信念原则才得以运转，那么建筑系统又何尝不可呢？

En: Nilsen called it "the highest expression of Communist principles, a society whose literal foundation is the faith of its people".
Zh: 尼尔森称之为“康米主义原理的至高表现形式，一个完全以人民的信念为基础的社会。”

En: On the following page you come across a few black-and-white reproductions from Nilsen's own notebooks. One sketch depicts a government ministry shaped like a great inverted pyramid, a hectare in width at the top, balanced atop foundations the size of common apartments...
Zh: 下一页上有几张从尼尔森的笔记上翻印下来的黑白手稿。其中有张草图勾绘着一座政府大楼，其造型就像是倒立的三棱锥，顶端面积足有一公顷，而地基仅有一间公寓大小……

En: Another depicts a leaning tower wrapped in a dramatic helix. The caption beneath it reads: 'The Tower of History'.
Zh: 另一张草图勾绘了一座笼罩在壮观的螺旋结构中的斜塔。下方的文字说明是：“历史之塔”。

En: Something about that tower looks awfully familiar. Could it be *that's* what the students were trying to recreate with the matchboxes?
Zh: 这座塔的造型令你感到特别熟悉。那两个学生试图用火柴盒重建的会不会就是*这个东西*？

En: Wait, but what if the people stop believing and the buildings collapse?
Zh: 等等，要是人民不信了呢？这建筑就完蛋了？

En: This might be too much, even for me.
Zh: 就算是我，也接受不了这么多。

En: Plausible or not, there's something beautiful about this idea. Is there any more?
Zh: 无论是否合理，这种思路都具备美的一面。还有吗？

En: A state that has lost the faith of its people has forfeited the right to exist.
Zh: “导致人民失去信心的国度没有存在的资格。”

En: That's alright, because you've reached the end of the book.
Zh: 无所谓。反正你已经读完最后一页了。

En: There is no more. You've reached the outer theoretical limits of communism, and in less than 200 pages.
Zh: 没了。只翻了不到200页，你就已经抵达了康米主义外延的理论边界。

En: If you'd like to read further, may we recommend 'A Brief Look at Occidental Architecture'?
Zh: 如果你想拓展阅读，不妨尝试下我们倾情推荐的“奥西登建筑学简论”吧？

En: If you'd like to try something different, may we recommend 'A Brief Look at Graadian Literature'?
Zh: 如果你想换个口味，不妨尝试下我们倾情推荐的“格拉德文学简论”吧？

En: But one subject he returned to time and time again was the fundamental relationship between thoughts and matter...
Zh: 但他总会反复提及一个主题：思想与物质的基本关系……

En: A true man of ideas, equal to any of the great Dolorian polymaths.
Zh: 真是一位高瞻远瞩的思想家，与德洛莉丝时代的大博学家们相比也毫不逊色。

En: That's... strangely beautiful.
Zh: 这……有种奇异的美感。

En: That's... probably not what happened.
Zh: 事实……很可能并非如此。

En: One of the minor tragedies of the late Revolutionary period is that few reliable accounts survive.
Zh: 在革命时代末期留下的诸多遗憾中，这只能算是其中无关紧要的一种：那个年代没留下多少可靠的记录。

En: Much of what we know of the communards' activities during this period come from memoirs and second-hand accounts, some only written down decades after the fact and of dubious authenticity.
Zh: 关于公社成员在这个时代的所作所为，我们所知的消息大多来自回忆录和对他人陈述的记录，还有些书面记载是在过了几十年之后才落笔，真实性相当可疑。

En: You must! Everyone knows books save their best parts for the end...
Zh: 务必如此！众所周知，每本书都会把压轴好戏藏到最后……

En: There are numerous stories from Samara involving bandits or fascist mercenaries being levitated by farmers from the most ideologically advanced communes. Of course, few of these incidents have ever been rigorously investigated or substantiated...
Zh: 来自思想最进步的公社的农民，凭借意念把强盗和法西斯的鹰犬送上天。萨马拉流传着无数关于这一主题的故事。当然了，这些事迹几乎从未得到严格调查或证实。

En: Among known attempts to channel third-level capabilities, the most well-documented is the curious case of Coalition Warship Debutante.
Zh: 在试图引发第三级潜力的案例中，最为广为人知的是与联盟国战舰名媛号相关的奇闻。

En: I've got to know what that's about.
Zh: 我想知道这是怎么回事。

En: I've heard enough.
Zh: 我听够了。

En: Of course, colourful anecdotes only scratch the surface of what infra-materialists believe may be possible in a truly *third-level* society...
Zh: 当然，这些精彩的逸闻轶事，对于亚唯物主义者坚信会在*第三级*社会中实现的宏伟愿景而言，不过是冰山一角而已……

En: In the corner of one of the reproduced notebook pages, you can make out the following words written in Nilsen's distinctive slashing script...
Zh: 你盯着翻印下来的一页笔记，从角落里认出了尼尔森独特的锐利字迹……

En: The form of these stories also recalls several well-known Samaran folktales, in particular the one commonly known as 'Clever Oleg and the Flying Magistrate'.
Zh: 这些故事的结构类似于家喻户晓的萨马拉民间传说，尤其是其中一篇俗称“聪明的奥列格与飞翔的地方长官”的故事。

En: Picture the lot of them, all standing with their fingers in the air one minute, the next reduced to a bloody mist!
Zh: 想象下那群人的样子吧：前一分钟还集体屹立于防御工事之上，齐刷刷地指向云端，下一分钟就被炸成了一团血雾！

En: Some have theorised that such a society would be fundamentally unrecognisable, lacking many of the institutions we typically take for granted in advanced societies, including organised governments, financial institutions, and law enforcement.
Zh: 按照一部分人的理论，这样的社会根本就是无法认知的。人们认为理所当然存在于发达社会中的机构——例如政府系统，金融机构和执法部门，不会存在于这个社会之中。

En: D-d-did that book just say there's no place for you in this future?
Zh: 这，这——这本书的意思难道是你在未来会无处容身？

En: Others have argued that people living under third-level conditions will be immune to such infirmities as hunger, disease, and mental illness...
Zh: 还有人声称在原质的三级功效之下，人民会免于饥饿，肉体与精神的疾病之苦……

En: This sounds like a seditious fantasy! How can they allow books like this in print?
Zh: 这简直就是煽动造反的白日梦呓！他们怎能批准这样的书籍出版？

En: Klaasje's hidden documents -- from the empty buoy.
Zh: 卡拉洁藏起来的证件——在一个空浮标里。

En: Klaasje's hidden documents. That she said would be in a buoy on the coast.
Zh: 卡拉洁藏起来的证件。她说放在海岸边的一个空浮标里。

En: Fuck if I know... maybe it's collecting shit? Like an octopus can get, like, curious?
Zh: “我他妈怎么知道……也是为了收藏？比如说章鱼之类的会感到，好奇？”

En: The woman on the photo does not reply. She looks at you with mysterious eyes, disguised as someone else.
Zh: 相片上的女人没有回应。她用那双神秘的眼睛看着你，伪装成了其他人的样子。

En: A curious octopus would do something similar. Or even a cuttlefish.
Zh: 充满好奇的章鱼就有类似的习性。就连乌贼也会这样。

En: A curious octopus sometimes collects things? Or a cuttlefish?
Zh: 一只偶尔喜欢收集东西的好奇章鱼？或者是一只乌贼？

En: Okay. So what? Loop Cuno in on this.
Zh: “好吧。那又怎么样？跟坤诺同步一下消息。”

En: Katarzine Alasije was supposed to be her *real* name. Where *Klaasje* comes from, remember? God dammit!
Zh: 她的*真名实姓*应该是卡特琳·艾拉洁。*卡拉洁*这个名字是这么来的，记住了？真见鬼！

En: It's not the name she said would be on here. I got lied to.
Zh: “这上面的名字跟她说的不一样。我被骗了。”

En: I told you she kept lying to you. She's probably lying to someone else right now. In another city. She moves fast.
Zh: 我告诉过你，她一直在对你撒谎，现在很可能又在跟别人撒谎呢。在另一座城市。她的动作很快。

En: I told you she kept on lying to you. She's probably lying to someone else right now -- another detective.
Zh: 我告诉过你，她一直在对你撒谎，现在很可能又在跟别人撒谎呢——跟另一个警探。

En: She told me it was her real name -- and it isn't this. It isn't even the name she said would be on here...
Zh: “她告诉我说这是她的真名——但并不是。这上面的名字甚至跟她说的都不一样……”

En: Never mind.
Zh: “没关系。”

En: Cool. Got lied to. Double-lied to. I get it. You cops need the Cuno on stuff like this. No one lies to the Cuno. He nods, with a stern and serious expression.
Zh: “酷。被骗了。两次被骗。我明白了。这种事你们警察要找坤诺。没人能跟坤诺说谎。”他点点头，表情严肃又认真。

En: Yeah -- okay. *Don't* loop Cuno in. Leave the Cuno in the dark. Fine. He pretends he doesn't care.
Zh: “哼——好啊。*别*告诉坤诺。让坤诺蒙在鼓里吧。随便。”他假装漠不关心。

En: She said the false passport would be for *Annouk Meijer-Smit*...
Zh: 她说假护照上的名字应该是*安诺·梅耶尔-斯密特*……

En: And not *Annouk Meijer-Smit*. Like she told you. Annouk -- Meijer -- Smit. Remember? You got lied to. Again.
Zh: 也不是*安诺·梅耶尔-斯密特*。她是这么跟你说的。安诺——梅耶尔——斯密特。还记得吗？你被骗了。又一次。
