En: I told you she kept on lying to you. She's probably lying to someone else right now -- another detective.
Zh: 我告诉过你，她一直在对你撒谎，现在很可能又在跟别人撒谎呢——跟另一个警探。

En: Even worse -- *Katarzine Alasije* was supposed to be her *real* name. Where *Klaasje* comes from, remember? God dammit!
Zh: 更糟糕的是——她的*真名实姓*应该是*卡特琳·艾拉洁*。*卡拉洁*这个名字是这么来的，记住了？真见鬼！

En: I told you she kept lying to you. She's probably lying to someone else right now. In another city. She moves fast.
Zh: 我告诉过你，她一直在对你撒谎，现在很可能又在跟别人撒谎呢。在另一座城市。她的动作很快。

En: This Katarzine Alasije -- it's safe to say it's not her either. In short: you got lied to, sire. Maybe it's best we let her go -- at least we don't have to question her again.
Zh: 这位卡特琳·艾拉洁——可以肯定也不是她。简而言之：您被骗了，大人。也许我们放她走最好——至少我们不用再审问她了。

En: This Katarzine Alasije -- it's safe to say it's not her either. In short: you got lied to, sire. Good luck to whoever is questioning her in the 57th right now.
Zh: 这位卡特琳·艾拉洁——可以肯定也不是她。简而言之：您被骗了，大人。不管57分局现在是谁在审问她，祝君好运。

En: Cuno's gonna say it's *not* any of the things you *think* it is. Cuno'd have to get deep into this shit, but whatever it is -- you got played here. That's for sure.
Zh: “坤诺会说这跟你*想到*的任何东西都*不一样*。坤诺要深入调查这点破事，不过不管是什么——你被耍了。这点很肯定。”

En: The winds are silent. The streets are empty of her. She is no longer in the city of Revachol.
Zh: 风沉默无语。街道上不见她的身影。她已经不在瑞瓦肖城了。

En: The girl on the passport looks at you with no answer. She is not Katarzine Alasije. Or Annouk Meijer-Smit. *Or* Klaasje. That much is sure.
Zh: 护照上的女孩看着你，没有回应。她不是卡特琳·艾拉洁。或者安诺·梅耶尔-斯密特。*或者*卡拉洁。这些是可以肯定的。

En: Precinct 57 needs to double the security around her.
Zh: 57分局需要加倍她周围的安全保护。

En: An old photo. Before life came and did what it does.
Zh: 一张老照片。拍摄于惨遭生活摧残之前。

En: Like a magpie? He looks around. "What a coincidence. Then it would also have *collected* the other objects, which would be highly unusual..."
Zh: “像喜鹊那样吗？”他看了看周围。“真是个巧合。那它可能还会*收集*其他物品，这一点是很不寻常的……”

En: By now, the lieutenant has accepted your unusual methods.
Zh: 事到如今，警督已经接受了你不寻常的办案方式。

En: What was this doing in the phasmid's nest?
Zh: “这玩意在竹节虫窝里干什么？”

En: Maybe our man, Mr. Dros, took it from Klaasje's -- or whatever her name was -- hiding place? Or...
Zh: “也许是我们的德罗斯先生从卡拉洁的——或者不管她叫什么名字——藏身之处拿走的？或者……”

En: It says -- Katarzine Alasije.
Zh: “上面写着——卡特琳·艾拉洁。”

En: He opens his notes. "She said it would be for *Annouk Meijer-Smit*. Annouk -- Meijer -- Smit."
Zh: 他翻开笔记本。“她说是*安诺·梅耶尔-斯密特*。安诺——梅耶尔——斯密特。”

En: Perhaps... But that still doesn't explain how it got into the nest.
Zh: “有可能……但还是解释不了这是怎么跑到巢穴里的。”

En: Perhaps -- for some blackmailing plan?
Zh: “也许——是为了什么勒索计划？”

En: I think the *phasmid* took it.
Zh: “我觉得是*竹节虫*拿走的。”

En: The phasmid took it -- and I *sensed* it do so. I saw something open up the buoy with spindly legs. (Point to your head.)
Zh: “是竹节虫拿走的——我*感觉*到它这么做了。我看见某种东西用细长的腿打开了那个浮标。”（指指你的头。）

En: Like a magpie? He looks around. "What a coincidence. Then it would also have *collected* the other objects, which would be highly unusual..."
Zh: “像喜鹊那样吗？”他看了看周围。“真是个巧合。那它可能还会*收集*其他物品，这一点是很不寻常的……”

En: I can see how the helmet could wash up on the island. And the scope -- maybe Mr. Dros lost it? But to seek this out would be *very* unusual behaviour for an arthropod.
Zh: “我可以理解头盔有可能被水流冲上这座岛。也可以理解为什么瞄准镜会出现在这里——可能是德罗斯先生弄丢了？但搜寻这种东西对节肢动物而言可是*非常*罕见的习性。”

En: Would it? Maybe it was simply curious.
Zh: 会吗？也许只是简单地好奇。

En: A curious octopus would do something similar. Or even a cuttlefish.
Zh: 充满好奇的章鱼就有类似的习性。就连乌贼也会这样。

En: Perhaps it was curious? Like an octopus?
Zh: “也许它很好奇？像是一只章鱼？”

En: Perhaps it had a vendetta against our Miss Oranje?
Zh: “也许它跟我们的奥兰治小姐之间有深仇大恨吧？”

En: An octopus belongs to a very different Class. It's not even an insect, it's a mollusc. But, yes -- I see your point.
Zh: “章鱼属于完全不同的种类。它甚至都不是昆虫，而是软体动物。不过，没错——我明白你的意思了。”

En: Phasmids are not capable of holding grudges. Only humans and some tracking predators do that.
Zh: “竹节虫是不会跟人结仇的。只有人类和某些跟踪的捕食者才会这么做。”

En: Look at the photo.
Zh: 看着相片。

En: It's Klaasje. With short, black hair and glasses. She looks boyish, younger somehow.
Zh: 是卡拉洁。黑色短发，戴着眼镜。看起来有些男孩子气，好像也更年轻一些。

En: Katarzine Alasije was supposed to be her real name. She lied to us.
Zh: “卡特琳·艾拉洁应该是她的真名。她对我们撒谎了。”

En: Katarzine Alasije was supposed to be her *real* name. Where *Klaasje* comes from, remember? God dammit!
Zh: 她的*真名实姓*应该是卡特琳·艾拉洁。*卡拉洁*这个名字是这么来的，记住了？真见鬼！

En: I told you she kept lying to you. She's probably lying to someone else right now. In another city.
Zh: 我告诉过你，她一直在对你撒谎，现在很可能又在跟别人撒谎呢。在另一座城市。

En: Maybe this is her real passport? Not a fake? Because this is her real name.
Zh: “也许这是她的真护照？不是为了伪装？因为这是她的真名。”

En: Yes, somehow she managed to lie to us -- *one more time*. In a way she's still lying to us right now. The lieutenant smiles.
Zh: “是的，不知怎么的，她成功骗过了我们——*又一次*。从某种程度上来说，她现在还在欺骗我们。”警督笑了。

En: No. She lied to us. Her so-called real name was not her real name. Somehow she's managed to lie to us about that too... He almost smiles.
Zh: “不。她对我们撒谎了。她那个所谓的真名也不是她的真名。不知怎么的，她连那个也设法骗过了我们……”他几乎笑了起来。

En: I told you she kept on lying to you. She's probably lying to someone else right now -- another detective.
Zh: 我告诉过你，她一直在对你撒谎，现在很可能又在跟别人撒谎呢——跟另一个警探。

En: What's her *real* name then?
Zh: “那她的*真名*叫什么？”

En: I don't know. But it was not Katarzine Alasije. Or Annouk Meijer-Smit. Or Klaasje. Whoever she is -- when we get back I need to warn our holding cells. We need to double the security around her.
Zh: “我不知道。不过不是卡特琳·艾拉洁或者安诺·梅耶尔-斯密特。或者卡拉洁。不管她是谁——我们回去之后，我需要警告我们的拘留室。她周围的安全保护必须加倍。”

En: I don't know. But it's not Katarzine Alasije. Or Klaasje. Or Annouk Meijer-Smit. We didn't even scratch the surface with her, detective.
Zh: “我不知道。不过也不是卡特琳·艾拉洁或者卡拉洁。或者安诺·梅耶尔-斯密特。我们甚至连她的表面都没有触及到，警探。”

En: He looks east. "Perhaps it's better that we didn't arrest her. Who knows what hell she'd be raising in my district by now..."
Zh: 他看向东边。“也许没逮捕她更好。谁知道这个见鬼的女人现在会把我们辖区搞成什么样……”

En: The winds are silent. The streets are empty of her. She is no longer in the city of Revachol.
Zh: 风沉默无语。街道上不见她的身影。她已经不在瑞瓦肖城了。

En: I don't know who Katarzine Alasije is, but it's safe to say it's not her either. In short: we got lied to, again. Good luck to whoever is questioning her in the 57th right now.
Zh: “我不知道卡拉林·艾拉洁是谁，不过可以肯定的说也不是她。简而言之：我们又被骗了。不管是谁现在在57分局审问她，真要祝对方好运了。”

En: I don't know who Katarzine Alasije is, but it's safe to say it's not her either. In short: we got lied to, again. In a way she's *still* lying to us... The lieutenant can't help but smile.
Zh: “我不知道卡拉林·艾拉洁是谁，不过可以肯定的说也不是她。简而言之：我们又被骗了。在某种程度上来说，她*还在*欺骗我们……”警督忍不住笑了。

En: Klaasje's hidden documents... The lieutenant looks at it in your hands. "From the empty buoy."
Zh: “卡拉洁藏起来的证件……”警督看着你手中的东西。“空浮标里的。”

En: Klaasje's hidden documents... The lieutenant looks at it in your hands. "That she said would be in a buoy on the coast."
Zh: “卡拉洁藏起来的证件……”警督看着你手中的东西。“她说东西在海岸边的一个空浮标里。”

En: This passport, issued by the Sovereign Republic of Oranje -- is issued to a black haired woman called Katarzine Alasije.
Zh: 这本护照，是由奥兰治独立共和国签发的——发给了一个名叫卡特琳·艾拉洁的黑发女子。

En: [Put the passport away.]
Zh: [把护照放在一边。]

En: The face of King Frissel smiles at you. For some reason the smile now strikes you as more forced than it did before.
Zh: 弗里塞尔国王向你微笑。不知为何，你总感觉这笑容显得比之前更生硬了。

En: The front of this quarterly journal features a large, satirical portrait of the late King Frissel. From the sides of his head, a pair of white antlers spread to the corners of the cover.
Zh: 这本季刊的封面上大幅印着一张已故国王弗里塞尔的讽刺肖像画。他的头颅两侧长出了一对白色鹿角，一直顶到封面的边缘。

En: Why antlers?
Zh: 为什么要画鹿角？

En: Why Frissel?
Zh: 为什么要画弗里塞尔？

En: Flip through the pages, see what catches your eye.
Zh: 迅速翻阅一遍，看看有没有感兴趣的内容。

En: Because white antlers are one of the symbols of communism. They represent a society in accord with the natural world and at the same time above it.
Zh: 因为白色鹿角是康米主义的象征之一。它代表与自然和谐一致，却又高于自然世界的社会。

En: Because Frissel was incompetent, foolish, and cruel. In short, the embodiment of everything the communards wished to overthrow. It's *satire*, you see.
Zh: 因为弗里塞尔是个昏君，愚昧且残暴。简而言之，他就是公社成员渴望推翻的一切的化身。如你所见，这就是*讽刺*。

En: To your disappointment, there aren't any full-colour pictures to direct your attention, just column after column of closely set text, interrupted occasionally by little doodles in black and white...
Zh: 这本书令你感到失望，里面连一张能吸引眼球的全彩插图都没有，只有排版密密麻麻的文字，零散夹杂着黑白的涂鸦……

En: No advertisements, you notice. So who's funding this *radical commentary*?
Zh: 你还注意到整本书都没有广告。到底是谁在为这种*激进的评论*提供资金？

En: After riffling the pages with your thumb several times, you return to the table of contents...
Zh: 靠拇指来回翻阅数次之后，你又翻回了这本书的目录页……

En: The magazine is divided into several sections: 'International Developments', 'Kunst und Kultur', and 'Local Concerns'. Just inside the cover there's also an editor's note.
Zh: 这本杂志被分为数个版块：“国际动态”，“Kunst und Kultur”，以及“国内热点”。封面上还写了一段编者注。

En: You flip back to the front of the magazine. The table of contents unfolds before you.
Zh: 你翻回杂志卷首。目录呈现于眼前。

En: A shameful way to treat a former king, even one as underwhelming as Frissel.
Zh: 就算是针对弗里塞尔这种昏庸之辈，以这种方式对待一位已逝的国王也算不上体面。

En: I want to catch up on 'International Developments'.
Zh: 我想了解一下“国际动态”。

En: Read the editor's note.
Zh: 阅读编者注。

En: What is 'Kunst und Kultur'?
Zh: “Kunst und Kultur”是什么意思？

En: Let's see what they mean by 'Local Concerns'.
Zh: 让我见识一下什么算是“国内热点”。

En: [Put the magazine away.]
Zh: [把杂志放在一边。]

En: Comrade, as you know, this journal takes its name from Mazov's immortal expression 'Du cristal à la fumée.' This was his way of describing the way the rigid, crystalline structures of capitalist ideology turn to smoke under communism...
Zh: “这位同志，如你所知，这本期刊的命名源自马佐夫那句不朽的名言‘结晶化为轻烟’。他以这种方式描述僵化，如结晶一般的资本主义意识形态结构被康米主义化为一股轻烟……”

En: (Whisper.) "Kim! I think this is a *communist* magazine."
Zh: （压低声音。）“金！我认为这是一本*康米主义*杂志。”

En: But like the structures of capitalist ideology, we too are at risk of going 'à la fumée.' Unlike many publications which are content to spoonfeed their readers reassuring drivel, *La Fumée* is committed to telling the radical truth, even when that truth may drive away potential subscribers.
Zh: “但我们的意识形态结构同样存在着‘化为轻烟’的风险。*与市面上大量给读者灌迷魂汤的出版物不同，*轻烟*杂志致力于传播激进的真相，就算这种真相会赶走潜在的订阅者也在所不惜。”

En: So please, if you value our radical Mazovian perspective on contemporary politics, culture, and international affairs, please consider subscribing today. \n\nYours in struggle, The Editors.
Zh: “如果你赏识我们对当代政治，文化及国际形势所持的激进马佐夫主义视角，敬请考虑订阅本刊。\n\n斗争不息的，编辑们。”

En: Play it cool. Pretend everything's in order.
Zh: 表现得酷一点。假装一切如常。

En: What do you expect? It was laying around the office of the Débardeurs' Union. They're probably *bankrolling* the thing.
Zh: “你还指望什么？码头工人工会里到处都摆着这本杂志。他们很可能就是这东西的*幕后金主*。”

En: Only four issues in and it sounds like they've already alienated their readership.
Zh: 虽然才出了四期，看来他们已经相当疏远自己的读者了。

En: This section includes a long, tedious critique of the latest round of free-trade negotiations between the EPIS nations and the Free State of Semenine.
Zh: 这个版块刊登了一篇冗长，乏味的评论文章，针对的是EPIS诸国与塞美尼自由州进行的最新一轮自由贸易谈判。

En: You also skip over an article about heavy fuel oil smuggling along the Mesque-Messina border, something about bear wrestling in Samara, book riots in Yugo-Graad...
Zh: 你还顺便跳过了一篇关于在梅斯克与梅西纳的边境走私重油的文章，外加一篇关于萨马拉狗熊摔角的文章，还有一篇关于南格拉德暴动的文章……

En: Face it, you really aren't interested in foreign affairs. You're not even sure where most of these countries are...
Zh: 得了吧。你对外国的时事根本不感兴趣。你连其中绝大多数国家在什么地方都不知道……

En: Apparently the material conditions of other countries hold no interest for you. Your brand of communism is strictly local. So be it...
Zh: 显然，你对其他国家的物质条件没什么兴趣。你信奉的康米主义狭隘到仅限本地。那就到此为止吧……

En: It seems to be part of a new public fitness initiative in the People's Republic of Samara (SRV), devised and promoted by none other than President Knezhinisky himself.
Zh: 这似乎是萨马拉人民共和国（SRV）最新全民健身倡议的组成部分，其设计和推广者不是别人，正是该国总统涅仁斯基。

En: According to this extremely credulous account, 'Uncle Sport' (as he's affectionately known to his people) has recently decreed bear wrestling the national sport of the SRV.
Zh: 据这篇轻信的报道所言，“体育大叔”（该国人民如此亲切地称呼这位领导人）规定狗熊摔角成为SRV的国家运动。

En: Whoa-whoa-whoa. *Bear wrestling*? Flip back to that!
Zh: 哇——哦——嚯。*狗熊摔角*？翻回那一页！

En: Seems a little too foreign for my tastes.
Zh: 这有点过于与我无关了。

En: Alright, let's see what this 'bear wrestling' is all about...
Zh: 好吧，那就瞧瞧这个“狗熊摔角”是怎么回事……

En: When it says 'bear wrestling', does that mean two bears are wrestling *each other*, or...
Zh: 这里写的“狗熊摔跤”是指两头狗熊*相互*摔角，还是……

En: Hang on, tell me more about this 'Uncle Sport'.
Zh: 稍等，我要多了解一点关于这位*体育大叔*的事。

En: Based on the description in this article, he appears to be an extremely competent and democratically elected president.
Zh: 根据这篇文章的描述，他似乎是一位极具才干，经由民主选举上台的总统。

En: Of course not. The article makes plain that this is man-on-bear (or in some cases, woman-on-bear) competition. Two competitors, one ring, five rounds (or until 'incapacitation')...
Zh: 当然不是。这篇文章表明，这是一种人对熊的竞赛。两位选手，一个擂台，五局三胜（或是直到“失去资格”）……

En: According to the SRV's Ministry of Information, the programme has been a tremendous success. Public support stands at 87%, and youth obesity and alcoholism rates are down 12% and 7% respectively.
Zh: SRV信息部宣称这一规划大获成功。公众支持率高达86%，青年肥胖率和酗酒率分别降低12%及7%。

En: This is the SRV we're talking about. If you think those are the real numbers there's probably a guy who wants to sell you a bridge in La Cherte.
Zh: 这毕竟是SRV的说法。如果你觉得这些数字可信的话，还不如去信那个打算把拉榭赫蒂的大桥卖给你的家伙。

En: I guess it's hard to fight a bear if you're drunk.
Zh: 我觉得人喝高了就很难和熊较量。

En: I guess it's hard to fight a bear if you're out of shape.
Zh: 我觉得人太肥的话就很难和熊较量。

En: Hmmm. Not sure I trust these numbers.
Zh: 唔。我不怎么相信这些数字。

En: What are you, a statistician? Did the Moralintern send you on a 'fact-finding' mission? This is the SRV party line, comrade.
Zh: 你算老几，统计学家吗？国道联派你来是为了“挖掘真相”吗？这都写进SRV的党章了，同志。

En: Precisely. For too long drink has been a scourge to the working class, and President Knezhinisky is deeply committed to supplying the people with more wholesome entertainments.
Zh: 正是如此。长久以来，酒精一直在祸害工人阶级，而涅仁斯基总统全心全意为人民带来更有益身心的娱乐项目。

En: That's very much the idea. President Knezhinisky has long maintained that a strong body is the best defence against capitalist aggression.
Zh: 英雄所见略同。长久以来，涅仁斯基总统一直在腔调强身健体是抵御资本主义侵略的最佳方式。

En: Good thing you're a goddamn ox, son. An ox that fights bears.
Zh: 还好你体壮如牛，孩子。一头能拱翻狗熊的蛮牛。

En: In shape, out of shape. You've always preferred to rely on your wits.
Zh: 在你看来，不管胖人瘦人，有智慧就是好人。

En: The Waste Land of Reality is an unforgiving place, but if you ever encounter a bear wandering its roads, you're pretty sure you could take it.
Zh: 现实的荒原冷酷无情，但如果你碰巧遇到一头正在闲逛的熊，你很有把握能干掉对方。

En: See? There are plenty of ways to find meaning in life. You don't *need* booze to feel alive.
Zh: 你瞧？寻求生命意义的方式多得是。你没*必要*醉生梦死。

En: This is all ass-backwards. Drinking makes it *easier* to fight a bear! Plus, then you'll have a bottle to smash on its head. Easy peasy!
Zh: 这都是所谓南辕北辙。喝酒只会让和熊较量更*轻而易举*！除此之外，喝完还能用酒瓶扇丫脑袋。小菜一碟！

En: Kim! Do you think there's any chance our case will take us to the SRV?
Zh: “金！你觉得这个案子有可能让我们去SRV出差吗？”

En: Always good to catch up on foreign affairs. (Return to the table of contents.)
Zh: 了解国际事务多多益善。（回到目录。）

En: The Samaran People's Republic? The lieutenant frowns. "No, I should expect not. Why do you ask?"
Zh: “萨马拉人民共和国？”警督皱起眉头。“不，我觉得没戏。你为什么会问这种问题？”

En: I was thinking it might be fun to partake in a bear fight.
Zh: “我只是觉得参与狗熊搏击肯定很有意思。”

En: No reason.
Zh: “没什么原因。”

En: I was thinking it might be fun to see a bear fight.
Zh: “我只是觉得观赏狗熊搏击肯定很有意思。”

En: I suppose that *would* be something to see. The lieutenant seems amused at the thought of you wrestling a bear.
Zh: “那*必定*是赏心悦目的一战。”警督想象着你与熊摔角的样子，似乎乐在其中。

En: What you do in your spare time is your business. In the meantime, I would prefer you focus on our case.
Zh: “你在闲暇时间做什么是你的自由。但现在我希望你专注于我们的案子。”

En: You know, not too long ago I listened to a *fascinating radio programme* on traditional Samaran folk dances. The costumes they wear are supposed to be extraordinary...
Zh: “说起来，不久前我听过一段*引人入胜的广播节目*，讲的是传统萨马拉民族舞。据说舞者的服饰非常……”

En: In any event, no, I haven't seen any evidence there's a *Samaran angle* to this case.
Zh: “无论如何都没戏的。我看不出这个案子会有*萨马拉切入点*的迹象。”

En: They're quite the spectacle, as I understand. I've heard they make the people in the front rows wear plastic sheets...
Zh: “在我看来，那种景象相当奇特。我听说他们会让前排的观众穿上塑料布……”

En: On account of the blood, you see.
Zh: “为了防止鲜血泼溅。”

En: Sapormat 'Sport' Knezhinisky is well into his third decade as president of the SRV. Outside Samara he's known for three things: *Brutal* repression of internal dissent, *monumental* corruption, and a near-pathological obsession with physical fitness.
Zh: 萨泊玛“体育”涅仁斯基担任SRV总统已有三十余年。对萨马拉境外的人而言，他以三个特点著称：对国内异见分子的*残暴*镇压，*极端*的腐败，以及对于体育锻炼近乎病态的痴迷。

En: Son, it's only an obsession if its unhealthy. And there's *nothing* healthier than maintaining a sound body. Also, you're forgetting about the *moustache*.
Zh: 小伙子，只有不健康的事才叫痴迷。保持健壮的体魄是天底下*最*健康的事。还有，你忘了*小胡子*。

En: Don't forget the *moustache*.
Zh: 别忘了*小胡子*。

En: Right. Like all Samaran men above the age of 40, President Knezhinisky wears a truly spectacular walrus moustache.
Zh: 对了。和所有年满40的萨马拉男性一样，涅仁斯基总统也留着一道令人印象深刻的海象胡。

En: Great. Now let's get to the bear wrestling.
Zh: 好棒。咱们这就来见识一下狗熊摔角吧。

En: It's quite straightforward. A standard match features one man (or in some cases, woman) against one bear. Two competitors, one ring, five rounds (or until 'incapacitation')...
Zh: 这是相当简单明快的运动。标准赛制是人与熊一对一。一个擂台，五局三胜（或是直到“失去资格”）……

En: It takes a moment, but gradually it dawns on you that 'Kunst und Kultur' must mean 'Arts and Culture'...
Zh: 你花了一段时间，才逐渐意识到“Kunst und Kultur”是“艺术与文化”的意思。

En: *Why* they decided to title this one section in *Walder* is beyond you.
Zh: 至于他们*为什么*要用*瓦尔德语*为这个版块命名，你实在是揣测不出其中的深意。

En: Obviously because it's *cool* to drop random phrases in foreign languages.
Zh: 显然是因为随机插入一些外语会显得*很酷*。

En: As you leaf through this section, you come across several reviews of recent radio-plays, as well as a brief 'Artist Spotlight' featuring a local artist identified only as 'C.S.'
Zh: 在草草翻阅这个版块的过程中，你发现了几篇对近期广播剧的评论，还有一篇简短的“艺术家聚焦”，其主角是一位代号为“C.S.”的本地艺术家。

En: It evokes a sense of recognition and fellowship among those privileged enough to understand.
Zh: 对那些有幸理解这些内容的人而言，这能唤起他们的认同感和同伴意识。

En: The main feature, though, is a long essay titled 'TipTop Tournée: A Critical Mazovian Perspective'.
Zh: 不过，杂志的专题是一篇标题为“大师巡回赛：马佐夫主义批判”的长篇大论。

En: The TipTop Tournée is an interisolary racing series, mostly known for its high speeds, ludicrous sponsorship deals, and high death toll. In Revachol it's held quadrennially on the prestigious Zéro Carrousel racing circuit.
Zh: 大师巡回赛是跨洲举办的系列赛事，以超高的車速，荒唐的赞助协议，以及惊人的死亡率举世闻名。每过四年，就会在瑞瓦肖著名的杰洛·卡罗塞尔赛道举办一届。

En: Read the profile of 'C.S.'
Zh: 读“C.S.”的档案。

En: Just a moment, what is this 'TipTop Tournée'?
Zh: 等一下，这个“大师赛”是什么东西？

En: Alright, let's see what these communists have to say about TipTop.
Zh: 好吧，我们来看看这些康米主义者对大师赛有什么看法。

En: Sounds dull. (Return to the table of contents.)
Zh: 听起来很无聊。（回到目录。）

En: This so-called 'Artist Spotlight' is really just a brief Q&A, made all the briefer by the subject's evident hostility to her interviewer.
Zh: 这篇所谓的“艺术家聚焦”只是一段非常简短的访谈。由于受访者对采访者持有的敌意显而易见，导致本文的简短程度更过分了。

En: The actual article is surprisingly light on details, but after skimming a page or two you gather that it has *something* to do with motor carriage racing...
Zh: 本文对细节的轻描淡写程度简直令人发指，在简略地读过一两页之后，你才总结出这件事*大体上*是和赛車有关……

En: You think you're settling in for a relaxing recap of the most recent season, maybe sprinkled with some nice anecdotes about a few of the more colourful drivers...
Zh: 你本以为会读到一篇关于最新赛季的轻松回顾，说不定还会夹杂几位个性丰富的车手的花边新闻……

En: Instead you find yourself skimming a 10,000-word feature about all the politically *problematic* aspects of TipTop Tournée.
Zh: 可实际上你在粗读的却是一篇万字专题，针对的是大师巡回赛中政治*不对劲*的方方面面。

En: Where to even start? For one, there's the crass commercialism of its sponsorships. And then there's the practically *criminal* emphasis on deadly motor crashes...
Zh: 该从哪里说起呢？比如说，这里批判的是利欲熏心，唯利是图的赞助方式。那里着重强调了車祸致命的*刑事*意义……

En: If you don't follow it, you only ever hear about the ludicrous sponsorships and obscene death toll...
Zh: 如果你不关注赛事的话，可能只听说过荒唐的赞助协议和骇人听闻的死亡率……

En: That certainly *sounds* like a certain *delinquent youth* who likes to harass you from her balcony.
Zh: 这*听起来*特别像某个乐于从她那个破阳台上骚扰你的*违法乱纪青年*。

En: Wait, what's wrong with TipTop?
Zh: 等等，大师赛哪里有问题？

En: So, all of it, basically.
Zh: 基本上来说，哪里都有问题。

En: Hang on, who wrote this stuff?
Zh: 等等，这东西是谁写的？

En: What's so bad about sponsorships?
Zh: 赞助有什么不好的？

En: What's wrong with a good motor crash? They just keep it interesting.
Zh: 赏心悦目的撞車有什么问题？这都是为了保持紧张刺激的氛围嘛。

En: Under capitalism, the article says, every pursuit has its price. Every pleasure, even one as elemental as the joy of racing others around a track, is reduced to an advertising opportunity.
Zh: 本文如此写道：在资本主义的统治下，万事万物都要明码标价。世间一切欢愉，即便是像在赛道上展开角逐这样简单的娱乐项目，都会沦为打广告的机会。

En: And that, precisely, is what's problematic about it. Were it not for the promise of random, *spectacular* violence, audiences would quickly lose interest...
Zh: 这正是大师赛不对劲的地方。要不是提前知道比赛过程中偶尔会发生*壮观*的暴力场面，观众们很快就会对此失去兴趣……

En: At the end of the day, it's the destruction of these 750,000-reál racers that you're *really* watching for.
Zh: 说到底，赛事*真正*的看点，是那些价值75万雷亚尔的赛車毁于一旦的场面。

En: Thus the so-called *tournée* becomes a competition between increasingly meaningless brand signifiers: your discount laundry detergent racing against a pack of Astra cigarettes or even a Frittte...
Zh: 因此，这个所谓的*巡回赛*已经沦为愈加无谓的品牌竞争：你那打折洗衣粉正在与一包阿斯特拉香烟，甚至是一家弗莱特展开殊死角逐……

En: Oddly enough, this article has *two* bylines: Nasteb Encalada-Bernal and Exilus Bücher.
Zh: 古怪的是，这篇文章有*两条*署名：文蒂史·恩卡拉达-伯纳尔与西利尤斯·布歇尔。

En: Right, but then you get to see them *crash*.
Zh: 没错，但你还能看到这些品牌*撞車*。

En: There's no way those are real names.
Zh: 这绝对不是真名。

En: Just a moment... those names seem awfully familiar.
Zh: 等一下……这些名字听起来有种诡异的熟悉感。

En: Wait, why aren't they real names?
Zh: 等等，这为什么不是真名？

En: You're right, because they're most likely *anagrams* of 'Steban' and 'Ulixes', the student communists.
Zh: 那就对了，因为那两个名字很可能是“史蒂文”和“尤利西斯”的*易位构词*，就是那两个康米主义者学生。

En: Have you ever met anyone named 'Exilus'? Come on, they're plainly pseudonyms.
Zh: 你难道见过哪个人叫‘西利尤斯’这种名字吗？得了吧，这个一看就是假名。

En: More than that, they're likely *anagrams*.
Zh: 不仅如此，它们还很有可能是*易位构词*。

En: No, I'm pretty sure I like it for the racing.
Zh: 不对，我可是冲着赛車去的。

En: Wow, you're right. I really *am* just in it for the violence!
Zh: 哇，你太对了。我还真就是*冲着*暴力场面去的！

En: You see? One cannot avoid the conclusion that TipTop Tournée is simply the apotheosis of "spectacular" entertainment under capitalism.
Zh: 你瞧？结论是不容置疑的：大师巡回赛不过是资本主义统治之下*作秀式*娱乐的典范。

En: Kim, this article says that no one really watches TipTop Tournée for the races.
Zh: “金，这篇文章声称大师巡回赛的观众都不是冲着赛車去的。”

En: Kim, you wouldn't believe how badly written some of these articles are.
Zh: “金，你都无法想象这里面有些文章写得有多烂。”

En: Kim, did you know TipTop Tournée is actually an orgiastic ritual of capitalistic destruction? I had no idea!
Zh: “金，你知道大师巡回赛其实是资本主义的毁灭性狂欢仪式吗？我真想不到！”

En: I can imagine. Something tells me there's not exactly a glut of young communists itching to write about TipTop for *La Fumée*...
Zh: “不难想象。我觉得天下没有多少年轻的康米主义者会渴望给*轻烟*杂志写关于大师巡回赛的稿子……”

En: Oh, come on. Can this article read your mind?
Zh: “得了吧，难道这篇文章能读你的心吗？”

En: I can safely say the thought had never crossed my mind, detective. The lieutenant furrows his brow.
Zh: “我保证自己从未产生过这种想法，警探。”警督皱起眉头。

En: I'm sure most things are, if the young men who wrote this article are to be believed.
Zh: “如果相信写这种文章的年轻人的说法，天底下恐怕就没有什么不是如此了。”

En: You can still like it, detective. Just because a couple of fools wrote an article doesn't mean it's true.
Zh: “随你喜欢，警探。只是几个傻瓜写出了一篇文章而已，这并不代表他们写的都是真的。”

En: If I had to wager, I'd say they've never even *seen* the inside of a motor, much less a motor race. I'd take whatever they write with a large grain of salt.
Zh: “我敢打赌，这些家伙根本就没*见识*过車的机构，更别提赛車现场了。”

En: The lieutenant sighs. "A certain kind of young man reads one or two books and suddenly thinks he understands the secret structures of reality..."
Zh: 警督叹了口气。“某种年轻人啊，在读过一两本书之后，突然就觉得自己参透了现实的神秘结构……”

En: Well, there you have it. No reason to get hung up on the pretentious ramblings of a few young men.
Zh: “好，你这不是明白了嘛。没必要纠结于几个年轻人装腔作势的长篇大论。”

En: I wonder what *else* is really just a metaphor for life under capitalism.
Zh: “真不知道*还有*什么其实是资本主义统治下的人生的缩影。”

En: It's too bad. I thought I liked TipTop.
Zh: “太遗憾了。我原本还挺喜欢大师赛的。”

En: *It* seems to think so.
Zh: “*它*似乎也是这么想的。”

En: No, I guess not.
Zh: “不，我觉得没这回事。”

En: Sure, you *think* that, but *subconsciously* it's the visions of brand-names being engulfed in flames that you crave.
Zh: 确实，你是这样*认为*的，可在*潜意识*里，你所渴望的是目睹那些品牌被烈火吞噬。

En: That may be true of your casual, factory-issue TipTopper, but *you're* a genuine torque dork. You practically live for the wail of those superchargers!
Zh: 对于那些玩票的，流水线上的大师赛参赛者而言，也许是这么回事。但*你*可是真正的扭矩呆子。你生来就是为了聆听这些增压器的哀鸣！

En: This flimsy magazine is right. Deep down you know the only *true* stakes are life and glory, or death by fiery dismemberment.
Zh: 这本破杂志说对了。在内心深处，你知道*真正*的赌局只有两种结果：赢家活在荣誉之中，败者在烈焰中化为灰烬。

En: I guess I've never thought about it that much.
Zh: 我还从未那么深入考虑过。

En: Hell yeah, crashes are what get the blood pumping.
Zh: 太对了，只有撞車才能让人血脉偾张。

En: Who do you think you're talking to, article? I've *forgotten* more about fuel-injection systems than you'll ever know.
Zh: 区区小破文章，你以为读者是哪位大爷？关于燃油喷射系统的事，老子*忘掉的*比你学过的都多。

En: I can't believe someone actually *published* this utter tripe. (Shake your head.)
Zh: 这种胡说八道也能*出版*，这叫什么世道。（摇头。）

En: (Take out your pen.) I'm just going to make a few quick changes...
Zh: （掏出笔。）我要改上几笔……

En: Armed with the lieutenant's trusty blue pen, you spend entirely too much time striking every meaningless word and unsubstantiated claim you come across...
Zh: 你握住警督那可靠的蓝色钢笔，宛如钢枪在手，为划掉视野中的每一句毫无意义的废话和毫无根据的结论而花费了大量时间……

En: Armed with Lena's friendly green ape pen, you spend entirely too much time writing constructive feedback in the margins of the page...
Zh: 你握住莉娜那友善的绿猩猩钢笔，为了在书页的空白处写满建设性反馈意见而花费了大量时间……

En: Armed with your trusty pen, you spend entirely too much time excising meaningless phrases and unsubstantiated claims...
Zh: 你握笔如握枪，为删掉毫无意义的废话和毫无根据的结论而花费了大量时间……

En: By the time you're finished the original article is nearly illegible.
Zh: 等你改完之后，原文已经难以辨认了。

En: The magazine stares back at you defiantly. It's pretty sure you just don't *get* it.
Zh: 杂志不服地回瞪着你。它很有把握，你根本就*不懂*。

En: Believe what you want, but your fascination with machines you'll never so much as *touch* in real life is itself a *textbook* example of commodity fetishism...
Zh: 随你怎么想，但你痴迷于在现实生活中连*摸*都摸不到的冰冷机械，这一事实就是商品拜物教的*教科书*般的实例……

En: In other words, it's not the engines you covet, but the feeling of freedom they're made to signify for you.
Zh: 换句话说，你渴望的不是赛車引擎，而是其象征的自由感。

En: That's because you haven't been reading *critically*. But don't worry, the whole point of these sorts of articles is to let someone else read critically *for you*.
Zh: 那是因为你没有*批判地*进行阅读。但别担心，这种文章存在的意义就在于让别人*替你*批判地阅读。

En: You know, *most* people would just write a letter to the editor...
Zh: “你是知道的吧，*大多数*读者会选择给编辑写信……”

En: Or even better, he stops to correct himself, "just leave it be."
Zh: “甚至还有更妥当的处理方式。”他停顿片刻，纠正了说法。“别管闲事。”

En: Unsurprisingly, much of this section is taken up with articles declaring unqualified support for the dockworkers' strike.
Zh: 不难预料，这个版块中的大部分文章都在宣布无条件支持码头工人的罢工。

En: You skim the headlines: 'Paint the Harbour Red and White!', 'Martinaise Tames the Wild Pines', 'A City in Revolt', 'First We Take Martinaise, Then We Take La Delta!'.
Zh: 你扫了一眼那些标题：《把港口涂成红白两色！》，《马丁内斯制伏野松公司》，《起义之城》，《先拿下马丁内斯，再拿下拉德尔塔！》。

En: Finally, there's a brief article by the writer 'G. Martin' accusing the owner of the Capeside Apartments of illegally attempting to evict certain communist tenants simply for not having paid their rent.
Zh: 最后，还有一篇署名“G·马丁”的短文，谴责岬岸公寓的房东试图非法驱逐某些康米主义者住户，仅仅是因为他们未付房租。

En: Ah, this must be Gaston's handiwork.
Zh: 啊，这一定是加斯顿的作品。

En: Damn communists. Should've killed the lot of them when they had the chance.
Zh: 该死的康米主义者。当年他们可真该趁机多杀几个。

En: If it weren't for the Wild Pines all those moochers would be out of a job.
Zh: 要不是野松公司大发慈悲，这群游手好闲的家伙早就失业了。

En: Charging rent just to live somewhere *is* pretty outrageous.
Zh: 让人们为居住而支付租金*实在是*荒谬绝伦。

En: Wouldn't it be better for everyone if labour and capital could reach some *reasonable* accommodation?
Zh: 若是劳动力和资本能实现某种*合理的*调解，是不是对所有人都是件好事？

En: Why should I care? This doesn't have anything to do with me.
Zh: 我为什么要关心这种事？这和我毫无关系嘛。

En: One of the editorials proclaims that the more the forces of reaction attempt to crush the body, the more defiant the spirit of communism becomes...
Zh: 有篇社论宣称，反动势力越是猖狂地镇压反抗，康米主义的斗争精神就越发昂扬……

En: According to these editorials, private enterprise is simply a means of organising labour that would already happen regardless...
Zh: 据这些社论所言，私营企业不过是组织劳动力的方式之一，而这是注定要发生的……

En: Sounds like their bodies just haven't been *crushed* enough yet.
Zh: 这语气听起来就像被*镇压*得还不够狠。

En: Absolute nonsense. Whoever wrote this has clearly never built a successful business before.
Zh: 一派胡言。写这种东西的人显然从未创业成功过。

En: It goes on to call the Wild Pines a 'nightmarish transnational leech, sucking the life from the labouring classes of Martinaise'.
Zh: 接下来，文章将野松公司称为“梦魇般的跨国水蛭”，谴责其“吸吮马丁内斯劳动人民的血肉”。

En: The writer G. Martin remarks dryly that 'capitalists *love* wealth redistribution, so long as it's only redistributed *upward*'...
Zh: 文章作者G·马丁干巴巴地评论道：“资本家*热爱*财富再分配，但仅限于*向上*的分配……”

En: Good thing your rent-paying days are behind you.
Zh: 幸好你不用再付房租了。

En: It's true. The rentiers have been bleeding you dry. You really ought to find your own place one of these days.
Zh: 确实如此。收租的家伙不把你吸干是不会善罢甘休的。你真该找块属于自己的地盘了。

En: According to these editorials, there can be no accommodation with the forces of international capital or their 'mucilaginous allies' in the Coalition...
Zh: 据这些社论所言，与跨国资本及其在联盟国中“靠黏液结成的同盟”的矛盾，是不可调和的。

En: Why do communists love puerile insults? And they wonder why they're always being left out in the cold.
Zh: 康米主义者为何如此热衷于幼稚的侮辱？他们这个样子，还不明白自己为什么总是遭排挤。

En: Hold on, what's this about a 'Coalition'?
Zh: 等等，这个“联盟国”是什么？

En: Judging from the context, it's something very large and fundamental. You should probably know about it already, honestly.
Zh: 根据上下文分析，这好像是某种规模庞大的存在，而且算是基础知识。说实在的，你应该已经知道这种事了。

En: The eternal problem, according to G. Martin, is that under capitalism the worker is rendered incapable of perceiving their own agency (or lack thereof)...
Zh: G·马丁表示，永恒的问题在于，在资本主义的统治之下，工人无法发觉自身的主观能动性（或是缺乏这种能动性）……

En: Thus, they tend to experience their own oppression as though it were happening to somebody else.
Zh: 因此，他们往往会在遭受压迫时表现得事不关己。

En: Is, is that *true*? Have you felt *estranged* from your own self recently?
Zh: 难，难道*确有其事*？最近你感到与自身*决裂*了吗？

En: Perhaps they've overestimated the market demand for this kind of *commentary*.
Zh: 也许他们高估了此类*评论*的市场需求。

En: It's the stilted language and overconfident tone that's the real giveaway here. You're almost certainly reading a recycled university essay, one that could probably use some edits.
Zh: 矫揉造作的行文和盲目自信的腔调充分暴露了作者的身份。你几乎可以肯定自己是在读一篇被重复利用的大学论文，很可能还是有很多地方要改的那种。

En: Sylvie found my papers clogging the toilet, so she threw them away.
Zh: “塞尔维发现我的文件堵住了厕所，所以她把东西扔掉了。”

En: I'm pretty sure I -- or someone else -- dropped it into the toilet in my hostel room bathroom. The tablet's the same. (Tap on your nose.)
Zh: “我很肯定，我——或者其他人——把它扔进了我旅社房间浴室的厕所。那个药片的味道是一样的。”（拍拍你的鼻子。）

En: Keep reading.
Zh: 继续阅读。

En: Stop, put it back...
Zh: 停下来，把它放回去……

En: You jam the compartment shut, it's hard to breathe. The air in your lungs feels sour somehow. The drawer is locked, blue ink drips from the white pages in your hand.
Zh: 你把隔间塞了回去，感觉呼吸有些困难。不知怎么的，你肺里的空气感觉有些酸臭。抽屉锁上了，蓝色墨水从你手中的白色页面上滴落下来。

En: Keep reading.
Zh: 继续阅读。

En: YOU CAN'T! This is *your* ledger. You're a COP and it's your official ledger. It's filled out in *your* hand and it *stinks* like you too. And looks like you.
Zh: 不可以！这就是*你的*手册。你是个警察，这是你的官方手册。是*你*用手亲自填写的，所以它的*恶臭*也像你一样。而且看起来也像你。

En: Needless. It looks great. Unlike yours, which is an unholy mass dripping with excrement.
Zh: 没必要。它看起来很棒。不像你的，是一团还滴着排泄物的污物。

En: Now, if you'll excuse me... He reopens his own *pristine* blue notebook. "That thing has filled me with the sudden urge to make sure mine is in *perfect* order."
Zh: “现在，如果你不介意的话……”他重新打开了自己那本*崭新*的蓝色笔记本。“那个东西让我突然产生了一种冲动——想保证我自己的这本*井然有序*。”

En: Oh god, that sounds dangerous. Make sure you don't throw it out again.
Zh: 天啦，听起来很危险的样子。千万别又把它扔掉了。

En: That's *a lot*. This kind of case load would explain your 'solved cases' tally, on the watermark... He stops. "I didn't mean to say you're making mistakes, by the way. That was presumptuous of me."
Zh: “那*很多*了。这种数量的话，就可以解释水印上你的‘结案数’了……”他停顿了一下。“顺便说一句，我的意思并不是说你有错。我有些冒昧了。”

En: Two? He raises both eyebrows. "That's a lot. I didn't mean to say you're making mistakes, by the way. That was presumptuous of me."
Zh: “两起？”他扬起眉头。“很多了。顺便说一句，我的意思并不是说你有错。我有些冒昧了。”

En: Don't mention it.
Zh: 别提了。

En: Your secret is safe with the rotten cabbage of papers in your hand. Writing covers almost all the pages.
Zh: 你的秘密就安全地隐藏在你手中的这个臭笔记板里。所有页面里几乎全是笔迹。

En: Sadly, the ledger only comes with an old worn-down led pencil. It's unfitting of this monumental event.
Zh: 可悲的是，这本手册只配了一支旧铅笔。跟这起不朽的案件一点也不相称。

En: The ledger only comes with an old worn-down led pencil. It will *do*, barely... but...
Zh: 这本手册只配了一支旧铅笔。用它应该也*可以*，勉强吧……不过……

En: Yes, how very childish of you, he mumbles. "In your -- and my -- defence, almost everyone in the RCM uses the *titular* system. In addition to the official alphanumeric."
Zh: “是啊，你还真是有够孩子气的，”他嘟哝到。“为你——还有我——辩解的话，RCM几乎所有人都会使用这种*标题*系统。除了字母数字编码之外。”

En: Lieutenant, have you by any chance *named* our case?
Zh: “警督，你有没有给我们这起案子*命名*呢？”

En: No -- actually. Any ideas?
Zh: “其实——还没有。有什么想法吗？”

En: It actually. Does. You've offended him.
Zh: 实际上是需要的。你冒犯到他了。

En: Yes -- all that remains now is to fill those forms and *hand* them to people: fines for wrongdoers, interview requests for bad guys, and field autopsies to *dead* guys.
Zh: 是的——现在唯一要做的就是填写那些表格，然后把它们*交给*别人：给违法犯罪者的罚款单，坏人的走访需求，还有*死人*的现场尸检表。

En: Harrier Du Bois -- H.D.B.
Zh: 哈里尔·杜博阿——H.D.B.

En: Harry Du Bois -- H.D.B.
Zh: 哈里·杜博阿——H.D.B.

En: It would be *very* interesting to read about these, wouldn't it? I mean -- there seems to be a *square*-shaped entry wound in the victim's forehead. She's been sitting there for weeks. On her rocking chair. With a square hole in her skull, staring at the wall, her mouth agape.
Zh: 阅读这些应该*非常*有意思，不是吗？我的意思是——受害人的额头上好像有个*方形*的入口伤。她已经在那里坐了好几个星期。在她那张摇椅上。头骨上有一个方孔，两眼凝视墙壁，嘴巴大张。

En: MURDER -- tam-tam-tam! -- AT THE HOOKAH PARLOUR! was a case originally assigned to an officer called Joseph Mills, who is now dead. Of circumstances completely unconnected to MURDER AT THE HOOKAH PARLOUR.
Zh: 水烟雅座——铛铛铛——凶杀案！最初是指派给约瑟夫·米尔斯警官的案件，现在他人已经死了。他的情况完全跟水烟雅座凶杀案无关。

En: Beaten to death by a throng of Villalobos gang-members when him and his partner J. M. (only initials mentioned) answered a call one night. It's a sad story and it isn't really represented in *your* case files. Stop stalling and get to the MURDER AT THE HOOKAH PARLOUR.
Zh: 他和他的搭档J.M.（只提到了缩写名）有天晚上接听电话的时候，被一群维拉洛博斯的帮派分子给打死了。这是个悲伤的故事，而且并没有真正记录在*你的*案件档案中。别拖延时间了，赶紧了解一下水烟雅座凶杀案吧。

En: Right. On with the murder.
Zh: 没错。继续凶杀案。

En: Wait -- how?
Zh: 等等——怎么回事？

En: Joseph Mills was on this case that he just couldn't solve. Was doing it solo. Said it was a real nutcracker. A real brain-twister. Was on it for, like, a month -- the captain got impatient. Shit or get off the pot, Mills.
Zh: 约瑟夫·米尔斯正在调查一件他破获不了的案子。独自一人。据说是个真正杀伐果断的人。一个真正的聪明人。调查了差不多，一个月吧——警长不耐烦了。别占着茅坑不拉屎，米尔斯。

En: Mills didn't get off the pot -- not yet. He kept at it for a couple of weeks more, racking his brains, running with every theory, as outlandish as they seemed -- still couldn't solve the MURDER AT THE HOOKAH PARLOUR. Tough case, he said. Toughest he's ever had.
Zh: 米尔斯没有占着茅坑不拉屎——暂时没有。他又调查了几个星期，绞尽脑汁，想遍了所有理论，不管到底有多古怪——-还是没能破获那起*水烟雅座凶杀案*。他说，这案子很难，是他见过最难的。

En: Wait -- was Joseph Mills a good cop?
Zh: 等等——约瑟夫·米尔斯是个好警察吗？

En: Go on.
Zh: 继续。

En: No, he was awful. Awful sense of humour too. The worst jokes you've ever heard. Really rapey. Still -- he'd been on it for *months* now. Said it was THE FINAL CASE. Said it was UNCRACKABLE. That the murderer vanished into THIN AIR. That goddamn hookah parlour was all he talked about.
Zh: 不，他很可怕。幽默感也很可怕。绝对是你听过的最糟糕的笑话。真的是强暴你的耳朵。不过——他已经调查*好几个月*了。说这是他的最后一案。说它根本无解。那个凶手人间蒸发了。他整天只知道说那个见鬼的水烟雅座案。

En: Okay, so the case is handed to you because Mills isn't getting anywhere. And you look into it. Here's the set-up: A young man is found dead in a hookah parlour. You know, those places where you go and smoke bubblegum-flavoured vapour all day.
Zh: 好吧，因为米尔斯束手无策了，这起案件就交到了你手上。然后你就开始调查。事情是这样的：一个年轻男子被发现死在了一个水烟雅座里。你知道的，就是那种你可以在里面抽一整天泡泡糖味的水蒸气的地方。

En: Really cool.
Zh: 真心酷。

En: Really stupid.
Zh: 傻不拉唧的。

En: Yeah, really lame. So anyway -- young man, in his twenties, found with his skull busted open. Right on the floor of the hookah parlour. In the middle of the day. No one else is in there. Only client that day. In perfect health too, some kind of movie producer.
Zh: 是啊，真的挺垃圾。总之——有个20多岁的年轻男子死了，被发现的时候脑袋开了花。就躺在水烟雅座的地板上。当时是中午。里面没有其他人。是那天唯一的客人。他的身体非常健康，好像是电影制作人。

En: Yeah. So anyway -- young man, in his twenties, found with his skull busted open. Right on the floor of the hookah parlour. In the middle of the day. No one else is in there. Only client that day. In perfect health too, some kind of movie producer.
Zh: 是啊。总之——有个20多岁的年轻男子死了，被发现的时候脑袋开了花。就躺在水烟雅座的地板上。当时是中午。里面没有其他人。是那天唯一的客人。他的身体非常健康，好像是电影制作人。

En: No one enters -- no one exits. He's just sucking on his watermelon hookah all morning, all noon, like he usually does. (He's a regular). No calls, nothing. Just sucking on the hookah, until 15.45. Then bam -- he's dead on floor with his skull busted open, blood everywhere. What happened? How can it be?
Zh: 没人进去过——没人出来。他只是在抽自己的西瓜水烟，一早上，还有一下午，就像以前那样。（他是个常客。）没有呼救声，什么也没有。就这么一直抽，直到15:45分。然后砰的一声——他就死在了地板上，脑袋开了花，血溅得到处都是。发生了什么？怎么会这样呢？

En: Mills has no idea. Invisible assassin. Movie deal gone sour. Girl at the counter did it. Nothing fits. Eerie. Man just dropped dead. So you go to the parlour. You see cushions around the table. Table's low, heavy, really sharp edge...
Zh: 米尔斯不知道。看不见的刺客。电影的事泡汤了。吧台的姑娘干的。什么都不符合。诡异。男人就这么死了。于是你去了一趟雅座。你看见桌子周围有些靠垫。桌子很矮，很沉，边缘非常尖锐……

En: He sucked hookah, stood up, passed out, hit his head on the table and died?
Zh: 他抽了水烟，站起来，晕了过去，脑袋撞到了桌子，然后死了？

En: Can you get high off it?
Zh: 抽它能抽嗨吗？

En: No, it's soot and water vapour. It doesn't do anything.
Zh: 不能，它就是煤烟和水蒸气。什么也做不了。

En: See! You can't even *read* the thing without solving it. Yeah. It was that. Turns out hookah *does* do something -- it turns off your brain's oxygen supply. And you don't notice it until you get up to go to the bathroom.
Zh: 看见没！如果不解决它，你甚至都*读*不明白这玩意。没错。就是那样。结果水烟*的确*做了点什么——它中断了你大脑的氧气供应。而且你根本注意不到，直到你站起来，准备去厕所。

En: And what was he *doing* there -- for six hours?
Zh: 那他在那里*做什么*——整整六个小时？

En: Smoking hookah. Didn't you hear? I don't know. Trying to come up with a movie script maybe. Anyway, that was MURDER AT THE HOOKAH PARLOUR, Joseph Mills wasn't a good detective, and about 30 minutes has passed piecing it together. Next?
Zh: 抽水烟啊。你没听见吗？我不知道。也许是为了想电影脚本吧。总之，这就是*水烟雅座凶杀案*，约瑟夫·米尔斯不是个好警探，拼凑起来的话大概花了30分钟。下一个？

En: He must have sucked a lot of it.
Zh: 他肯定抽了不少。

En: Yeah, he liked his hookah. Steven was his name.
Zh: 是啊，他喜欢水烟。他的名字叫史蒂文。

En: This one's bad. Not that far from Precinct 41 -- in Central Jamrock -- there is an eight story apartment building with two hundred residents inside. It's the dead of winter, January. Snowing. Someone's beating their wife. It's half past midnight.
Zh: 这个挺糟糕的。离41分局并不远——加姆洛克中央区——有一栋8层楼高的公寓，里面有200个住户。当时是1月份，隆冬时节。下雪天。有人正在打老婆。大约半夜三点钟。

En: You get a complaint -- no one's there to take it. So you do call duty. The beating is taking place on the eighth floor. You take the elevator up. The building's creaking around you. Cold as hell. It's a run down old place. Concrete panels, rats everywhere.
Zh: 你接到一起投诉——局里没人接。所以是你值班。殴打事件发生在8楼。你乘着电梯上了楼。建筑在你周围嘎吱作响。冷的跟地狱一样。那里是个破败的老地方。混凝土面板，老鼠到处都是。

En: And it's not pretty in the apartment either. By the time you arrive the husband's left. His wife has got her lip busted, face swollen. Eyes shut. Can't leave him, they're a *financial unit*. Enjoying this beautiful life in this beautiful tenement.
Zh: 公寓里的状况也不怎么样。当你抵达现场时，丈夫已经离开。妻子的嘴唇破裂，脸上浮肿，双眼紧闭。她离不开他，他们是一个*财务整体*，在这个美妙的廉租公寓中尽享幸福生活。

En: So anyway -- you take a leak before you leave.
Zh: 总之——你走之前去撒了泡尿。

En: In the apartment I got the call to?
Zh: 在呼叫我的那个房间里面吗？

En: Yes, cops do that. *While* you're taking a piss, you see a big crack in the wall. In the outer wall of the building. You can *feel* the cold air blow in. You take the elevator down, look up -- a big crack runs on the outer wall of the whole building.
Zh: 是的，警察就会这么做。*当*你撒尿的时候，你看见墙上有一条大缝。就在建筑的外墙上。你可以*感觉*到冷风从那里吹了进来。你乘电梯下了楼，抬头一看——整栋建筑的外墙都裂了一个大口子。

En: Right from the foundation -- up to the eighth floor.
Zh: 从基座开始——向上一路延伸到八楼。

En: Oh god...
Zh: 天啦……

En: That's right. They trained you for this in cop school. Everyone has to pick a civil specialization so they can keep the city running: fire safety, first aid training, and so on. You took *building safety regulation*. And it tells you that this one -- is coming down.
Zh: 没错。你在警察学校接受过这种训练。每个人都需要挑选一种民事专业，这样才能保证城市的运行：消防安全，急救训练，等等。你选了*建筑安全标准*。而它告诉你，这栋楼——要塌了。

En: The tilt is beyond the point of maximum deviation from the gravity bearing perpendicular. This means that the building is falling -- and will fall no matter what.
Zh: 倾斜度超过了重力轴承垂直的最大偏差点。这意味着建筑物正在倒塌——而且无论如何都会倒塌。

En: No, no, no, no...
Zh: 不不不……

En: Fucking hell.
Zh: 真他妈见大鬼了。

En: Maybe not tonight. Maybe not even tomorrow. Maybe not the day after, but it's coming down. In a matter of days. It is physically impossible for it not to. And the two hundred people in there? They are all going down with it. The woman with the busted lip, the husband who beats her, their kids in the other room...
Zh: 也许不是今晚。甚至也许不是明天。也许不是后天，但是它会倒塌。不过几天之内的事。依照自然规律来看，它不倒塌是不可能的。里面那200户居民呢？他们也会跟它一起掉下去。那个嘴唇被打破的女人，打她的丈夫，还有另一个房间的孩子……

En: The drunk in the hallway. The girl in the elevator when you were going up. The youths on the stairs, laughing, smoking. The couple next to the apartment -- who made the call. They're all going to die.
Zh: 走廊里的醉鬼。你上去的时候电梯里的那个女孩。楼梯上欢笑、抽烟的年轻人。公寓隔壁的夫妻——就是他们打的电话。他们全都会死。

En: But, you see, it's *freezing* outside. And there is no municipal government in Revachol. Nowhere to put these people. Two hundred people can't go to their *aunt's for a couple of days*. And above all -- there is no one to *tell* the building is coming down. No authority but you.
Zh: 不过，你也看见了，外面*冷的要命*。而且瑞瓦肖没有市政府。没地方安置这些人。200多住户总不能都去他们的*姑妈*那里*待个几天*吧。而且最重要的是——没人*告诉*他们这栋建筑会塌。没有地方当局，除了你。

En: I'm gonna have to knock on every door and explain them how load bearing perpendiculars work?
Zh: 我去敲每一户的门，跟他们解释承重垂线的运作原理是怎样的？

En: I'm gonna have to go home and do nothing, not think about this ever again.
Zh: 我会回家，什么也不做，以后再也不想这件事。

En: I'm gonna have to find my captain somewhere, first thing tomorrow. Maybe even *tonight*.
Zh: 明天早上第一件事，就是找我的警长报告这件事。也许甚至是*今晚*。

En: Bad, bad thoughts go through your head. So what you do is -- you call your partner. JV (only initials available) is up. He comes immediately. He didn't take building safety, but he believes you. He brings five more officers. Together you knock on *every door* and explain the situation.
Zh: 你的脑海里掠过很糟糕，很糟糕的想法。所以你是这么做的——打给你的搭档。JV（只有缩写）起来了。立即赶了过来。他没有选择建筑安全专业，不过他相信你。他又叫来了5名警官。你们一起去敲了*每一扇门*，给他们解释了现在的情况。

En: Your captain puts them in a half-burned building 10 km South. It's got black mould and no roof, but hey -- it's better than death.
Zh: 你的警长把他们安置在南部10公里外一栋被烧毁一半的建筑里。那里有黑霉菌，还没有屋顶。不过——总比死好吧。

En: And then what?
Zh: 然后呢？

En: And then the building doesn't come down. And it still hasn't. That was 52 days ago. BUT THAT IS NOT THE POINT. The point is -- it *will* come down.
Zh: 然后那栋建筑没有倒塌。现在还是没有。那是52天前的事了。不过这不是重点。重点是——它*会*倒塌的。

En: Am I *absolutely* sure?
Zh: 我*绝对*肯定吗？

En: And when it does, everyone in there will die.
Zh: 而且当它倒塌的时候，里面所有人都会死。

En: If it hasn't, it won't. I was wrong.
Zh: 如果没有，就不会有了。我错了。

En: Oh god, I don't know...
Zh: 天啦，我不知道……

En: At the end of the day -- no one knows. The math says it *must* collapse. And the optics show that it doesn't. It's as if some kind of *evil spirit* is holding the tenement together, like the jaws of a trap. Luring the people back in. One by one. Already they're going... At least 40 of them are living there now.
Zh: 说到底——没人知道。依照数学原理，这建筑*必然*会倒塌。但仿佛有某种*邪灵*把整栋廉租公寓固定住了，如同陷阱的钢颚一般。它还在不断引诱着人们回去。你亲眼目睹人们一个接一个地走入其中……目前里面至少住了四十人。

En: As we speak. And you can't *keep* them from going back, because they all hate you. They despise you. They think you threw them out of their homes. Every day they despise you more -- and every day, more of them go back.
Zh: 正如我们所说。你不能*阻止*他们回去，因为他们都很恨你。他们鄙视你。觉得是你把他们从家里赶了出来。每过一天，他们对你的鄙视就加深一点——每过一天，更多人回去了。

En: And every day is a day closer to the day the building will fall.
Zh: 而且每过一天，这栋建筑就越接近倒塌的那一天。

En: Exactly. These notes have been very clear. Seems you have been thorough with documenting this one. So -- which one of these do you want to read *next*? (Because there is nothing you can do about THE COLLAPSING TENEMENT).
Zh: 没错。这些记录不是很清晰。似乎这起案子你记得特别详尽。所以——*接下来*你想看哪个呢？（因为你对这个*崩溃的公寓*已经做不了什么了。）

En: Some assholes brought their couch outside and *hung out* on it. In the middle of the street, on the roof, on the hillside by the motorway. You know -- at an *unexpected location*. They were young and they thought they looked cool on it.
Zh: 某些混账把他们的沙发搬到了外面，在上面*瞎闹*。在街道中央，在屋顶上，在高速公路旁的山坡上。你知道的——就是那些*意外的地点*。他们很年轻，而且他们觉得这样很酷。

En: They actually looked like assholes.
Zh: 事实上，他们看起来像混账。

En: The load bearing perpendicular. The maximum deviation. All of it... Some people believe you. Most don't. Some you have to *forcibly* remove. Some even pull guns on you. It takes 20 hours to evacuate the whole building. 200 people stand outside in the cold. Children cry.
Zh: 承重垂线。最大偏差值。所有那些东西……有人相信你。大部分不信。有些人你必须*强制*他们搬走。有些人甚至拿枪指着你。疏散整栋建筑的人花了20小时。200户人站在寒风中。孩子开始哭泣。

En: They looked really cool. Like models.
Zh: 他们看起来真的酷。像模特。

En: They looked really cool. Like a rock band.
Zh: 他们看起来真的酷。像一支摇滚乐队。

En: Yes. As you've said here: total assholes. Young people are the worst.  So anyway -- you got a complaint about the damn sofa. Or couch. Or whatever it was.
Zh: 是的。正如你所说的：完完全全的混账。年轻人是最糟糕的。总之——你接到了关于那个沙发的投诉。或者叫沙发椅。或者随便它是什么。

En: Yes. As you've said here: insufferable rock-and-roll assholes. Young people are the worst. So anyway -- you got a complaint about the damn sofa. Or couch. Or whatever it was.
Zh: 是的。正如你所说的：令人厌恶的摇滚混球。年轻人是最糟糕的。总之——你接到了关于那个沙发的投诉。或者叫沙发椅。或者随便它是什么。

En: Insufferable dicks. Young people are the worst.  So anyway -- you got a complaint about the damn sofa. Or couch. Or whatever it was.
Zh: 令人厌恶的混账。年轻人是最糟糕的。总之——你接到了关于那个沙发的投诉。或者叫沙发椅。或者随便它是什么。

En: They were leaving it out in all these *unexpected and whimsical locations* they took it to. Where they also took photos of themselves -- on it. And smoked cigarettes. And drank coffee, because they felt it's *intellectual*.
Zh: 他们把它放在了所有那些*意想不到和异想天开的地方*。他们还在跟它一起拍照——在沙发上。抽烟。喝咖啡，因为他们感觉这样很*明智*。

En: Cigarette butts, coffee cups, stupid couch. You had to clean it all up. And you *did* -- so congratulations to you. Case solved.
Zh: 烟头，咖啡杯，愚蠢的沙发。你必须全部清理干净。而且你*做到*了——所以，恭喜你。案子破了。

En: Did I ever catch those guys?
Zh: 我有没有抓到那群家伙？

En: No. You didn't have time for that, these notes show that you have what is called *a real god damn job*. You don't have time to be chasing down the couch-assholes. You have a real job to do. What next?
Zh: 没有。你没那个时间，记录显示你手上有一份*真正的工作*。你没时间去追查这群沙发混球。你还有真正的工作要做。好吧，下一个？

En: But?
Zh: 但是？

En: That's all you got. From the half hour you've spent piecing it together, all you know is: the entry wound was square-shaped. You never found the bullet. And then -- another body showed up. Also with a square hole in his forehead.
Zh: 只有这么多了。你花了大约半个小时把它拼凑了起来，你只知道：入口伤是方形的。你一直没有找到子弹。然后——又出现了一具尸体。额头上也有一个方孔。

En: A sequence killer?
Zh: 一个连环杀手？

En: Who knows? Those pages are missing. What next?
Zh: 谁知道呢。那些页面不见了。下一个？

En: Don't worry. One day.
Zh: 别担心。总有一天会抓到的。

En: The blue heart. Don't look into it.
Zh: 蓝色的心。不要调查它。

En: ...is *STILL* NOT WHAT HAPPENS. Fuck this compartment. You should throw it away.
Zh: ……实际*依然*没有发生。去他妈的隔间。你应该把它丢掉。

En: Strange? Why does it make you so angry?
Zh: 奇怪？它为什么让你这么生气？

En: Slide the drawer open.
Zh: 把抽屉打开。

En: The Ledger of (Oblivion Induced) Mental Health is just as shabby as the Damaged Ledger anyway. A bunch of sodden papers on a clipboard. Better not to waste more time with it.
Zh: 反正精神健康手册（包括失忆）跟损坏的手册一样破旧。一个笔记板，上面耷拉着一堆湿乎乎的纸。最好还是不要在它上面浪费时间了。

En: The Ledger of (Oblivion Induced) Mental Health is just as shabby as the Damaged Ledger was: a bunch of sodden papers sags from the clipboard in your hand.
Zh: 精神健康手册（包括失忆）跟损坏的手册一样破旧：一堆湿乎乎的纸张从你手里的笔记板上掉落了下来。

En: Kisses, kisses, kisses. You feel the air sucked out of your lungs and the blood sucked out of your head. Everything around you gets dark. Small white dots appear...
Zh: “吻你，吻你，吻你。”你感觉肺里的空气全都被抽干，大脑里的血液也被全部吸了出来。你周围的一切变得黑暗起来。一些小白点开始浮现……

En: No, you don't -- not without official police backup. It's simply impossible to open it. Your hands refuse to move.
Zh: 不，你做不到——没有警方支援就没戏。这根本就打不开。你的双手拒绝行动。

En: Somewhere in a station lazareth lieutenant-detective Kitsuragi turns on the side and says: ouch. The wounds are agonizing.
Zh: 在警局检疫所的某个地方，曷城警督侧着身，说到：哎哟。伤口让他痛苦难耐。

En: Hmm... it's as if there is an automatic *self-defence* structure in your hand, keeping you from mind-fucking yourself with letters from past lovers.
Zh: 嗯……就好像你手里有一种自动*自我防御*结构，不让你用旧情人的信来折磨自己。

En: It's me. I'm keeping his hand from moving. We're not doing that anymore. We're not reading those words. People have died. He needs to work, not ache his heart for something that will never return.
Zh: 是我。我不让手动的。我们不会这么做了。我们不会去看这些词句。有人死了。他需要工作，而不是为某种永远不会回来的东西头痛。

En: Nod and return to the case notes.
Zh: 点点头，回到案件笔记上。

En: An aluminium block runs the width of the board, biting down on the paperwork. Its crocodile teeth are the only thing keeping the papers together. On it, the perforated watermark counting the achievements of the person you used to be.
Zh: 一个铝块横跨在木板上，压在文件上。它那鳄鱼般的牙齿是唯一能把这些纸张固定在一起的东西。在那上面，穿孔水印记录着曾经那个你的辉煌成就。

En: There's pain in there, if you want some. That much you know.
Zh: 那里面有痛苦，如果你想要的话。你只知道这么多。

En: How would I open it? (Conclude.)
Zh: 我该怎么打开它呢？（总结。）

En: With your hands? U4-sized pages hang from the clip screwed to the top of the board.
Zh: 用你的手？U4大小的纸张挂在固定在木板顶部的夹子上。

En: This stinking tissue will show everyone what a bum you are. How little you give a shit. This toilet paper will PUNISH the world.
Zh: 这张散发着恶臭的纸巾会让所有人看到你到底是个怎样的废物。你到底有多不在乎。这张厕纸会惩罚全世界。

En: This toilet paper will show everyone what a bum you are. How little you give a shit. This toilet paper will PUNISH the world.
Zh: 这张厕纸会让所有人看到你到底是个怎样的废物。你到底有多不在乎。这张厕纸会惩罚全世界。

En: Maybe yours will have how many cases you've solved?
Zh: 也许你的上面有迄今为止的结案数记录？

En: Your kill count. Yours will surely have your kill count.
Zh: 你的击杀数。你的肯定有击杀人数。

En: ...while a bunch of sodden papers sag from the clipboard in your hand. It's a sorry sight.
Zh: ……一堆浸透的纸张耷拉在你手中的笔记板上。这是个遗憾的景象。

En: Not you. You are an *eternal machine*.
Zh: 你不会。你是一台*永动机*。

En: That's okay. He nods, then turns back to his own case files. "We all do, sooner or later."
Zh: “没事的。”他点点头，然后又忙活起自己的案件。“我们都会这样，迟早的事。”

En: God knows I've made my share... he tries not to think of them.
Zh: 天知道，我已经尽力了……他尽量不去想起它们。

En: A tool for manipulation. Give the lowest amount and people will be ingratiated to you.
Zh: 一种操控工具。只要给出最低数额，大家就会迎合你。

En: Browse the case files again.
Zh: 再次浏览案件档案。

En: Usual is boring. We don't do that.
Zh: 通常都很无聊。我们不会那样做。

En: Yes-yes, you know what that normal name is -- but it's so plain... *anything* else, please.
Zh: 是的，是的，你知道那个普通名字是什么——但是它实在是太简单了……拜托，*除此之外*都行。

En: Fun is *outlawed* with this guy. What a narc.
Zh: 这家伙*取缔*了一切寻欢作乐。真不愧是缉毒战警。

En: You don't exactly *close* them, so much as *distance* yourself from the smelly papers. They're a little further from your nose now.
Zh: 你并没有完全*靠近*它们，甚至还*疏远*了这些臭烘烘的纸。现在它们离你的鼻子更远一些了。

En: This means you can read the watermarks, if you just turn the lights on.
Zh: 这就意味着你可以阅读水印里的信息，只需要把灯打开就行。

En: How many years you've been on the force, he's thinking. It'll have that.
Zh: 你都加入警队多少年了，他想到。那个总会有的。

En: Still feels like there's *something* missing from that...
Zh: 感觉那个还是少了*某种*东西……

En: Fine, then. I give up on Raphaël...
Zh: 那好吧。我放弃拉斐尔……

En: Does that sound like a Raphaël Something Costeau to you, sir?
Zh: 在你听起来，像是拉斐尔·什么什么·库斯托吗，先生？

En: One last time -- YES.
Zh: 最后一次——是的。

En: Okay, you're right, it doesn't.
Zh: 好吧，你是对的，它不像。

En: Fine, then -- then you've earned it
Zh: 那好吧——那就是你应得的。

En: Dammit, take it anyway. Since you so obviously *want* to be a detective Costeau.
Zh: 见鬼，管他的呢，你就接受呗。因为你的样子明显就*想*当库斯托警探。

En: I like it, but can't we wreak havoc on *other nations* instead?
Zh: 我喜欢，不过我们就不能对*其他国家*造成严重破坏吗？

En: Did someone ask what you believe in, man with the smelly toilet ledger? What do you want to tackle next? Or are we done?
Zh: 有人问过你的信仰吗，拿着厕所味手册的男人。接下来你想处理哪个？还是就到此为止？

En: Which is also why the officer responsible (narrowly) escapes a disciplinary hearing. The end.
Zh: 这也是负责的警官（勉强）逃过纪律听证会的原因。结束。

En: Toxicology showed the entry wound was laced with an incredibly potent psychoactive substance called HUV4-Nil. No street name.
Zh: 毒物学报告显示，入口伤周围检测出一种名为HUV4-Nil的强效精神活性物质。没有诨名。

En: One day you may still catch the man with the square gun.
Zh: 总有一天，你会抓住这个带着方口枪的男人。

En: The Ledger of Oblivion is a special item that can be used both as an INTERACTABLE and a TOOL equipped in your hand for skill bonuses. Find it under the TOOLS tab in your INVENTORY.
Zh: 遗忘手册是一种特殊道具，既可以用于交互，也可以装备在手中，获得装备特效。它就在道具栏里的工具界面。

En: The Ledger of Oblivion is a special item that can be used both as an INTERACTABLE and a TOOL equipped in your hand for skill bonuses. Find it under the TOOLS tab in your INVENTORY.
Zh: 遗忘手册是一种特殊道具，既可以用于交互，也可以装备在手中，获得装备特效。它就在道具栏里的工具界面。

En: You feel the ledger slip from your hand.
Zh: 你感觉到手册在你手中滑落。

En: The lieutenant clears his throat. "I don't think this is the time to be going through personal affects," he says. "Let's move on."
Zh: 警督清了清喉咙。“我不认为现在是经历个人情感的时候，”他说到，“我们继续吧。”

En: I know, I know. Sylvie already told me I dunked it in the toilet.
Zh: 我知道，我知道。塞尔维已经告诉过我，是我把它扔进厕所的。

En: If you knew it was dunked in the toilet before getting chucked into the trash, why're you sniffing it?
Zh: 如果你知道它被扔进垃圾箱之前还在厕所里泡过，又为什么要闻它呢？

En: The option to sniff was there, so...
Zh: 有一个闻的选项啊，所以……

En: Maybe, uh -- maybe it's my fetish? Maybe? Ever think of that, nose?
Zh: 也许，呃——也许我有恋物癖？也许吧？有没有想过这一点，鼻子？

En: ... so you wanted to get in on the trash-toilet stink collab? Find out what happens when those two get *funky* together? Okay, pal.
Zh: ……所以你想加入垃圾-厕所的臭味联名组合？想知道这两种东西*臭上加臭*会发生什么？好吧，兄弟。

En: Garbage-toilet stink is not your fetish and you know it. Your nose does *not* fucking like this.
Zh: 你没有垃圾-厕所恶臭的恋物癖，而且你也知道这一点。你的鼻子他妈的*不喜欢*这个。

En: I'm a police man. This is police work. I'm just following the leads, you know?
Zh: 我是个警察。这是警察的工作。我只是在追查线索，你懂的吧？

En: Torturing your own nose -- for no reason! -- is not *conducive* to case-cracking, bud.
Zh: 让你自己的鼻子受罪——还是无缘无故的！——对破案是*毫无益处*的，老兄。

En: This is getting weird and hostile. Sorry, nose -- let's just move on.
Zh: 这好像变得越来越奇怪了，而且还充满敌意。抱歉，鼻子——我们继续吧。

En: Are you angry with me, nose?
Zh: 你生我气了吗，鼻子？

En: Yeah, turns out your nose doesn't like self-indulgent literal-shit huffing. *Quelle fuckin' surprise*. Come on, no more -- the ledger is going back down, away from your nose now.
Zh: 是啊，结果你的鼻子不喜欢放纵的吸气，字面意义上那种。*这他妈还真是个惊喜呢*。来吧，不要再这样了——把手册放下去，离你的鼻子远远的。

En: Your nose is somewhere between grateful and resentful.
Zh: 你的鼻子正介于感激和愤慨之间的某个位置。

En: You feel that thing in the back of your head? That little voice, that quiet scream? You already felt this was a bad idea, but especially right now it's even worse. Try as you might, you can't read it now.
Zh: 你感觉到后脑勺的那个东西了吗？那一点点声音，那一声安静的尖叫？你已经觉得这是个挺糟糕的主意了，尤其到了现在，感觉更加糟糕。你尽管试，现在你是看不懂的。

En: You've got *business* to deal with first. Talk to the Union boss first, at least... I can't stop you for long, but there's just enough of an excuse now.
Zh: 首先，你有*生意*要处理。至少先跟工会老大聊聊吧……我不能阻止你太久，不过现在的借口已经足够了。

En: It's the ledger you found in the trash: a pitiful cabbage of white and yellow papers hanging from plastic board, barely held together by a metal clip. This sad display is made complete by the faint smell of urinal cleaner.
Zh: 这是你在垃圾箱里找到的手册：塑料板上挂着一些可怜的黄色和白色的纸张，被金属夹子勉强固定在了一起。那丝微弱的便池清洁剂的味道，让这般凄惨的模样变得完整起来。

En: You know -- like the bits they put into public piss bowls; probably called *Fermi-Discrete* or *Axel* or something. At some point in its journey the ledger has seen the inside of a public toilet.
Zh: 你知道那是什么味道——就像是往公厕小便池里扔的那种东西。可能是叫*费米-离散*或是*阿克塞尔*之类的。这说明在这本手册颠沛流离的旅途中曾进过公厕。

En: Anything *else*?
Zh: 还有*别的*吗？

En: [Put the ledger away.]
Zh: [把手册放在一边。]

En: There's a piece of toilet paper -- or is it cleaning tissue? No, it's toilet paper -- *desperately* sticking to the back of the blue plastic clipboard.
Zh: 有一张厕纸——或者是一张餐巾纸？不，是厕纸——*绝望*地粘在塑料笔记板背后。

En: It's a metaphor -- for you.
Zh: 这是一种隐喻——对你来说。

En: Thank you, waterlogged ledger, for spelling it out for us.
Zh: 谢谢你，水淋淋的手册，帮我们讲出来了。

En: Below the pathetics -- terror. Do not look into its blue heart.
Zh: 在悲惨的下面——是恐怖。不要看那颗蓝色的心脏。

En: Browse the *white* papers.
Zh: 浏览*白色*的纸。

En: Browse the forms again.
Zh: 再次浏览一下表格。

En: Browse the *yellow* papers.
Zh: 浏览*黄色*的纸。

En: Look at the clipboard.
Zh: 看看笔记板。

En: [Put the ledger away.]
Zh: [把手册放在一边。]

En: Inspect the clip.
Zh: 检查夹子。

En: Inspect the toilet paper.
Zh: 检查厕纸。

En: It's just toilet paper, sticking to the back of the plastic clipboard. You can take it off if you want.
Zh: 有一张厕纸——或者是一张餐巾纸？不，是厕纸——*绝望*地粘在塑料笔记板背后。

En: (Take it off.)
Zh: （把它扯下来。）

En: (Leave it there, it's cool.)
Zh: （让它留在那里，多酷啊。）

En: Maybe it's kitchen tissue? They look exactly the same.
Zh: 也许是厨房用纸？它们看起来基本是一样的。

En: It's not though. It's toilet paper.
Zh: 并不是。它是厕纸。

En: If you *want* it to be kitchen tissue, it can be kitchen tissue.
Zh: 如果你*希望*它是厨房用纸，那就是厨房用纸咯。

En: Still wet, the toilet paper peels off the plastic easily. All you have to do is shake it off your finger and voila! The ledger now looks (marginally) better.
Zh: 厕纸还是湿的，很容易就从塑料上揭了下来。现在你要做的就是把它从你的手指头上甩下去。看啦！现在手册看起来（稍微）好多了。

En: *Way* cool.
Zh: *更*酷了。

En: Mhm, cool toilet paper... I mean kitchen tissue.
Zh: 嗯，很酷的厕纸……我是说厨房用纸。

En: Still wet, the toilet paper -- I mean *kitchen tissue*, sorry -- peels off the plastic easily. All you have to do is shake it off your finger, and voilà, the ledger now looks (marginally) better.
Zh: 厕纸还是湿的——我的意思是*厨房用纸*，抱歉——很容易就从塑料上揭了下来。现在你要做的就是把它从你的手指头上甩下去。看啦！现在手册看起来（稍微）好多了。

En: A bunch of sodden papers sag from the clipboard in your hand. It's a sorry sight.
Zh: 一堆湿漉漉的纸从你手中的笔记本上耷拉下来。这是一个令人遗憾的景象。

En: It says: everyone look at me and my toilet paper covered cop ledger! I don't *care*. My ledger is droopy and it smells like a urinal. Not a new addition, though. So keep that *styling case* open, my friend...
Zh: 它说：所有人都看看我，还有这本粘着厕纸的警察手册！我不*在乎*。我的手册无精打采的，闻起来有便池的味道。不过，这也不是什么新的添加物。所以就让那个*造型箱*开着吧，我的朋友……

En: It says: everyone look at me and my toilet paper covered cop ledger! I don't *care*. My ledger is droopy and it smells like a urinal.
Zh: 它说：所有人都看看我，还有这本粘着厕纸的警察手册！我不*在乎*。我的手册无精打采的，闻起来有便池的味道。

En: Smell the ledger.
Zh: 闻闻手册。

En: The acidic stench of rotting food has rubbed off on the cellulose. It now forms the *base* of the experience. This base surrounded by a faint air of spoiled meat -- the stuff of death itself! -- and then sprinkled liberally with the citrus zest of toilet cleaner.
Zh: 腐烂食物的酸臭味已经沾染到了纤维素上。现在它组成了体验的*基调*。在此基调上，周围环绕着一些变质肉类的气味——就是死亡本身！——然后喷洒了大量柑橘味的厕所清洁剂。

En: Which toilet would that be?
Zh: 哪个厕所？

En: *Which*? You're not sure you've smelled this one yet, or worse... you have the nagging feeling you might have missed it.
Zh: *哪个*？不确定自己之前闻过这个，或者更糟糕……你有种不安的感觉——你也许是错过了。

En: Remember when I said the smell of the upstairs bathroom was so rank they should have sent a *poet* to describe it?
Zh: 还记得我说楼上浴室的味道很难闻，他们应该派个*诗人*来专门描述一下吗？

En: Is "sprinkled liberally with the citrus zest of toilet cleaner" a line this poet might have used?
Zh: 那这首诗里有一行是不是用了“喷洒了大量的柑橘味厕所清洁剂”？

En: Not really.
Zh: 并没有。

En: Maybe you should pay more attention then? Anyway, what your nose is trying tell you is -- the ledger was dropped into a toilet in the Whirling-in-Rags, by you. The toilet cleaner in your room smells exactly the same.
Zh: 也许你应该再稍微留意一点？无论如何，你的鼻子是在试图告诉你——这本手册曾进过褴褛飞旋的厕所，还是被你扔进去的。你房间里的厕所清洁剂就是这股味道。

En: Why yes it is! Among many other things. This cleaning tablet is used by the Whirling-in-Rags -- perhaps that's where the ledger was dropped in the toilet? By you.
Zh: 可不是嘛！还有其他证据。褴褛飞旋用的就是这种清洁片剂——也许手册就是掉进了那里的厕所？而且还是你扔的。

En: Hold on, what do you mean a metaphor for *me*?
Zh: 等等，*我*的隐喻是什么意思？

En: Ignorantly pass through the eighth thousandth sign that it's yours.
Zh: 无知地穿过第八千个符号，代表它是你的。

En: FACE THE DESOLATION! This is *your* ledger. You're a COP and it's your official ledger. It's filled out in *your* hand and it *stinks* like you too. And looks like you.
Zh: 直面这种凄凉吧！这就是*你的*手册。你是个警察，这是你的官方手册。是*你*用手亲自填写的，所以它的*恶臭*也像你一样。而且看起来也像你。

En: Shut up, nose. I don't need your help -- I don't need *anyone's* help!
Zh: 闭嘴，鼻子。我不需要你的帮助——我不需要*任何人*的帮助！

En: Your nose does not understand what it did wrong.
Zh: 你的鼻子不明白自己哪里做错了。

En: The lieutenant observes you raising and lowering the toilet-water-logged mass under your nose, trying to get a good whiff. A good *analytical* whiff.
Zh: 警督观察你——拿着被厕所水泡过的手册在鼻子下面上下晃动，试着好好嗅一嗅。一种很好的*解析*嗅探动作。

En: I think I didn't want to be a cop anymore -- so I flushed my career down the toilet, literally.
Zh: “我觉得我不想当警察了——所以我把整个职业生涯都扔进厕所冲走了，就是字面意义上的那种。”

En: I think I didn't want to be a cop anymore.
Zh: “我觉得我不想当警察了。”

En: It must have been cramping my style.
Zh: “一定是它束缚了我的风格。”

En: Easier said than done -- how could this pathetic mess of copy paper and plastic ever become *très disco*?"
Zh: 说起来容易做起来难——这个乏味又糟乱的复印纸和塑料怎么可能变成*极乐迪斯科*呢？

En: Officer, however damaged -- that... he points to it, "...is still an official piece of paperwork. It probably contains notes on *numerous* ongoing investigations. It could even list undercover operatives. Certainly informants. I suggest integrating it into your *style*. For all our sakes."
Zh: “警官，不管破损有多严重——那个……”他指向它。“……还是一份正式的官方文书。里面可能包含*许多*正在进行的调查笔记。甚至还可能列出了卧底的信息。确定的告密者。我建议你把它融入你的*风格*。这样我们大家都好。”

En: I'm sad to hear that. For a moment he doesn't know what to reply.
Zh: “很抱歉听到这个消息。”有那么一瞬间，他都不知道该回复些什么。

En: It has a.. foreboding quality to it. I'm picking up dark vibes. Maybe I *had* to lose it?
Zh: “它有一种……不祥的特性。我感觉到了黑暗的共鸣。也许我*必须*丢掉它？”

En: It has a.. foreboding quality to it. Maybe I *needed* to lose it, for the great bloodletting to begin...
Zh: “它有一种……不祥的特性。也许我*需要*丢掉它，才能让伟大的杀戮降临……？”

En: You had to lose it for *the bloodletting* to begin? What are you talking about?
Zh: “你为了*杀戮*降临而必须丢掉它？你在想什么呢？”

En: He's not mad -- that smelly mess of paper in your hand is making him worry about the well being of his own paperwork, that's all.
Zh: 他没疯——你手里那个臭纸团让他开始担心自己文书的健康，就这么回事。

En: Yeah, I still don't want to discuss it.
Zh: “是啊，我还是不想讨论这个。”

En: I don't know.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: Okay, no problem. He turns away to start organizing his own notes -- leaving you with yours.
Zh: “好吧，没问题。”他转身开始整理自己的笔记——把你跟你自己的手册留在一起。

En: I speaketh the tongue.
Zh: “我是说舌头。”

En: In any case, we're lucky you found it. Make sure it's all in there. Official notes are... well they're dangerous, they contain confidentialities.
Zh: “无论如何，能找到它是我们走运。要保证里面的东西完好无损。官方笔记是……好吧，它们很危险，里面包含了机密信息。”

En: Someone from the Whirling threw it in the trash? I don't know. I'm boring.
Zh: “飞旋旅社的某个人把它扔进垃圾箱了？我不知道。我觉得有点无聊。”

En: What are you talking about? No one's out to get you -- get real man! Upgrade that cop-ledger!
Zh: 你在说什么呢？没人想伤害你——现实点吧，兄台！好好整理一下那本警察手册！

En: I don't know... have you seen how *basic* that thing is? Basic clip, unglamorous toilet-smelling papers. It could've done with some *pizzazzo*! Anyway, go on. Sorry I said anything.
Zh: 我不知道……你没看见这东西有多*基础*吗？基础的夹子，散发着乏味的厕所味道的纸张。它本来可以在*潇洒*一些的！不管怎么说，继续吧。抱歉我说了这些话。

En: I don't know, it seems foreboding to me somehow. Like there's something in there that's out to get me.
Zh: 我不知道，不知道为什么，在我看来它有种不祥的感觉。就好像里面有些东西想伤害我。

En: Alright then. Seems like a good idea.  (Accept.)
Zh: 那好吧。好像是个好主意。（接受。）

En: He's sorry for you.
Zh: 他为你感到抱歉。

En: What I should do is get back to making sense of this thing. (Discard.)
Zh: 我要做的是重新搞清楚这件事。（放弃。）

En: It is -- look at how basic that thing is! Basic clip, unglamorous toilet-smelling papers. It could do with some *pizzazzo*" Cheer you right up.
Zh: 没错——看看这东西有多基础！基础的夹子，散发着乏味的厕所味道的纸张。它本来可以在*潇洒*一些。让你能振作起来。

En: I don't know either... He points to the ruined notes. "You should take stock of those, make sure it's all there. Official notes contain informants' names, undercover operatives even. If some of it has fallen into the hands of the RCM's adversaries, dark vibes may well ensue."
Zh: “我也不知道……”他指着破损的笔记。“你应该估算一下，保证所有东西都在。官方笔记里包含告密者的名字，甚至是卧底的信息。如果有些落入到RCM的对手那里，很可能会产生黑暗共鸣。”

En: I don't know either... He points to the ruined notes. "You should take stock of those, make sure it's all there. Official notes contain informants' names, undercover operatives even. If some of it has fallen into the hands of the RCM's adversaries, bloodletting may well ensue."
Zh: “我也不知道……”他指着破损的笔记。“你应该估算一下，保证所有东西都在。官方笔记里包含告密者的名字，甚至是卧底的信息。如果有些落入到RCM的对手那里，很可能会发生流血事件。”

En: Do-eth thou also *thorough inventories*? He points to the ruined notes. "You should take stock of those. Official notes contain informants' names, undercover operatives even. If some of it has fallen into the hands of the RCM's adversaries, dark vibes may well ensue."
Zh: “你们也会做彻底盘查吗？”他指着破损的笔记。“你应该估算一下，保证所有东西都在。官方笔记里包含告密者的名字，甚至是卧底的信息。如果有些落入到RCM的对手那里，很可能会产生黑暗共鸣。”

En: Do-eth thou also thorough inventories? He points to the ruined notes. "You should take stock of those. Official notes contain informants' names, undercover operatives even. If some of it has fallen into the hands of the RCM's adversaries, bloodletting may well ensue."
Zh: “你们也会做彻底盘查吗？”他指着破损的笔记。“你应该估算一下，保证所有东西都在。官方笔记里包含告密者的名字，甚至是卧底的信息。如果有些落入到RCM的对手那里，很可能会发生流血事件。”

En: Boring? Try *dangerous*. You should do a thorough inventory of that -- make sure some has not fallen into the hands of the RCM's enemies: organized crime, or worse... Official notes sometimes contain informants' names, even undercover operatives.
Zh: “无聊？试试*危险*吧。你应该彻底盘查一边——保证这些东西不会落到RCM敌人的手里：有组织犯罪，甚至更糟糕……官方笔记里包含告密者的名字，甚至是卧底的信息。”

En: Challenge accepted. You should be on the lookout for stylistic elements that elevate this cabbage to heights unforeseeable.
Zh: 接受挑战。你应该注意格式上的元素——那些可以把这玩意提升到难以预见的高度的元素。

En: After a while he can't take it anymore. "Excuse me, I just have to ask again -- *how* could you have let this get tossed out?"
Zh: 过了一会儿，他再也受不了了。“不好意思，我需要再问一遍——你*怎么*会把它给扔了呢？”

En: After a while he can't take it anymore. "Excuse me, I just have to ask again -- *how* did it get in the trash?"
Zh: 过了一会儿，他再也受不了了。“不好意思，我需要再问一遍——它到底是*怎么*跑进垃圾箱的？”

En: Okay, good. There is a moment's silence. "But *why* were they there in the first place?"
Zh: “好吧，很好。”片刻沉默。“但是它们一开始*为什么*会出现在那里呢？”

En: Okay, good. There is a moment's silence. "But *why*?"
Zh: “好吧，很好。”片刻沉默。“但是*为什么*呢？”

En: An aluminium block runs the width of the board, biting down on the paperwork. Its crocodile teeth are the only thing keeping the papers together. A regular pencil, the tip worn down to nothing, has been attached to the clip.
Zh: 一个铝块横跨在木板上，压在文件上。它那鳄鱼般的牙齿是唯一能把这些纸张固定在一起的东西。一支普通的铅笔——笔尖磨得几乎已经没有了——连在夹子上。

En: Run your finger across the aluminium.
Zh: 用你的手指拂过铝块。

En: Enough of the clip. (Back.)
Zh: 夹子的事就到这里吧。（返回。）

En: The surface is interrupted by a silvery *sticker*. It's rectangular, sparkling with iridescence. You don't know how you didn't notice it before...
Zh: 表面被一张银色的*贴纸*中断了。它是矩形的，闪烁着彩虹色的光芒。你不知道自己之前怎么没注意过它……

En: Looks like an official mark, made to be low visibility outside the right circumstances.
Zh: 看起来像是一个官方标志，在适当的环境之外能见度很低。

En: It is similar to the RCM watermark on your blazer the lieutenant mentioned. Didn't he say something about the headlights of his motor carriage? That you can read these there?
Zh: 这跟警督提到的——你夹克上的RCM水印很相似。他不是说过什么汽車前灯的事？还说可以阅读里面的信息？

En: Lieutenant, is this one of the *hologram watermarks* you mentioned? (Point to the sticker.)
Zh: “警督，这就是你提过的那种*全息水印*吗？”（指着贴纸。）

En: Forget about it for now -- how is the rest of this thing doing?
Zh: 暂时先忘掉这个——其他部分怎么样了？

En: What? He is lost in his own notes. It takes a moment for him to see it. "Yes, a halogen watermark used for adding information to RCM property."
Zh: “什么？”警督沉浸在自己的笔记里。过了一会儿他才看到了它。“是的，这是个卤素水印，用来为RCM的财产增加信息的。”

En: Interesting. What kind of information?
Zh: “有意思。什么样的信息？”

En: How can I read it?
Zh: “我怎么才能阅读它呢？”

En: That's all, thank you. (Conclude.)
Zh: “就这些，谢谢你。”（总结。）

En: It depends. Aside from an anti-counterfeiting stamp, mine has my station number and address. The information varies by date of issue.
Zh: “这要看情况。除了防伪印章之外，我的手册里还附有警局编号和地址。根据签发日期，手册上的信息会略有不同。”

En: Any capable light with the right wavelength will do.
Zh: “任何波长合适的灯都可以。”

En: The lieutenant would rather not have you rummaging around, looking for the lights in his vehicle...
Zh: 警督不太愿意让你在他的車里到处找灯……

En: You mentioned the headlights of your Kineema?
Zh: “你提过你那辆锐影的前灯？”

En: Before, you mentioned the headlights of your vehicle?
Zh: “之前你不是提过你的汽車前灯？”

En: Yes. There is a pause. "RCM vehicles have headlights tuned especially to reveal halogen watermarks."
Zh: “是的。”略微停顿了一下。“RCM的前灯特别调整过，可以显示卤素水印。”

En: The lieutenant fears this will lead to fiddling with the delicate, folding headlights on his motor carriage. They're dear to him.
Zh: 警督担心最后的结果就是你去瞎摆弄他汽車上那对娇弱的可折叠前灯。它们可是他的宝贝。

En: Okay. He returns to his neatly kept notes...
Zh: “好吧。”他又开始忙乎自己整洁的笔记了……

En: Hey lieutenant. What is this? (Point to the sticker.)
Zh: “警督。这是什么？”（指着贴纸。）

En: What? He is lost in his own notes. It takes a moment for him to see it. "That thing? It's a halogen watermark. We use it for adding information to RCM property."
Zh: “什么？”警督沉浸在自己的笔记里。过了一会儿他才看到了它。“是的，这是个卤素水印，用来为RCM的财产增加信息的。”

En: Like -- for example?
Zh: “比如——举个例子？”

En: All RCM vehicles have headlights designed to reveal halogen watermarks. Mine too.
Zh: “所有RCM汽車的前灯都是专门设计过的，可以显示卤素水印。我的也是。”

En: Oh, and an incredibly cool piece of *kitchen tissue* is stuck to the back.
Zh: 哦，还有一张特别酷炫的*厨房用纸*粘在背后呢。

En: Oh, and a *cool* piece of toilet paper is stuck to the back.
Zh: 哦，还有一张*酷炫*的厕纸粘在背后呢。

En: It's the ledger you found in the trash: a cabbage of papers hanging from the board, with the permeables' drawer inside. It's barely held together by a clip -- then made complete by the faint smell of urinal cleaner.
Zh: 这是你在垃圾箱里找到的手册：一堆纸耷拉在木板上，里面有透水抽屉。它们被金属夹子勉强固定在了一起——还有那丝微弱的便池清洁剂的味道，让这般凄惨的模样变得完整起来。

En: It's the ledger you found in the trash: a cabbage of papers hanging from the board, barely held together by the clip -- and made complete by the faint smell of urinal cleaner.
Zh: 这是你在垃圾箱里找到的手册：一堆纸耷拉在木板上，被金属夹子勉强固定在了一起——还有那丝微弱的便池清洁剂的味道，让这般凄惨的模样变得完整起来。

En: In the back you see thin translucent copier paper -- some neon yellow, some bright red -- all covered in boxes, like marching armies. These look like official forms, waiting to be filled out...
Zh: 在背面，你看见轻薄透明的复印纸——有些是荧光黄色，有些是亮红色——全都装在盒子里，像行军的军队一样。这些看起来像是官方表格，等待着被人填满……

En: What is this?
Zh: 这是什么？

En: To be honest: you have little to no idea. You're dumb. And alone.
Zh: 老实说：你基本不知道。你很蠢。而且很孤独。

En: Somewhere -- not here -- the helpful Lieutenant Kitsuragi is making notes in his blue notebook. He tilts it ever so slightly, a glowing rectangle catches the light. He could advise you with this. The next moment...
Zh: 在某个地方——而不是此地——乐于助人的曷城警督正在他那蓝色的笔记本上做着笔记。随着他的笔记本微微倾斜，有个发光的矩形物体露了出来。关于这种事，他可以给你提些建议。在下一个瞬间……

En: Not good. A bunch of sodden papers sags from the clipboard in your hand.
Zh: 不太好。一堆湿乎乎的纸张从你手里的笔记板上掉落了下来。

En: You should turn them on and get in front of those headlights -- see what happens. There may be information on there, a *halogen watermark* he called it.
Zh: 你应该把灯打开，站到前面——看看会发生什么。上面可能会有什么信息，他称之为*卤素水印*。

En: When I get the chance. (Accept.)
Zh: 等有机会吧。（接受）

En: Not interested, Logic. I don't take orders from anyone. (Decline.)
Zh: 我不感兴趣，逻辑。我不会接受任何人的命令。（咀嚼）

En: Obviously, yes. The papers still sag from the clipboard in your hand. It's a defiant sight. You're a hero.
Zh: 显然是的。纸张还在从你手中的笔记板上掉落。这是一个挑衅的景象。你是个英雄。

En: It'd be nifty if you found something that way, wouldn't it? The papers still sagging from the clipboard in your hand -- not so nifty.
Zh: 如果你能发现些什么就太好了，不是吗？纸张还在从你手中的笔记板上掉落——这可不太好。

En: They're not *exactly* white. They're yellowed in patches by sunlight and alcohol, and covered in dense blue handwriting. Ink escapes into watercolour patterns, reaching its tendrils across entire pages. The paper itself is chequered with faint red lines forming short paragraphs.
Zh: 它们并不*完全*是白色的。它们被阳光和酒精染上了斑驳的黄色，上面密密麻麻的全是蓝色的笔迹。墨水渗入到水印图案上，将自己的触角伸向整张页面。纸张本身有很多方格，上面那些暗红色的线组成了简短的段落。

En: Once in a while there's a red stamp that exclaims: CASE FILES; COMMIT TO PAPER. The *CASE FILES* themselves are plenty. You count more than a hundred sodden, crumpled up, earmarked pages falling apart in your hands. They appear to be sufficiently organized and extremely dense, if mostly illegible.
Zh: 上面偶尔会出现一个红色的印章，上面写着：案件档案；文字记录。*案件档案*本身很多。你数了数，有100多张浸透的、皱巴巴的预留页面在你手中四分五裂。它们似乎组织地很充分，而且非常密集，不过大部分都难以辨认。

En: (Count the pages.) I have to open an official case. Is there room?
Zh: （点一下页面的数量。）我得开一个正式的案件。还有空间吗？

En: There is -- for precisely *one* more. Fifteen pages near the end remain untouched by the damage. The chequered grid forms a structure of passages, breaking the case into sub-tasks to accomplish.
Zh: 确实还有——准确来说还够*一个*。接近最后的15页还完好无损。方格形成了一个段落结构，将案件分解成了需要完成的一些子任务。

En: Once all the tasks are accomplished, the case is complete.
Zh: 等到所有任务完成之后，这个案件就完成了。

En: COMMIT TO PAPER -- using the pen Lena gave you.
Zh: 记录下来——用莉娜给你的那支笔吧。

En: COMMIT TO PAPER -- using the pen lieutenant gave you.
Zh: 记录下来——用警督给你的那支笔吧。

En: COMMIT TO PAPER.
Zh: 记录下来。

En: The tasks you've completed flow out of the Kind Green Ape pen in a brash freehand similar to the rest of the letters. The wording comes easily, it's almost robotically simple; a language developed for mental rigour and simplicity:
Zh: 你完成的任务——以一种跟其余字母类似的仓促手绘方式——从绿人猿铅笔下流淌而出。行文来的很容易，几乎像机器人一样容易；这是一种为严谨精神和简易方法开发的语言：

En: The tasks you've completed flow out of the Blue Oblong pen in a brash freehand uncannily similar to the rest of the letters. The wording comes easily, it's almost robotically simple; a language developed for mental rigour and simplicity:
Zh: 你完成的任务——以一种跟其余字母类似的仓促手绘方式——从蓝色椭圆型的笔下流淌而出。行文来的很容易，几乎像机器人一样容易；这是一种为严谨精神和简易方法开发的语言：

En: The tasks you've completed flow out of the pen in a brash freehand similar to the rest of the letters. The wording comes easily, it's almost robotically simple; a language developed for mental rigour and simplicity:
Zh: 你完成的任务——以一种跟其余字母类似的仓促手绘方式——从笔记流淌而出。行文来的很容易，几乎像机器人一样容易；这是一种为严谨精神和简易方法开发的语言：

En: Kim, do you have a pen?
Zh: “金，你有笔吗？”

En: The lieutenant looks at his blue notebook. *Two* fat, shiny pens hang from the binder, like large calibre bullets on an ammo belt.
Zh: 警督看着自己的蓝色笔记本。*两只*圆乎乎、亮闪闪的笔挂在活页夹上，就像挂在弹药带上的大口径子弹一样。

En: It's downright *incriminating*. He has little choice but to give you one, although he really does not want to.
Zh: 这是彻头彻尾的*连累*。他没有多少选择，只能递给你一支，虽然他真心不想这么做。

En: You know you have to give one to me, right? That *is* how human society works, I believe.
Zh: “你知道你必须给我一支的，不是吗？我相信人类社会*就是*这么运作的。”

En: Fine, I'll just use this crappy pencil...
Zh: “好吧，我用这只蹩脚的铅笔就好……”

En: Can I have one?
Zh: “能给我一支吗？”

En: He is not really saying anything. Just standing there -- looking at them.
Zh: 他没说什么。只是站在那儿——看着它们。

En: Wordless, he pulls one from the loop and hands it to you. The pen is cold, blue, and ready to write.
Zh: 沉默不语的他从圆圈上取下一支，递给了你。笔很冷，很忧郁，做好了书写的准备。

En: Absolutely motionless at first, then animated slowly, imperceptibly even, the lieutenant begins to browse his notes again. Leaving you to the case files.
Zh: 起初是纹丝不动，然后慢慢地，甚至有些让人难以察觉，警督又开始浏览他的笔记了。把你跟案件档案晾在了一边。

En: The threat has passed. He is thankful it has passed, and that he's gotten to keep both pens.
Zh: 危机解除。他很感激，这样他就能留住两支笔了。

En: Inspect victim's body.
Zh: “检查受害人尸体。”

En: Interview the cafeteria manager.
Zh: “走访餐厅经理。”

En: Cross out the ones you've already finished.
Zh: 划掉你已经完成的事项。

En: A satisfying slash sounds across the paper. *You're done* it seems to say. And *you!* -- and *you!*
Zh: 一阵愉悦的嘶啦声划过纸页。好像在说“你完了”。还有*你*！——和*你*！

En: AND A FUCK YOU TO YOU *TOO*! That last one cuts a slash right through the paper.
Zh: 请容我说一句，*也*去你妈的吧！最后一个在纸上划出了一道口子。

En: Things to be done and things already done -- the composition of reality. This is an extremely useful tool for a detective of the Citizens Militia. Now all that remains is to name the case.
Zh: 待办事项和已完成事项——现实的构成。对于公民武装的警探来说，这是一种非常有用的工具。现在唯一剩下的，就是给这起案件命名。

En: Things to be done and things already done -- the composition of reality. This is an extremely useful tool for a detective of the Citizens Militia.
Zh: 待办事项和已完成事项——现实的构成。对于公民武装的警探来说，这是一种非常有用的工具。

En: Know that I give this to you... He pulls one from the loop. "...with *resentment*."
Zh: “要知道我把这个给了你……”他从圆圈上取下一支笔。“……是带着*怨气*的。”

En: (Back to the case files.)
Zh: （回到案件档案上来。）

En: Like a fan of gills the chequered papers dry in your hand. The handwriting is extremely dense, if mostly illegible.
Zh: 方格纸在你手里变干了，就像一片鱼腮。笔迹非常密集，不过大部分都难以辨认。

En: What is in there -- what are they about?
Zh: 这里面有什么——是关于什么的？

En: Work. Strife. Poverty. The Jamrock Quarter. These are handwritten logs of investigations dating back to January '51, this year. The exact number is hard to estimate due to missing pages -- and an *odd* naming convention -- but there are at least twenty, maybe thirty cases. Undertaken, not completed, mind you.
Zh: 工作。冲突。困难。加姆洛克地区。这些手写的调查记录可以追溯到51年1月，就是今年。由于一些页面的缺失，具体的数字很难估计——还有一个*古怪的*命名惯例——不过至少有20起，甚至是30起案件。注意，是接手，未完成。

En: Inspect the handwriting in the files.
Zh: 检查文件里的笔迹。

En: There was mention of a... *naming convention* here?
Zh: 这里是不是提到了一个……*命名惯例*？

En: Yes. It appears you employ a... shall we say *robust yet literary* system. Each investigation has its case number written on the margins. Yet, still more tellingly, most are accompanied by a *name*.
Zh: 是的。看来你采用了一种……不如就叫它*粗鲁又文艺*的系统。每次调查的案件编号都写在了边缘。不过，更为显著的是，大部分都附带着一个*名字*。

En: A *title* one might say even. One that draws inspiration from snoop fiction and Vespertine cop show staples.
Zh: 甚至可以说是一个*标题*。从侦探小说和维斯珀警察秀获取灵感的名字。

En: You're a swashbuckler with that pen, Harry. And it feels good. Feels like *completion*.
Zh: 有了那支笔，你就成了一个游荡剑客，哈里。而且这种归纳觉很好，很*完整*。

En: It's the middle of March -- you have attempted two cases a week on average.
Zh: 现在是三月中旬——你平均每周会处理两起案件。

En: Is two cases a week a good case load, lieutenant?
Zh: “一周两起案件数可以吗，警督？”

En: How much is that -- two new cases a week?
Zh: 那是多少——如果一周两起新案件的话？

En: Oh my, and they're written in capital letters too...
Zh: 天啦，它们也是用大写字母写的……

En: Yes, all caps. One is called THE NEXT WORLD MURAL, another THE SQUARE BULLET HOLE MURDERS. Another yet: THE UNSOLVABLE CASE.
Zh: 是的，全部大写。有一个名叫*来世壁画*，另一个叫*方形弹孔凶案*。还有一个叫：*无解案件*。

En: I don't wanna... (Back to the case files.)
Zh: 我不想……（回到案件档案上。）

En: More?
Zh: 还有吗？

En: Others appear more light-hearted: THE GUYS ON A COUCH IN AN UNEXPECTED LOCATION and THE MURDER AT THE HOOKAH PARLOUR, even the rare article-free COLLAPSING TENEMENT. Murder features prominently throughout.
Zh: 其他的好像更轻松一些：*坐在意外地点沙发上的人们*和*水烟雅座凶杀案*，甚至还有个罕见的没内容的：*崩溃的公寓*。凶杀贯穿始终。

En: You like this grimy *murdering*, don't you?
Zh: 你喜欢肮脏的*凶杀*，不是吗？

En: Wish there was one in there about a drug den, you love those. Gets the *blood* pumping.
Zh: 真希望里面有个讲毒窝的，你就爱那些。能让你*热血*沸腾。

En: Huh? He raises his nose from his notes. "Two *complex* cases to undertake is a lot, yes. You *really* have to push yourself. I would not suggest it. Lest you start making mistakes."
Zh: “哈？”他从笔记中抬起头来。“接手两起*复杂*的案件是挺多的，没错。你*真的*需要督促自己才行。我不会建议你这么做。以免你开始犯错。”

En: Two cases a week appears to have been my load, lieutenant. I'm not sure I completed them though.
