Zh: “我的工作量好像是一周两起案件，警督。不过，我不确定自己到底破案了没有。”

En: It's *inornate*. Nearly illegible, yet marching in orderly lines.
Zh: 这是*不加修饰的*。基本上很难辨认，不过还是排成了整齐的队形。

En: *Pedagogical* somehow. Brash. It must be yours for you to be able to read it. These are the lines of someone who has written by hand *a lot* and has developed a style only they themselves -- or *you yourself* -- can decipher.
Zh: 不知道怎么的，有点*教育学*的感觉。很仓促。肯定是只为了你一个人能够看懂。这些线条，像是某个*经常*用手的人写下的，而且还开发出了一种只有他们自己——或者*你自己*——才能破译的方式。

En: Written in a rush. In pain. A race to beat your own heart's pulse to some dark finish line.
Zh: 仓促间写下的。一场让你的心跳加速到黑暗终点线的比赛。

En: Get the body down.
Zh: “把尸体放下来。”

En: It's not exactly poetry -- but poetry would be out of place.
Zh: 确切的说，这不是诗歌——不过诗歌也不太合适。

En: I'm sure I made plenty of mistakes.
Zh: “我肯定自己犯了不少错误。”

En: I burned out all right.
Zh: “我应该是精疲力竭了。”

En: A nice brisk pace. The way I like it.
Zh: 敏锐快速的速度。正是我喜欢的方式。”

En: A lot? A little? You have no idea, this all depends.
Zh: 很多？一点点？你也不知道，这得看情况。

En: Does it? It feels like a *lot*. Or maybe even more than a lot. It feels like *too much*.
Zh: 有吗？感觉好像是*很多*。或者甚至比那个还多。感觉有点*太多*了。

En: ...then *rip* them from the binder, and hand them out. According to type of form.
Zh: ……然后从活页夹上*撕扯*下来，把它们交出去。根据表格种类，是这样没错。

En: Three. The topmost are MISCONDUCT FINES, the middle ones  are STATION CALLS, and the bottommost are FIELD AUTOPSY FORMS. Each is easy enough to make sense of.
Zh: 有三种。最上面的是违规行为罚款单，中间的是警局传唤通知，最下面的是现场尸检表。每一个都很容易理解。

En: What types of forms are there?
Zh: 这是什么类型的表格？

En: Enough of these. (Close forms.)
Zh: 够了。（合上表格。）

En: These are quite sinister in tone. They give a date and time for the person to 'appear' at the specified Precinct Police Station. Below the 'call' are the criminal charges you risk by 'not appearing'.
Zh: 这里面的口气就很凶狠。上面给出了某人必须‘出现’在指定警察分局的日期和时间。‘传唤通知’下面是冒险‘不出现’时面临的刑事指控。

En: A dozen pages of thin copy paper, bright red in colour. You see the parameters of a deceased human form, waiting to be filled in: age, sex, condition of internal organs...
Zh: 一堆薄薄的复印纸页，颜色是亮红色。你看见尸体特征表，等待着被填满：年龄，性别，内脏状况……

En: But they appear... pleasantly vague.
Zh: 不过，它们似乎……有一种令人愉悦的模糊感。

En: All in a print so small it could be considered downright *cute*.
Zh: 所有印刷体都很小，小到完全可以说是*可爱*。

En: Colour of the irises. Predation marks. Condition of sexual organs.
Zh: 虹膜颜色。捕食记号。性器官状况。

En: Kim, look, *field autopsy*.
Zh: “金，看，*现场尸检*。”

En: Let it be.
Zh: 随它去吧。

En: Good, yes. We can return to the dead body -- by now it smells *exquisite*... Looks like he would rather concentrate on taking notes for now.
Zh: “很好，没错。我们可以回到尸体那边去——现在味道应该比较*高雅*了……”看来他现在更愿意集中精神做笔记。

En: The rest of the stinking cellulose is *much* worse for wear. Being sandwiched between the board and the rest of the paperwork must have spared the fragile copier paper.
Zh: 剩下来的恶臭纤维素让人*更加*难以忍受。被夹在木板和其余文书之间，肯定让那些易碎的复写纸逃过了一劫。

En: MISCONDUCT FINE.
Zh: 违规行为罚款单。

En: STATION CALL.
Zh: 警局传唤通知。

En: FIELD AUTOPSY.
Zh: 现场尸检表。

En: A monetary penalization ranging from 20 to 250 reál. Severe cases allow for 1000 reál, but that requires special paperwork. The details of issuing these fines are spread out over the rest of the fields.
Zh: 罚款金额范围在20-250雷亚尔之间。重案允许1000雷亚尔的罚款，不过那个需要特殊文书工作。签发罚款单的细节分布在表格内剩余的部分。

En: You don't have to be an intellectual giant to do police-work.
Zh: 要完成警察工作，你不用成为一个知识巨人。

En: What delicious power hid within this pathetic mess... you feel better.
Zh: 在这种可悲的混乱里中隐藏着多么美丽的权利啊……你感觉好多了。

En: The forms feel moist, like the rest. Wavy to touch.
Zh: 像其他表格一样，它也是潮乎乎的。摸上去有些拨动起伏的感觉。

En: Thin see-through copier paper -- mostly neon yellow, some bright red -- all covered in boxes like marching armies. These are your official forms, easy enough to make sense of...
Zh: 轻薄透明的复印纸——大部分是荧光黄色，有些是亮红色——全都装在盒子里，像行军的军队一样。这些是你的官方表格，很容易理解……

En: That's *it*?
Zh: 就*这样*？

En: The notebook is *annual*, it says '51 on what remains of its cover -- a molten strap of cardboard. Everything prior to this must have belonged to a previous volume. In short: there was more.
Zh: 这本手册是*按年*签发的，封面上残余的部分写着51年——那是一份熔铸的硬纸板。在这之前的所有内容肯定都记录在之前的手册上。简而言之：这种手册还有更多。

En: Your hand cramps up merely looking at the scribbles, drawn -- as by some magnet -- to the red chequered margins. Fast, but always *straight*...
Zh: 仅仅是看着这些潦草的字迹，你的手就开始抽搐起来，就像磁铁一样，被牵扯到红色的方格边缘。很快，不过总是很*直*……

En: That's okay. He nods, then turns back to his own case files. "We all make mistakes."
Zh: “没关系。”他点点头，然后转回到自己的案件档案上。“我们都会犯错的。”

En: I prefer a *normal* case load. He turns back to his own case files. "It's a matter of methods."
Zh: “我更喜欢*普通*一点的案件数。”他转回到自己的案件档案上。“这是一个方法问题。”

En: Who is this person?
Zh: 这人是谁？

En: Return to the case files.
Zh: 回到案件档案上来。

En: Wearing out. Nearly gone. Sometimes he forgets to keep the pen on the paper as he moves his hand and the lines vanish from underneath...
Zh: 精疲力竭。行就将木。他移动手部的时候偶尔会忘记把笔放在纸上，而且线条也从下面消失了……

En: Arson. Petty theft. Spousal abuse -- handwritten logs on dozens of investigations date back to January, '51 -- stamped CASE FILES; COMMIT TO PAPER. These are your last couple of months in Revachol, Precinct 41, Jamrock Quarter.
Zh: 纵火罪。小偷。虐待配偶——调查上的手写记录可以追溯到51年的1月份——盖上了*案件档案，文件记录*的印章。这些就是你在瑞瓦肖的最后几个月，41分局，加姆洛克地区。

En: Arson. Petty theft. Spousal abuse -- handwritten logs on investigations dating back to January, ´51 -- stamped CASE FILES, COMMIT TO PAPER. These are the last couple of months of your life.
Zh: 纵火罪。小偷。虐待配偶——调查上的手写记录可以追溯到51年的1月份——盖上了*案件档案，文件记录*的印章。这些就是你生活的最后几个月。

En: I'm done inspecting these. (Close the case files.)
Zh: 我已经检查完了。（合上案件档案。）

En: What do you mean? Is that *all*?
Zh: 你是什么意思？这些就是*全部*了吗？

En: Judging by the creases on your forehead and the lines on your cheeks -- too much more. Going back years, decades even....
Zh: 从你额头的皱纹还有脸颊上的线条来看——太多了。可以回溯到几年，甚至是十几年前……

En: Kim, all my cases employ a naming convention similar to what we used on ours.
Zh: “金，我手上所有案件采用的命名惯例跟我们现在这起很相似。”

En: Kim, my cases appear to employ some kind of... naming convention.
Zh: “金，我的案件似乎采用了某种……命名惯例。”

En: You mean the alphanumeric -- officer, precinct, time of arrival at the scene?
Zh: “你是说字母数字吗——警官，分局，还有到达犯罪现场的时间？”

En: That's the one. (Lie.)
Zh: “是那个没错。”（撒谎。）

En: No, I mean a... *non-numeric* one. With titles.
Zh: “不，我的意思是……*非数字*的那些。带标题的那种。”

En: Good. That's a good one...
Zh: “很好。那个还挺不错的……”

En: He understands you're *concealing* something, sire.
Zh: 他能明白，您在*隐藏*着什么，大人。

En: ...but -- do you also use the *titular*?
Zh: “……但是——你是不是也用了*标题*呢？”

En: Oh, you mean the *titular*. Yes, well... So do I. In our defence -- almost everyone in the RCM does.
Zh: “哦，你是说*标题*。是的，这个嘛……我也会。为我们自己辩解的话——RCM几乎每个人都会。”

En: Okay. Yes. It appears I do.
Zh: “好吧，是的。看起来好像是这样的。”

En: No. I don't know what you're talking about.
Zh: “不，我不知道你在说什么。”

En: Right. Of course. He returns to his notes, leaving you to yours.
Zh: “好吧。当然了。”他又开始忙乎自己的笔记，让你自己忙自己的。

En: Well... so do I. In our defence, officer, almost everyone in the RCM does.
Zh: “呃……我也会。为我们自己辩解的话，警官，RCM几乎所有人都会这样做。”

En: Why is that?
Zh: “为什么会这样？”

En: It's a holdover from the early days of the RCM -- right after the Revolution, when the organization had little idea how to do things. It persists in an *unofficial* capacity. Officers use these titles to refer to their work among themselves.
Zh: “这是RCM早期留下的习惯——就在大革命结束之后，组织也不怎么知道该怎么做事。它是以*非官方*的身份存在的。警官用这些标题来彼此交流工作。”

En: I seem to have named a case THE SQUARE BULLET HOLE MURDERS."
Zh: “我手上好像有一起名叫*方形弹孔凶案*的案子。”

En: Again, in your defence, I seem to have named one... He peeks into his notes. "THE MAN WITH THE HOLE IN HIS HEAD. That was a real person, his death was real. Still I named it that. To amuse myself."
Zh: “要为你辩护的话，我好像起了一个名字……”他瞥了一眼自己的笔记。“脑袋上有洞的男人。那是个真人，他的死亡也是真实的。不过我还是给它起了那个名字。给自己找点乐子。”

En: He smiles. "I pray his loved ones never find out."
Zh: 他笑了。“我祈祷他的亲人永远也不会知道。”

En: What happened to him?
Zh: “他出什么事了？”

En: Rail spike through the head. He died. It was a work place accident.
Zh: “道钉穿透了他的脑袋。他死了。是一起車间事故。”

En: No, you don't. You're a human measuring instrument, almost entirely intellectual.
Zh: 不，你不是的。你是个人形测量仪，基本上是完全是智能的。

En: I think I got it.
Zh: 我想我明白了。

En: Right, what more do you need? A rubber condom stuck to the back? A graffito that says 'DEFEATED'? I think you catch the drift.
Zh: 好吧，你还想要什么？背后粘个套套吗？一个写着‘挫败’的涂鸦？我想你应该明白我的意思。

En: Something rattles inside, ever so lightly. Is there... a hidden compartment?
Zh: 有什么东西在里面响动，动静很轻。难道……有个隐藏的隔间？

En: And something small inside? Light?  Made of paper or cardboard? Or dried flowers perhaps?
Zh: 难道里面有什么小东西？很轻？用纸或者硬纸板做成的？或者也许是干花？

En: What did you say the colour was?
Zh: 你说是什么颜色？

En: Peek inside.
Zh: 往里面瞄一眼。

En: Blue.
Zh: 蓝色。

En: It's made of dark blue plastic hard enough to beat someone to submission with. The edges are rounded, however. The U4-sized board feels thick and heavy in your hand. Light shimmers on its wet surface. On the back you see the embossed letters: RCM.
Zh: 它是由深蓝色塑料制成的，坚硬到能够打倒一个人。不过边缘是圆形的。这个U4大小的木板在你手上感觉很厚重。湿滑的表面闪闪发光。在背面，你看到一些浮雕字母：RCM。

En: Shake the ledger.
Zh: 摇晃手册。

En: Back to the rest of this mess.
Zh: 回到其他的烂摊子上面。

En: U4-sized pages hang from the clip screwed to the top of the board.
Zh: U4大小的纸张挂在拧在木板顶部的夹子上。

En: The plastic shimmers like lapis lazuli, but it is not see-through. You cannot see to its centre...
Zh: 塑料发出的光芒像天青石一样，不过它不是透明的。你看不到它的中心……

En: There's something there. A rectangular shape, like a card. Or a postcard...
Zh: 那里好像有点什么。一个矩形的东西，像一张卡片。或者是一张明信片……

En: There's something there. A rectangular shape, like one of those *postcards* that Annette told you about...
Zh: 那里有什么矩形的东西，好像是安妮特跟你提过的*明信片*……

En: Open the hidden compartment in the clipboard.
Zh: 打开笔记板上隐藏的隔间。

En: Crack it on the knee like Burke!
Zh: 像秘密谋杀一样用膝盖把它折断。

En: Amber Die With A Mosquito.
Zh: 里面有只蚊子的琥珀骰子。

En: Hanged man's advice.
Zh: 吊人的建议。

En: Lonesome Long Way Home.
Zh: 漫漫孤寂回家路

En: Found lost motor carriage.
Zh: 找到丢失的汽車

En: Beat the payphone.
Zh: *打*公用电话

En: Harry Debby Berenger?
Zh: 哈里·黛比·贝朗格？

En: Horus Debby Berenger?
Zh: 荷鲁斯·黛比·贝朗格？

En: Harry Darjan Binzakin?
Zh: 哈里·达尔罕·宾扎吉？

En: Hram Darjan Binzakin?
Zh: 哈拉姆·达尔罕·宾扎吉？

En: Those aren't my initials, I'm not *feeling* them.
Zh: 那些都不是我的名字缩写，我对它们没有*感觉*。

En: Can't you see the *sire* is tired of these... fabrications. *RAC* is clearly what it says on the case files. These are official documents. There is no arguing with *official* documents.
Zh: 你看不出*大人*已经对这些……粗制滥造的东西感到厌倦了吗？案件档案上清清楚楚地写着*RAC*。这些可是官方文件。跟*官方*文件争辩是没有意义的。

En: Wow. I don't know what to say to that.
Zh: 哇哦。我不知道该说什么好。

En: Good. How long does it take to read one of these *Raphaël-stamped* cases? (Persist.)
Zh: 很好。要花多久才能看完这些印着*拉斐尔*的案件？（坚持。）

En: I got nothing here either. Logic really isn't the best faculty to have this conversation with, But it's the one you got, so sorry.
Zh: 我这边也没看出些什么。逻辑真的不是与之对话的最佳能力，不过这你只有这个，我很抱歉。

En: But what is HDB?
Zh: 不过HDB是什么？

En: That... is improbable.
Zh: 那个……不大可能。

En: Read a case file.
Zh: 阅读一份案件档案。

En: *Feeling* isn't really the jurisdiction we're in right now.
Zh: *感情*现在真的不在我们的管辖范围之内。

En: Can I read the case files now?
Zh: 现在我能阅读这些案件档案了吗？

En: Kim explained the system.
Zh: 金解释了这个系统。

En: Icosahedral Dice Set "Sirens".
Zh: 二十面体骰子组“塞壬”。

En: Finished autopsy.
Zh: 完成尸检。

En: Finished with Titus.
Zh: 跟提图斯断绝关系。

En: Probably for the best. Because HDB feels like *bad news* from yesteryear. Like shit, honcho.
Zh: 这样可能最好。因为HDB感觉像是去年的*坏消息*。感觉很糟糕，老板。

En: Fine. It takes about half an hour for you to piece one together, Mr... Raphaël. Which one do you want?
Zh: 好吧。拼凑一个大约要花上半小时，拉斐尔……先生。你想要哪一个？

En: Yeah, we're staying out of this business for now. HDB is *bad news* from yesteryear. It's shit, honcho.
Zh: 是啊，我们暂时不会插手这件事。HDB代表去年的*坏消息*。它就是一坨屎，老板。

En: It takes about half an hour to piece one together, using the system you've devised. Which one do you want?
Zh: 用你发明的系统，拼凑一个大约要花上半小时。你想要哪一个？

En: It takes about half an hour to piece one together, using the system you've devised. Where do you want to start?
Zh: 用你发明的系统，拼凑一个大约要花上半小时。你想从哪里开始？

En: Yes! At last you find a way to piece them together using the alphanumeric code on the margin -- HDB41, date of initialization and time of arrival on the scene. Followed by the title. For example: HDB41120117:00 (THE NEXT WORLD MURAL).
Zh: 没错！至少你可以用边缘的字母数字代码把它们拼凑起来——HDB41，起始日期和到达犯罪现场的时间。之后是标题。比如：HDB41120117:00（无解案件）。

En: Yes! You can piece them together using the alphanumeric code on the margin. It always begins with HDB41, then date of initialization and time of arrival on the scene. Followed by the title. For example: HDB41120117:00 (THE NEXT WORLD MURAL).
Zh: 没错！你可以用边缘的字母数字代码把它们拼凑起来。这些总是以HDB41开头的，然后是起始日期，还有到达犯罪现场的时间。之后是标题。比如：HDB41120117:00（无解案件）。

En: Yeah... no.
Zh: 是啊……不了。

En: It's proving to be harder than expected. You just don't have the intellectual rigour to patch the quilt back together -- try again later?
Zh: 已经证实这比你期望的要困难很多。你真的没有那种聪明才智来把这些碎片重新拼凑起来——要不以后再试吧？

En: It's possible: yes. Easy: no. You need to come up with a small archaeological system to re-order the remains of your past works. At the moment all they do is fall apart in your hands. Some dates and the numeric titular system is all you have.
Zh: 是可能的：没错。简单吗：不是。你需要想出一套小考古学系统，才能把剩下这些之前的工作重新排列起来。自此此刻，他们所做的就是在你手中四分五裂。你只有一些日期和数字标题系统。

En: Forty one is your precinct.
Zh: 41代表你所属的分局。

En: I think what you *meant* to say was RAC -- *Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau*.
Zh: 我觉得你*想*说的是RAC——*拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托*。

En: It's safe to say those are the initials of the officer responsible for the case -- *your* initials.
Zh: 可以肯定的说，那些是案件负责警官的名字缩写——*你的*缩写。

En: No, the alphanumeric begins with HDB.
Zh: 不，字母数字编码是HDB开头的。

En: And HDB...
Zh: 还有HDB……

En: Wait... HDB41? Weren't those: 'Officer, precinct'?
Zh: 等等……HDB41？难道不是：‘警官，分局’吗？

En: Why yes. Your precinct number is 41.
Zh: 当然是了。你的分局数字就是41。

En: Actually, I'm ready to admit I'm not Raphaël and go on with my life.
Zh: 其实我已经准备好承认自己不是拉斐尔，就这样继续以后的生活。

En: How long does it take to read a case?
Zh: 阅读一份案件档案需要多久？

En: Every last alphanumeric in the files begins with it -- and these are *your* case files. It's safe to say H.D.B. are your initials.
Zh: 档案里每一个字母数字编码都是以它开始的——而且这些是*你*的案件档案。可以肯定的说H.D.B是你名字的缩写。

En: Wait! What is HDB41?
Zh: 等等！HDB41是什么？

En: That's comforting to hear. Now, detective. It takes half an hour to piece one of these together -- if you still want to. Here are your options.
Zh: 听到这个真让人欣慰。现在，警探。把这些拼凑起来需要花费半小时——如果你还想这么做的话。选择权在你。

En: MURDER AT THE HOOKAH PARLOUR
Zh: 水烟雅座凶杀案

En: THE NEXT WORLD MURAL
Zh: 来世壁画

En: THE SQUARE BULLET HOLE MURDERS
Zh: 方形弹孔凶案

En: THE COUCH IN AN UNEXPECTED LOCATION
Zh: 意外地点的沙发

En: THE UNSOLVABLE CASE
Zh: 无解案件

En: I can revisit this. (Put the case files away.)
Zh: 我可以稍后再看。（把案件档案放在一边。）

En: COLLAPSING TENEMENT
Zh: 崩溃的公寓

En: Not much has changed in the meanwhile -- a bunch of sodden papers still sags from the clipboard.
Zh: 在此期间，基本没发生什么变化——笔记板上还是耷拉着一堆浸透的纸张。

En: This one is relatively easy to reconstruct. Overnight on 12/02. a graffito -- nay, a mural! -- appears on an eight story tenement overlooking Central Jamrock. The building is a sparsely inhabited ghost tower, part of a failed real estate development called Grand Couron.
Zh: 这个重构起来要相对简单。12月2日夜间。一副涂鸦——不对，是一副壁画——出现在8层楼高、可以俯瞰加姆洛克中心区的房子上。这栋建筑是一座人烟稀少的鬼塔，隶属于一个名叫*大科戎*的房地产开发项目。

En: The mural is enormous. Two silhouettes -- a man and a woman -- are kissing. The text cut into their forms reads:
Zh: 这是一幅巨大的壁画。两个轮廓——一个男人和一个女人——正在拥吻。割开两人身形的文字是：

En:
TRUE LOVE IS POSSIBLE
ONLY IN THE NEXT WORLD -- FOR NEW PEOPLE
IT IS TOO LATE FOR US

WREAK HAVOC ON THE MIDDLE CLASS
Zh:
真爱是可能的
但只会发生在来世——发生在新人类身上
对我们来说已经太迟

资产阶级在肆虐

En: People call it *that thing* and *that fucking thing*. It’s visible for miles. In two days the station's complaints desk gets clogged with requests to remove *The Bummer*. You and your partner are assigned to the case.
Zh: 人们称之为*那玩意儿*和*那个破玩意儿*，隔着几哩都能看见。在短短两天之内，警察局的投诉台上就堆满了移除*那个倒霉东西*的要求。你和你的搭档被任命负责此案。

En: The graffito crew is easy to track down. Only the Belles Lettres have the *literage* of industrial paint to cover the surface. (One of the graffito artists is rumoured to be *rich*.) They take responsibility for the execution, but not the design. The ideologue of the *Next World Mural* -- as the crew calls it -- remains an unknown.
Zh: 涂鸦帮很容易追踪。只有纯文学才会用*一船的*工业漆来覆盖它的表面（谣传其中一位涂鸦艺术家很*富有*）。他们只负责执行，而不管设计。提出*来世壁画*的理论家——那帮人就是这么称呼它的——依然未知。

En: The crew agrees to clean up after themselves. However, your partner -- JV -- is against the removal, citing public support for conservation. This leads to a debate in Precinct 41, which then spreads to the streets of Jamrock. Ending in a rare plebiscite -- organized by you and the rest of Row III.
Zh: 那帮人同意把它清理干净。不过，你的搭档JV表示反对，还援用了公众支持保留它的意见。这让41分局内部发生了一场辩论，后来还扩散到整个加姆洛克的街道。最后这件事以罕见的公民投票结束了——组织者是你和III行的其余人。

En: The nine thousand people subjected to the mural’s message -- all of Lakeside (Central Jamrock) and Villalobos, plus half of the Eminent Domain -- participate in the vote. Although the case begins with what appears to be a lot of rumbling on the streets as to how juvenile and stupid the mural is, given a choice between two options...
Zh: 9000名受到壁画信息影响的人——整个湖滨（加姆洛克中央区）和维拉洛博斯，再加上一半的艾米涅特区——参与了这次投票。虽然案件一开始，街上就有很多关于这幅壁画是多么幼稚和愚蠢的怨言，现在给出了两种选择：

En: A) REMOVE THE MURAL, IT IS WRONG
Zh: A)清除壁画，它是错的

En: B) KEEP THE MURAL, IT IS RIGHT
Zh: B)保留壁画，它是对的

En: A staggering seventy-eight percent of voters choose to keep it. Turns out the opposition were a loud minority. And that love truly is possible in the next world -- for new people. And it is too late for us.
Zh: 令人惊愕的是，有78%的投票选择保留它。事实证明，反对者才是少数派。而且，的确来世才可能有真爱——那是为新人类准备的。对我们来说，真的太迟了。

En: All that remains is to wreak havoc on the middle class.
Zh: 现在唯一剩下的，只有资产阶级在肆虐。

En: The middle class are not to be blamed. It's *human nature*.
Zh: 不应该谴责资产阶级。这就是*人性*。

En: I must have voted (and possibly even lobbied) to remove the thing because I don't believe in that rubbish one bit.
Zh: 我肯定投了（很可能甚至游说过）清除这玩意，因为我完全不相信那个破玩意。

En: No one cares what you believe in, man with the smelly toilet ledger. What do you want to tackle next? Or are we done?
Zh: 没人在乎你相信什么，拿着厕所味手册的男人。接下来你想处理哪个？还是就到此为止？

En: Did anyone ask what you believe in, man with the smelly toilet ledger? What do you want to tackle next? Or are we done?
Zh: 有人问你相信什么了吗，拿着厕所味手册的男人。接下来你想处理哪个？还是就到此为止？

En: In any case, it appears to have been a rare case of civil activity in the Quarter. And agreement as well. What do you want to tackle next?
Zh: 不管怎么说，这在区域内都是一起罕见的民事活动案件。还有最后的一致意见。接下来你想处理哪个？

En: Wait, do I ever find out who came up with it?
Zh: 褴褛飞旋等等，我有没有发现到底是谁想出来的？褴褛飞旋

En: Read on.
Zh: 继续看下去。

En: The case files do not show you finding the author of the design.
Zh: 根据案件档案显示，你并没有找到设计者。

En: AKA LESLIE & BURKE, AKA THE PUBLIC INDECENCY DRUNK & THE PROPERTY DAMAGE DRUNK is a *cursed* case. It has been passed from unsuspecting officer to unsuspecting officer for ten years. On January 29, THE UNSOLVABLE CASE made its way to you. Why you accepted it is unclear. Every officer and indeed most civilians in Jamrock know it's UNSOLVABLE.
Zh: 又名莱斯利&伯克，又名公然猥亵酒鬼&财产损坏酒鬼，这是一起*被诅咒*的案件。整整十年，它从一个毫无戒心的警官手里传到一个又一个毫无戒心的警官手里。1月29日，*无解案件*落到了你头上。你接受它的理由还不清楚。每一位警官，还有几乎每一位加姆洛克市民都知道，它是无法解决的。

En: You were so drunk you didn't remember what it was when you signed on. That, or you were high.
Zh: 你醉得太厉害，记不起接手时是什么情况。或者，要么你当时就是磕嗨了。

En: Leslie will always take his pants off when he's drunk. Burke will always trash everything. It's just what they do. It is their nature -- you cannot change the nature of a man. And you can't lock them away, because public indecency and small-scale property damage are not punishable by incarceration.
Zh: 莱斯利喝醉的时候总是会脱裤子。伯克会把到处搞得一团糟。他们就喜欢做这种事。这是他们的天性——你无法改变一个人的天性。而且，你也不能把他们锁起来，因为公然猥亵罪和小规模财产损失是不会受到监禁处罚的。

En: The only way for Leslie to stop flashing his genitals to by-passers, and for Burke to stop dismantling signage and rear view mirrors, would be for them to *stop drinking alcohol*. Which, in their forties, or fifties -- it's hard to tell because of their distorted features -- is a medical improbability on par with you ceasing to produce *The Expression*.
Zh: 唯一让莱利斯停止向过路人暴露下体，还有让伯克不再拆除标牌和后视镜的方式，就是让他们*戒酒*。对于40来岁，或者50来岁的他们来说——因为面容已经扭曲，很难说是多大——存在医学上的不可能性，跟你停止做出*那个表情*基本属于同等水平。

En: The only way for Leslie to stop displaying his genitals, and for Burke to stop attacking things, would be for them to *stop drinking alcohol*. Which in their forties, or fifties -- it's hard to tell because of their distorted features -- is a medical improbability.
Zh: 唯一让莱利斯停止暴露下体，还有让伯克不再搞破坏的方式，就是让他们*戒酒*。对于40来岁，或者50来岁的他们来说——因为面貌都已经扭曲了——存在医学上的不可能性。

En: Couldn't we just keep them off the streets?
Zh: 就不能让他们远离街道吗？

En: Proceed.
Zh: 继续。

En: You would think that, but you're wrong. Where's the fun in exposing your genitals, or breaking stuff in your own home? No, Leslie and Burke are on the corner of Main Street and Perdition, because that's where the *action* is.
Zh: 你是会这么想，不过你错了。在自己家里暴露下体，或者打砸破坏还有什么乐趣呢？不，莱斯利和伯克会在主路和永劫路的拐角，因为那里是他们*行动*的地方。

En: Can you keep *yourself* off the streets?
Zh: 你能让*自己*远离街道吗？

En: Threatening, fines, dragging them to the station, locking them up in the hell holes they live in, locking them up in the station, hypnotherapy -- even trying to get a local gang of *zemlyakis* to take them out (the zemlyakis gave them ethanol so Burke and Leslie would expose and rampage even harder) -- you tried it all. And still the complaints wouldn't stop. As they hadn't stopped for *ten years*.
Zh: 威胁，罚款，把他们拖到警局，把他们关在生活的地狱里，把他们锁在警局里，催眠疗法——甚至试过找本地的一帮*泽姆拉基*干掉他们（泽姆拉基把乙醇给了他们，所以伯克和莱斯利闹的反而更凶了）——你全都试过了。但是投诉还是源源不断。因为他们已经*十年*没有停下来了。

En: It's plain to see from the files that you, Satellite-Officer JV, and special consultant TH had more important cases to attend to. You uncover cross-reference to several ongoing investigations, each brought to a standstill every time you drive down Main Street. Because there they are! On the corner of Perdition, and what is Leslie doing?
Zh: 从档案里明显可以看出，你，随迁警官JV，还有特别顾问TH还有更重要的案子要办。你对几起现行的调查案件进行了互相参阅，发现每当你开車经过主路的时候都会停下来。因为他们就在那里！就在永劫路的拐角，莱斯利在做什么？

En: Property damage.
Zh: 财产损失。

En: Public indecency.
Zh: 公共猥琐罪。

En: Good, you're learning. If the files are to be trusted -- that's all there is to it. That and Burke breaking things. And the fact that they're both drunk. But then again, so are you. The case becomes *considerably* less comic one day, when Burke takes a swing at your ledger.
Zh: 很好，你在学习。如果可以相信这些档案——那情况就是这样。那件事，还有伯克搞破坏的事。事实是他们都喝醉了。不过，你也是。有一天，当伯克攻击你的手册时，这起案件就变得*相当*不滑稽了。

En: No, that's Burke. Leslie's *schtick* is flashing his genitals, remember? How will you ever solve THE UNSOLVABLE CASE if you can't even get that straight? Well, turns out it's pretty simple. The case becomes *considerably* less comic one day, when Burke takes a swing at your ledger.
Zh: 不，那是伯克。莱斯利的*把戏*是裸露下体，还记得吗？如果你都不能把这些理清楚，那还怎么能破获*无解案件*呢？呃，最后其实挺简单的。有一天，当伯克攻击你的手册时，这起案件就变得*相当*不滑稽了。

En: He must have it confused with the *property* he likes to damage. But the joke's on him -- the officer is also drunk. Way more drunk than Burke there, and let's be fair, you also have *party eyes*. You slam the hardened plastic board in his face, then proceed to beat him unconscious with it.
Zh: 他一定是把它跟他喜欢破坏的*财产*搞混了。不过命运跟他开了个玩笑——警官也喝醉了。你比伯克喝的还醉，而且说句良心话，你也是一副*想狂欢的样子*。你把坚硬的塑料板砰的一声砸到他脸上，然后继续用它把伯克打到失去意识了。

En: He must have it confused with the *property* he likes to damage. But the joke's on him -- you're also drunk. Drunk out of your mind on Potent Pilsner. You slam the hardened plastic board in his face; then proceed to beat him unconscious with it.
Zh: 他一定是把它跟他喜欢破坏的*财产*搞混了。不过命运跟他开了个玩笑——你也喝醉了。喝了高度比尔森啤酒，醉得神志不清。你把坚硬的塑料板砰的一声砸到他脸上；然后继续把他打到不省人事了。

En: In the process the ledger sustains damage. The compartment within -- reserved for permeable documents -- is jammed shut. You stop your assault on the now unconscious Burke to open it, but are unable to do so. *The officer began to cry*, reports Leslie, who at this point is tending to Burke.
Zh: 在那个过程中，手册遭到了破坏。里面的隔间——为透水文件保留的——被卡死了。你不再殴打已经失去意识的伯克，想把它打开，但却打不开了。莱斯利报告说，*警官开始大哭*——当时他正在照看伯克。

En: *He came at us* -- *And at us* -- *I think he was trying to kill Burke-o*. While trying to kill Burke-o, you slowly come around. The permeables' compartment is open. You've smashed it open on poor Burke'o's kneecaps. The good news is, Burke can't walk anymore.
Zh: *他朝我们冲过来*——*是冲我们来的*——*我想他是想杀了伯克*。在试图杀死伯克的时候，你慢慢清醒过来。透水隔间打开了。你用伯克那可怜的膝盖骨把它砸开了。好消息是，伯克再也走不了路了。

En: Kill them. They broke it.
Zh: 杀了他们。他们把它弄坏了。

En: Can't get out of his apartment. An invalid. With Burke to tend to, Leslie cuts back on the indecent exposure. Maybe he flashes his genitals to Burke, who knows, but both drunks are off the street. The complaints stop, the unsolvable case is solved.
Zh: 不能走出他的公寓。一个残疾人。因为需要照看伯克，莱斯利减少了裸露下体的频率。也许他会朝伯克裸露下体，谁知道呢，不过两个醉鬼都从街上消失了。投诉停止了，*无解案件*解决了。

En: Which is also why the officer responsible (narrowly) escapes a disciplinary hearing. The end. Do you want to read another one?
Zh: 这也是负责的警官（勉强）逃过纪律听证会的原因。结束。你还想再看一个吗？

En: ...is *not* what you end up doing. You squeeze the plastic to slide it open, but nothing happens. Then you bend it some. Then crack it. The god damn thing is stuck.
Zh: ……最后你并*没有*做到。你挤压塑料板，想把它弄开，不过什么也没发生。之后是弯折它。然后是砸它。但是这破玩意就是卡得纹丝不动。

En: Just relax. The two sides of the board are slightly misaligned. Like a drawer that's come off the slides. All you need to do is *bend* the plastic on your knee, slowly...
Zh: 放松点。这个双面笔记板似乎有点错位了。就像脱离滑轨的抽屉一样。如果你用膝盖*弯曲*这个塑料板，慢慢的……

En: Hmm. The two sides of the board appear slightly misaligned. Like a drawer that's come off the slides. If you *bend* the plastic on your knee, slowly...
Zh: 嗯。这个双面笔记板似乎有点错位了。就像脱离滑轨的抽屉一样。如果你用膝盖*弯曲*这个塑料板，慢慢的……

En: Permeables. It's not hidden per se, the compartment is made for permeable materials that would get damaged if something happened to it.
Zh: 透水的。它本身不是隐蔽的，隔间是用来存放透水材料的，这样如果发生什么事，东西就能被破坏掉。

En: A hot flash of rage comes over you. For a moment there -- before it recedes -- you feel as though you might just squeeze a tear of anger out of your duct. Makes you wonder... why?
Zh: 一股怒火涌上你的心头。有那么一瞬间——在它消退之前——你觉得自己也许会从泪腺里挤出一滴愤怒的泪水。让你不禁感叹……为什么会这样？

En: It's unjust, that's why. You can't even *get* to that thing anymore!
Zh: 不公平，这就是为什么。你以后都可能*拿不到*那里的东西了！

En: The fucking drunks, it's their fault. You should've drunk the liquor out of that fucker's SKULL...
Zh: 该死的醉鬼，都是他们的错。你应该把那个混账脑袋里的液体全喝光……

En: The ledger quivers in your hand. As it shakes the pages rustle. This pathetic mess suddenly afraid of you for some reason.
Zh: 手册在你手中颤抖。页面也随着它的晃动发出了沙沙的响声。由于某种原因，这个可怜的小东西突然开始害怕你了。

En: ...the slides *snap* back into place. It should be possible to just... you know...
Zh: ……隔间*啪*的一声回到原位。应该可以……你知道的……

En: Slide the hidden drawer open.
Zh: 把隐藏抽屉打开。

En: Fuck no -- put the ledger away. [Leave.]
Zh: 他妈的，不要——把手册放在一边。[离开。]

En: Wait... somehow I don't want to.
Zh: 等等……不知道为什么，我不想这么做。

En: That's because you know where this leads to.
Zh: 那是因为你知道结果是什么。

En: What are you waiting for? Just...
Zh: 你还在等什么？只要……

En: That doesn't sound like it's gonna be *fun*.
Zh: 听起来好像并不会太*有趣*。

En: Without resistance -- or sound -- the two panels move against each other. The compartment is now open. Inside you see two ticket stubs.
Zh: 没有阻力——或者声音——两块嵌板互相移动起来。隔间打开了。你在里面看到两张票根。

En: Without resistance -- or sound -- the two panels move against each other. The compartment is now open. Inside you see two ticket stubs and a handmade post card.
Zh: 没有阻力——或者声音——两块嵌板互相移动起来。隔间打开了。你在里面看到两张票根和一张手工明信片。

En: Without resistance -- or sound -- the two panels move against each other. The compartment is now open.
Zh: 没有阻力——或者声音——两块嵌板互相移动起来。隔间打开了。

En: Pick up the ticket stubs.
Zh: 取出票根。

En: Pick up the card again -- now that you're outside.
Zh: 再次取出明信片——现在你在外面了。

En: Pick up the card again.
Zh: 再次取出明信片。

En: Pick up the card. (Proceed.)
Zh: 取出明信片。（继续。）

En: Fucking kill yourself you asshole.
Zh: 妈的，你个混账是想自杀吗。

En: Close the permeables' compartment.
Zh: 关上透水隔间。

En: The words just... *crossed* your mind somehow.
Zh: 不知道为什么，这些词语就这么……从你脑海里*一闪而过*。

En: Who were they for?
Zh: 这是给谁的？

En: Who do you think?
Zh: 你觉得是谁？

En: Blink.
Zh: 眨眨眼。

En: You see two ticket stubs.
Zh: 你看见两张票根。

En: You see two ticket stubs and a handmade card.
Zh: 你看见两张票根和一张手工明信片。

En: It's slightly -- ever so slightly -- difficult to breathe once you've done so. The drawer is locked, blue ink drips from the white pages in your hand.
Zh: 你这样做的时候，感觉到有那么一点点——很少的一点点——呼吸困难。抽屉锁上了，蓝色墨水从你手中的信息数据库上滴落下来。

En: Your hand shakes. You're flushed with adrenaline.
Zh: 你的手在颤抖。肾上腺素激增。

En: (Cause of failure: rent too high.)
Zh: （失败原因：租金过高。）

En: He must have it confused with the *property* he likes to damage. But the joke's on him -- the officer is also drunk. Way more drunk than Burke there, and let's be fair, you also have *party eyes*. You slam the hardened plastic board in his face, then proceed to beat him unconscious with it.
Zh: 他一定是把它跟自己喜欢破坏的*财产*搞混了。不过倒霉的是他——警官也喝醉了。比伯克喝的还醉，而且说句良心话，你也是一副*想狂欢的样子*。你把坚硬的塑料板砰的一声砸到他脸上，然后继续用它把伯克打到失去意识了。

En: He must have it confused with the *property* he likes to damage. But the joke's on him -- you're drunk out of your mind on Potent Pilsner. You slam the hardened plastic board in his face. Then you proceed to beat him unconscious with it.
Zh: 他一定是把它跟自己喜欢破坏的*财产*搞混了。不过倒霉的是他——高度比尔森啤酒让你的醉得神志不清。你把坚硬的塑料板砰的一声砸到他脸上，然后继续用它把伯克打到失去意识了。

En: Two octopuses are smiling, reaching their tentacles toward each other in the coloured pencil drawings. The tickets permit access to a zoo in Revachol East. The aquarium costs extra -- they let you go there too. You should go and kill yourself there.
Zh: 彩色铅笔画的是两只微笑的章鱼，朝彼此伸出了它们的触手。这张票是瑞瓦肖东部一家动物园的入场券。水族馆费用是额外的——这些里面已经包含了。你应该去那里自杀。

En: Two octopuses are smiling, reaching their tentacles toward each other in the coloured pencil drawings. The tickets permit access to a zoo in Revachol East. The aquarium costs extra -- these let you go there too.
Zh: 彩色铅笔画的是两只微笑的章鱼，朝彼此伸出了它们的触手。这张票是瑞瓦肖东部一家动物园的入场券。水族馆费用是额外的——这些里面已经包含了。

En: Thin wax paper has been glued to a piece of cardboard. Sounds like leaves rustling when you pick it up -- you see violet flowers, floral patterns, patches of glue.
Zh: 薄薄的蜡纸粘在一张硬纸板上。当你拾起它的时候，它发出了树叶一般的沙沙响声——你看见紫色的花朵，花朵的图案，还有胶水印。

En: Smell it first.
Zh: 先闻闻。

En: Open it.
Zh: 打开它。

En: Put it back.
Zh: 把它放回去。

En: Your fingers are sweaty. There is a sweat mark where they held the wax paper. The card lies next to the ticket stubs.
Zh: 你的手指出汗了。蜡纸上留下了汗渍。明信片就躺在票根的旁边。

En: It smells of chewing gum -- apricot flavoured.
Zh: 闻起来是口香糖的味道——杏子口味的。

En: A touch of cinnamon, the end of summer. You think the label says: 'Tutti Frutti'.
Zh: 一丝肉桂的味道，夏季的末尾。你觉得品牌名称是：‘百果蜜饯’。

En: Familiar handwriting lines the inside of the card -- looped, round letters in a woman's hand.
Zh: 熟悉的字迹排列在明信片内侧——环状的圆形字母出自一个女人之手。

En: A young woman in her twenties. There is care, effort, and a *smile*, you think -- although that is not something you can read from someone's handwriting.
Zh: 一个20多岁的年轻女子。这里有关心，努力和一个*微笑*，你想到——虽然从别人的笔迹里是无法辨认出来的。

En: Harry, it begins -- you're already reading. "I wanted to write you a letter, so you can read it when you wake up. Maybe it will make you happy."
Zh: “哈里，”它开始了——你已经开始阅读了。“我想给你写封信，这样你醒来就能看到了。也许它能让你感到开心。”

En: Throw it away. Please.
Zh: 把它扔掉。拜托。

En: But it will make me *happy*...
Zh: 但是它会让我感到*开心*……

En: No! A merciful wind blows in from the Bay of Revachol, dusting the ground at your feet and raising newspapers far away. You feel the card slipping into it... now that you're out! Let go...
Zh: 不！瑞瓦肖海湾吹来一阵悲悯的轻风，扫清你脚下的地面，把报纸高高卷起。你感觉明信片溜了进去……居然你已经走出来了！就随它去吧……

En: A merciful wind blows in from the Bay of Revachol, dusting the ground at your feet and raising newspapers far away. You feel the card slipping into it...
Zh: 瑞瓦肖海湾吹来一阵悲悯的轻风，扫清你脚下的地面，把报纸高高卷起。你感觉明信片溜了进去……

En: LET GO.
Zh: 随它去吧。

En: Frisson covers your entire body. A feeling of cold. A persistent chill.
Zh: 你浑身上下颤抖不已。一种冰冷的感觉。一股挥之不去的寒意。

En: Outside you hear the wind howl. A sudden gale blows in from Martinaise, flapping street signs and window blinds.
Zh: 你听见外面的风在呼号。突然，一阵大风从马丁内斯吹了进来，拍打着路标和百叶窗。

En: COME OUTSIDE.
Zh: 出来吧。

En: Keep reading.
Zh: 继续阅读。

En: Your hand shakes, holding the card. "Every morning when I step out and you're asleep behind me," it says. "I find a little piece of sadness in me. I carry it in my chest down Voyager Road..."
Zh: 你握住这张明信片，双手颤抖不已。“每天早晨，当我走出门外的时候，你还在我身后酣睡。”上面这样写着。“每当此时，都会有一阵悲伤涌上心头，伴我走向远航路……”

En: What was that?
Zh: 那是什么？

En: Throw the card in the wind.
Zh: 把明信片扔进风中。

En: Hold on to it. Read the card.
Zh: 握紧它。阅读明信片。

En: No! Don't throw away the baggie! There's dust on the drug baggie! It's not good but at least it's *some* kind of way to be together...
Zh: 不！不要扔掉这个垃圾袋！这个毒品袋上面有灰！虽然不太好，不过至少也是*某种*在一起的方式……

En: What's inside?
Zh: 里面有什么？

En: Two ticket stubs and a handmade postcard.
Zh: 两张票根和一张手工明信片。

En: Throw the card in the wind.
Zh: 把明信片扔进风中。

En: Just like that the wind picks it from your fingers. Cold, they let go, the wax paper rustles, a whisper light and low... Then a sudden gust raises the postcard to the drizzle grey sky above. Away from you...
Zh: 就这样，风从你的指尖将它拾起。寒冷，它们放手了，蜡纸在沙沙作响，一声低柔的耳语……接着，突如其来的一阵狂风将明信片卷到了天空灰蒙蒙的细雨中。远离你身边……

En: A small piece of paper dances above Martinaise -- above slow waves crashing the shore; and the war torn houses and the new *batiments nouveau* alike; above you, looking up to the grey sky with your hair greasy and wet..
Zh: 一张小纸片在马丁内斯上空舞蹈——在它之下，轻柔的海浪拍打着海岸；被战争摧毁的房屋和新*建筑*几乎没什么区别；你抬起头，仰望那片灰蒙蒙的天空，头发油腻又潮湿……

En: The wind carries the keepsake away from you -- to the southwest, the pale violet dot disappears.
Zh: 风带着纪念品吹向远方——前往西南方，直到那个淡紫色的小点彻底消失不见。

En: What?
Zh: 什么？

En: I'll take this outside. [Leave.]
Zh: 我把它拿到外面去。[离开。]

En: Far away and outside... the reeds hiss with desperation. Step out.
Zh: 外面某个遥远的地方……芦苇丛发出了绝望的嘶嘶声。走出去吧。

En: And above the distant streets and the 8/81 -- even above the old fish market and the church. Its material existence is lost. This great city will pocket it for you. For your own sake -- forget about it.
Zh: 在遥远的街道和8/81高速公路之上——甚至高过了旧鱼市和教堂。它的物质存在消失了。为了你，这座伟大的城市会把它装进口袋。为了你自己好——忘记它。

En: Synapses can be rerouted. The mind takes a new shape.
Zh: 神经突触可以改变方向。心灵有了新的形状。

En: Not for yourself -- for the people of Revachol.
Zh: 不是为了你自己——而是为了瑞瓦肖人民。

En: For the eighty thousand under your jurisdiction.
Zh: 为了你管辖的那8000人。

En: Under Precinct 41, where typewriters fire long into the night, and officers walk the great steps and the bridge. Long after the card has landed in the cold shore waters, its writing dissolved. Material disintegrated.
Zh: 在41分局下面，打字机一直工作到深夜，警官们走在大台阶和桥面上。很久之后，明信片降落在冰冷的海水里，上面的字迹融化了。材料解体了。

En: Every step I take, it grows. By the time I reach the fuel station it has filled me entirely. I step on the light rail and look back, sparks fall from the bow collector. I know it will be like this until late afternoon, when I get off the 42 -- and walk back to you...
Zh: “悲伤随我迈出的每一步逐渐增长。当走到加油站时，悲伤已经充溢了我的脑海。我踏上轻轨，回头张望，看到集电弓上火花散落。我知道此情此景会一直持续至下午时分，直到我走下42路——回到你的身旁……”

En: You, you... Every step I take will get lighter. It almost makes me run! Sometimes I do. I can't believe I met you. I can't believe the happiness I feel with you. You have a vast, vast soul and I will always, always, always come back to it.
Zh: “你啊你。我每走一步，脚步就越发轻盈。几乎让我想要开始奔跑。我有时会那样做……不敢相信我会遇见你，不敢相信跟你在一起的那些快乐。你拥有如此浩瀚又博大的灵魂，而我也将一直，永远回到它身边。”

En: Kisses, kisses, kisses. You feel the air sucked out of your lungs and the blood sucked out of your head. Everything around you gets dark. Small white dots appear...
Zh: “吻你，吻你，吻你。”你感觉肺里的空气全都被抽干，大脑里的血液也被全部吸了出来。你周围的一切变得黑暗起来。一些小白点开始浮现……

En: Fall sideways.
Zh: 朝一旁倒下。

En: Your hands shake holding on to it. "Every morning, when I step out and you're asleep behind me," it says. "I find a little piece of sadness in me. I carry it in my chest down Voyager road..."
Zh: 你用颤抖的手紧握着它。“每天早晨我走出去的时候，你还在我身后酣睡，”它说到。“我感到一阵小小的悲伤。我把它攥在胸口，沿着远航路一路走……”

En: Sparks fall like snow from the bow collector. A street-car distancing...
Zh: 火花像白雪一样从弓形集电器上飘落下来。一辆有轨电車渐行渐远……

En: No! No! Hold on.
Zh: 不！不！抓紧了。

En: Hold on.
Zh: 抓紧了。

En: To what? There's nothing...
Zh: 抓紧什么？什么也没有……

En: Detective, is everything alright?
Zh: “警探，你还好吧？”

En: Fine. He concedes. "For inter-district cooperation -- let's try one more time. What do you want to name it?"
Zh: “好吧。”他退了一步。“为了跨区合作——我们再试一次。你想让我给它起什么名字？”

En: Huh? Oh, yes. The name, good. What is it?
Zh: “哈？哦，没错。你说名字，挺好的。它怎么了？”

En: THE HANGED MAN.
Zh: “吊人。”

En: SHIT ON A STICK.
Zh: “棍上一坨屎。”

En: THE FURIES ARE AT HOME IN THE MIRROR.
Zh: “自家镜子里的复仇女神。”

En: THE SETTING SUN.
Zh: “落日。”

En: I don't have another name.
Zh: “我想不出其他名字。”

En: Actually, I don't have one.
Zh: “其实吧，我也不知道。”

En: Ha! Yes. He isn't actually laughing. "I have to tell you officer, I don't appreciate *ironic* titles. Other officers will have to use this as reference. If it's 'IDIOT', or 'COCK FINGER'..."
Zh: “哈！没错。”他其实并没有笑。“我必须告诉你，警官，我不太欣赏*讽刺性*的标题。其他警官会用它做参考的。如果叫‘白痴’，或者‘牛皮精’……”

En: Okay. Okay. He nods. "It's a good name, but it has *one* problem -- this case has nothing to do with the setting sun. At all. It has nothing to do with that. So..."
Zh: “好吧，好吧。”他点点头。“这是个好名字，不过有*一个*问题——这起案件跟落日无关。一点也没有。完全毫无干系。所以……”

En: Furies. Yes. Well. It's obvious he doesn't like it. "I don't know. I have to be honest -- I'm not experiencing the *internal strife* that refers to. And also..." He furrows his brow.
Zh: “复仇女神。是啊，好吧。”他显然不喜欢。“我不知道。我必须说实话——我不想提到它的时候引起*内讧*。所以……”他皱起眉头。

En: Could we make it less *poetic* somehow? Just a normal case name, you know? Think -- what would that be? A good *normal* name?
Zh: “我们就不能让它别这么*诗意*吗？你知道的，只要起个普通的案件名就好。想一想——会是什么呢？一个又好又*普通*的名字？”

En: Great! That's great. That's actually what *I* was thinking too -- THE HANGED MAN. Good, strong name. We have a very good name for the case now.
Zh: “太棒了！真的很棒。其实*我*想的也是这个——吊人。很好，很有力量的一个名字。现在我们这起案件有个好名字了。”

En: He flips the pages of his notebook. "I'm going to start calling it THE HANGED MAN. It's good we sorted this out."
Zh: 他翻阅着笔记本的页面。“我要叫它吊人了。很高兴我们能解决这个问题。”

En: Oh... Well, then maybe I can suggest one?
Zh: “哦……那么，要不我提个建议？”

En: Let me guess -- the HANGED MAN?
Zh: “让我猜猜——吊人？”

En: No.
Zh: “不行。”

En: Go ahead.
Zh: “说吧。”

En: THE HANGED MAN.
Zh: “吊人。”

En: Okay then. He returns to his notes, abruptly. "The case doesn't need to have a name."
Zh: “那好吧。”他又忙乎自己的笔记去了，很突然。“这起案件不需要名字。”

En: ...something more concrete perhaps? Do you have something *concrete*? Mundane. *Usual*.
Zh: “……也许可以想个再具体一点的名字？你能想点*具体*的名字吗？平凡的。*普通*的。”

En: They're not going to *get* it. They're going to think: an idiot and a cock finger were on this case. So -- do you have something less *funny*?
Zh: “他们是*理解*不了的。他们会想：一个白痴和一个牛皮精在查案。所以——你有没有不那么*有趣*的名字？”

En: This would also be a good time to finally *name* the case -- if only Lieutenant Kitsuragi were here so you could coordinate. No use naming it without him... maybe at a later time you can come back to this?
Zh: 这本该是为本案*命名*的绝佳时机——倘若曷城警督在场，你就可以和他相互协调，达成一致。可他不在，你就算命名也无济于事……也许你可以稍后再回顾一下这件事？

En: Kim, I'm sorry. Let's name this case again, okay?
Zh: “金，抱歉。我们再给案子起个名字好吗？”

En: Kim, I have a name for this case.
Zh: “金，我给这个案子想了个名字。”

En: Oh! That's actually what *I* was thinking -- THE HANGED MAN. He nods. "I like that. A good, strong name. We have a very good name for the case now."
Zh: “哦！其实*我*想的也是这个——吊人。很好，很有力量的一个名字。现在我们这起案件有个好名字了。”

En: He really likes that name.
Zh: 他真心喜欢那个名字。

En: Solid name.
Zh: “很可靠的名字。”

En: That's idiotic, (mockingly,) "*the hanged man*. Way too simple."
Zh: “这也太蠢了吧，”（嘲弄到，）“*吊人*。太简单了。”

En: I'm not *feeling* it.
Zh: “我没什么*感觉*。”

En: Thank you, the lieutenant nods. "I think so too. Good and *normal*."
Zh: “谢谢，”警督点点头。“我也这么觉得。又好又*普通*。”

En: Are you sure? I think it's pretty serviceable...
Zh: “你确定吗？我觉得挺好用的……”

En: Okay, let's keep it.
Zh: “好吧，那就保留这个。”

En: Yeah, not feeling it.
Zh: “是啊，没感觉。”

En: What was in there?
Zh: 里面有什么？

En: Look at the ledger again.
Zh: 再看看手册。

En: Put the ledger away. [Leave.]
Zh: 把手册放在一边。[离开。]

En: The Ledger of (Oblivion Induced) Mental Health is just as shabby as the Damaged Ledger was: a bunch of sodden papers sags from the clipboard in your hand.
Zh: 精神健康手册（包括失忆）跟损坏的手册一样破旧：一堆湿乎乎的纸张从你手里的笔记板上掉落了下来。

En: No. You should not have thought back at it... Now some of it is on your mind again.
Zh: 不。你不应该回想起它……现在有些东西又出现在你的脑海里了。

En: Put it away!
Zh: 把它拿走！

En: It says: everyone look at me and my *kitchen tissue* covered cop ledger! I don't *care*. My ledger is droopy and it smells like a urinal.
Zh: 它说：所有人都看看我，还有这本粘着厕纸的警察手册！我不*在乎*。我的手册无精打采的，闻起来有便池的味道。

En: It says: everyone look at me and my *kitchen tissue* covered cop ledger! I don't *care*. My ledger is droopy and  smells like a urinal. Not a new addition, though. So keep that *styling case* open, my friend...
Zh: 它说：所有人都看看我，还有这本粘着厕纸的警察手册！我不*在乎*。我的手册无精打采的，闻起来有便池的味道。不过，这也不是什么新的添加物。所以就让那个*造型箱*开着吧，我的朋友……

En: You had to lose because of *dark vibes*? What are you talking about?
Zh: “你必须为了*黑暗共鸣*丢掉它？你在想什么呢？”

En: It's going to take an effort to piece these case files together, but it can be done -- later, once you're done inspecting them up close.
Zh: 把这些案件拼凑起来需要费不少功夫——不过是可以做到的——等你仔细检查之后可以。

En: No matter. I'll just use the pencil...
Zh: 没关系。我用铅笔就好……

En: You start scratching the tasks you've completed onto the paper with a brash freehand uncannily similar to the rest of the letters. The wording comes easily, it's almost robotically simple; a language developed for mental rigour and simplicity:
Zh: 你开始划去纸上那些已经完成的任务，仓促的笔迹跟其余的字母惊人的相似。行文来的很容易，几乎像机器人一样容易；这是一种为严谨精神和简易方法开发的语言：

En: *Exactly* like the gum wrapper you found. The same brand of chewing gum...
Zh: 跟你找到的口香糖包装纸*非常*相似。同一个品牌的口香糖……

En: You stare at the card, willing your hand to move. It refuses. It's too much. Not again.
Zh: 你凝视着明信片，希望自己的手能移动。它拒绝了。这样太过了。不要再来一次。

En: You.
Zh: 你。

En: What can I do?
Zh: 我能做什么？

En: With this beauty -- *COMMIT TO PAPER*.
Zh: 用这个小美人——*记录下来*。

En: Play the tape.
Zh: 播放磁带。

En: Not so fast. Somewhere between the GRIH and Le Cycle, the good lieutenant steps out of his Kineema. A brisk, chill wind greets him. You get the feeling he should be here for this. Listen when he's here.
Zh: 先别。在GRIH和赛科尔之间的某个地方，警督从他的锐影里走了出来。一阵凛冽而冰冷的风向他发出了问候。你有种感觉，他此刻应该在这里。等他在场的时候再听吧。

En: Not so fast. The lieutenant is out there somewhere. Doing something. You get the feeling he should be here for this. Listen when he's here.
Zh: 先别。警督正在处理别的事。你有种感觉，他此刻应该在这里。等他在场的时候再听吧。

En: There's more going on here than we know.
Zh: 发生的事情远比我们知道的要多。

En: Bad news.
Zh: 坏消息。

En: The porta-reel is just what you needed -- the reels attach to the apparatus with a satisfying click. The tape is routed behind the magnetic reader.
Zh: 你正好需要音响——带盘贴合在设备上，发出一声令人满意的咔哒声。磁带在录放磁头背后转动起来。

En: Take out the tape.
Zh: 取出磁带。

En: As you take out the tape, the boombox tunes itself back to the cheery radio again, spewing out beats like it's a Friday night.
Zh: 你取出磁带，手提音响再次调回到欢快的电台，节拍喷涌而出，仿佛这是一个周五的夜晚。

En: The contrast feels chilly -- inappropriate even.
Zh: 这种对比感觉让人发冷——甚至有些不太合适。

En: I think he sounded all right. Just letting off some steam.
Zh: “我觉得听起来他好像还好。只是发泄一下。”

En: Yes, he says incredulously. "It was just *locker room talk*." The lieutenant looks at the tape. You are familiar with this look now -- it's his *look of suspicion*.
Zh: “是的，”他说到，口气有些怀疑。“这只是所谓的*更衣室闲谈*。”警督看着磁带。现在你对这个表情已经很熟悉了——这是他*怀疑的表情*。

En: Play the tape.
Zh: 播放磁带。

En: You push 'Commencer' and the tape starts spinning. Violent static and machine sounds fill the air....
Zh: 你按下“开始”，磁带开始旋转。强烈的电流声和机器的声响弥漫在空气中……

En: ...this isn't Revachol, a man's voice says. "This is a fucking village, I can almost see the elephants." Another loud screech. Some kind of machinery.
Zh: “……这不是瑞瓦肖，”一个男人的声音说到。“这是个该死的村子，我几乎都能看见大象了。”又一个响亮而刺耳的声音。像是某种机械的声音。

En: The harbour. The lieutenant takes out his notebook. "That's the sound of a Kvalsund crane."
Zh: “港口。”警督掏出了笔记本。“那是库瓦桑德起重机发出的声音。”

En: More static. "When this shit is done I'm gonna tear that place up, Soldier of the Apocalypse style -- kill shit, dogs and chickens too..."
Zh: 更多电流声。“等这些破事弄完之后，我要把这个地方撕成碎片，末日战士的风格——杀掉那群狗屎，野狗还有野鸡……”

En: I'll fucking do them all in, rape that disco cunt on the counter. You know, the dancer whore upstairs. Do it Co Hoi-style.
Zh: “我他妈的要把他们全部做掉，在吧台上强暴那个迪斯科骚货。你知道的，就是楼上那个婊子。科霍依的风格。”

En: What was that at the very end? Silence?
Zh: “最后是什么？沉默？”

En: Who's this *Korty*?
Zh: “这个*科迪*是谁？”

En: Okay then. What now? (Remove the tape.)
Zh: “那好吧。现在怎么办？”（取出磁带。）

En: End of recording.
Zh: “录音结束了。”

En: Korty could be short for Kortenaer. One of the other mercenaries, the one he was talking to -- probably the mountain at the harbour gates. Mr. *Right To Work*.
Zh: “科迪可能是科顿艾尔的简称。正在与他对话的人是另一个雇佣兵——很可能就是码头门口的那个大块头。*工作权*先生。”

En: Korty could be short for 'Kortenaer'. One of the other mercenaries, the one he was talking to. The third one must be relaying the signal.
Zh: “科迪可能是*科顿艾尔*的简称。另外那个雇佣兵，就是跟他对话的那个。第三个人肯定是在转播信号。”

En: One of the other mercenaries I think. The one he was talking to. A friend of his?
Zh: “我想可能是其他雇佣兵。就是跟他对话的那个。他的一个朋友？”

En: What's Co Hoi?
Zh: “科霍依是什么？”

En: A village on the Samaran isola, in South Safre. Graad committed war crimes there -- the kind of thing he talks about.
Zh: “萨马拉洲上的一个村庄，在南萨弗里。格拉德在那里犯下了战争罪行——就是他说的那些事。”

En:
You think he was there?	“你觉得他去过那里？”
0x0100004500012ABC	Alternate1	INVENTORY / LELY TAPE	Kim Kitsuragi	Who knows? Maybe the tattoos would have an answer. We would need to know the story of this man's service."
Zh: “谁知道。也许纹身会有答案。我们需要知道这个男人的服役历史。”

En: Who knows? Maybe the tattoos would have an answer. Or maybe Co Hoi is just merc talk for atrocity. Slaughter.
Zh: “谁知道。也许纹身会有答案。又或者科霍依只是雇佣兵口中的暴行。大屠杀。”

En: No. The tattoos did not say so. They said he served on Semenine. I think Co Hoi is merc talk for atrocity.
Zh: “不。纹身不是这么说的。它们说他在塞美尼群岛服役。我觉得科霍依在雇佣兵口中是指暴行。”

En: A symbol of *Soldier of the Apocalypse*-style conduct in a civil environment.
Zh: 这种符号象征着在文明环境中做出的*末日战士*行径。

En: I agree. He also sounded inebriated.
Zh: “我同意。听起来他像是喝醉了。”

En: But he *did* say he's going to do it. You can't edit words into someone's mouth.
Zh: “不过他*确实*说过自己要这么做。你是不能这些话编辑进别人嘴里的。”

En: Still... The lieutenant looks at the tape. You are familiar with this look now -- it's his *look of suspicion*.
Zh: “不过……”警督看着磁带。现在你对这个表情已经很熟悉了——这是他*怀疑的表情*。

En: Indeed, but... The lieutenant looks at the tape. You are familiar with this look now -- it's his *look of suspicion*.
Zh: “确实，不过……”警督看着磁带。现在你对这个表情已经很熟悉了——这是他*怀疑的表情*。

En: The South Safre conflict is an ongoing proxy war between Graad and Safre. It has been hot for 12 years with the atrocities piling on. Mostly committed by the Graad.
Zh: 南萨弗里冲突是格拉德和萨弗里之间正在进行的一场代理战争。这场战争已经热火朝天地打了12年，暴行也在不断累积。主要是格拉德犯下的。

En: I think we've got a few more questions for Klaasje, don't you? He looks around. "This seems to contradict her testimony. At least to some degree."
Zh: “我想我们还有些问题要问卡拉洁，不是吗？”他看了看周围。“这跟她的证词好像是矛盾的。至少在某种程度上是的。”

En: The Kvalsund crane in the harbour. Its screaming is distinctly apocalyptic.
Zh: 港口的库瓦桑德起重机。它的尖叫声确实像末日一样。

En: He's right.
Zh: 他说得对。

En: What do you think?
Zh: “你怎么想的？”

En: It seemed authentic enough. Probably recorded off their shortwave, then *edited* to seem more incriminating. He sounded like he was on patrol around the harbour walls.
Zh: “似乎已经足够可信了。可能是用短波电台录下来的，然后*编辑*成更加有罪的样子。听起来他像是在港口围墙附近巡逻。”

En: Somewhere between the GRIH and Le Cycle, the good lieutenant steps out of his Kineema. A brisk, chill wind greets him. You get the feeling he should have been here for this. Listen again when he's here.
Zh: 在GRIH和赛科尔之间的某个地方，警督从他的锐影里走了出来。一阵凛冽而冰冷的风向他发出了问候。你有种感觉，他此刻应该在这里。

En: Never did get that taste out of my mouth... A click -- then silence. The rest of the tape is empty.
Zh: “那种味道现在还残留在我的嘴里……”一阵咔哒声——然后是沉默。磁带的其余部分是空白的。

En: Gonna rent a room, Korty, a real nice one... This part is unintelligible. "I don't give a shit, I'm fucking done. I'm done mentally."
Zh: “我要租个房间，科迪，租个好的……”这部分有些莫名其妙。“我不在乎，我他妈受够了。我心里受不了了。”

En: You stare at the Great Doorgunner Megamix. If only you had a boombox, you would be able to play Titus' tape.
Zh: 你盯着这盘伟大的门枪手精选集。如果你有一台手提音响，就能播放提图斯的磁带了。

En: The tape feels ominous... Upon it, the dead speak. Respect the tape!
Zh: 这盘磁带有种不祥的感觉……里面有死人在说话。要尊重这盘磁带！

En: The porta-reel is just what you needed -- the reels attach to the apparatus with a satisfying click. The tape is routed behind the magnetic reader.
Zh: 你需要的就是手提音响——卷轴附着在设备上，发出了一声令人满足的咔哒声。磁带在磁头背后旋转。

En: Don't play the tape. [Put porta-reel away.]
Zh: 不要播放磁带。[把手提音响放在一边。]

En: The lieutenant is out there somewhere. Doing something. You get the feeling he should have been here for this. Listen again when he's here.
Zh: 警督在那里的某个地方。做自己的事。你有种感觉，他此刻应该在这里。

En: The lieutenant presses the button marked 'Arrêter' on your porta-reel; the tape stops spinning.
Zh: 警督按下音响上标有*停止*的按钮；磁带停止了旋转。

En: Good idea. There was plenty of information here to go by.
Zh: 好主意。这里有大量信息可以作为参考。

En: Billie is a unisex name. Could be the deceased *or* his family member?
Zh: 比莉是一个男女通用的名字。会是死者的名字吗，*或者*是他家庭成员的？

En: We already decided to let the station handle this case, so there's no need for us to call the library.
Zh: “我们已经决定把这件事交给警局去处理了，所以没必要打给图书馆。

En: You already decided to let the station handle the case, so there's no need to call the library.
Zh: 你已经决定让警局去处理这件事情，所以没必要打给图书馆。

En: We should first decide what to do with the body, before taking any other actions. Let's take another look at the body and talk things through.
Zh: “我们应该先决定怎么处理这具尸体，然后再采取其他行动。我们再看一眼那具尸体，充分讨论一下。”

En: You should first decide what to do with the corpse, before taking any further actions. Talk to the lieutenant about it.
Zh: 在采取进一步行动之前，你应该先决定如何处理那具尸体。和警督谈谈这件事吧。

En: Good, we've examined the library card -- that should be enough for our colleagues. Now we can call the station from my Kineema and hand over the case.
Zh: “很好，我们已经检查过借书证——对我们的同事而言，这就够了。现在可以用我的锐影呼叫警察局，把这个案子交给他们。”

En: You put the library card away.
Zh: 你把借书证放在一边。

En: Something tells you you should give them a call.
Zh: 有什么东西告诉你，你应该给他们打个电话。

En: The library card is folded into two and still slightly wet to the touch. The front side reads: "Central Jamrock Public Library Card. Issued to Billie Méjean, expires July '53."
Zh: 这张借书证被对折了起来，摸起来还有点潮湿。正面写着：“加姆洛克公共图书馆借书证。签发给比莉·梅让，截至53年7月。”

En: Look inside.
Zh: 看看里面。

En: Look at the backside.
Zh: 看看背面。

En: [Put it away.]
Zh: [把它放在一边。]

En: If lost, please return the card to the library. Dial 005-02-55-211 or visit us at Meroe Street 78, Jamrock. Business hours: 09.00 to 18.00.
Zh: “如有遗失，请将借书证归还给图书馆。拨打005-02-55-211或者前往加姆洛克，麦罗埃街街78号。开放时间：上午9时至下午18时。”

En: Whoever owns this card is an avid reader: you find a list of books written in blue pencil: 'Radiothriller', 'Stand a Little Less Between Me and the Sun'. The last one in the list is 'The Glinting Curve', by M. Thibault. A library stamp indicates that the book has been returned.
Zh: 不管这张借书证的主人是谁，肯定是位热心读者：你发现一张用蓝色铅笔书写的借书清单：“惊魂无线电”，“靠近我和太阳近一些”。清单上的最后一个是M·蒂博的“闪耀曲线”。图书馆印章显示这本书已经归还了。

En: Most of these titles seem to be in the sci-fi genre. Some thrillers too.
Zh: 大部分标题似乎都是科幻类的。还有一些惊悚类。

En: Good. He takes a note. "We should give them a call from my Kineema, see if we can learn anything about Billie Méjean."
Zh: “很好。”他记下笔记。“我们应该去锐影那边打个电话，看看能不能了解一下比莉·梅让的信息。”

En: For a more detailed view of the Map, go to your JOURNAL and select the MAP tab.
Zh: 要想查看更详细的地图，打开日志界面，然后点击地图分页。

En: The worn map features the patchwork grid of the streets of Martinaise, with directions to appropriately *tourist-y* locations. Year '48 resides on the upper-right corner.
Zh: 这张破损的地图上绘制着马丁内斯街道的拼接网格，还有适当的*旅游*景点指示。右上角印着48年的字样。

En: [Put the map away.]
Zh: [把地图放在一边。]

En: Trace a path through the grid.
Zh: 在网格中追踪一条道路。

En: Your finger moves through the various streets, across Rue de Saint-Ghislaine and Rue Saint-Cispare, over Saint-Brune and Martinaise North...
Zh: 你的手指在一条条街道上穿行，穿过圣吉斯林路和圣西斯帕路，越过圣布伦和马丁内斯北部……

En: ...finally coming to a halt on the spot where you are currently standing, although the map gives no such indication itself.
Zh: ……最终停在了你现在站立的位置，虽然地图本身并没有给出这样的提示。

En: It's just a racist mug, what's there to *read* here? Not much.
Zh: 这只是一个种族主义马克杯，有什么好*阅读*的？不多。

En: There's quite a lot to read into here, actually. Look at all that content.
Zh: 其实这里隐藏着很多东西。看看那些内容。

En: Got it. [Leave]
Zh: 明白了。[离开]

En: Oh, boy. Here we go. What are you going to say about a broken, tossed away mug that *you* dug out of the garbage?
Zh: 天啦。我们开始吧。对于一个*你*从垃圾堆里翻出来的被扔掉的坏马克杯，你有什么想说的？

En: I'm going to push this into the face of every merchant I find and tell them: this is *your* inane ideology.
Zh: 我要把它推到所有能找到的商人脸上，然后告诉他们：这是*你的*愚蠢的意识形态。

En: The mug didn't belong in the trash. It was just a funny mug. Can't anyone laugh anymore?
Zh: 这个马克杯不属于垃圾堆。它只是个有趣的马克杯。难道没人笑的出来了吗？

En: Understood. You're *that* kind of detective. Why don't you get back to your case?
Zh: 理解。你是*那种*警探。为什么不回去继续查你的案子呢？

En: So again, you dug it out of the trash *why*? It wasn't going to offend anyone there. Why don't you just get back to your case?
Zh: 又来了，你*为什么*要把它从垃圾堆里翻出来？在那里它不会冒犯到任何人。你为什么不回去继续查案呢？

En: The mug will be useful. By denouncing it I can earn *political* capital to mask my bad ass hustling (ie, fraud and embezzlement).
Zh: 这个马克杯能派上用场的。通过谴责它，我可以赢得*政治*资本，用卑鄙的勾当（比如说，欺诈和贪污）来掩饰自己。

En: This mug is an example of prejudice. I'm going to use it as an example of what *not* to do.
Zh: 这个马克杯是歧视的案例。我要把它当做*不该*怎么做的范例。

En: If you want to earn some change by guilting people, go for it. But if you want to earn real dough, finish the case and start getting *paid* again.
Zh: 如果你想通过让别人产生内疚来换点零钱，那就去吧。不过如果你想赢得真正的金钱，那就破获这起案件，重新开始拿*工资*。

En: I want to get into that mug talk again.
Zh: 我想再谈谈这只马克杯的事。

En: But it will not happen. The mug talk is exhausted. It is time to return to work.
Zh: 但是这种事不会发生的。关于马克杯的对话已经结束了。是时候回去工作了。

En: But it was in the trash. Why not just call it out when you see it? Or do some volunteering work? Just finish your case, detective.
Zh: 但是它在垃圾箱里。为什么看见的时候你没有喊出来呢？或者做点自愿者的工作？还是去查你的案子吧，警探。

En: Got it. [Leave]
Zh: 明白了。[离开]

En: The man's a prosecutor's nightmare. Solving a murder counts for nothing if all the evidence gets thrown out in court over police misconduct.
Zh: 这个人是检察官的噩梦。如果所有证据都因为警察的不当行为而被法庭抛弃，那么破获一起凶杀案将变得一文不值。

En: I'm not sure I'm happy with this, but maybe the story will turn it around.
Zh: 我不确定自己会不会为此感到高兴，不过也许故事会扭转局势呢。

En: Your partner. Kim will be there for me.
Zh: 你的搭档。金会站在我身边。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Will *you* be there for *him*, though, when push comes to shove? Time will tell. In the meantime, let's get on with the story...
Zh: 不过，面临紧要关头的时候，*你*又会不会站在*他*身边呢？时间会证明一切。与此同时，我们还是继续看故事吧……

En: Uh, I'll think about it. Better get on with the story first.
Zh: 呃，我会考虑一下。最好还是先继续看故事吧。

En: Don't you sass me. Get on with the story.
Zh: 别跟我顶嘴。继续看故事吧。

En: I don't really like where this is going. (Close the book.)
Zh: 我真的不喜欢事情的进展。（合上书。）

En: Yep, it's pretty fucking evil.
Zh: 是啊，他妈的的确挺邪恶的。

En: Drugs. You know it's all about the drugs.
Zh: 毒品。你知道全都跟毒品有关。

En: The smooth taste of some ultra-high-proof booze.
Zh: 酒精含量超高的烈酒那爽滑的口感。

En: The sweet taste of a good cigarette, which I still need to find.
Zh: 一支上等香烟的香甜气息，还等着我去寻找呢。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: There are worse things in the world than the pursuit of chemical pleasures. But there are better things, too...
Zh: 这个世界上存在着比追求化学带来的快乐更糟糕的东西。不过也有更好的东西……

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: There are worse things in the world than the pursuit of chemical pleasures. But there are better things, too...
Zh: 这个世界上存在着比追求化学带来的快乐更糟糕的东西。不过也有更好的东西……

En: What's a *book* know about real pleasure? What's better than feeling your mesolimbic pathway flood your mind-crevices with *pure* dopamine?
Zh: 一本*书*懂什么真正的乐趣？除了感觉中脑缘系统通路用*纯正*的多巴胺填满你脑海中的裂缝，还会有什么更美妙的吗？

En: Why are you even still reading this garbage? Heed the bottle's call.
Zh: 你怎么还在看这些垃圾？留心酒瓶的召唤吧。

En: Why are you even still reading this garbage? Baby, get some of that sweet speed or spooky pyrholidon... it's the good stuff.
Zh: 你怎么还在看这些垃圾？宝贝，搞些甜美的思必得或者骇人的吡嚯浣酮……那些都是好东西。

En: Why are you even still reading this garbage? Rip some sweet cigs instead!
Zh: 你怎么还在看这些垃圾？还是去搞点甜蜜的香烟吧！

En: Love did them in.
Zh: 爱情毁了他。

En: Communism killed them.
Zh: 是康米主义杀了他们。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: What? That doesn't even make sense. There's never a straight answer with you, is there? You just get hooked on random stupid notions and then repeat them every chance you get.
Zh: 什么？这根本说不通啊。你一直都喜欢拐弯抹角，不是吗？你只会对一些随便的愚蠢观念着迷，每次一有机会就反反复复说个不停。

En: Stupid old horse glue.
Zh: “愚蠢的老马胶。”

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Dozens of pages scatter across the ground. The last fifth or so of the book seems to have been lost. It's possible that you could gather and reassemble the pages, but it would take way too long.
Zh: 数十页纸散落在地面上。这本书最后的五分之一似乎是不见了。你也许能把书页收集起来再重新组装好，不过那样会花不少时间。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: In your hand, you hold four-fifths of "Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity."
Zh: 你手中握着的是五部曲里的第四部：“迪克·马伦与错误身份”。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: In your hand, you hold "Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity." The brittle paperback feels fragile to the touch.
Zh: 你手中握着的是“迪克·马伦与错误身份”。这本平装书在你的触碰下显得很脆弱。

En: Examine the cover.
Zh: 查看封面。

En: Start reading.
Zh: 开始阅读。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: The cover features a pastiche of different scenes. In the foreground, a man in a dark overcoat clutches a pistol to his chest. Rising up behind him are two silhouettes wrapped in a passionate embrace.
Zh: 封面上有些不同的场景混合在了一起。前景是一个穿着黑色大衣的男人，握着一支枪紧紧贴在胸前。在他身后，两个身影正热情地拥抱在一起。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: The tagline reads: "Detective Dick Mullen must prove his innocence after an old friend is murdered -- by someone who looks just like Dick Mullen!" That seems to sum up the premise nicely.
Zh: 宣传语：“迪克·马伦警探必须证明自己的清白，查清一位老朋友被谋杀的真相——而且凶手看起来几乎跟迪克·马伦一模一样！”这段内容似乎很好地概括了前提。

En: Needless to say, it violates nearly every RCM regulation for a detective to investigate a murder in which he is a suspect.
Zh: 不用说，作为嫌疑人的警探参与凶杀案调查，几乎违反了RCM的每一条规则。

En: Are you really reading that, detective?
Zh: “你真的在看那个吗，警探？”

En: I really need to know who this Dick Mullen guy is.
Zh: “我真的需要知道这个叫迪克·马伦的家伙是谁。”

En: I'm looking for advice on being a real detective.
Zh: “我正在寻找如何成为一名真正警探的建议。”

En: I'm just skimming it.
Zh: “我只是略读一下。”

En: Well, you won't find it in *there*. Those books are much more about ravishing dames and gun play than real policework.
Zh: “是吗，那你在*那里面*是找不到的。这些书里说的更多的是迷人的女性还有枪战，而不是真正的警察工作。”

En: Good luck finding it. He's not much of a character, I think you'll find. Just a stand-in for the reader.
Zh: “那就祝你好运了。我想你迟早会发现——他并不太像是一个人物角色。只是读者的一个替身。”

En: The point is for the reader to feel like *they're* solving the crime along with Dick Mullen. A crude narrative convention, but no less effective for it.
Zh: 关键是要让读者感觉他们在*跟迪克·马伦一起破案*。这是一种粗糙的叙述惯例，不过效果并没有减弱。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: The story opens with a knock at the door. Detective Dick Mullen is greeted by an old friend, Charlie Spillane, who's come to Mullen to ask a favour on this dark and cold night...
Zh: 故事的开头是一个敲门声。警探迪克·马伦迎来了一位老朋友，查理·斯皮兰，在这样一个漆黑又阴冷的夜晚，他来请马伦帮一个忙……

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Spillane needs Mullen to drive him in from Vesper to a small town along the Insulindian coast. Despite his friend's apparent agitation, Mullen does as he's asked, then returns home where he passes out drunk, as he does most nights...
Zh: 斯皮兰需要马伦跟他一起开車从维斯珀出发，一路开到伊苏林迪海岸附近的一个小城镇。虽然他的朋友明显很焦虑，马伦还是按照他说的做了，之后便返回了家中，像大多数夜晚一样，喝得烂醉如泥，昏睡过去……

En: An extremely unprofessional and *hurtful* stereotype that's offensive to all upstanding officers of the law.
Zh: 这是一种极其不专业而且*有害*的成见，对于所有正直的执法人员来讲都是一种冒犯。

En: Look, I can't judge.
Zh: 看，我不能评判什么。

En: Keep reading.
Zh: 继续阅读。

En: I don't need to read this. I'm already living it. (Close the book.)
Zh: 我不用看这个，我已经过上这种生活了。（合上书。）

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Two days later, Mullen is arrested by the Vesper police and charged with the murder of Charlie Spillane. At his interrogation, Mullen learns that Charlie Spillane was shot in a bar in the very town Mullen dropped him off in, by a man matching *Mullen's* description...
Zh: 两天后，马伦被维斯珀警方因为谋杀查理·斯皮兰的罪名而逮捕。在审讯过程中，马伦了解到查理·斯皮兰在一间酒吧被射杀了，地点正好就是他下車的那个小城镇，而且嫌疑人的描述很符合*马伦*……

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Desperate to clear his name, Mullen manages to convince the Vesper police to release him for *three days* so that Mullen may solve his friend's murder and prove his innocence.
Zh: 渴望洗清自己罪名的马伦说服了维斯珀警方，给他*三天时间*来破获好友被杀的案子，证明自己的清白。

En: The cops release their prime murder suspect so he can find 'the real killer'? Are you shitting me?
Zh: “警察释放了凶杀案的主要嫌疑人，让他去找出‘真正的凶手’？妈的跟我开玩笑呢？”

En: No way Mullen did it. (Keep reading.)
Zh: 不可能是马伦干的。（继续阅读。）

En: They're not shitting you, detective. This is what the writers think passes for police procedure.
Zh: 他们没有跟你开玩笑，警探。这就是作者心中警察的办案程序。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Of *course* Mullen didn't do it. That's the whole premise of the book! Anyway, Mullen returns to the seaside bar where Spillane was murdered, and meets a *beautiful*, *mysterious* woman named Deanna Deneuve.
Zh: 马伦当然没有做了。这是整本书的大前提。总之，马伦回到了斯皮兰被谋杀的那间海滨酒吧，在那里遇到了一个*美丽*又*神秘*的女人，名字叫狄安娜·德诺芙。

En: Nice, a dame.
Zh: 很好，一位女士。

En: Now it's getting interesting. (Keep reading.)
Zh: 现在就有点意思了。（继续阅读。）

En: I feel like I've read this before. (Close the book.)
Zh: 我觉得之前好像看过。（合上书。）

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: And not just any dame -- she's truly one-in-a-million, a knock-out whose *mind* is as dangerous as her *curves*. But she's got a *secret*.
Zh: 并不是普通的女士哦——她是一个真正意义上的百里挑一，美得让人神魂颠倒的女人，她的*头脑*就像她的*身体曲线*一样危险。不过她有一个*秘密*。

En: Man, who doesn't?
Zh: 老兄，谁没有呢？

En: Sounds like my kind of woman.
Zh: 听起来是我喜欢的那种女人。

En: Are you s-s-sure about that? Something about her gives you a *bad* feeling. What kind of *secret* is she hiding?
Zh: 你确定吗？她身上有些东西让你有一种*不好*的感觉。她到底隐藏着什么*秘密*？

En: Your secrets are unknown even unto you, so does that make you a rich man or a beggar?
Zh: 你甚至都不知道自己的秘密，所以这会让你变成一个富翁还是乞丐呢？

En: You couldn't handle a woman like that. Look at yourself. You couldn't even handle your --
Zh: 你掌控不了那种女人的。看看你自己。你甚至连自己都——

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Deneuve reveals that she was Spillane's lover, and that he was mixed up with a local amphetamine smuggling operation. As soon as Mullen begins pulling at strings, the whole conspiracy begins to unravel...
Zh: 德诺芙透露自己是斯皮兰的情人，而且他卷入了当地的一起安非他命走私交易中。马伦一开始操纵局势，整个阴谋的面目也跟着揭露出来……

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Not only is the local police captain in on the amphetamine ring, so is the son of a powerful politician, and a strung-out art collector named Torvald. Each of whom has his own reasons for wanting Spillane dead...
Zh: 不仅本地警长参与了安非他命走私团伙，还有一位有权有势的政治家的儿子，和一个吸毒成瘾、名叫托瓦尔德的艺术收藏家。他们每一个人都有希望斯皮兰死去的理由……

En: Tell me about the corrupt police captain.
Zh: 跟我说说这个腐败的警长。

En: I want to hear about the politician's son.
Zh: 我想了解一下政治家的儿子。

En: What was that about an art collector?
Zh: 这个艺术收藏家是怎么回事？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: He would certainly have the motive and the means, but the captain walks with a noticeable *limp* from an old war injury. Is it possible he was able to conceal it long enough to commit the murder?
Zh: 他肯定有动机和手段，不过警长因为战争留下的旧伤，走路*一瘸一拐*的很明显。他可不可能把秘密隐瞒了足够久，从而犯下了这起凶杀案呢？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Outwardly, the old police captain is a real law-and-order crypto-fascist, a barrel-chested man who's beaten his share of suspects to pulp. But he's also dirty, and increasingly paranoid that someone's going to expose his role in the drug ring...
Zh: 从表面上看，老警长是一个真正严肃法纪的秘密法西斯主义者，一个胸肌发达、能把嫌疑人打成肉酱的男人。不过他也是个黑警，对有人要暴露他跟贩毒团伙有勾结这件事越来越偏执……

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: A typical privileged twat. In all likelihood, he's just in over his head. He does bear a personal grudge against Spillane though, a former prosecutor who nearly brought down his father's administration...
Zh: 一个典型的特权阶级蠢蛋。他十有八九都自身难保。不过他的确对斯皮兰——那个差点把他的父亲拉下马的前任检察官——怀恨在心……

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: The kid doesn't exactly have Dick Mullen's manly build, but he is the correct height, and while interrogating him at his home, Mullen *did* notice a certain overcoat that looks suspiciously like his own...
Zh: 这个孩子虽然没有迪克·马伦那副魁梧的身形，不过他的身高相当，在他家审讯他的时候，迪克·马伦*确实*注意到一件大衣，看起来跟自己那件可疑地相似……

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: That said, Torvald and Spillane have a long history, and while interrogating him, Mullen discovers that Torvald was once *involved* with Deanna Deneuve. Could it be that this is all over a sordid *love triangle*?
Zh: 即便如此，托瓦尔德跟斯皮兰之间有一段很长的历史，在审讯过程中，马伦发现托瓦尔德曾经跟狄安娜·德诺芙有过*关系*。难道这一切都是因为肮脏的*三角恋*吗？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Torvald the Art Collector is a strung-out mess. Frankly, it's hard to imagine him holding a pistol steady enough to actually hit someone, let alone plug them three times in the chest the way old Spillane got did...
Zh: 艺术收藏家托瓦尔德是一个吸毒成瘾的混蛋。说实话，很难想象他能稳稳地握住枪，真正射中某人，更不用说像老斯皮兰那样，在胸口连发三枪……

En: Okay, let's get on with the story.
Zh: 好吧，继续看故事。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: One evening, Deanna Deneuve comes to Mullen's hostel room in tears. The two of them drink half a bottle of vodka, and soon they're seeking comfort in each other's arms...
Zh: 一天晚上，狄安娜·德诺芙哭着来到马伦的旅社房间。两人一起喝了半瓶伏特加，很快就开始在彼此的臂弯中寻求安慰……

En: How does Mullen expect to solve the murder if he's sleeping with witnesses!
Zh: 如果马伦跟目击者上床的话，还怎么能破获凶杀案啊！

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: As the two lovers share a post-coital cigarette, Deanna Deneuve turns to Mullen and says, "By the way, Dick, there was something else I meant to tell you..."
Zh: 一对情人在分享事后烟的时候，狄安娜·德诺芙转向马伦，然后说到：“顺便说一句，迪克，我还有件事要告诉你……”

En: Well, *that* testimony won't be admissible any longer.
Zh: 呃，*那份*证词就不会再被采纳了。

En: Nice. Get it, Mullen.
Zh: 漂亮。上啊，马伦。

En: Yes, comfort and pleasure. The warmth of another human's touch, the burning taste of liquor on her full, sweet lips...
Zh: 是的，舒服又愉悦。他人碰触传来的温度，她甜美嘴唇上传来的烈酒的味道……

En: That's just Dick Mullen's *modus operandi*. He might bend the rules, but he closes cases no one else can.
Zh: 那只是迪克·马伦的*做派*。他可能会篡改规则，但是他能解决别人无法破获的案件。

En: I love you?
Zh: “我爱你？”

En: The name of the true killer?
Zh: 凶手的真名？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Whatever it is, Mullen never hears the words -- A blow to the base of his skull knocks him out cold instantly.
Zh: 不管是什么，马伦永远都听不到那些话了——他的脑袋挨了一击，人一下子就晕过去了。

En: Always aim for the centre of mass?
Zh: “总是瞄准惯性中心”？

En: Fuck.
Zh: 卧槽。

En: Can't trust a dame. (Shake your head.)
Zh: 不能相信女人。（摇摇头。）

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: When Mullen comes to, Deneuve is dead on the hostel bed next to him. To make matters worse, his clothes are covered with her blood!
Zh: 马伦苏醒之后，德诺芙就躺在旅社的床上，死在了他身边。更糟糕的是，他的衣服上沾满了她的血迹！

En: Double fuck!
Zh: 双倍的卧槽！

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Mullen trashes his blood-stained clothes and flees the hostel, knowing it's only a matter of hours before the cops discover Deneuve's body, if they haven't been tipped off already...
Zh: 马伦扔掉了染血的衣物，然后逃出了旅社，他知道警察发现德诺芙的尸体只是个时间问题，如果他们还没有收到消息的话……

En: Fleeing a crime scene, destroying evidence... Even if Detective Mullen *didn't* commit the murder, he should be facing *years* behind bars.
Zh: 逃离犯罪现场，销毁证据……就算迪克·马伦*没有*犯下这起凶杀案，他也应该面对*数年*的牢狱之灾了。

En: Dick Mullen won't be sent to the clink for the sake of some *legal niceties*!
Zh: 迪克·马伦不能因为某种*法律空子*就被送进牢房！

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: So who did it, detective? Who killed Charlie Spillane and Deanna Deneuve?
Zh: 那是谁做的，警探？谁杀了查理·斯皮兰和狄安娜·德诺芙？

En: The dirty police captain?
Zh: 腐败的警长？

En: The junkie art collector?
Zh: 瘾君子艺术收藏家？

En: The politician's twat son?
Zh: 政治家的蠢儿子？

En: Uh... Dick Mullen?
Zh: 呃……迪克·马伦？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Who can you trust in this wicked, messed-up world?
Zh: 在这个邪恶又混乱的世界，你还能相信谁呢？

En: Your partner. (Look at Kim.)
Zh: 你的搭档。（看着金。）

En: The lieutenant gives you a quizzical expression in return. You go back to the story...
Zh: 警督回给你一个狐疑的表情。你又回到故事中去了……

En: No one. Just your own two eyes.
Zh: 没有人。只有你自己的一双眼睛。

En: Unfortunately, *your* eyes might not be up to the task. Speaking of... should you maybe consider getting corrective lenses? Your nose is like two centimetres from the page.
Zh: 不幸的是，*你的*眼睛可能无法胜任这个任务。说到这里……也许你是不是该考虑弄一副矫正眼镜？你的鼻子几乎离书页只有2厘米了。

En: No way. I'm no binoclard.
Zh: 不可能。我不是四眼仔。

En: Whatever you say, Detective Hyperopia. Why don't you get back to the story?
Zh: 随你怎么说吧，近视警探。为什么不继续看故事呢？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: That's exactly what Dick Mullen says in "The Dame Who Did It."
Zh: 这正是迪克·马伦在“幕后黑手”里说过的话。

En: No one. You gotta go with your gut.
Zh: 没有人。你只能相信自己的直觉。

En: Speaking of, *your* gut's seen better days. It's a cesspool of burbling acids and noxious fumes, the kind of thing the government might declare a Zone of Irredeemable Catastrophe.
Zh: 说到这里，*你的*直觉已经今非昔比。它是一个粪坑，里面满是刺鼻的酸液和有毒的烟气，那种可能会被政府划为无可救药灾难区的东西。

En: Why is my gut in such bad shape?
Zh: 为什么我的直觉这么糟糕？

En: Could it be an underlying condition aggravated by years of greasy meals scarfed down between shifts? Or the litres upon litres of hard liquor guzzled on an empty stomach?
Zh: 也许是一种潜在状况，因为多年在轮班之间进食过多油腻食物导致了情况恶化？或者是空腹时大量引用烈酒引起的？

En: No point in worrying about it just now. Why don't you get back to the story?
Zh: 现在担心也毫无意义。你为什么不继续看故事呢？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: That's exactly what Dick Mullen says in "Dauntless Dick and the Fatal Appointment."
Zh: 这正是迪克·马伦在“无畏迪克与致命约会”里说过的话。

En: [Put the book away.]
Zh: 把书放在一边。

En: What's the matter, detective? The lieutenant turns to you with a start.
Zh: “出什么事了，警探？”警督突然转向了你。

En: I don't know who's writing this shit, but I get the feeling they aren't experts on homicide investigations.
Zh: “我不知道这种破烂是谁写的，不过我有种感觉，他们肯定不是凶杀案调查的专家。”

En: The lieutenant shrugs, resigned to the idea that his profession will rarely, if ever, be accurately represented in art and literature.
Zh: 警官耸耸肩，认为在描绘自己本职工作的艺术和文学作品之中——如果存在的话——恐怕很少能做到准确的表述。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: The heat is on! If Dick Mullen can't solve both murders before the cops catch up to him, he's going away for life...
Zh: 情况危急！如果迪克·马伦不能在警察抓住他之前破获两起凶杀案，他就会离开人世了……

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: You begin furiously flipping through pages. Even as you know these books follow a series of well-worn tropes, you find yourself completely engrossed. You're turning pages so fast you don't even notice the ancient spine coming unglued...
Zh: 你开始疯狂地翻阅书页。虽然明知这种书都是遵循一套陈腐的套路，你还是深深沉迷其中。你翻书的速度是如此之快，甚至都没留意古老的书脊正在逐渐脱胶……

En: Gah! Now I'll never know!
Zh: “靠！现在我永远都不会知道了！”

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Can you solve the case before the cops close in?
Zh: 你能在警察到来之前破案吗？

En: Wait -- I've got some questions first.
Zh: 等等——首先我有些问题。

En: I've figured it out.
Zh: 我已经搞明白了。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: What is it, detective?
Zh: 是什么，警探？

En: Why does everyone close to Dick Mullen wind up dead?
Zh: 为什么每个接近迪克·马伦的人最后都死了？

En: Why did Dick Mullen become a detective in the first place?
Zh: 为什么迪克·马伦一开始要成为一个警探呢？

En: Why bother solving crimes when the world is so evil?
Zh: 这个世界如此邪恶，何必还要费神去破案呢？

En: I don't have any more questions. I've figured it all out.
Zh: 我没有别的问题了。我已经全部弄清楚了。

En: When was the last time *you* ravished a dame, detective? Besides your crusty old sock, that is.
Zh: *你*最后一次跟女的上床是什么时候？除了你那只硬邦邦的破袜子。

En: That's probably for the best. Those books aren't exactly famed for their tight plotting.
Zh: “这样可能最好吧。那些书并不是以情节紧凑而出名的。”

En: It's much more about the dark and deadly atmossssphere.
Zh: 更多是关于黑暗和致命的氛围。

En: But also extremely *accurate*, in your case.
Zh: 不过像你这种情况，也还蛮*精确*的。

En: Hey, I'm trying at least.
Zh: 喂，至少我在努力尝试啊。

En: Are you, though?
Zh: 你有吗？

En: STOP. Don't say it!
Zh: 住嘴。别说！

En: Don't say what?
Zh: 别说什么？

En: Nothing. Forget it. Go back to your story.
Zh: 没什么。忘了吧。继续看你的故事去。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: It's a dangerous line of work, but somebody has to do it. That's why Dick Mullen never lets anyone get *too* close...
Zh: 这是一项危险的工作，不过总得有人做吧。这就是迪克·马伦永远也不会让任何人*太过*靠近的原因……

En: What's the matter? Afraid you've been hacking up your friends' bodies in the night?
Zh: 有什么事吗？害怕半夜把你的朋友碎尸万段？

En: Have you gone over everyone? Is anybody missing?
Zh: 你仔细检查过所有人了吗？有人不见了吗？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Dick Mullen was made to crack skulls and solve cases. It's who he is. He could no more stop being a detective than a tiger could cease to be a predator in the night.
Zh: 迪克·马伦生来就是打破坏人脑袋和破获案件的。他就是这种人。他无法不做警探，就像老虎无法停止夜间捕食一样。

En: But why is he like that?
Zh: 不过他为什么会这样呢？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: There was never a time when he wasn't a detective. He was born a detective.
Zh: 他从来就没有不做警探的时候。他生来就是个警探。

En: Was I *not* born to be a detective?
Zh: 我难道*不该*生来就是警探吗？

En: From nowhere, you hear the screech of sneakers on a waxed floor, and you feel the burn of rope against your hands. Are these figments of some other life?
Zh: 不知道从什么地方，你听见运动鞋在打过蜡的地板上摩擦的声音，你感觉到绳子在手中燃烧。这些难道是另一种生活的臆想吗？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: For a moment, you cease to read the story on the page and see the book for what it is, a collection of brittle, cheaply printed pages, held together by glue made from the hooves of horses...
Zh: 有那么一会儿，你不再阅读书中的故事，而是看到了书的本来面目，一个易碎而廉价、用马蹄制成的胶水黏在一起的印刷纸集合体……

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: You won't find the answers you're looking for here, in other words.
Zh: 换句话说，你无法找到正在寻找的答案。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Is it really so evil, detective?
Zh: 它真的如此邪恶吗，警探？

En: Okay, maybe not. There are parts worth saving.
Zh: 好吧，也许没有。有些部分还是值得挽救的。

En: Unfathomably evil. Death is infinitely preferable to this torment.
Zh: 深不可测的邪恶。死亡都比这种折磨更好一些。

En: Good, evil, it makes no difference. These are the end times we're living in.
Zh: 美好，邪恶，没有区别。这是我们生活的最后时刻。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Could be. Who knows? Only one way to find out.
Zh: 也许吧。谁知道呢？只有一种办法能查清。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Have you been paying attention *at all*? It wasn't Mullen! It. Was. An. Imposter. Now go on, find out who *really* did it.
Zh: 难道你*根本*没注意到吗？那不是马伦！他是一个冒名顶替的骗子。现在继续吧，找出*到底*是谁做的。

En: Finish the book.
Zh: 看完这本书。

En: You know what? I don't even care. (Close the book.)
Zh: 你知道吗？我根本不在乎。（合上书。）

En: Too bad, detective. If it's any consolation, the resolution is almost never very satisfying...
Zh: “太可惜了，警探。如果能算是安慰的话，结局基本从来都不能让人满意……”

En: And on that note, perhaps we should get back to making sense of our own case?
Zh: “说到这一点，也许我们应该继续调查我们自己的案件了？”

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Why? There's truly no point. You'll never know who did it, because those pages have literally been scattered to the wind.
Zh: 为什么？真的毫无意义。你永远也不会知道是谁做的，因为那些书页已经散落在风中了，字面意义上的。

En: I just want to relive the experience.
Zh: 我只是想重温这段经历。

En: You're right. I should just quit. (Close the book.)
Zh: 你说得对。我应该退出。（合上书。）

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: If you insist. But again, it's really pointless...
Zh: 如果你坚持的话。不过再说一次，这样真的毫无意义……

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: And you'll never know what happens! Because the rest of the book is gone.
Zh: 而且你永远也无法知道发生了什么！因为剩下的书页已经不在了。

En: I can surmise, can't I? If Mullen goes to jail there wouldn't be like fifty more of these books.
Zh: 我可以猜测，不是吗？如果马伦进监狱的话，那就不太可能会有50本这样的书了。

En: Guess that's it, then. (Close the book.)
Zh: 我想那就这样吧。（合上书。）

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: What makes you think this isn't *the final Dick Mullen case*?
Zh: 你为什么会觉得这不是*迪克·马伦最后的案子*呢？

En: Isn't there already a book called "Dick Mullen and the Final Case" or something?
Zh: 不是已经有一本叫“迪克·马伦与最后的案件”之类的书吗？

En: Okay, whatever, this is getting ridiculous. (Close the book.)
Zh: 好吧，无所谓了，这真是太荒谬了。（合上书。）

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: How do you know this isn't the *final* final case?
Zh: 你怎么知道这不是*最后*的最后的案件呢？

En: This could go on forever. End it now.
Zh: 这样就没完没了了。现在就结束吧。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: That's right. You never will. But then, isn't that how it is in real life, detective?
Zh: 没错。你永远也不会知道的。不过，现实生活不也是这样吗，警探？

En: Last year, more than 71% of murders in Revachol went unsolved. In Revachol West, that number was closer to 85%.
Zh: 去年，瑞瓦肖大约有70%的凶杀案没有解决。而在瑞瓦肖西部，这个数字接近85%。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Yes, blame a dead horse for your fat, clumsy fingers.
Zh: 是啊，你的手指肥胖又笨拙，别白费口舌了。

En: You try to grab the pages as they come loose but your fingers aren't quick enough... they're gone.
Zh: 书页松动的时候，你试着抓住它们，但是你的手指不够迅速……它们跑了。

En: Don't forget, it could have been the *delirium tremens*.
Zh: 别忘了，那可能是*震颤性谵妄*。

En: Ah, yes. The problem with mass market paperbacks... They're not made to last...
Zh: “啊，是的。大众市场平装书的问题……并没有讲究持久性……”

En: Perhaps this is a sign that you should get back to solving your own murder, eh, detective?
Zh: “也许这是一个信号，代表你应该继续回到自己的案件调查上，怎么样，警探？”

En: You're no tiger, though, Harry. You're a man. It's your curse to have to choose.
Zh: 不过你也不是什么老虎，哈里。你是个人。选择是你的诅咒。

En: You're no tiger, though, detective. You're a man. It's your curse to have to choose.
Zh: 不过你也不是什么老虎，哈里。你是个人。选择是你的诅咒。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Like what?
Zh: 比如说？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Then why are you still here?
Zh: 那你为什么还在这里？

En: Because I'm too chickenshit to do anything about it.
Zh: 因为我太渺小了，什么也做不到。

En: Because I don't want to let Kim down.
Zh: 因为我不想让金失望。

En: I... don't know.
Zh: 我……不知道。

En: Because I need to solve this case. Not for anyone else's sake. For me.
Zh: 因为我需要解决这起案件。不是为了别人。而是为了我自己。

En: Friends and family.
Zh: 好友和家人。

En: The taste of a good cigarette.
Zh: 好香烟的味道。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Do you even *have* those?
Zh: 你真的*拥有*那些吗？

En: I've got Kim.
Zh: 我有金。

En: I might. I just need to find them.
Zh: 可能吧。我只需要把他们找到。

En: I did, but I lost them.
Zh: 我有过，不过后来失去了。

En: The pleasure of contemplating art!
Zh: 欣赏艺术的乐趣！

En: What's a *book* know about real pleasure? What's better than feeling your mesolimbic pathway flood your mind-crevices with *pure* dopamine?
Zh: 一本*书*懂什么真正的乐趣？除了感觉中脑缘系统通路用*纯正*的多巴胺填满你脑海中的裂缝，还会有什么更美妙的吗？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: You haven't been treating the lieutenant much like a friend, though, have you?
Zh: 不过，你并没有把警督当成朋友来对待，不是吗？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Just make sure you don't lose him. You'll not find another like him...
Zh: 只要别失去他就好。你再也找不到他这样的人了……

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Did you lose them? Or did they leave you? Do you even know the difference?
Zh: 你失去他们了？还是他们离开了你？你到底知不知道这里面的差别？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: There are worse things in the world than the pursuit of chemical pleasures. But there are better things, too...
Zh: 这个世界上存在着比追求化学带来的快乐更糟糕的东西。不过也有更好的东西……

En: Why are you even still reading this garbage? You've got smokes to find!
Zh: 你怎么还在看这些垃圾？你还要找烟抽啊！

En: What's a *book* know about real pleasure? What's better than feeling your mesolimbic pathway flood your mind-crevices with *pure* dopamine?
Zh: 一本*书*懂什么真正的乐趣？除了感觉中脑缘系统通路用*纯正*的多巴胺填满你脑海中的裂缝，还会有什么更美妙的吗？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: You don't know how you know, but you can feel this book *laughing* at you.
Zh: 你不知道自己为什么会知道，但是你能感觉到这本书在*嘲笑*你。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: *Art*? You think contemplating *art* is going to save you?
Zh: *艺术*？你觉得欣赏*艺术*能拯救你？

En: Yeah, I do. I'm the god damn art cop, after all.
Zh: 是的，没错。毕竟，我他妈可是个艺术警探。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Whatever you say, *art cop*. The book has stopped laughing, but you can feel it grinning smugly at you.
Zh: 随你怎么说了，*艺术警探*。这本书停止了嘲笑，但你能感觉到它还在沾沾自喜地咧嘴朝你笑呢。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: You know you look like a mad man, right? Giving an inanimate object the finger like that.
Zh: 你知道自己看起来像个疯子，是吧？居然朝一个没有生命的物体竖中指。

En: Hey, fuck you book! (Give the book the finger.)
Zh: 去你妈的，破书！（朝书竖起中指。）

En: What's the matter, detective?
Zh: “出什么事了，警探？”

En: This book is making fun of my sincerest beliefs.
Zh: “这本书在嘲笑我最虔诚的信念。”

En: Then perhaps you should stop reading it.
Zh: “那也许你就不要再看了。”

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: But you can't stop, can you? You've got to find out what happens.
Zh: 但是你停不下来，不是吗？你必须知道到底发生了什么。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
