En: You haven't been treating the lieutenant much like a friend, though, have you?
Zh: 不过，你并没有把警督当成朋友来对待，不是吗？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Just make sure you don't lose him. You'll not find another like him...
Zh: 只要别失去他就好。你再也找不到他这样的人了……

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: It sounds like you're in for a long, hard road, detective...
Zh: 听起来你的路途漫长又艰难，警探……

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: But then what does this book know? It's just a poorly made piece of pulp garbage, made to be consumed and discarded.
Zh: 不过这本书能知道什么呢？这只是一本制造粗糙的纸垃圾，天生就是被消费和抛弃的命。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: What are you trying to prove, exactly? And what will change if you *do* manage to prove it?
Zh: 你到底想证明什么？如果你*的确*成功了，又会有什么改变呢？

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: But then what does this book know? It's just a poorly made piece of pulp garbage, made to be consumed and discarded.
Zh: 不过这本书能知道什么呢？这只是一本制造粗糙的纸垃圾，天生就是被消费和抛弃的命。

En: Yes, yes. They're out there waiting for you, just over the next rise, up on Marvel Hill!
Zh: 是的，是的。他们就在那里等着你，就在下一个山坡，在马弗尔山上！

En: You haven't. But it's not too late. You share a bond that's thicker than blood.
Zh: 你没有。不过现在还不晚。你们之间的关系比血缘还要浓厚。

En: It's true in more ways than you know.
Zh: 这是真的，在你不知道的很多方面都是如此。

En: You haven't. But it's not too late. You share a bond that's thicker than blood.
Zh: 你没有。不过现在还不晚。你们之间的关系比血缘还要浓厚。

En: It's true in more ways than you know.
Zh: 这是真的，在你不知道的很多方面都是如此。

En: Okay, so Mullen didn't do it. (Keep reading.)
Zh: 好吧，所以不是马伦做的。（继续看。）

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: She's any man's kind of woman, according to this book.
Zh: 根据书上写的，她是所有男人喜欢的类型。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Secrets are the currency of human relations.
Zh: 秘密是人际关系的筹码。

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Now go on, find out who *really* did it.
Zh: 现在继续吧，找出*到底*是谁做的。

En: You *are* the murderer, after all...
Zh: 毕竟，你*是*个杀人犯……

Actor: Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity
En: Dozens of pages scatter across the floor. The last fifth or so of the book seems to have been lost. It's possible that you could gather and reassemble the pages, but it would take way too long.
Zh: 数十页纸散落在地面上。这本书最后的五分之一似乎是不见了。你有可能能把书页收集起来再重新组装好，不过那样会花不少时间。

En: Give it time. You'll fall off the wagon sooner or later.
Zh: 给它一点时间。你迟早会旧瘾复发的。

En: Modus: Mullen.
Someone has scribbled: "S, I can't believe the off-site copy is still here! The illiterate ginger kid keeps stealing stuff from the studio, so I had to hide it somewhere safe."
Zh: 有人潦草地写着：“S，我真的不敢相信，场外拷贝还在这里！那个小盲流一直在从我们工作室里偷东西，所以我必须把它藏到一个更安全的地方。”

En: You'll find the filament memory with the off-site copy in the frozen ice cream maker. Please take it home ASAP, it's important. I'd do it myself if I lived in a civilized place with a freezer. Take care, Sulisław.
Zh: “场外拷贝的纤维内存被我放在结冰的冰淇淋制造机里了。请尽快把它带回家，这很重要。如果我住在一个有冰箱的文明地方，我就自己带走了。要小心，苏里斯洛。”

En: Sulisław... wasn't he one of Miss Luukanen-Kilde's coworkers? He tilts his head. "I remember coming across this name when we were reading her personal log."
Zh: “苏里斯洛……他是不是卢凯伦-吉尔德小姐的同事？”警督歪着脑袋。“我记得咱们在她的个人日志里见过这个名字。”

En: Wasn't he one of Soona's former coworkers?
Zh: 他不是苏娜的一个前同事吗？

En: It's like the *production schedule* you found, only this one's an *off-site copy*.
Zh: 它很像你发现的*生产计划*，只不过这个是*场外拷贝*。

En: This is tangential at best, but the lieutenant's *detective instinct* is still active.
Zh: 这充其量只能算是偏题，不过警督的*警探直觉*还是很活跃的。

En: Does it say anything interesting? The lieutenant leans closer to read the crumpled note over your shoulder.
Zh: “有没有什么有意思的内容？”警督靠近了一些，从你的肩头探出脑袋，想看看这份起皱的记录。”

En: Remind me again -- what's a filament memory?
Zh: “再提醒我一次——纤维内存单元是什么？”

En: What's a filament memory?
Zh: 纤维内存单元是什么？

En: Read the note.
Zh: 阅读记录。

En: The note doesn't specify.
Zh: 这份记录说的不是很详细。

En: [Put the note away.]
Zh: [把便条收起来。]

En: It's part of a radiocomputer. When not in use, filaments are stored at temperatures below zero.
Zh: 它是无线电脑的一部分。没有使用的时候，纤维内存单元需要被储存在0度以下的环境中。

En: Who's the illiterate ginger kid?
Zh: “这个小盲流是谁？”

En: Who's the illiterate ginger kid?
Zh: 这个小盲流是谁？

En: I don't know... I assume it's somewhere close to the ice bear fridge.
Zh: “我不知道……我猜是在靠近冰熊冰箱的某个地方吧。”

En: The note is written with a blue pencil on a piece of lined office paper. The kitchen magnets have left spots on its surface.
Zh: 这份记录是用蓝色铅笔在一张横格办公纸上写下的。厨房磁贴在表面留下了斑点。

En: Someone has scribbled: "S, I can't believe the off-site copy is still here! The illiterate ginger kid keeps stealing stuff from the studio, so I had to hide it somewhere safe."
Zh: 有人潦草地写着：“S，我真的不敢相信，场外拷贝还在这里！那个小盲流一直在从我们工作室里偷东西，所以我必须把它藏到一个更安全的地方。”

En: You'll find the filament memory with the off-site copy in the frozen ice cream maker. Please take it home ASAP, it's important. I'd do it myself if I lived in a civilized place with a freezer. Take care, Sulisław.
Zh: “场外拷贝的纤维内存被我放在结冰的冰淇淋制造机里了。请尽快把它带回家，这很重要。如果我住在一个有冰箱的文明地方，我就自己带走了。要小心，苏里斯洛。”

En: Do you have any idea where the frozen ice cream maker could be?
Zh: “你知不知道那台结冰的冰淇淋制造机在哪？”

En: Where's the frozen ice cream maker?
Zh: 那个结冰的冰淇淋制造机在哪？

En: Didn't you see a *melting* ice cream maker right next to the breaker box?
Zh: 难道你没看见开关箱旁边有个*正在溶化*的冰淇淋制造机吗？

En: And you *did* unplug the black wire from said breaker box.
Zh: 而且，你*确实*把上述开关箱里的黑色电线拔掉了。

En: Didn't you see one right next to the breaker box?
Zh: 难道你没看见开关箱旁边就有一个吗？

En: Somewhere among the shadows in the cellar.
Zh: 在地下室的阴影之中。

En: It belongs inside a radiocomputer, storing its memory. It's like a tape -- you listen to disco tapes, right? It's like one of your disco tapes, only for a computer.
Zh: “这是无线电脑的内存。类似于磁带——你听过迪斯科的磁带吧？这和那些差不多，区别在于只能用在电脑上。”

En: Some local miscreant probably. There are tons of them running around in Martinaise, ready to stir up trouble. We usually dispatch our junior officers to deal with them.
Zh: “也许是当地的无赖吧。这种人在马丁内斯多了去了，随时准备惹麻烦。我们一般会派初级警员去对付他们。”

En: Really? The lieutenant looks at you, the corner of his mouth curved into a smug grin. "You don't have a single guess?"
Zh: “真的吗？”警督望着你，嘴角露出一丝得意洋洋的微笑。“你都不知道该从何处猜起吗？”

En: The damage is truly extraordinary. It's a miracle you don't shit yourself.
Zh: 伤害真的非同一般。你没大便失禁真是个奇迹。

En: You mean Cuno?
Zh: “你的意思是坤诺？”

En: No, no idea at all.
Zh: “没有，完全不知道。”

En: Nope. Drawing a blank here.
Zh: 没有。大脑一片空白。

En: I'm placing my bets on a certain red-haired miscreant we met before. He'd probably try to pawn it for speed, based on our encounter.
Zh: “我会把赌注押在之前我们见过的那个红发小无赖身上。根据我们之前的邂逅推测，他可能想过把它当掉换思必得。”

En: Oh, I'm sure that child would *love* to get his hands on a filament memory -- even if he doesn't know what to do with it. He'd probably try to pawn it for speed, based on our encounter.
Zh: “哦，我肯定那小子会*很愿意*把纤维内存搞到手的——即使他不知道该拿它怎么办。根据我们之前的邂逅推测，他可能想过把它当掉换思必得。”

En: No idea.
Zh: 不知道。

En: The note does not specify.
Zh: 这份记录说的不是很详细。

En: Really? You sure it isn't a certain redhaired little gremlin who refers to himself in the third person?
Zh: 真的吗？你确定不是某个喜欢用第三人称称呼自己的红发小鬼？

En: Are you serious? You sure you don't want to hazard a guess here, detective?
Zh: 你是认真的吗？你确定不想大胆猜测一下吗，警探？

En: Are you talking about Cuno?
Zh: 你是在说坤诺吗？

En: Oh, you mean Cuno?
Zh: 哦，你的意思是坤诺？

En: Ding, ding, ding! We have a winner!
Zh: 叮叮叮！获胜者诞生了！

En: I wonder who wrote that note?
Zh: “不知道这张便条是谁写的？”

En: Someone has moved the filament! But who?
Zh: “有人把内存取走了！是谁呢？”

En: Who wrote that note?
Zh: 这张便条是谁写的？

En: Looks like someone from that radiogame company upstairs. I'm a little surprised they just left their property lying here.
Zh: “看起来像是楼上无线游戏公司的某个人。我有点吃惊，他们把自己的资产就这么放着不管。”

En: Someone who owns a radiocomputer? My guess is as good as yours, officer.
Zh: “某个拥有无线电脑的人？我跟你猜测的差不多，警官。”

En: Which is to say -- not very good.
Zh: 也就是说——不是很好。

En: Guess we'll never know.
Zh: “看来我们永远也不会知道的了。”

En: The lieutenant nods.
Zh: 警督点点头。

En: Maybe they had to leave in a hurry.
Zh: “也许他们是匆忙间离开的。”

En: That's a plausible hypothesis.
Zh: “这是一种合理的假设。”

En: Maybe it's because of the Entity.
Zh: “也许是因为那个存在。”

En: According to the note, someone named 'Sulisław'.
Zh: 根据记录，是某个名叫‘苏里斯洛’的人。

En: Someone who owns a radiocomputer, one might assume.
Zh: 有人可能会推测是一个拥有无线电脑的人。

En: Thanks, smart ass.
Zh: 谢了，机灵鬼。

En: Makes sense.
Zh: 有道理。

En: Perhaps someone who belonged to the defunct radiocomputer company?
Zh: 也许是那个破产无线电脑游戏公司的人？

En: That makes sense.
Zh: 言之有理。

En: But why did they leave a filament lying in a freezer?
Zh: 但是他们为什么要把纤维内存放在冰箱里呢？

En: Sounds like a question for a detective, detective.
Zh: 听起来没毛病，是一个为警探准备的问题，警探。

En: It would be *oh so* fun to try that radiocomputer out for yourself.
Zh: 要是能亲自试试那台无线电脑，那该*多么*有趣啊。

En: It does, but who might that be?
Zh: 是的，不过可能是谁呢？

En: You're welcome.
Zh: 不客气。

En: That's implausible.
Zh: “那不太可能吧。”

En: The tome seems to get heavier with every passing minute.
Zh: 每过去一分钟，这本巨著似乎就变得更沉重了一分。

En: You close the book and find yourself staring at the familiar cover once more.
Zh: 你合上书，发现自己又盯上了那个熟悉的封面。

En: [Put the book away.]
Zh: [把书放在一边。]

En: Medicinal Purposes of the Pale. The cover of this heavy tome features a number of esoteric symbols.
Zh: “灰域的医学用途。”这本厚书的封面上面印着许多深奥难懂的符号。

En: Open the book.
Zh: 翻开书。

En: Flipping through the book you find a number of sections on the general benefits of the pale. A large pharmacopoeia makes up nearly half of the book.
Zh: 翻阅书页的时候，你发现有很多章节都在描述灰域的好处。整本书几乎有一半都被一张大药典占据了。

En: What's a pharmacopoeia?
Zh: 药典是什么？

En: I think I just want some general health advice.
Zh: 我想我需要的只是一般的健康建议。

En: It's all quackery, in other words.
Zh: 换句话说，这全都是江湖艺术。

En: You come across the following explanation: "While modern pharmacopoeia are continually updated by so-called 'experts' based on the results of 'clinical trials,' readers will find assembled here the timeless wisdom relied upon by generations of traditional Seolite medicine practitioners, Mesque mystics, and Iilmaraan folk doctors."
Zh: 你无意间发现如下说明：“当现代药典根据所谓‘专家’的‘临床试验’结果不断升级时，读者会发现这里汇集了代代传承的传统西奥医疗从业者、梅斯克神秘主义者以及伊尔玛民间医生的永恒智慧。”

En: It seems to contain descriptions of the medicinal properties of various ingredients that may be gathered from the pale, as well as instructions for producing a variety of herbal remedies.
Zh: 它似乎包含着从灰域收集到的各种成分的药用价值描述，还有生产各种草药的说明。

En: Anything about curing an apocalyptic hangover?
Zh: 有任何关于治愈末日宿醉的内容吗？

En: Is there anything in there about restoring lost memories?
Zh: 有任何关于恢复失去记忆的内容吗？

En: I just want something that will soothe the relentless torment of my existence.
Zh: 我只想要一些能够抚慰我的存在带来的无尽折磨的东西。

En: Finally, something to calm the angry spirits that have been plaguing you.
Zh: 总算有了，能够平息一直困扰你的愤怒情绪的东西。

En: What you're describing is booze. You don't need any herbs for that.
Zh: 你描述的东西是酒。那个是用不着任何草药的。

En: Have you taken any walks through the pale recently?
Zh: 你最近有没有在灰域里散步？

En: I don't even know what this 'pale' thing is, how could I have walked through it?
Zh: 我甚至都不知道这个‘灰域’是什么东西，又怎么会在那里散步呢？

En: Maybe?
Zh: 也许？

En: The book is frustratingly vague on this topic, but it seems that if you've walked through the pale, you would know it.
Zh: 这本书在这个话题上含糊地令人沮丧，不过看起来如果你在灰域里散过步的话，你就可能会知道。

En: Yes, twice a week.
Zh: 是啊，一周两次呢。

En: And on your walks, do you stop to meditate on the nature of your being?
Zh: 那你散步的时候，有没有停下来，冥想你存在的本质呢？

En: Totally. Every time.
Zh: 完全有。每次都有。

En: No, I don't.
Zh: 不，我没有。

En: You need to do that. It's important for opening up your agiogenous zones.
Zh: 你需要这么做。这对打开你的血管生成区很重要。

En: What are agiogenous zones?
Zh: 血管生成区是什么？

En: They're a series of psychic nodes located throughout your body. Unfortunately, the stresses of modern life often cause us to close off our agiogenous zones, which can result in all manner of physical and psychic ailments...
Zh: 它们是遍布你全身的一系列精神节点。不幸的是，现代生活的压力通常会导致我们的血管生成区关闭，这就可能导致各种各样的身体和精神疾病……

En: Regular meditation in the pale, however, can help to open up these zones, permitting the freeflow of psychic energy throughout the body.
Zh: 然而，在灰域里定期冥想，能够有助于开启这些区域，让心理能量能够在你的身体里自由流动。

En: Okay, that's enough. (Close the book.)
Zh: 好吧，差不多够了。（合上书。）

En: Good. And when you meditate, are you in the nude?
Zh: 很好。当你冥想的时候，是不是裸体呢？

En: What? No.
Zh: 什么？没有。

En: Oh, yeah. Buck naked.
Zh: 哦耶，一丝不挂。

En: Nudity is the most important part. Of the many ways modern society prevents our true selves from manifesting, clothing may be the most repressive...
Zh: 裸体是最重要的部分。在现代社会阻止我们展现真实自我的众多方式中，衣服可能是最压抑的……

En: You're doing everything as you should. Regular pale meditation has been shown to help keep your body lithe and strong, your mind alert and expansive...
Zh: 你已经做了所有该做的一切。在灰域里定期冥想能让你的身体保持轻盈而强壮的状态，你的心灵也会更加警醒和坦荡……

En: You probably can't even take off your pants, can you?
Zh: 你甚至连裤子都不敢脱，对吧？

En: I could, I just don't feel like it.
Zh: 我可以，我只是不想这么做。

En: I could, but Kim's here.
Zh: 我可以，不过金在这里。

En: Oh god, you're right. I *can't* take my pants off!
Zh: 天啦，你说的没错。我*不能*把裤子脱掉！

En: That feeling is the weight of society bearing down on you. Until you get out from under it, you will never feel true freedom.
Zh: 那种感觉就是社会施加在你身上的重量。除非你能从重压下走出来，不然永远也无法感受到真正的自由。

En: Your partner is even more repressed than you are. It would do you both a world of good to spend a little more time getting in touch with your bodies.
Zh: 你的搭档甚至比你还要压抑。多花点时间来接触你的身体，对你们双方都有好处。

En: There are a number of Seolite tonics that promise to improve your short-term memory, but nothing that speaks to your condition.
Zh: 有很多西奥补药承诺可以改善你的短期记忆，不过这些跟你的状况都不一样。

En: Enough of this.
Zh: 够了。

En: There's nothing in here that speaks to hangovers directly. However, while browsing through the various descriptions you become convinced that you could assemble something from the ingredients listed here. Want to give it a shot?
Zh: 这里没有任何跟宿醉直接相关的内容。不过，在浏览各类描述的时候，你确信自己可以从里面列出来的材料中组合出一些东西来。想不想试一试？

En: I don't have time for this.
Zh: 我没时间弄这个。

En: (Rub your hands together.) Alright, book, let's see what you got.
Zh: （摩擦双手。）好吧，书，看看你都有些什么。

En: First, you need to choose a base ingredient.
Zh: 首先，你需要选择一种基础材料。

En: Next, you'll need to combine the base ingredient with an appropriate vehicle.
Zh: 接下来，你需要将基础材料跟合适的媒介组合起来。

En: You've created a julep of mint. It won't help your hangover, but it does seem like it would be refreshing on a warm summer's day.
Zh: 你创造了一种薄荷糖浆。它不能帮助治愈你的宿醉，不过似乎能为炎热的夏日带来一丝清爽。

En: God only knows what to call this thing you've created, but drinking it would likely make your problems worse, not better.
Zh: 只有老天知道你刚刚创造的那个东西该叫什么，不过喝下去可能只会让你的问题变得更加严重，而不是变好。

En: This tastes so bad it can only be good for you. According to the pharmacopoeia, it should help you perform your *marital obligations*.
Zh: 味道实在是太糟糕了，肯定会对你有好处。根据药典记载，它应该能帮助你履行*婚姻义务*。

En: Ginger root.
Zh: 生姜根。

En: Common meadow rue.
Zh: 普通唐松草。

En: A sprig of fresh mint.
Zh: 一束新鲜薄荷。

En: Boil it with a tea made from birch bark.
Zh: 跟桦树皮茶一起煮。

En: Mix it with a dram of whiskey.
Zh: 跟微量的威士忌混合。

En: Mash it into a paste with some aloe vera extract.
Zh: 加入一些芦荟汁，将其捣成糊状。

En: Lastly, is there anything else you'd like to add?
Zh: 最后，还有什么要加的吗？

En: A few pomegranate seeds.
Zh: 再来一些石榴籽。

En: A spoonful of sugar.
Zh: 一匙糖。

En: Some wild ginseng root.
Zh: 一些野生人参根。

En: The ginger root will help with the nausea and the birch bark tea will help you flush out the toxins. The sugar just makes it taste better.
Zh: 生姜根能治疗恶心反胃，桦树皮茶可以帮助排出身体毒素。糖分的加入让它的味道变得更好了。

En: The ginger root will help with the nausea and the birch bark tea will help you flush out the toxins. The pomegranate seeds are a natural aphrodisiac, as a nice bonus.
Zh: 生姜根能治疗恶心反胃，桦树皮茶可以帮助排出身体毒素。石榴籽作为一种天然催情剂，是一种很不错的增益。

En: The ginger root will help with the nausea and the birch bark tea will help you flush out the toxins. The ginseng should give you more energy, as a nice bonus.
Zh: 生姜根能治疗恶心反胃，桦树皮茶可以帮助排出身体毒素。额外添加的人参能为你提供更多能量。

En: The mint and aloe vera make this poultice ideal for soothing inflammation and minor burns. It won't do anything for your hangover, though.
Zh: 薄荷和芦荟让这种膏药变成了舒缓炎症和治疗轻微烧伤的理想选择。不过对治疗宿醉没有太大作用。

En: The rue and aloe vera make this poultice ideal for alleviating minor injuries and inflammation. It won't do anything for your hangover, though.
Zh: 芸香和芦荟让这种膏药变成了舒缓小伤口和轻微烧伤的理想选择。不过对治疗宿醉没有太大作用。

En: The ginger and aloe vera make this poultice ideal for the treatment of arthritis. It won't do anything for your hangover, though.
Zh: 生姜和芦荟让这种膏药变成了治疗关节炎的理想选择。不过对治疗宿醉没有太大作用。

En: The combination of mint and birch bark make this tea relaxing and good for the digestion. It won't do much for your hangover, though.
Zh: 薄荷和桦树皮的组合让这种茶能够放松精神，利于消化。不过对治疗宿醉没有太大作用。

En: The tincture you've invented sounds intriguing, but its medicinal value is suspect, to say the least. Safe to say it won't cure your hangover.
Zh: 你发明的这种药町听起来很有趣，不过退一步说，它的药用价值很值得怀疑。可以肯定的是，它肯定无法治愈你的宿醉。

En: You've created a delicious tincture of whiskey and ginger. According to the pharmacopoeia, it should increase your energy and focus, though in your experience it's only done the opposite.
Zh: 你创造出了一种美味的威士忌生姜药町。根据药典记载，它应该能增加你的能量和专注力，不过以你的经验来看，它的效果完全是相反的。

En: The tincture you've invented sounds intriguing, but its medicinal value is suspect, to say the least. Safe to say it won't cure your hangover.
Zh: 你发明的这种药町听起来很有趣，不过退一步说，它的药用价值很值得怀疑。可以肯定的是，它肯定无法治愈你的宿醉。

En: You should drink it anyway.
Zh: 不管怎么说，你都应该喝下去。

En: Whoever wrote this never heard of the hair of the dog. Fuck this book. See if there's a bar open that'll still serve you.
Zh: 不管写这个的人是谁，肯定从来没有听说过以毒攻毒。去他妈的吧，这本破书。看看还有没有开着的酒吧，能招待你进去喝一杯。

En: The cool damp of the city has penetrated to your very marrow. Summer is a long way away.
Zh: 这座城市的阴冷潮湿已经渗入你的骨髓。夏天还离的很远呢。

En: If only you could find one of these right now and drink the shit out of it. The book said it's good for you, so it would be okay.
Zh: 只要你现在能找到一个，然后他妈的痛痛快快一饮而尽。这本书说这样对你有好处，所以肯定没问题的。

En: Would you like to try again?
Zh: 你想再试一次吗？

En: The very thought of this tea causes your muscles to relax and your mind to clear. You're more present and in control than you were a moment ago.
Zh: 一想到这种茶，你的肌肉就会放松，头脑也会变得清醒。你比之前变得更有主见，更有控制力了。

En: Be that as it may, it might have been useful to have such a thing for the trials you'll soon be facing. It's a shame you can't get your hands on these ingredients.
Zh: 尽管如此，在面对即将到来的宣判时，拥有这种东西可能会有所帮助。可惜的是，你没办法弄到这些材料。

En: Yeah, let's try again.
Zh: 是啊，我们再试一次。

En: No, this stuff is all a bunch of hooey, anyway. (Close the book.)
Zh: 不，反正这里面都是一些胡言乱语的玩意。（合上书。）

En: Yeah, let's try again.
Zh: 是啊，我们再试一次。

En: No, this stuff is all a bunch of hooey anyway. (Close the book.)
Zh: 不，反正这里面都是一些胡言乱语的玩意。（合上书。）

En: Would you like to try again?
Zh: 你想再试一次吗？

En: Very well. First, choose a base ingredient.
Zh: 很好。首先，选择一种基础材料。

En: You imagine yourself with a mug of this tea in a chair by the window. It's a beautiful image, but will it ever come true for you?
Zh: 你想象自己端着一杯茶，坐在床边的椅子里。这是一幅美丽的画面，不过它能在你身上实现吗？

En: Mmmm. (Savour the feeling a moment longer.)
Zh: 嗯。（再多体味一下这种感觉。）

En: Alright, time to get back to work. (Close the book.)
Zh: 好吧，该回去工作了。（合上书。）

En: A tingle rushes down your spine and you feel your toes uncurl. If only it could always be like this.
Zh: 一阵刺痛感顺着你的脊椎疾驰而下，你感觉自己的脚趾头伸展开来。如果一直能这样就好了。

En: You close the book and find yourself staring at the familiar cover once more.
Zh: 你合上书，发现自己又盯上了那个熟悉的封面。

En: Great, so where can I get all this stuff?
Zh: 很好，那我能在哪里弄到这些东西呢？

En: You close the book and find yourself staring at the familiar cover once more.
Zh: 你合上书，发现自己又盯上了那个熟悉的封面。

En: From the pale, of course. Or perhaps from some enterprising entroponaut. Where you would find one of those, though, the book does not say.
Zh: 当然是灰域啊。或者从某些有胆量的企业家那里。不过，这本书里没说你能在哪里找到那种人。

En: You close the book and find yourself staring at the familiar cover once more.
Zh: 你合上书，发现自己又盯上了那个熟悉的封面。

En: A note of caution, however. Be sure to limit your exposure to the pale to no more than an hour a day, and no more than three hours total per week.
Zh: 然而，需要注意一点。保证每天暴露在灰域中的时间不能超过1小时，每周总共不能超过3小时。

En: You glance over at the lieutenant, who is standing, as usual, with his hands neatly folded behind his back. What he's thinking about, if he's thinking about anything at all, is absolutely opaque to you.
Zh: 你朝警督的方向瞥了一眼，他像往常一样站着，双手整齐地叠放在背后。他脑子里想的东西——如果他真的在思考的话——对你来说绝对是不透明的。

En: At the Remote Viewers Division we're trained to keep our agiogenous zones maximally dilated.
Zh: 在遥视者部队，我们会接受一种训练，将我们的血管生成区保持在最大扩张状态。

En: Remote Viewers Division? What kind of woo-woo shit is that?
Zh: 遥视者部队？这又是什么鬼迷信？

En: Nor should you. Nudity is *shameful*. No officer of the law should ever be caught *sans pantalones*.
Zh: 你也不应该这样。裸体是*不体面*的。任何执法人员都不应该因为*不穿裤子*被抓。

En: Free your body! You long to feel the cool breeze tickle against your inner thighs and midriff.
Zh: 解放你的身体！你渴望凉爽的轻风在你的大腿内侧和上腹部挠痒痒。

En: What about your junk?
Zh: 那你的老二呢？

En: Well, yeah, on your junk, too.
Zh: 哦耶，老二也要。

En: Your genitalia are the site of your most important agiogenous zone. It's vitally important to aerate them in the pale.
Zh: 你的生殖器是最重要的血管生成区。把它暴露在灰域中是至关重要的。

En: This book contains serious ancient wisdom, not whacko conspiracy theories. If you don't respect it, it will never reveal its secrets.
Zh: 这本书里包含了严肃的远古智慧，而不是什么疯狂的阴谋论。如果你不尊重它的话，它永远也不会揭露自己的秘密。

En: Excellent.
Zh: 太好了。

En: Oh... then I do, very much so. I meditate *all the time*.
Zh: 哦……。那我有啊，是这样没错。我*一直*都在冥想。

En: That's just ridiculous. (Close the book.)
Zh: 这真是太荒谬了。（合上书。）

En: I want to try concoct one of those hangover-cures again.
Zh: 我想再试着调配一下治疗宿醉的药物。

En: This book is written by an angry humanitarian sciences doctor from the La Cherte University in Sur-La-Clef, a Dr. Benoit Mejean. He does not like Sur-La-Clef, La Cherte -- or in fact even the University he teaches in -- but what he likes even less is...
Zh: 本书作者是一位愤怒的人道主义科学院博士，来自苏拉菲榭赫蒂大学的贝努瓦·梅让。他既不喜欢苏拉菲，也不喜欢榭赫蒂——甚至连他任教的大学他都不喜欢——但他更不喜欢……

En: Read it.
Zh: 阅读。

En: Capitalism.
Zh: 资本主义。

En: Can't stand the shadow of it. This book promises to *really* get into everything that's wrong with capitalism -- to do so once and for all in an *exhaustive* and *final* manner.
Zh: 无法忍受它的阴影。这本书承诺将*真正*深入探讨资本主义存在的所有问题——以一种*详尽*和*终极*的方式，一劳永逸地做到这一点。

En: Why would I want to?
Zh: 我为什么要这么做？

En: Critical theorists the world over agree: once *everyone* knows *every last shortcoming* of capitalism, capitalism will end. You are but one of billions, and there are over seven billion things wrong with capitalism, but one has to start somewhere. And Benoit Mejean, PhD, wants to start with...
Zh: 全世界的批判理论学家都同意一点:一旦*每一个人*了解资本主义的*每一个缺点*，资本主义就会终结。你不过是数十亿人中的一个，而资本主义存在70亿个问题，不过总得有个起点。而贝努瓦·梅让博士，想要从以下这点开始……

En: It's capitalism, isn't it?
Zh: 是资本主义，对吗？

En: Enough. [Put the book away.]
Zh: 够了。[把书放在一边。]

En: Nope. [Put the book away.]
Zh: 不了。[把书放在一边。]

En: Will this book make me more communist?
Zh: “这本书会让我变得更加信仰康米主义吗？”

En: Will this book turn me into a commie?
Zh: 这本书会把我变成一个康米党员吗？

En: Sure. The more communist books you read the more communist your brain becomes. That's how communism works.
Zh: 当然。你阅读的康米主义书籍越多，你的大脑就会变得越来越康米主义。康米主义就是这么运作的。

En: Yes, it will make you a little more communist. Not a lot. It won't *unmake* whatever else you are but you will also become a tad bit more communist, yes.
Zh: 是的，它会让你变得更康米主义一些。不是很多，只有一点。它不会*摧毁*你其他的一切，不过你还是会变得更加康米主义一些，没错。

En: It will. The more communist books you read the more communist your brain becomes. That's how communism works.
Zh: 会的。你阅读的康米主义书籍越多，你的大脑就会变得越来越康米主义。康米主义就是这么运作的。

En: No it won't. I'm impervious to critical theory.
Zh: 不会的。我不会受到批判理论影响。

En: I don't think I want that.
Zh: 我不觉得自己想要那样。

En: Good. Here -- almost in the end, limping and bleeding -- is a good time to get into some communism.
Zh: 很好。现在——几乎是在最后，步履蹒跚，血流成河——是一个了解康米主义的好时机。

En: Then don't read it. The more communist books you read the more communist your brain becomes. That's how communism works.
Zh: 那就不要看。你阅读的康米主义书籍越多，你的大脑就会变得越来越康米主义。康米主义就是这么运作的。

En: Then you've found the right book. The more communist books you read the more communist your brain becomes. That's how communism works.
Zh: 那你找对书了。你阅读的康米主义书籍越多，你的大脑就会变得越来越康米主义。康米主义就是这么运作的。

En: You.
Zh: 你。

En: You feel an ominous tingling in your fingertips as they browse the pages...
Zh: 当他们浏览书页的时候，你的指尖传来一种不祥的刺激感……

En: You start reading it, but it's hard to really understand anything. It's all some sort of garbled word-wankery. After a while you give up and put the book down.
Zh: 你开始阅读，不过真正理解的东西并不多。里面全都是某些断章取义而又矫揉造作的词汇。过了一会儿，你放弃了，把书放在了一边。

En: Someone close to the victim might know.
Zh: 与死者亲近的人或许知情。

En: Someone who knows about history could tell you.
Zh: 某个了解历史的人也许能告诉你。

En: An intricate web of blue lines stretches across the torso of the hanged man, from the right shoulder to the solar plexus. Each time the lines intersect, a small white star is formed in their crossing. Hundreds of fading asterisks riddle his skin; their concentration is highest around his heart.
Zh: 一张错综复杂的蓝色线网覆盖了躯干。从右肩横跨至腹腔神经丛，每当它们相交时都会形成一颗白色的星星。他的皮肤上点缀着数百个褪了色的星号，尤其是心脏周围最为密集。

En: Who are you?
Zh: 你是谁？

En: [Put the photo away.]
Zh: [把照片放在一边。]

En: It still kind of looks like a map of the stars in the night sky, but... something's not right.
Zh: 它看起来还像是那种夜空中的星图，不过……有些不太对劲的地方。

En: It almost looks like the electronic patterns inside a radiocomputer or an old filament memory, but not quite.
Zh: 看起来几乎就像是无线电脑里的电子图案，或者是一个老旧的纤维内存单元，不过也不完全是。

En: The pattern still kind of has an ethnic feel to it, but nothing familiar...
Zh: 这个图案还有一种民族特有的感觉，不过一点也不熟悉……

En: It could be anything... religious symbols, a road map. It could even be purely decorative. Who knows?
Zh: 它可能是任何东西……宗教符号，一份公路路线图。甚至可能只是纯粹的装饰物。谁知道呢？

En: Gone.
Zh: 死者。

En: What's the meaning of this tattoo?
Zh: 这个纹身是什么意思？

En: For you to discover. You've gotten as far as you will without assistance.
Zh: 等你去发现。在没有帮助的情况下，你已经走到这么远了。

En: Exactly what I need!
Zh: 我需要的就是这个！

En: Mhm. This book will show you the score; get you oriented with those basic concepts you appear to be hazy on. The anthropomorphic bear will give you the *low-down* of your life...
Zh: 嗯。这本书向你展示了总谱；熟悉那些在你看来很模糊的基础概念。这只拟人化的熊会告诉你生活的*内幕*……

En: On what?
Zh: 关于什么的？

En: The alphabet.
Zh: 字母表。

En: Let's do this.
Zh: 我们开始吧。

En: No time.
Zh: 没时间。

En: Of course. Obviously you already *know* all this. It's the alphabet. Who doesn't know the alphabet?
Zh: 当然，你显然已经全都*了解*过了。这是字母表。谁会不知道字母表呢？

En: A is for Azimuth, B is for Boreas, C is for Cosign, D is for Diamat, E is for Ellips, F is for Flogiston, G is for Gamut.
Zh: A是方位，B是北风，C是联署，D是狄亚玛，E是椭圆，F是燃素，G是音域。

En: You. You don't know the alphabet. But never mind.
Zh: 你。你不知道字母表。但是没关系。

En: Mmhm.
Zh: 嗯。

En: Yeah!
Zh: 哦耶！

En: Nod stoically.
Zh: 坚忍地点点头。

En: That's it?
Zh: 就这些？

En: That's it. You know the alphabet now.
Zh: 就这些。现在你知道字母表了。

En: Kim, I know the alphabet now.
Zh: “金，现在我知道字母表了。”

En: Good. I also know the alphabet.
Zh: “很好。我也知道字母表。”

En: It is a very useful skill to have, he thinks. For all sorts of life-activities. Like reading, and...
Zh: 这是一种非常有用的技能，他想到。对于各种各样的生产活动来说都是。比如说看书，还有……

En: You hold in your hand the colourful primer. The title reads: "A Primer for Small Kids." There's a bear involved.
Zh: 你手中握着的是一本色彩鲜艳的识字课本。标题是：“幼儿识字入门”。上面有一只熊。

En: Flip through the pages.
Zh: 翻阅书页。

En: Every page has one word designating one letter of the alphabet, with a faded illustration -- most of them are scientific and cultural principles. It goes as follows...
Zh: 每一页都指定了字母表中的一个单词，还配有褪色的插图——其中大多数都是科学和文化原理。具体是这样的：

En: [Put the book away.]
Zh: [把书放在一边。]

En: H is for Homeboy, I is for Iikon, J is for Jura, K is for Kollaps, L is for Laudanum, M is for Myriad, N is for Nadir, O is for Oreole, P is for Perihelion.
Zh: H是老乡，I是圣像，J是侏罗纪，K是崩溃，L是鸦片酊，M是无数，N是最低点，O是黄鹂，P是最高点。

En: Q is for Quasar, R is for Rhododendron, S is for Sinus, T is for Tricoleur, U is for Ultra, V is for Vector, W is for Warheit, X is for Xylophone, Y is for Ystävä, Z is for Zenith.
Zh: Q是类星体，R是杜鹃花，S是窦，T是三色旗，U是极端主义者，V是矢量，W是真理，X是木琴，Y是朋友，Z是顶峰。

En: In what is called the IL -- the International Language, developed by scientists from Graad in the Twenties! Sinus means 'Sign', for example.
Zh: IL——即国际语，是格拉德科学家在二十年代的发明！举例来说，在这种语言中，Sinus是“Sign”（标志）的意思。

En: Even when I leave here (if I leave here alive) -- what's my next move?... Staging a lynching is a crime, even if I'm not accused of murder on top of that. Forever on the run? Not really my idea of the open road. \n\nMan, I was really looking forward to winning.
Zh: “就算是我要离开这里的时候（如果能活着离开）——下一步打算怎么走？……即使我没有被指控谋杀，伪造私刑也是一种犯罪。永远亡命天涯吗？这可不是我想要的阳关大道。\n\n天啊，我曾经真的很想赢。”

En: This was important to her -- when it was still hers.
Zh: 这对她很重要——在它仍然属于她的时候。

En: You have no idea what she means. These are personal notes, don't expect to understand all of it.
Zh: 你不知道她是什么意思。这些是个人笔记，不要指望能全部搞懂。

En: No, stop... He wouldn't do that.
Zh: 不，快停下……他不会那样做的。

En: No, not even the old you. No you would do that.
Zh: 不，甚至不是以前的你。你是绝对不会那样做的。

En: He truly does not believe you are. Perhaps he shouldn't be so trusting...
Zh: 他是真心相信您的。也许他不该这么轻信……

En: His trust is well placed. You aren't. You can *feel* it.
Zh: 他的信任是对的。你不会的。你能*感觉*到。

En: Something tells you you should be extra careful from now on.
Zh: 有什么东西告诉你，从现在开始，你必须加倍小心。

En: The head of a major Jamrock gang specializing in drug production and trafficking -- a very bad person.
Zh: “一个加姆洛克大黑帮头目，专门从事毒品生产和贩卖的——是个很坏的人。”

En: Could have been Titus. Then again... He pauses to think.
Zh: “可能是提图斯。不过……”他停下来想了想。

En: Kim, am I really a La Puta Madre agent?
Zh: “金，我真是马德雷教父的代理人吗？”

En: I just don't think she killed the mercenary. It's mostly a gut feeling, though, since we let her walk out without hearing her side of the story.
Zh: “我真的不觉得是她杀了那个雇佣兵。不过这基本只是一种直觉，毕竟我们没听过她那个版本的故事就把她放跑了。”

En: It looks like she might have been... framed? The lieutenant taps on the page.
Zh: 警督拍了拍页面。“看起来她也许是被……陷害了？”

En: No, I don't think you are. He looks you straight in the eye for a moment, then sighs. "Ask someone in your precinct if you want to be sure."
Zh: 他直直地盯了你一会儿，然后叹了口气。“不，我不觉得你是。如果你想确定的话，去问问你们分局的人吧。”

En: She was in plenty of trouble even without the murder charge. He looks down. "Still, it's a nasty business."
Zh: “就算没有凶杀指控，她也遇到了不少麻烦。”他低下头。“不过，这还是个不光彩的营生。”

En: Who *is* La Puta Madre?
Zh: “马德雷教父*是*谁？”

En: So she died for nothing?
Zh: “所以说她白死了？”

En: Either way, we need to keep an eye on him. Somewhere along the way, we might have been fed a lie or two...
Zh: “不管怎么说，我们都需要留心他。一路走来的某些地方，我们可能被灌输过一两个谎言……”

En: Seems plausible.
Zh: “似乎有点道理。”

En: I don't know...
Zh: “我不知道……”

En: Maybe she had an accomplice. Either way, we should go back to the Whirling, see if we can find out more about her time there.
Zh: “也许她有个同谋。不管怎么说，我们应该回到飞旋旅社，看看能不能在那里了解到她的更多信息。”

En: Maybe she had an accomplice. Either way, we need to keep an eye on her.
Zh: “也许她有个同谋。不管怎么说，我们都需要留心她一些。”

En: No, it's dirty.
Zh: “不，这样太卑鄙了。”

En: She killed the merc, then turned the gun on herself -- case closed. It's so tidy!
Zh: “她杀了雇佣兵，然后把枪口转向了自己——案子破了。井井有条！”

En: He's not 100% sure you're not indebted to this very, very bad person.
Zh: 他没有100%肯定你没有亏欠这个非常非常坏的人。

En: Kim's posture is unusually rigid -- he's used to this sort of thing, but he's not particularly happy about being used to it.
Zh: 金的姿态不同寻常地僵硬——他已经习惯了这种事，但是对于这种习惯并没有感到特别高兴。

En: Then who do you think killed the merc? (Conclude.)
Zh: “那你觉得是谁杀了那个雇佣兵？”（总结。）

En: Then who do you think killed Lely? (Conclude.)
Zh: “那你觉得是谁杀了莱利？”（总结。）

En: But no one heard the shot...
Zh: “但是没人听到枪声……”

En: Klaasje was the one who pointed the finger at Ruby. Perhaps she was trying to steer us away from herself? Or... He stops to think.
Zh: “指控露比的人是卡拉洁。也许她是想把我们的调查视线从她身上引开”或者……”他停下来想了想。

En: I wouldn't go so far as to say that -- we have other reasons to arrest her. He frowns.
Zh: “我还不至于这么说——我们有其他理由可以逮捕她。”

En: One thing is for certain: We have business back in the Whirling-in-Rags. Questions to ask. Let's be careful though -- the tip she gave us about the tribunal was... disturbing.
Zh: “可以确定的是：我们在褴褛飞旋还有事要办，还有问题要问。不过务必谨慎行动——她提到的宣判一事……令人不安。”

En: One thing is for certain: We have business back in the Whirling-in-Rags. Questions to ask. We should get to it.
Zh: “可以确定的是：我们在褴褛飞旋还有事要办，还有问题要问。我们应该立刻着手去办。”

En: Seems plausible.
Zh: “似乎有点道理。”

En: I don't know...
Zh: “我不知道……”

En: Besides, I'm not sure her life as a fugitive is going to be much better than with us. Especially if she has problems with the Madre.
Zh: 他皱起眉头。“再说了，我不确定她作为逃犯的生活会不会比被我们抓住好一些。特别是她跟马德雷之间有过节的时候。”

En: Besides, I'm not sure her life as a fugitive is going to be much better than with us.
Zh: 他皱起眉头。“再说了，我不确定她作为逃犯的生活会不会比跟我们在一起要更好一些。”

En: Either way, we should go back to the Whirling, see if we can find out more about her time there.
Zh: “不管怎么说，我们应该回到飞旋旅社，看看能不能在那里了解到她的更多信息。”

En: Either way, we need to keep an eye on her.
Zh: “不管怎么说，我们都要留心她。”

En: But no one heard the shot...
Zh: “但是没人听到枪声……”

En: If she didn't do it, then maybe it's good that we didn't catch her?
Zh: “如果不是她做的，那我们没抓她也许是好事？”

En: Maybe there's a Hardie boy we've yet to meet who acted as his accomplice? Either way, we need to keep an eye on him.
Zh: “我们之前遇见的哈迪兄弟里，也许有一个是他的同谋？不管怎么说，我们都需要留心他一点。”

En: I don't think she killed the mercenary. The lieutenant taps on the page. "It looks like she might have been... framed? Too bad we didn't really get to hear her side of the story."
Zh: “我不觉得是她杀了那个雇佣兵。”警督拍了拍页面。“看起来她也许是被……陷害了？可惜我们没能听到她那边的故事版本。”

En: I don't think she killed the mercenary. The lieutenant taps on the page. "It looks like she might have been... framed?"
Zh: “我不觉得是她杀了那个雇佣兵。”警督拍了拍页面。“看起来她也许是被……陷害了？”

En: Perhaps too confidently -- many phrases and even paragraphs have been crossed out, with tiny corrections scrawled above and in the margins.
Zh: 可能有点过于自信了——很多短句，甚至是整段整段的文字，都被划掉过，还在字里行间附有潦草的修正。

En: We could learn a lot from this.
Zh: “我们可以从中了解到很多东西。”

En: It's good she left in a hurry. We could learn a lot from this...
Zh: “还好她是匆忙离开的。我们可以从中了解到很多东西……”

En: Professionalism is his coping mechanism.
Zh: 专业是他的应对机制。

En: Staff issues, always tough on the leadership. You smell traces of betrayal.
Zh: 员工问题，对领导层来说总是很难处理的。你嗅到了背叛的痕迹。

En: She is referring to betraying her previous employer. Does this suggest she did it in self-defence?
Zh: 她提到了背叛前任雇主的事。这是否意味着她这么做是为了自卫？

En: Small wonder -- would you talk about La Puta Madre in your journal? You do see an 'M' though -- La Puta Madre? 'M' is mentioned on March 9th and March 15th.
Zh: 不足为奇——你会在日记里提马德雷教父吗？不过你的确看见一个‘M’——代表马德雷教父？在3月9号和15号都提到了这个‘M’。

En: What about this 'M'? He points at the page you're staring at. "Could this be La Puta Madre? Here: March 9th and March 15th."
Zh: “那这个‘M’呢？”他指向你盯着的位置。“也许是指马德雷教父？这里：3月9号和3月15号。”

En: Not now.
Zh: 现在不行。

En: The call? Did M call her personally? Why?
Zh: “电话？”难道M给她本人打电话了？为什么？

En: This is not me. I wouldn't do something like that.
Zh: 这不是我。我不会做那种事的。

En: Maybe it's the old you? Or maybe she was just paranoid...
Zh: 也许是以前的你？或者也许她只是个偏执的妄想狂……

En: On purpose? That's pretty far-fetched.
Zh: 故意的？有点太牵强附会了吧。

En: Am I sure this is a mistake? Look at me.
Zh: 我能确定这是个错误吗？看看我。

En: This is a coincidence. I would never kill people for a mob boss.
Zh: 这是个巧合。我永远也不会为一个黑帮老大去杀人的。

En: Whatever you may *look* like, you don't *feel* like a hired assassin.
Zh: 不管你*看起来*可能是什么样子，你并不*觉得*自己是个受人雇佣的刺客。

En: The thought steadies your nerves. The journal stops shaking in your hand.
Zh: 这种想法让你的神经镇定了下来。手中的日记也停止了颤抖。

En: That must have been one hell of a conversation.
Zh: 那肯定是一场可怕的谈话。

En: The call was a courtesy. He must have held her in high regard to personally tell her he knew about her plan to run drugs for the competition.
Zh: 打这个电话是出于一种礼貌。他肯定很尊重她，才会亲自打电话告诉她，说他已经知道她打算贩毒来抢生意。

En: No mention of La Puta Madre...
Zh: “没有提到马德雷教父……”

En: This whole thing was a detour -- and a fatal one, he thinks.
Zh: 整件事情都是在兜圈子——而且是致命的，他这样想到。

En: That would be a first -- or a fourth -- but who's counting, he thinks. Very rarely does anyone actually get framed.
Zh: 那可能是第一次——或者第四次——不过谁会数呢，他想到。很少有人能真的被陷害。

En: Okay. What do you mean, framed?
Zh: “好吧。你说陷害，是什么意思？”

En: Don't get emotional.
Zh: 别太情绪化。

En: By the Hardie boys, by Klaasje, by us.
Zh: “被哈迪兄弟，卡拉洁，还有我们。”

En: T-man wouldn't fuck me over!
Zh: “提图斯是不会利用我的！”

En: You should show all this to the lieutenant, see what he makes of it.
Zh: 你应该把这些都拿给警督看，看看他有什么看法。

En: Flip through the pages again.
Zh: 再次翻看页面。

En: A thick journal. The cover is worn, like someone used to carry it around in their back pocket.
Zh: 一本厚日记。封面已经磨损，似乎有人经常把它装在后口袋里。

En: Unwind the strap. (Move on.)
Zh: 松开皮带。（继续。）

En: Put the journal away. [Leave.]
Zh: 把日记放在一边。[离开。]

En: The thick journal bound in brown leather is full of candour and diagrams.
Zh: 这本棕色皮革装订的厚日记本里面满是日历和图表。

En: Flip through the pages.
Zh: 翻阅书页。

En: The journal falls open. About two thirds of its ruled pages have been filled.
Zh: 日记本打开了。大约有2/3的横格页面都被写满了。

En: Study the handwriting.
Zh: 研究笔迹。

En: Examine the cover.
Zh: 检查封面。

En: It's made of full-grain leather. The lower left corner of the back cover sports an embossed brand name: "SCHNELLER."
Zh: 它是由全粒面革制成的。封底左下角印着一个浮雕品牌名：“施内勒尔”。

En: Schneller is a stationary brand from Gottwald beloved among architects and engineers.
Zh: 施内勒尔是来自戈特伍德的一个驰名品牌，备受建筑师和工程师的喜爱。

En: She's got good taste and must have taken whatever she recorded here seriously.
Zh: 她的品味很好，不管她在里面记了些什么，一定都很认真。

En: She had good taste and must have taken what she recorded here seriously.
Zh: 她的品味很好，不管她在里面记了些什么，一定都很认真。

En: It's a mix of logistical notes, diagrams, and personal reflections, all dated.
Zh: 里面记录着物流备注，图表和个人反思，全都标注了日期。

En: How far back do the entries go?
Zh: 最早的记录是什么时候的？

En: What kinds of logistics?
Zh: 什么类型的物流？

En: Anything personal?
Zh: 有什么个人信息吗？

En: The large cursive of someone who writes quickly and confidently.
Zh: 某个写字又快又自信的人留下的大草书体。

En: The first entry is from August 2nd of last year. It reads simply: "I know my position is precarious. All I can do is make myself as useful as I can while looking for a way out. (Remember: no one is indispensable.)"
Zh: 第一条记录是去年的8月2日。内容很简单：“我知道自己的处境很危险。我唯一能做的就是在寻找出路的同时，尽可能地让自己发挥用场。（记住：没人是不可或缺的。）”

En: Hard to tell exactly -- it's mostly noted down in code. Looks like contact information, quantities, directions.
Zh: 很难说清楚——大部分都是用代码的形式记录下来的。看起来像是联系信息，数量和方位。

En: Short, wry observations of people and places -- probably a way to pass the time on the road. Also, what appear to be attempts to sort through some difficult decisions. There are a few passages with many questions in them.
Zh: 对人和地点的简短而揶揄的观察——可能是路上消磨时间的一种方式。还有一些像是打算厘清一些困难的决定。有些页面上写下了不少问题。

En: There could be useful information about local operations in those notes. We have a junior sergeant at my station who's good with codes -- I can give this to her after we finish this.
Zh: “这些笔记里可能有关于本地运营活动的有用信息。我们分局有一个初级警员，很擅长解码——我们完事之后，我可以把这个交给她看看。”

En: The way some of those question marks trail off into ellipses -- she was going through a tough time.
Zh: 有些问号变成了省略号——她度过了一段艰难的时期。

En: (Look at the cover again.)
Zh: （再看一眼封面。）

En: What did she write the day Lely died?
Zh: 莱利死的那天她写了什么？

En: Nothing on March 4th... March 5th, though: "Well, *that's* bound to come back and bite me in the ass. I'm bad at this -- loyal to a fault. Except... But that's another matter entirely."
Zh: 3月4日什么也没有……不过3月5日有：“哎，*那个*迟早会找回我头上的，造成很坏的结果。我不擅长这种事——做到极度的忠诚。只不过……但那完全是另一个问题了。”

En: Anything about... La Puta Madre?
Zh: 有没有关于……马德雷教父的内容？

En: That name isn't mentioned as far as you can tell.
Zh: 据你所知，那个名字并没有被提到。

En: What's the most recent entry?
Zh: 最近的记录是什么？

En: What's the most recent entry? (Conclude with journal.)
Zh: 最近的记录是什么？（结束日记。）

En: What are the diagrams of?
Zh: 这些图表是干什么的？

En: Esoteric radio technology. The most recent ones probably pertain to the Latitude Compressor: sketches, calculations of distance and density. You make out a familiar spiral shape.
Zh: 深奥难懂的无线电技术。最近的好像是关于维度压缩机的：草图，距离和密度的计算。你辨认出一个熟悉的螺旋形状。

En: The most recent entry is from today. It reads:
Zh: 最近的记录是今天。内容是：

En: Read the entry from March 9th first.
Zh: 先阅读3月9号的记录。

En: Read the entry from March 12th.
Zh: 阅读3月12号的记录。

En: Great. M's peone is coming to town, no doubt to investigate the lynching, but also -- I feel it in my gut -- to finally put a bullet in my head. While I'm napping in my lorry or on a smoke break... Well, I won't stick around just to twist my own neck by constantly looking over my shoulder.
Zh: “太棒了。M的傀儡进城了，毫无疑问是来调查私刑的，不过——我有种直觉——也是为了来枪杀我的。要么乘我在货車里小睡的时候，或者是抽烟休息途中……呃，我可不会就这么待在这里，不断地回头张望把自己的脖子扭折。”

En: Been holed up here for three days now. I'm used to being alone and all, but -- I don't know when I'll be able to leave, or if I'll be ratted out. They *will* rat me out, of course. I've made it a point to believe in the best in people (the boys, for example), but -- experience tells me...
Zh: “现在我在这里已经躲了3天了。我很习惯孤独，不过——我不知道自己什么时候能够离开，或者我会被出卖。当然了，他们*肯定*会出卖我的。我努力想要相信人类最好的一面（比如说，兄弟们），不过——经验告诉我……”

En: Then again, isn't that what I've been doing ever since I got the call.
Zh: “不过，自从接到那个电话之后，我不是一直在做这种事情吗？”

En: Things played out just as she had feared. Except *you* didn't shoot her.
Zh: 她害怕的事情还是发生了。只不过*你*没有朝她开枪。

En: Were you *supposed* to find her, even apart from the investigation, then? On M's request? No, you wouldn't do something like this. This must be a mistake.
Zh: 所以，且不说调查的事，你*应该*把她找出来吗？为了完成M的任务？不，你不会做这种事的。这一定是搞错了。

En: It's in you to do something like this -- kill her. Physically at least. You could pull the trigger if you had to.
Zh: 你应该这么做——杀了她。至少身体上可以。如果有必要，你可以扣动扳机。

En: You didn't follow through. You should have shot her in the head.
Zh: 你没有自始自终地完成自己的工作。你应该朝她的脑袋开一枪的。

En: Goddamn right, I should have.
Zh: 他妈的没错，我应该这样做的。

En: No, I couldn't.
Zh: 不，我不能。

En: What a coincidence...
Zh: 真是个巧合……

En: Yes, you could. You'd pull it one, two, three times -- and watch her fall.
Zh: 褴褛飞旋是的，你可以。你会扣下扳机，一次，两次，三次——然后看着她倒下。褴褛飞旋

En: I'm okay with the idea.
Zh: 我赞同这个想法。

En: Is it? Can you be sure? What if you are secretly being manipulated by La Puta Madre as you pursue this investigation. What if he wiped your memory on purpose?
Zh: 是吗？你确定吗？如果你被马德雷教父秘密操纵了，所以才来进行这次调查的呢？如果他是故意清除你的记忆呢？

En: That's a good, pragmatic approach.
Zh: 这是一个很好、也很务实的方法。

En: Did M feel truly betrayed by me? I was feeling threatened -- he'd have to know if he threatened people, they'd take measures to protect themselves. Even I know that. Economic measures first of all. Gotta make a living, right. I can still hear his voice in the receiver, taste the plastic...
Zh: “M真觉得被我背叛了吗？我感觉到了威胁——他必须知道，如果他威胁别人，他们肯定会采取措施来保护自己。就连我也知道这一点。首先是经济手段。要想办法维持生活，没错。我还能在听筒里听见他的声音，尝到一股塑料味……”

En: The entry ends abruptly.
Zh: 这条记录突然中断了。

En: Even when I leave here (if I leave here alive) -- what's my next move?... Staging a lynching is a crime, even if I'm not accused of murder on top of that. Forever on the run? Not really my idea of the open road. \n\nMan, I was really looking forward to winning.
Zh: “就算是我要离开这里的时候（如果能活着离开）——下一步打算怎么走？……即使我没有被指控谋杀，伪造私刑也是一种犯罪。永远亡命天涯吗？这可不是我想要的阳关大道。\n\n天啦，我真的很渴望胜利。”

En: Too late to do that now, *assassino grande*! Better start looking over your shoulder now.
Zh: 现在这么做已经太迟了，*杀手先生*！现在你自己最好也要小心一点了。

En: A pawnshop -- a pawnshop would have a tape player...
Zh: 一家当铺——当铺里可能有录音机……

En: Nothing to do with this right now.
Zh: 现在没什么能做的了。

En: Yup. They're all here. All three verses.
Zh: 没错。全都在这里。所有三个章节。

En: The lieutenant watches you pack up the boombox. He doesn't say anything.
Zh: 警督看着你把手提音响打包好。他一言不发。

En: The porta-reel is just what you needed -- the reels attach to the apparatus with a satisfying click. The tape is routed behind the magnetic reader.
Zh: 你正好需要音响——带盘贴合在设备上，发出一声令人满意的咔哒声。磁带在录放磁头背后转动起来。

En: Remove the tape.
Zh: 取出磁带。

En: Immediately the boombox tunes itself back to the Friday night beats, vibrating on your shoulder.
Zh: 手提音响立即调回了周五夜节拍，在你的肩膀上震动。

En: The lieutenant looks at you as you remove the tape from the boombox. He doesn't say anything.
Zh: 警督看着你把磁带从手提音响中取了出来。他一个字也没说。

En: You press the large button marked *Commencer* and the tape starts spinning. There's a small delay before the song starts playing...
Zh: 你按下标着*开始*的大按钮，磁带开始旋转。略微延迟之后，歌曲开始播放……

En: But even just holding the tape makes you feel a little sad...
Zh: 不过，仅仅是握着磁带本身，你就能感到一丝悲伤……

En: It sounds like someone's moving in the room, getting comfortable. Then the organ starts playing a simple melancholic tune, echoing in the hallway. A lone singing voice joins in...
Zh: 听起来像是某人在房间里移动的声音，想让自己舒服一些。然后风琴开始播放一段简单又忧郁的曲调，声音在走廊里回荡。接着，一个孤寂的歌声加入了……

En: Press your ear against the speaker.
Zh: 把你的耳朵贴在扬声器上。

En: Keep the porta-reel at the harmless distance and wait.
Zh: 让音响保持在一个安全距离，静静等待。

En: Then the organ starts playing a simple melancholic tune, echoing in the hallway. A lone singing voice joins in...
Zh: 然后风琴开始播放一段简单又忧郁的曲调，声音在走廊里回荡。接着，一个孤寂的歌声加入了……

En: Telling you about the tiniest church in Saint-Saëns, surrounded by even tinier yard. You almost feel the seaside mist on your skin. It's *mega* sad.
Zh: 跟你说说圣桑最小的教堂，被更小的院子包围的教堂。你几乎能感觉到海滨的薄雾覆盖在你的皮肤上。这是一种*巨大的*悲伤。

En: Could I sing this for karaoke? I think I could sing this.
Zh: 我可以用卡拉OK唱这个吗？我想我会唱这个。

En: No, I couldn't! It's too sad, I might cry and trash the room again.
Zh: 不，我不要！它太悲伤了，我可能会哭，把房间又弄得一团糟。

En: Crying is good, it gives your performance authenticity. Besides, you already know the lyrics since you've listened to it, like, a million times!
Zh: 哭泣是很好的，它能让你的表演更加真实。况且你已经知道歌词了，因为你大概是听过，100万次了！

En: Of course you could sing this! You could take sad to a whole new level with this. And you already know the lyrics since you've listened to it, like, a million times!
Zh: 你当然可以唱这个了！这样你就能把悲伤提升到一个全新的水准。况且你已经知道歌词了，因为你大概是听过，100万次了！

En: Within seconds you know -- this is the one. The *real shit* you've been looking for. The one you trashed your room to that Klaasje told you about. Perform it!
Zh: 不消几秒，你就知道——就是它。你一直在寻找的*真正的狗屎*。卡拉洁告诉过你，就是它让你把房间搞得一团糟。上台表演吧！

En: Within seconds you know -- this is the one. The *real shit* you've been looking for. The one you trashed your room to. This one tells it like it is. This is your tune.
Zh: 不消几秒，你就知道——就是它。你一直在寻找的*真正的狗屎*。让你把房间搞得一团糟的罪魁祸首。它本身就是这么说的。这是你的曲调。

En: Scratch that, I'm already crying. And I look dumb. And old.
Zh: 算了吧，我已经哭了。而且我看起来很蠢，还很老。

En: *And* the B-side of the tape contains the instrumental version. It's like the world itself is telling you to do it. Only one obstacle stands on your way...
Zh: *而且*磁带B面还有演奏版。就好像宇宙本身在告诉你这么做。现在只有一个障碍挡在你面前……

En: What?
Zh: 什么？

En: Nothing can stop me now.
Zh: 现在已经没什么能阻挡我了。

En: Garte. You have to convince Garte to let you sing karaoke in the Whirling. After you've won him over, you can *express* yourself. Let the pain out. Make everyone understand.
Zh: 加尔特。你必须说服加尔特让你在飞旋旅社唱卡拉OK。等你说服他之后，就能*表达*你自己了。释放出体内的悲伤。让所有人都能明白。

En: There's no way to listen to the tape without a working tape player or porta-reel at hand.
Zh: 如果手上没有好用的录音机或者音响的话，根本没办法听磁带。

En: The porta-reel is just what you needed -- the reels attach to the apparatus with a satisfying click. The tape is routed behind the magnetic reader.
Zh: 你正好需要音响——带盘贴合在设备上，发出一声令人满意的咔哒声。磁带在录放磁头背后转动起来。

En: Press play on tape.
Zh: 按下播放键。

En: Not now. [Put the tape away.]
Zh: 现在不行。[把磁带放在一边。]

En: A click -- then silence, for a bit. Then the tape stops spinning.
Zh: 咔哒一声——接着是短暂的沉默。然后，磁带停止了旋转。

En: The pearlescent paper adamantly resists being held open -- it demands struggle at both ends. The print inside is just as taxing due to its fine and curlicued font. The most attractive part is the image emblazoned across the top.
Zh: 泛着珠光宝气的纸张顽固地拒绝保持展开状态——非得用力按住两端才行。上面印刷着满是精美花饰的字体令人看着费劲。其中最吸引人的部分莫过于顶部装饰的图案。

En: Feisty little thing! But don't worry -- it's under control.
Zh: 顽固的小东西！但别担心——我按得住。

En: Devious use of Swish there. It's a font literally *designed* to win legal agreements by way of being almost unreadable through flamboyance.
Zh: 在这种地方用上*华贵*字体也未免太狡猾了。*设计*这种字体的初衷就是通过花里胡哨到令人看不懂，在签订法律协议时占尽便宜。

En: Look at the top of the document.
Zh: 看文档的顶部。

En: Attempt to read the words.
Zh: 试图阅读文字。

En: [Put it away.]
Zh: [置之不理。]

En: Each letter is shaped like a laugh line being pulled in a million different  directions. They're all grinning at you -- pleased with practically being indecipherable.
Zh: 每个字母都像是一道被扯向百万个不同方向的法令纹。字里行间都在冲你笑着——为你什么都看不懂而心满意足。

En: You can do this. Maybe squinting will help?
Zh: 你能做到的！也许眯眼能够帮你看清？

En: Start with the biggest letters in order to learn the characteristics of the style. Then work your way down.
Zh: 从最大的字母开始研究这种字体的特点。一步步破解这个谜团。

En: Focus on the header.
Zh: 专注于标题。

En: Squint in hope of seeing something within the lines.
Zh: 眯起眼睛，指望窥见字里行间的玄机。

En: Give it up. (Go back.)
Zh: 放弃。（返回。）

En: The certificate threatens to coil back into itself as you relax a little.
Zh: 你一稍微松手，这张股权证明就摆出架势要卷回去。

En: Your vision blurs into a haze of dark scribbles. It's like looking at a wrought iron fence through a greasy lens. Not a single recognisable symbol appears to you.
Zh: 你的视野被卷入一片模糊的黑色涂鸦之中。宛如透过沾满油污的镜片观察一道铁艺围栏。你无法从中认出任何符号。

En: That really didn't help, did it? How about opening your eyes as wide as possible?
Zh: 那样根本没用，对吧？你不如努力把眼睛瞪大？

En: Open them wide.
Zh: 瞪大双眼。

En: Nah. (Give up.)
Zh: 不行。

En: The scrawl across the paper opens wide its many grins back at you. A mess of mouths ready to swallow you up. Intertwined and interflowing, impenetrably. Your eyes begin to water.
Zh: 纸上潦草的字迹也纷纷向你咧开大嘴。无数张血盆大口仿佛要将你吞噬。纸面上的涂鸦纠缠不休，川流不息，你仍不知该从何处开始解读，双眼逐渐湿润起来。

En: Okay, okay -- enough of that. It's starting to sting. Better try another approach.
Zh: 得了，得了——拉倒吧。你都感到刺痛了。还是换个方式吧。

En: A bold, black mesh curves around the oval image at the top of the document. It almost looks as if it were penned with intention. Perhaps it was. You're starting to think it might actually *say* something.
Zh: 一片加粗的黑色网格环绕着位于文件顶部的椭圆形图案。看起来几乎像是故意圈起来的。很可能正是如此。你不由得开始感到这其实*别有深意*。

En: Focus on the negative space -- this will tell you the parameters of each letter. Isolate each one and *decipher*.
Zh: 把注意力集中在空白处——这样就能看出每个字母的范围。先把字母单独摘出来，再逐个*破解*。

En: Slowly, it happens -- individual letters coalesce. It's like watching ice melt to reveal the shrubs underneath. A few seconds later and you can read the entire heading: M -- K -- M -- G. This is a start. Can you read the rest?
Zh: 你逐渐做到了——线条逐渐结合成单个的字母。整个过程像是望着冰雪消融，露出下面的灌木。数秒之后，你终于能认出整个标题了：M——K——M——G。以此为起点，你能读出剩下的字吗？

En: If it does, it remains hidden from you. All you see is a baffling enigma, a coiled nest of viper-letters -- if they even *are* letters.
Zh: 就算这东西确实别有深意，也深到了你无法参透的程度。你只能看出一个令人费解的谜团，大量文字如同蝰蛇盘绕于巢穴之中——如果这些真能算是*文字*的话。

En: Try to read the tiny print.
Zh: 试着去读那些小字。

En: Phew! Reading is hard. That's enough for now.
Zh: 呜呼！学海无涯。暂且作罢。

En: Oh brother, you have tumbled into a *chophouse* of words. This is where letters come to die. A pile of discarded alphabet limbs lie before you in spidery formation. Why are they all so hairy?
Zh: 兄弟啊，你这是闯进文字的*馆子*了。这是字母的丧命之地，字母表的残肢断臂像蜘蛛腿一样堆在你面前。这些东西怎么长了这么多毛？

En: You start to carefully shave it all with your razor eyes. A few 'R's stick out. There's an 'E'. Then, suddenly, some whole words appear: 'This certifies that (unreadable) is entitled (unreadable) in the capital stock of Mauer-Koshlai Mercury Group.'
Zh: 凭借着剃刀般的锐利目光，你开始小心翼翼地给字母刮毛。首先现出原形的是“R”，接着又有个“E”。几个完整的单词突然呈现在眼前：“兹证明（无法辨识）被授予（无法辨识）玛瓦-科什莱墨丘利集团的股本。”

En: I know business-speak when I see it. This is an official, legitimate, checked-by-the-board certificate. You really are a stock market big-boy now. Sound the money klaxon and bring out the hustle-clowns -- they dance for us today!
Zh: 我一眼就能认出这种商务文件的调调。这是一张正式的，合法的，由董事会一致通过的股权证明。你已经成为股市大户了。快去吹响暴发户的喇叭，召集一群喧闹的小丑吧——今天就靠他们跳舞助兴了！

En: Inside an oval frame is a grey sketch of a curly-haired girl enthusiastically riding an industrial drill. It's churning up hard chunks of rocky ground and scattering them into the air. In the hole created beneath is a silvery river of liquid metal.
Zh: 椭圆形的边框里是一张灰色的素描图：卷发的女孩充满热情地骑着一台工业钻头。钻头将地下坚硬的岩石搅成碎片，撒向空中。钻开的洞口下流淌着银光闪闪的液态金属。

En: Her form is terrible! She needs to straighten her back and increase the downward pressure on that machine. Has *no one* taught her about ergonomics?
Zh: 她的姿势真是不像样！她应该挺直腰板，用力下压那台机器。难道*没人*教过她人体工程学吗？

En: An underground kingdom of shimmering spillways! Silver stars melted down and filtered for their precious discharge. What a place of inspiration!
Zh: 由闪耀的溢洪道交织而成的地底王国！银色的群星在此熔化，再滤出其中珍贵的宝藏。真是令人灵感迸发的宝地！

En: There must be *some* form of logic hidden away in there...
Zh: 这里一定藏着*某种*形式的逻辑……

En: The cover features a charming illustration depicting a mass of grinning labourers loading goods onto a ship while a richly dressed administrator oversees their work.
Zh: 封面上有一副迷人的插画，描绘的是一群笑嘻嘻的工人正把货物装载到一艘船上，还有一个衣着华丽的管理员在监督他们的工作。

En: Shake the box.
Zh: 摇摇盒子。

En: Remove the plastic wrap.
Zh: 撕掉塑料包装。

En: [Leave it perfect and undisturbed.]
Zh: [让它保持完好，不被打扰。]

En: Good thinking. It could be a collectible some day.
Zh: 好主意。说不定有天会变成收藏品呢。

En: In your hands you hold a brand new copy of the game 'Suzerainty'. It's snugly wrapped in a skin of plastic...
Zh: 你手中握着一个名为‘大领主’游戏的全新版本。它被一层塑料包装紧紧地包裹着……

En: The exact location and time period are left deliberately vague, but it's clearly meant to represent the economic relationship between the Revacholian Suzerainty and its many vassals.
Zh: 确切的地点和时间故意被模糊掉了，不过显然是在描述瑞瓦肖领主和很多附属国之间的经济关系。

En: The box has a nice heft to it. You hear the rattle of individual wooden tokens and feel their weight shifting back and forth...
Zh: 盒子的分量很足。你听见了每一个木制代币的咔哒声，能感觉到它们的重量在反复来回移动……

En: Even before you open it, you can tell that this will be a meaty game of grand strategy and complex player interactions.
Zh: 甚至还没有打开，你就能知道这会是一个包含宏大战略和复杂玩家互动、而且内容极为丰富的游戏。

En: The plastic wrap rips off as easily as a bodice in a tawdry historical romance.
Zh: 这种塑料包装撕起来，就像扯掉俗丽的历史演义小说里那些紧身胸衣一样容易。

En: Open the box.
Zh: 打开盒子。

En: What treasures wait in store for you?
Zh: 里面有什么宝藏在等待着你呢？

En: There's a hiss as the lid slides off. Inside you find a thick, full-colour rulebook and more than a dozen pouches of various wooden components.
Zh: 伴随着一阵嘶嘶声，盖子滑落开来。你在里面找到了一本厚重的全彩规则手册，还有几十袋各种各样的木制组件。

En: Ahhh! Savour that new board game smell! A mix of wood, paper, and ink, all wrapped in the sweet must of cardboard.
Zh: 啊！品味一下新桌游的味道吧！混合着木头，纸张和墨水的味道，全都被包裹在甜美的硬纸板里。

En: Read the rulebook.
Zh: 阅读规则手册。

En: Examine the components.
Zh: 检查组件。

En: Hey, Kim, wanna play?
Zh: “喂，金，想玩玩吗？”

En: [Put the game away.]
Zh: [把游戏放在一边。]

En: The lieutenant looks over the rulebook before he sees something that makes his eyes go wide...
Zh: 警督翻看着规则手册，直到他看到了什么让他双眼圆睁的东西……

En: Convince Kim to play with you.
Zh: 说服金跟你一起玩游戏。

En: Plenty of time.
Zh: 很多时间。

En: Intriguing components.
Zh: 有趣的组件。

En: Persistent.
Zh: 坚持不懈。

En: Really persistent.
Zh: 真的坚持不懈。

En: What is detective work if not an elaborate game? You need logical inference, attention to detail, the ability to analyse your opponents' motives...
Zh: 如果警探的工作不是一场精心设计的游戏，那又会是什么呢？你需要逻辑推理，留心细节，还有分析对手动机的能力……

En: What, does the lieutenant hate fun? Is he the *fun* police?
Zh: 什么，难道警督讨厌乐趣吗？他不是个*有趣的*警察吗？

En: Who died and made you the fun police?
Zh: “谁死了，让那个你成了一个有趣的警察？”

En: Come on, it might help us think of more creative solutions to the case.
Zh: “拜托，它也许能帮我们更有创意地解决这起案件呢。”

En: Alright, you've convinced me. How do we play?
Zh: “好吧，你说服我了。我们怎么玩？”

En: I don't know, let's wing it.
Zh: “我不知道，就即兴发挥吧。”

En: Hmm, we should probably read the rules, huh?
Zh: “嗯，也许我们应该先看看规则？”

En: I read the rules already, I'll show you...
Zh: “我已经看过规则了，我演示给你看……”

En: I see you bring the same *unorthodox technique* to playing board games as you do to detective work...
Zh: “我能看出，你在玩桌游的时候，也像完成警探工作一样，用了同样的*非传统技术*……”

En: When I thrash you I want to make sure it's *by the book*.
Zh: “当我重创你的时候，我需要确保是在*照章办事*。”

En: You explain the basic set up procedures to the lieutenant, who seems to be a quick study. You each take your bags of tokens and counters and unfold the board between you...
Zh: 你向警督解释了基本的准备程序，他好像学的很快。你们俩都拿出一些代币和计数器，然后在中间展开了棋盘……

En: In the centre is the crown of Revachol. Radiating outward are her colourful vassals, each one supplying some raw material desired by the suzerain...
Zh: 中央是瑞瓦肖的王冠。周围辐射出的是那些生动多彩的附属国，每一个都能为大领主提供其渴望的原材料……

En: Apricots from Safre, archaeological treasures from Ile Marat, sugar from the Semenine Islands, and magenta cocaine from Supramundi and Saramiriza...
Zh: 来自萨弗里的杏子，伊莱马拉特的考古宝藏，塞美尼群岛的糖，还有苏帕穆迪和萨拉米尔泽的洋红可卡因……

En: But not good enough, the lieutenant seems to be saying.
Zh: 警督似乎在说，但是还是不够好。

En: Just like there are no *honour points*. None whatsoever.
Zh: 就好像根本没有*荣誉点*一样。一点也没有。

En: *Drugs*. Specifically uppers. You should find some the first chance you get.
Zh: *毒品*。特别是安非他命。你应该抓住一切机会去寻找。

En: The lieutenant flips through the manual more quickly than you're able to keep up with. Then you each take your bags of tokens and counters and unfold the board between you...
Zh: 警督翻阅手册的速度要快到你完全跟不上。然后你们俩都拿出一些代币和计数器，在中间展开了棋盘……

En: The 'Suzerainty' box is heavy and slightly awkward in your hands. You give it a light shake, and feel the pieces shift around inside.
Zh: ‘大领主’盒子很重，拿在手里有一点别扭。你轻轻摇晃了一下，感觉里面有什么东西在移动。

En: 'Welcome to Suzerainty: A game of economic strategy for the whole family!' The rulebook is sumptuously illustrated and thick as a Graadian novel.
Zh: ‘欢迎来到大领主：一个可供全家人游玩的经济策略游戏！’规则手册上的插图很奢华，厚的跟格拉德小说一样。

En: 'Economic strategy'? More like rapacious plunder and exploitation.
Zh: ‘经济策略’？更像是贪婪的掠夺和开发吧。

En: Finally, a proper game to teach children about the importance of trade and the global economy.
Zh: 总算是找到一个恰当的游戏了，可以教会小孩贸易和全球经济重要性。

En: Keep reading.
Zh: 继续阅读。

En: Finally, a proper game to teach Revacholian children about their glorious history.
Zh: 总算是找到一个恰当的游戏了，可以教会瑞瓦肖小孩那曾经光辉的历史。

En: Hmmm, this history seems *problematic*, but it *is* important to teach children basic economic concepts.
Zh: 嗯，历史好像*有些疑问*，不过教会小孩基础的经济概念*确实*很重要的。

En: The colourful illustrations depict cheerful workers picking apricots, hauling marble sculptures out of crumbling temples, and harvesting a strange, magenta-leafed plant. Everyone is smiling.
Zh: 彩色插画描绘一群快乐工人，他们采摘杏子，从摇摇欲坠的寺庙中搬运着大理石雕像，还有收割一种奇怪的、长着洋红色叶子的植物。所有人都笑眯眯的。

En: The instructions are opaque at first, and introduce many concepts you're not familiar with. Fortunately, there are many diagrams and examples throughout...
Zh: 这些说明一开始有些模糊，介绍了很多你不熟悉的概念。幸运的是，有很多图标和例子贯穿其中……

En: You soon figure out the basic conceit: Each player represents an administrator for the *Suzerain of Revachol*. Your objective is to increase the suzerain's wealth and renown by accumulating *victory points*.
Zh: 你很快就明白了基础概念：每个玩家都代表*瑞瓦肖大领主*的一名行政官。目标就是增加大领主的财富，通过积累*胜点*来增加名望。

En: That's where the suzerain's vassals come in. The game features four vassal nations, each one home to an economically important resource...
Zh: 这就是附属国的用武之地。游戏拥有四种附属国，每个都是一种重要经济资源的原产地……

En: How do you accumulate victory points?
Zh: 该怎么积累胜点呢？

En: Fuck the suzerain, what about *my* wealth and renown?
Zh: 去他妈的大领主，那*我*的财务和名望呢？

En: I've read enough. (Put the rulebook away.)
Zh: 我已经看够了。（把规则手册放在一边。）

En: From the Empire of Safre: orange apricot tokens. From Ile Marat (the ancestral name of Iilmaraa): gray marble block tokens. From the Semenine Islands: white sacks of sugar tokens. And from Supramundi and Saramiriza: magenta tokens for unprocessed cocaine leaves.
Zh: 萨弗里帝国：橙色的杏子代币。伊莱马拉特（伊尔玛的旧名）：灰色的大理石胚料代币。塞美尼群岛：白色的糖袋代币。苏帕穆迪和萨拉米尔泽：洋红色的未加工可卡因叶子代币。

En: Each turn the player collects resources from vassals where they've placed workers. They may then rearrange their workers, fulfil contracts for coin and bonuses, or build structures back in Revachol...
Zh: 每一回合，玩家需要将工人放置在各附属国来收集资源。之后他们可以重新安排工人，完成契约收获硬币和奖金，或者是返回瑞瓦肖建造建筑物……

En: The actual scoring system appears infinitely complex, with a series of tables and appendices required to compute each player's final victory point total. You skip that part for now.
Zh: 实际的计分系统看起来极其复杂，需要一系列表格和附件来计算每一位玩家最后的总胜点。暂时先跳过那个部分吧。

En: There is no path to wealth and renown but through the suzerain. As one of the suzerain's trusted administrators, your very function is the glorification of Revachol...
Zh: 只有通过大领主才能获得财富和名望。作为大领主信赖的行政官，你的职责就是赞颂瑞瓦肖……

En: Boring, boring, BORING. Tear up this rulebook and commit some old-school atrocities!
Zh: 无聊，无聊，无聊。撕掉规则手册，直接来点老派的暴行吧！

En: How is the winner determined?
Zh: 怎么决定赢家呢？

En: Isn't there any way to invade or commit atrocities or anything fun like that?
Zh: 难道就没办法入侵或者犯下暴行，做些诸如此类有趣的事情吗？

En: Suzerainty is a family game. The only 'atrocities' you'll be committing are against the social standing of your rival administrators, as you bring in ever more resources and power for the suzerain. Speaking of...
Zh: 大领主是一个家庭游戏。你唯一能犯下的‘暴行’就是对抗其他执行官的社会地位，为大领主带去更多的资源和权利。说到这里……

En: As you leaf through the pages, your eye catches on a sidebar labelled 'ADVICE FOR BEGINNERS'.
Zh: 当你浏览书页的时候，你的目光注意到一个侧边栏，标题是‘初学者建议’。

En: You begin to suspect there may be a *political* agenda to this so-called 'family game'. Only one way to find out...
Zh: 你开始怀疑这个所谓的‘家庭游戏’可能包含了某种‘政治’议程。唯一能找出来的办法只有……

En: You open up a number of pouches containing wooden tokens. There are also several punchboards with other cardboard components that will need to be punched out before you can play.
Zh: 你打开一些装着木制代币的小袋子。还有一些附带其他纸板组件的打孔卡片纸，在游戏之前需要先打孔。

En: Punch out the cardboard pieces, one by one.
Zh: 给纸板打孔，一个接一个。

En: Check out the wooden tokens.
Zh: 检查木制代币。

En: Each cardboard token makes a satisfying *chhhk* as you pop it out. Soon a neat pile of cardboard coins and counters has accumulated before you.
Zh: 你把纸板代币抠出来的时候，每一个都会发出一阵愉悦的*咔哒*声。很快，一堆整齐的纸板代币和计数器就堆积在你面前。

En: A good, orderly task that leaves you feeling relaxed and accomplished.
Zh: 一个良好而有序的任务，能让你产生一种放松和成就感。

En: In addition to the worker and building tokens used by each player, there are also several piles of colourful resource tokens, each representing one of the game's four principal resources...
Zh: 除了每个玩家使用的工人和建筑代币之外，还有几堆彩色的资源代币，代表着游戏中的只要的四种资源……

En: Put the components away.
Zh: 把组件放在一边。

En: You hold the open game box before you.
Zh: 你把开封的游戏盒子放在身前。

En: What, you're not going to offer to let *me* punch any of them out?
Zh: “怎么，你不准备留点给*我*抠吗？”

En: I'm not sure we can afford to set aside *that* kind of time for a *game*.
Zh: “我不知道咱们有没有*那么多*时间可留给一场*游戏*。”

En: The lieutenant goes first. He draws a contract card and moves several of his workers to the Safre territory of the board and the others to the Semenine Islands...
Zh: 警督先手。他抽出一张契约卡，把一群工人移动到棋盘上萨弗里的领土，另一部分放到了塞美尼群岛……

En: You have a few options available to you: Will you try to fulfil contracts right away or rearrange your workers to maximize production on future turns?
Zh: 你有几个选择：你是会立即尝试完成契约，还是重新安排工人，让未来几轮的产出最大化呢？

En: Try to fulfil a contract.
Zh: 试着完成一份契约。

En: Let your workers rest for a while.
Zh: 让你的工人休息一会儿。

En: You draw a contract card offering a number of coins in exchange for archaeological treasures...
Zh: 你抽出一张契约卡，可以用一些硬币交换考古宝藏……

En: What? It's the very beginning of the game. Your workers haven't even done any work yet.
Zh: 什么？游戏才刚刚开始。你的工人甚至什么都没做呢。

En: Meanwhile, the lieutenant spends two of his sugar and one of his apricot tokens to complete his contract card. He is rewarded with four coins and a round wooden token that he places in the centre of the board...
Zh: 与此同时，警督花费了两枚糖币和一枚杏子币，完成了他的契约卡。他的奖励是4枚硬币，以及一枚圆形木制代币——被他放在了棋盘中央……

En: That's a market. It's worth *two victory points*.
Zh: “那是市场。价值*两个胜点*。”

En: Hey, why don't I get one of those?
Zh: “喂，为什么我没有呢？”

En: Glower silently.
Zh: 默默地怒视着。

En: Don't worry, it was mostly just luck. You'll earn some points soon enough.
Zh: “别担心，主要还是靠运气。你很快就能挣到胜点的。”

En: The lieutenant returns your baleful look with a satisfied grin.
Zh: 警督用满意的笑容回敬了你充满恶意的目光。

En: Remember what the rulebook said! You'll want to choose a strategy early on and stay committed to it.
Zh: 还记得规则书上是怎么说的吗！你需要尽早选择一个策略，然后保持下去。

En: Oh, those are nice. The lieutenant picks up a sugar token and admires it.
Zh: “哦，那些挺不错的。”警督拾起一枚糖币，拿在眼前欣赏着。

En: It's a shame there's no one for you to play with.
Zh: 遗憾的是没人能陪你玩。

En: You've reached a critical strategic juncture. How do you respond to the lieutenant's aggression?
Zh: 你来到了一个关键的战略决策时刻。你该如何应对警督的侵略呢？

En: You draw a new contract card. According to the text, there's an aristocrat willing to trade a large supply of cocaine for a number of coins and access to a rare bonus: amplified music, worth *seven victory points*...
Zh: 你抽出一张新契约卡。根据上面的内容，有一个贵族愿意用大量可卡因换取一些硬币，还能获得一个罕见的奖励物品：增强音乐，价值*7个胜点*……

En: Sorry, Kim, you're not going to like this... (Introduce the lieutenant's workers to cocaine.)
Zh: “抱歉，金，你不会喜欢这个的……”（向警督的工人介绍可卡因。）

En: Rock and roll, baby. (Go for the contract.)
Zh: “摇滚起来吧，宝贝。”（获得契约。）

En: Hrm... The lieutenant's face goes stony as you take your turn. He does not appreciate you getting all his workers addicted to cocaine...
Zh: “嗯……”你的回合之后，警督的脸变得像冷漠无情。他不太喜欢你让他的所有工人染上可卡因的行动……

En: It takes several turns, but you slowly begin accumulating the cocaine necessary to complete the contract. When you do, it practically rains cardboard coins on your side...
Zh: 几个回合之后，你慢慢开始累积起完成契约所需的可卡因。当你这样做的时候，你这边就像是下了一场纸板硬币雨……

En: Pick up where you left off.
Zh: 从中断的地方继续。

En: With each passing turn you slowly bleed the lieutenant of coins as his own workers become less productive and more dependent on your magenta cocaine tokens.
Zh: 每个回合过去，你开始慢慢吸取警督的硬币，他手下工人的生产力变得越来越低，而且也越来越依赖你的洋红色可卡因代币。

En: Realising victory is slipping away, the lieutenant launches a desperate gambit: *Protectionism*. By erecting tariffs on your cocaine he hopes to starve you out of the market at the risk of incurring the suzerain's disfavour...
Zh: 意识到胜利正在悄悄溜走的警督决定孤注一掷：*贸易保护主义*。通过提高可卡因关税，他希望能让你饿死在市场之外，不过自己同时也要冒着失去大领主宠爱的风险……

En: It's on, Kim. (Launch a trade war.)
Zh: “开战吧，金。”（发动一场贸易战。）

En: Oh, yeah, baby. Cocaine and rock music go together like... cocaine and rock music.
Zh: 哦耶，宝贝。可卡因和摇滚乐的组合就像是……可卡因和摇滚乐。

En: *Cha-CHING*, Kim!
Stack the coins in neat little piles to annoy the lieutenant.
Zh: 把硬币整齐地堆在一起，惹恼警督。

En: Despite your trash-talking, the lieutenant still has a formidable store of coins and resources...
Zh: 尽管你满嘴废话，警督的硬币和资源储量还是多的可怕……

En: Despite your conspicuous display of wealth, the lieutenant still has a formidable store of coins and resources...
Zh: 尽管你在炫富，警督的硬币和资源储量还是多的可怕……

En: The lieutenant nods gravely as you erect tariffs against his apricots and sugar. This is going to get ugly...
Zh: 警督郑重地点点头，你开始对他的杏子和糖征收关税。这下场面可就不会太好看了……

En: With every turn tariffs are raised, until neither you nor the lieutenant are producing any income or generating resources for the suzerain...
Zh: 每一回合关税都会增加，直到你和警督都无法产生任何收入，或者为大领主生产资源……

En: As a matter of historical fact, this is almost *exactly* what happened in Safre. To this day, fully half of the former Safre Empire remains dependant on international aid in exchange for a steady supply of cheap produce.
Zh: 从历史的实际情况上看，萨弗里的状况几乎跟这个是*一致的*。直到今天，整整一半的前萨弗里帝国依然依赖国际援助，以此来交换稳定的廉价农产品供应。

En: Isn't this what they did in the Apricot Suzerainty in real life?\n
Zh: “事实上他们不就是这么对待杏子宗主权的吗？”

En: Just like they did to Safre.
Zh: “就像他们对萨弗里做的一样。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: More or less, the lieutenant says, but he's thinking less about matters of historical wrongdoing than how to get out of the jam you've put him in...
Zh: “差不多吧，”警督说到，不过他没怎么考虑历史错误问题，而是如何摆脱你带给他的困境……

En: For almighty Revachol! (Go for the victory column.)
Zh: “为了强大的瑞瓦肖！”（查看胜点栏。）

En: Build a public education system for your workers.
Zh: 为你的工人建造一个公共教育系统。

En: See? I warned you investing in your workers wasn't going to pay off.
Zh: “看见了吗？我警告过你，在工人上投资是不会有回报的。”

En: My worker tokens have a right to an education, Kim.
Zh: “我的工人币有权接受教育，金。”

En: Can I go back and do my turn over?
Zh: “我能撤回这一步，重新开始这一轮吗？”

En: A little late for that, I think...
Zh: “我觉得好像有点太迟了……”

En: Noble as that sentiment may be, it's not exactly a *winning* strategy in a game that revolves around resource extraction and exploitation...
Zh: “尽管那种情操可能很高尚，在一个围绕资源开采和开发的游戏里，这并不是一个*致胜*策略……”

En: With a triumphant flourish, you remove a rectangular token from one of the satchels and place it next to your museum-cylinder in the centre of the board...\n
Zh: 你得意洋洋地从背包里取出一个矩形代币，把它放到了棋盘中央博物馆圆柱体的旁边……

En: With a triumphant flourish, you remove a rectangular token from one of the satchels and place it in the centre of the board...
Zh: 你得意洋洋地从背包里取出一个矩形代币，把它放到了棋盘中央……

En: Behold my vast accomplishments.
Zh: “看看我的伟大成就吧。”

En: Hmmm, this feels strangely anti-climactic.
Zh: “嗯，这个有种奇怪的虎头蛇尾的感觉。”

En: They're nice tokens, yes. Very impressive...
Zh: “确实是不错的代币。让人印象深刻……”

En: The lieutenant sighs. "That's the problem with certain games. At some point they just *end*, as though no one expected the players to make it that far..."
Zh: 警督叹了口气。“某些游戏确实会遇到这种问题。在某一时刻它们就这么*结束*了，好像没人指望玩家能走得那么远……”

En: Now, let's tally up the scores, shall we?
Zh: “现在，我们把胜点结算一下，怎么样？”

En: Just do whatever Kim did.
Zh: 不管金在做什么，跟着做就行了。

En: You move a few of your workers to the Ile Marat section of the board and several others to Supramundi and Saramiriza. Soon you have several units of archaeological treasure and cocaine.
Zh: 你把一些工人移到了棋盘上的伊莱马拉特地区，其他的放到了苏帕穆迪和萨拉米尔泽。很快你就收获了一些考古宝藏和可卡因。

En: Glancing over the board, you see several possible strategies: Pressing more workers into service would increase your economic output and help you survive a possible conflict with the lieutenant, or you could ignore your labour supply and focus on fulfilling contracts for points and resources...
Zh: 扫了一眼棋盘，你发现几种可能的策略：强迫更多工人工作来增加经济产出，能让你在可能与警督发生冲突的时候幸存下来，或者你可以无视劳动力供应，集中完成契约，以此来换取胜点和资源……

En: What do you do?
Zh: 你会怎么做？

En: Invest in your existing workers.
Zh: 投资现有的工人。

En: Press more workers into service.
Zh: 强迫更多工人工作。

En: Focus on fulfilling contracts.
Zh: 集中完成契约。

En: Kim, what should I do?
Zh: “金，我该怎么做？”

En: To the lieutenant's puzzlement, you spend several turns building various improvements to your territorial infrastructure...
Zh: 让警督费解的是，你用了好几个回合来提升你所属领土的基础设施……

En: Soon your workers have access to clean water, paved roads, and basic hobbies. In return they produce... one extra resource per turn.
Zh: 很快你的工人就能获得干净的水源，平整的马路还有基本的嗜好。作为回报，他们……每回合能够产出额外的资源。

En: Using your powers of 'persuasion', you 'convince' more workers to join your cause...
Zh: 通过使用‘劝导’的力量，你‘说服’更多工人加入了你的事业……

En: Who cares about workers and territories when the real action is in Revachol? You spend your turns fulfilling contracts for sweet coins and one-time bonuses.
Zh: 当物权诉讼发生在瑞瓦肖时，谁还会在乎工人和领土呢？你用了几个回合完成契约，换回了一些甜美的硬币和一次性的红利。

En: After several turns you have a neat pile of cardboard coins and several units of archaeological treasure, which you trade in to build... a *museum*...
Zh: 几轮之后，你积累了整齐的纸板代币和一些考古宝藏，你用交换它们的资源建造了一个……*博物馆*……

En: You place a cylindrical piece of wood on the Revachol section in the middle of the board. It's meant to stand in for a beautifully adorned edifice filled with ancient wonders.
Zh: 你把一个圆柱形的木块放置在棋盘中央的瑞瓦肖地区。它代表了一座装饰华丽、里面满是古代奇迹的大厦。

En: After several turns your worker tokens greatly outnumber the lieutenant's. A vast army of expendable labour is at your command.
Zh: 几轮之后，你的工人代币大大超过了警督。一支庞大的可消耗劳动力大军任你掌控。

En: That's up to you, detective. But remember, the objective of the game is to earn victory points for the suzerain...
Zh: “这取决于你自己，警探。不过要记住，这个游戏的目标是为大领主赢得更多胜点……”

En: Gaze on your workers like a benevolent parent.
Zh: 像一个慈爱的父亲凝望着你的工人。

En: Grimace at them for not working harder.
Zh: 向不努力工作的他们扮个鬼脸。

En: Well played, detective. That museum is worth three victory points...
Zh: “干得好，警探。那座博物馆价值3个胜点……”

En: Hmmm, too bad investing in your workers just isn't worth many points.
Zh: “嗯，可惜你投资工人的行为没有带来太多胜点。”

En: Now it's the lieutenant's turn to respond. He moves aggressively onto the Safre territory. Soon his workers are producing a steady supply of extremely valuable *apricots*...
Zh: 现在轮到警督做出回应了。他更有侵略性地朝着萨弗里地区进发。很快他的工人就开始源源不断地供应价值连城的*杏子*……

En: Kim, can I do my first turn over? I think I messed up.
Zh: “金，我能不能重新开始第一个回合？我觉得我好像搞砸了。”

En: Come on, detective, don't be a spoil sport. You'll have plenty of chances to earn your own points.
Zh: “拜托，警探，别这么扫兴。你还有很多机会能为自己赢取胜点的。”

En: How can you let the lieutenant *dominate* you like that? You need to hit back, and *hard*!
Zh: 你怎么可以让警督那样*支配*你呢？你需要反击，*重重*反击！

En: But what about the workers?
Zh: “那工人怎么办？”

En: Why don't we say 'screw the suzerain' and keep it all for ourselves?
Zh: “我们为什么不说‘去他妈的大领主’，把这一切全留在自己手里呢？”

En: The workers are just there to produce raw resources. You're not supposed to worry about them too much.
Zh: “工人是用来生产原材料的。你不应该太担心他们。”

En: Because the way you earn points is by pleasing the suzerain, and the player with the most points wins the game.
Zh: “因为只有取悦大领主才能赢得胜点，而拥有最多胜点的玩家会赢得这个游戏。”

En: Give back to the workers.
Zh: 回馈工人。

En: For several turns you struggle to respond to the lieutenant's burgeoning apricot empire. Eventually you relocate the majority of your workers to Supramundi and Saramiriza, where they begin producing a bumper crop of cocaine tokens...
Zh: 过去的好几个回合，你都挣扎着应对警督的杏子帝国。最终你把大部分工人都重新分配到苏帕穆迪和萨拉米尔泽，在那里他们开始产出大量的可卡因代币……

En: What do you mean? I just did.
Zh: “你是什么意思？我刚刚这么做了。”

En: Attack the lieutenant and steal all his resources.
Zh: 进攻警督，偷走他的所有资源。

En: You can't do that, detective, the lieutenant says as you move all of your pieces onto one of his territories.
Zh: “你不能这么做，警探，”看着你把所有东西都移到他的领土上，警督开了口。

En: Well, I suppose you could try fulfilling some contracts, or you might try to relocate your workers. There are any number of strategies open to you.
Zh: “呃，我觉得你可以试着完成契约，或者重新安排工人。你还可以采取很多策略呢。”

En: So what *can* I do?
Zh: “那我*能*做什么呢？”

En: It's true. Had you read the rules more carefully this would have been abundantly clear.
Zh: 是真的。如果你能更仔细地阅读规则，这一点就会非常清楚了。

En: This is a game of *economic* strategy. You can't directly attack the other players. You'll have to do something else.
Zh: “这是一个*经济*策略游戏。你不能直接进攻其他玩家。你需要做点别的事。”

En: It takes a while, but you and the lieutenant manage to put everything more or less where it was...
Zh: 你和警督花了一段时间把所有东西差不多都放回了原位……

En: Instead of focussing on amassing points, you embark on a quixotic project to win the hearts of your worker tokens. After several turns you've unlocked advanced hobbies and health insurance. Your workers now produce an extra two resources per turn...
Zh: 你没有集中积累胜点，而是开始了一个唐吉柯德式的计划，来赢得工人代币的心。几回合之后，你解锁了高级嗜好和健康保险。现在你的工人每回合能够额外生产两个资源……

En: You do realize the game is called *Suzerainty* and not *Workers' Paradise*, right?
Zh: “你已经意识到这个游戏叫*大领主*，而不是*工人天堂*了，对吧？”

En: I'm just trying to do right by the people who actually generate the suzerain's wealth.
Zh: “我只是为那些真正为大领主创造财富的人做点好事。”

En: I don't think the suzerain *cares* much about the welfare of its workers. Here, watch this...
Zh: “我觉得大领主并不*在乎*工人的福利。来，看看这个……”

En: The lieutenant continues his strategy of aggressively producing apricots for export. He fulfils contract after contract, racking up ever more victory points...
Zh: 警督继续生产并出口杏子的激进策略。他完成了一个又一个地契约，积累了更多胜点……

En: The endgame is upon you. What will be your crowning achievement as the suzerain's territorial administrator?
Zh: 游戏尾声已经临近。作为大领主一名地方行政官，你将获得怎样的最高成就呢？

En: Alternatively you could actually try to win the game by building the extremely valuable *victory column* in Revachol or by crushing the lieutenant in a devastating *trade war*...
Zh: 或者你可以在瑞瓦肖建造极其贵重的*胜利之柱*，或是在毁灭性的*贸易战*中击败警督，来赢得这场游戏的实际胜利……

En: You could continue your efforts to win the hearts of your worker tokens by building the rarely attempted *public education system* for your territories...
Zh: 你可以在自己的领土上建造极少有人尝试的*公共教育系统*，来继续赢取工人代币的欢心……

En: The choice is yours.
Zh: 选择权交给你了。

En: Soon the entire board is a field of economic carnage. Worker tokens lie strewn across the board, which is also bereft of valuable resources.
Zh: 很快整个棋盘就变成了经济大屠杀的战场。工人代币七倒八歪地散落在棋盘上，同时也失去了宝贵的资源。

En: No, not at all. I suspect this is going to be a rather *low-scoring* game...
Zh: “不，一点也不。我怀疑这将会是一场*低比分*的比赛……”

En: I hope you learned your lesson, Kim.
Zh: “希望这次你能吸取教训，金。”

En: Hmmm, that wasn't very productive for *either* of us, was it?
Zh: “嗯，对于我们*两个人*来说，都不算是很有成效，不是吗？”

En: What happens now?
Zh: “现在会怎么样？”

En: I have no idea what 'lesson' you think you taught me. Somehow I don't think this is going to work out well for *either* of us...
Zh: “我不知道你觉得自己给我上了一堂什么*课*。不知道为什么，我觉得*我们俩*都不会有什么好结果……”

En: Now we find out what's left of our economies...
Zh: “现在要检查一下我们的经济还剩下什么……”

En: I'm not even sure what points *are* -- that's part of the problem.
Zh: “我甚至都不确定胜点*是*什么——问题就在这里。”

En: Points are nothing. They don't exist. They're just an arbitrary convention that games use to reward or punish players for certain behaviours.
Zh: “胜点什么都不是。它们并不存在。它们只是一种随心所欲的约定，让游戏用来奖励或者惩罚玩家的某些行为。”

En: The lieutenant shrugs. "There are some *paranoid* types who believe the Moralintern keeps detailed score sheets for everyone in the Reál Belt, but that's obviously nonsense..."
Zh: 警督耸耸肩。“有一些*偏执狂*会相信国际道德伦理委员会保留了一份详细的计分表，里面包含了雷亚尔货币体系内的所有人，但是这显然是很荒谬的……”

En: Now, I believe it's your turn...
Zh: “现在，我觉得应该轮到你了。”

En: Am *I* getting points right now? (Look around.)
Zh: “现在*我*得到胜点了吗？”（四下看看。）

En: Oh, that makes sense.
Zh: “哦，那还有点道理。”

En: That's just the way it is. The lieutenant doesn't seem to find much value in dwelling on the subject...
Zh: “就是这么回事。”警督似乎觉得细想这个问题没有多大价值。

En: If points are really *arbitrary*, as the lieutenant says, what's to stop you from playing the game the way *you* want?
Zh: 如果就像警督所说，胜点真是*随心所欲的*，那又有什么能阻止你按照*你*想要的方式来游戏呢？”

En: That's terrifying.
Zh: “这也太可怕了吧。”

En: Game designers, I imagine.
Zh: “我想，大概是游戏设计师吧。”

En: That's reassuring.
Zh: “这就让人放心了。”

En: Otherwise, no. There are no points, just your actions and the consequences of those actions.
Zh: “另外，你没得到胜点。根本就没有胜点，只有你的行动和那些行动的后果。”

En: Who came up with such a concept?
Zh: “谁想出这个概念的？”

En: The lieutenant nods. "Now, I believe it's your turn..."
Zh: 警督点点头。“现在，我想应该轮到你了……”

En: Nonsense. Remember what the lieutenant said? If points are arbitrary, who cares about *winning*? You should reject their system and just play how you want.
Zh: 胡说。还记得警督是怎么说的吗？如果胜点是随心所欲的，那谁还在乎*取胜*呢？你应该拒绝他们的系统，按照你的想法来游戏。

En: You taught me that points are arbitrary. I decided to play the game how I wanted instead.
Zh: “你教我说胜点是随心所欲的。所以我决定要按照我想要的方式来游戏。”

En: You know, detective, there's... something *admirable* about that, in its own way...
Zh: “你知道吗，警探，这里面……有种*令人钦佩*的东西，难以言喻……”

En: Let's tally up the scores, shall we?
Zh: “我们把胜点结算一下，怎么样？”

En: Interestingly, this is more or less what happened in real life as well. To this day, if Iilmaraans want to see their nation's priceless treasures they have to visit Revachol or Gottwald or Sur-la-Clef.
Zh: 有意思的是，现实生活中发生的事跟这个也差不多。直到今天，如果伊尔玛人想欣赏本国的无上珍宝，还必须专程跑到瑞瓦肖，戈特伍德或者苏拉菲。

En: Take pity on their miserable lives.
Zh: 怜悯一下他们悲惨的生活吧。

En: Gaze on your workers like a harsh, but proud, deity.
Zh: 像严酷而又骄傲的神明一样凝视着你的工人。

En: The lieutenant assumes you're playing by the rules as written. But what's the point of playing if you can't make your own choices?
Zh: 警督假定你在按照书写下来的规则游戏。不过，如果你都不能自主选择的话，还玩什么游戏呢？

En: I'm rejecting the arbitrary so-called 'points' system developed by this game's politically compromised game designers.
Zh: “我拒绝在政治上持妥协态度的游戏设计师设计的这个随心所欲的所谓‘胜点’系统。”

En: Yes, you're *really* sticking it to the game designers, just like I'm about to stick it to *you*...
Zh: “是的，你*真的*对这些游戏设计师很严苛，就像我准备这么严苛地对待*你*一样……”

En: I'm bored with this. Let's finish the game later.
Zh: “我有点厌倦了。还是以后再结束这场游戏吧。”

En: Are you sure? The lieutenant looks a little disappointed. "It's going to take a while to set everything up again."
Zh: “你确定吗？”警督看起来有些失望。“把这一切重新布置起来要花上不少时间的。”

En: I'm bored with this. Let's do something else.
Zh: “我有点厌倦了。我们做点别的吧。”

En: Are you sure? The lieutenant looks a little disappointed. "It's going to take a while to set everything up again."
Zh: “你确定吗？”警督看起来有些失望。“把这一切重新布置起来要花上不少时间的。”

En: I'm bored with this. Let's finish the game later.
Zh: “我有点厌倦了。还是以后再结束这场游戏吧。”

En: C'mon, detective. This is the endgame! Let's just finish up quickly.
Zh: “拜托，警探。已经到最后阶段了。我们尽快结束这场游戏吧。”

En: What is that patronizing tone? You're one of the suzerain's trusted administrators! You may need to put the lieutenant in his place...
Zh: 这种自认高人一等的口吻是几个意思？你可是大领主信赖的一名行政官！你需要挫挫警督的傲气……

En: Now's your chance. Show the lieutenant what happens when he patronizes you.
Zh: 现在你的机会来了。让警督看看觉得自己高你一等会有什么后果。

En: What is that patronizing tone? Someone needs to put the lieutenant in his place.
Zh: 这种自认高人一等的口吻是几个意思？看来需要某人来挫挫警督的傲气。

En: C'mon, is that your game face? You're practically broadcasting your position to the lieutenant with that expression.
Zh: 拜托，这就是你认真的样子？你这幅表情基本上就是在向警督传播你的姿态吧。

En: Nope, I'm done. (Start putting the pieces away.)
Zh: “不，我玩够了。”（开始把组件放在一边。）

En: Okay, let's finish up.
Zh: “好吧，让我们结束这场游戏。”

En: You should probably pick one strategy and stick to it. Trying to do a little of everything is usually a bad idea.
Zh: “你应该挑选一种策略，然后坚持下去。每种东西都尝试一般不是个好主意。”

En: Read the advice.
Zh: 阅读建议。
