En: Ignore it. Just tell me how the winner is determined.
Zh: 无视它。只要告诉我怎么确定胜者就行。

En: Remember, there are many paths to victory in *Suzerainty*, but successful players will find *one* strategy and commit to it wholeheartedly.
Zh: “记住，在*大领主*里，有很多条通往胜利的道路，不过成功的玩家会找到*一种*策略，然后全心全意地投入其中。”

En: Using the substantial coins and resources you've accumulated from diligently fulfilling contracts and constructing trade routes, you spend your final turns constructing the ultimate structure in the game, the Revacholian *victory column*...
Zh: 你孜孜不倦地履行契约和建造贸易路线，积累了大量的硬币和资源，在游戏的最后一回合，你把它们花在了建造游戏中的终极建筑，瑞瓦肖*胜利之柱*上……

En: Building the ultimate structure requires diligent economic planning, which you completely failed to do...
Zh: 建造终极建筑需要费心费神地进行经济规划，而你完全没有做到这一点……

En: Rather than build a glorious monument to Revachol's economic superiority, you have to settle for a handful of post offices and school for the blind.
Zh: 与其为歌颂瑞瓦肖的经济优势建造一座光荣的纪念碑，还不如勉强接受一部分邮局和盲人学校。

En: A risky gambit, detective. Sorry it didn't pay off.
Zh: “这是一招险棋，警探。很抱歉没有好结果。”

En: Not bad, detective.
Zh: “还不赖嘛，警探。”

En: An aggressive opening, detective, the lieutenant says, appearing to relish the challenge.
Zh: “一个很激进的开局，警探，”警督说到，他似乎很享受这个挑战。

En: You place all of your workers on the Ile Marat territory. On your next turn you produce six units of archaeological treasure and fulfil the contract for a handful of coins.
Zh: 你把所有工人都放在了伊莱马拉特地区。下一回合，你生产出6个单位的考古宝藏，成功完成契约，获得了一些硬币。

En: Let them rest anyway.
Zh: 让他们休息吧。

En: Okay, make them work a little, but not *too* much.
Zh: 好吧，让他们稍微工作一下，但是不要*太多*。

En: That's more like it. You produce a handful of archaeological treasures and a smattering of other resources.
Zh: 这才像话。你生产出一些考古宝藏，还有少数其他资源。

En: There's no concept of *rest* in Suzerainty. Workers have to work. You produce a handful of archaeological treasures and a smattering of other resources.
Zh: 在大领主里，并没有*休息*的概念。工人必须工作。你生产出一些考古宝藏，还有少数其他资源。

En: I make the rules now.
Zh: “现在规则由我制定。”

En: What's the point of playing a game if I can't make my own choices?
Zh: “如果我都不能自由选择，那还玩什么游戏啊？”

En: Well, *winning* is usually the point of playing games. Here, watch this...
Zh: “呃，一般来说，玩游戏的目的是为了*获胜*。来吧，看看这个……”

En: I'm not going to play with you if you're going to act like a child. Now come on, take your turn.
Zh: “如果你打算耍小孩子脾气的话，我是不会跟你玩游戏的。现在，轮到你了。”

En: The lieutenant isn't joking. You'd better stop fooling around.
Zh: 警督没有开玩笑。你最好别再鬼混了。

En: With your army of cheap, not-cocaine-addicted labour, you're able to survive the tit-for-tat of the trade war, though it leaves both your and the lieutenant's economies worse-off than before...
Zh: 有了这支廉价而没有可卡因依赖症的劳动力大军，你就能够在这场针锋相对的贸易战中幸存下来，虽然这让你和警督的经济状况变变得比之前更加糟糕了……

En: Even in the best of cases it's impossible to really "win" a trade war. But this is far from the best case, and the lieutenant's apricot-powered economic engine crushes yours...
Zh: 即使是在最好的情况下，要想真正“赢得”一场贸易战也是不太可能的。不过这里还远远算不上是什么最好的情况，警督那以杏子为驱动的经济引擎是碾压你的……

En: Your commitment to doing right by your workers pays off and you manage to construct the rarely attempted *public education system*. You truly have the best-educated worker tokens in the entire world...
Zh: 你格守承诺为工人谋福利的举动得到了回报，你成功建造了极少有人尝试的*公共教育系统*。现在，你真正拥有整个世界上教育程度最高的工人代币……

En: Unfortunately, you were sent to the territories to produce raw resources, not overly educated second-class citizens. If anything, this might *negatively* impact your final score.
Zh: 不幸的是，你被派往这些地区是为了生产原材料，而不是过度教育二等公民的。如果有什么不同的话，这可能会*消极地*影响到你的最终胜点。

En: There's a reason very few players ever attempt to built a public education system for their worker tokens: It's extremely expensive and never pays off, by design...
Zh: 极少有玩家尝试为他们的工人代币建造公共教育系统是有理由的：它极其昂贵，而且没有回报，游戏就是这样设计的……

En: Your boondoggle nearly bankrupts the suzerain's treasury and you suffer a significant penalty to your final score...
Zh: 你的无效投入几乎让大领主的国库破产，你的最终胜点将会遭受严重的惩罚……

En: The lieutenant opens his mouth as if to issue a blistering retort, then hesitates...
Zh: 警督张开嘴，似乎想要发表激烈的反驳，然后他犹豫了……

En: You know, this isn't *unlike* the situation the historical Revacholian suzerainty faced in Safre in the middle of the last century...
Zh: 你知道吗，这跟历史的上世纪中叶——瑞瓦肖宗主权在萨弗里遇到的情况没什么*不同*……

En: Tell me more.
Zh: 再跟我多说说。

En: Ignore the thought.
Zh: 忽略这个想法。

En: Well, the suzerain was looking for new markets for all the cocaine it was producing, and it settled on Safre...
Zh: 呃，大领主正在为生产出来的可卡因寻找新市场，最后选定了萨弗里……

En: By introducing cocaine into Safre under exclusive contract, the suzerain created an extremely valuable captive market for an extremely *addictive* product.
Zh: 通过排他性合同将可卡因引入萨弗里之后，大领主为一个极其容易*上瘾*的产品创造了一个非常有价值的垄断市场。

En: That's fucked up!
Zh: 那不是搞得一团糟吗。

En: That's brilliant.
Zh: 这也太聪明了。

En: What does that have to do with the game?
Zh: 这跟游戏有什么关系呢？

En: If you could somehow get the lieutenant's workers *addicted* to your cocaine, you could not only make them less productive, you would also force the lieutenant to *pay* you for your cocaine tokens *each turn*...
Zh: 如果你能让想办法让警督的工人*迷上*你的可卡因，不仅能让他们的产量减少，*每回合*还能强迫警督*花钱*购买你的可卡因代币……

En: Can you even *do* that?
Zh: 居然还能这么*玩*的吗？

En: Yes, you can. It's right there in the rules...
Zh: 是的，当然可以。规则里写了……

En: Those aren't your *only* options.
Zh: 那并不是你*唯一*的选择。

En: You could also show your workers how much you appreciate them by *investing* some of that wealth in them. After all, they're the ones *producing* wealth for the suzerain.
Zh: 你可以在工人身上*投资*一些财富，向他们表达你的感激之情。毕竟，他们才是为大领主*生产*财富的人。

En: Well, sure, you *can* do that. It's just not a terribly *effective* strategy. But then, it's up to you...
Zh: 呃，当然，你*可以*这么做。这并不是一个非常*有效*的策略。不过，决定权在你……

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: Take a look at the scoring tables in the back... The lieutenant turns to one of those appendices you skipped over earlier...
Zh: “看看后面的计分表……”警督翻开你之前忽略掉的一份附录……

En: You see in Table 8c that investing in territorial infrastructure multiplies your final victory point total by 1x, which is to say, not at all, whereas erecting monuments in Revachol gives you a multiplier of *5x*...
Zh: “在表格8c中可以看到，投资领土的基础设施可以让你的最终胜点乘上1x，也就是说，一点也不会，然而，在瑞瓦肖建造纪念碑可以让结果乘以*5x*……”

En: Yes, precisely.
Zh: “没错，就是这样。”

En: So you're saying I fucked up?
Zh: “所以你是说我搞砸了？”

En: So you're saying I should treat my workers like disposable labour instead?
Zh: “所以我应该把工人当成是一次性劳动力来对待？”

En: So you're saying the values of the ruling class are completely divorced from the well-being of the people who generate their wealth?
Zh: “所以你是说，统治阶级的价值观完全剥离了那些为他们生产财富的人民的福祉？”

En: Computing the final scores is almost a game unto itself. You each spend an inordinate amount of time making stacks of coins, consulting tables, and struggling with basic addition and multiplication...
Zh: 计算最终胜点本身几乎就是一场游戏。你们都花了大量的时间堆硬币，查表格，在基础的加减法里挣扎……

En: Now, let's tally up the scores, shall we?
Zh: “现在，我们把胜点结算一下，怎么样？”

En: Computing the final scores is almost a game unto itself. You each spend an inordinate amount of time making stacks of coins, consulting tables, and struggling with basic addition and multiplication...
Zh: 计算最终胜点本身几乎就是一场游戏。你们都花了大量的时间堆硬币，查表格，在基础的加减法里挣扎……

En: Computing the final scores is almost a game unto itself. You each spend an inordinate amount of time making stacks of coins, consulting tables, and struggling with basic addition and multiplication...
Zh: 计算最终胜点本身几乎就是一场游戏。你们都花了大量的时间堆硬币，查表格，在基础的加减法里挣扎……

En: Soon your coffers are empty and the map lies strewn with your worker tokens.
Zh: 很快，你的金库就空了，地图上散落着你的工人代币。

En: I hope you learned your lesson, the lieutenant says with a sharp smile.
Zh: “希望这次你能吸取教训，”警督说着，脸上露出了刺眼的微笑。

En: Yeah, never get involved in a trade war in Insulinde.
Zh: “是啊，永远不要在伊苏林迪打贸易战。”

En: Yeah, never play board games.
Zh: “是啊，永远也不要玩桌游。”

En: Never fuck with Kim Kitsuragi.
Zh: “永远也不要招惹金·曷城。”

En: Now, let's tally up the scores, shall we?
Zh: “现在，我们把胜点结算一下，怎么样？”

En: Computing the final scores is almost a game unto itself. You each spend an inordinate amount of time making stacks of coins, consulting tables, and struggling with basic addition and multiplication...
Zh: 计算最终胜点本身几乎就是一场游戏。你们都花了大量的时间堆硬币，查表格，在基础的加减法里挣扎……

En: Now, let's tally up the scores, shall we?
Zh: “现在，我们把胜点结算一下，怎么样？”

En: Computing the final scores is almost a game unto itself. You each spend an inordinate amount of time making stacks of coins, consulting tables, and struggling with basic addition and multiplication...
Zh: 计算最终胜点本身几乎就是一场游戏。你们都花了大量的时间堆硬币，查表格，在基础的加减法里挣扎……

En: This dumb game is rigged, rigged I say!
Zh: “这个愚蠢的游戏被人操纵了，我是说，被操纵了！”

En: Guess I should have gone with a more consistent strategy.
Zh: “我想我应该采取更加始终如一的策略。”

En: Don't be a sore loser, detective. Your moves were your own...
Zh: “别做个输不起的人，警探。你的行动都是自己决定的……”

En: That might have helped, yes...
Zh: “那样可能是会有所帮助，没错……”

En: The lieutenant gives you a sour look. He may or may not hate fun, but either way he does not appreciate your attitude...
Zh: 警督愤怒地瞪了你一眼。他也许并不讨厌找点乐子，不过不管怎么说，他都不太欣赏你的态度……

En: So he says, but his gaze lingers a moment longer on the rulebook than is strictly necessary. He could *make* time, if he really wanted to.
Zh: 他话是这么说，不过他的目光在规则手册上停留的时间要比绝对必要的时间要长。如果真想的话，他可以*腾出*时间。

En: Have you tried... goading him?
Zh: 你有没有试过……刺激他？

En: You just chickening out because you know I'd crush your ass.
Zh: “你临阵退缩，是因为你知道我会把你打得屁股尿流。”

En: The lieutenant scoffs. "In your *dreams*, maybe. I've thrashed my share of trash-talkers before. Now come on, let's put childish things aside."
Zh: 警督嘲弄到。“在你*梦里*，也许可以吧。我以前也喜欢说废话。不过现在，我们还是把这些孩子气的东西放在一边吧。”

En: At this point, plain badgering may be your best bet.
Zh: 此时此刻，简单的死缠烂打可能是最好的办法。

En: Ignore the mock umbrage. Now he wants to prove it to you.
Zh: 无视了假装生气。现在他想证明给你看。

En: Come on, Kim, *puh-leeeeeze*.
Zh: “拜托，金，*求你了~喵*。”

En: The lieutenant sighs so hard for a moment it seems like he may asphyxiate...
Zh: 警督重重地叹了一口气，有那么一瞬间，他似乎都快要窒息了……

En: Holy shit, the average playing time for this game is one to *six* hours...
Zh: “我了个去的，这个游戏的平均游戏时间是1到*6*小时……”

En: Hmmm, I do feel like my thinking has become somewhat *rigid*. Maybe a little diversion to keep the mind limber is just what's in order...
Zh: “嗯，我的确觉得自己的想法变得有些*死板*了。稍微分散一下注意力，也许能保持我思维的敏捷性……”

En: There's also a neat little log to keep track of your progress, in case you need to put the game away and return to it later...
Zh: 还有一个整洁的小日志来追踪你的进程，以防你需要先把游戏放到一边，之后再继续……

En: Alright, detective. Your turn.
Zh: “好吧，警探。轮到你了。”

En: What's the... Moralintern?
Zh: “什么是……国际道德伦理委员会？”

En: That's a subject for another time. All you need to know is that they're a powerful transnational organization, of which the *Coalition* and by extension the RCM are but one small piece.
Zh: “这个话题以后再说吧。你只需要知道他们是一个强大的跨国组织，*联盟*，还有引申开来的RCM，都是其中的一个小部分。”

En: Put the game away.
Zh: 把游戏放在一边。

En: Keep playing.
Zh: 继续游戏。

En: It's your move.
Zh: 轮到你了。

En: Keep playing.
Zh: 继续游戏。

En: Put the game away.
Zh: 把游戏放在一边。

En: As I recall, it was your turn, detective.
Zh: “如果我没记错，应该轮到你了，警探。”

En: As I recall, it was your turn, detective.
Zh: “如果我没记错，应该轮到你了，警探。”

En: Hmmm... this doesn't look right to me. I think we need to start over, unfortunately...
Zh: “嗯……在我看来好像有些不太对。很不幸，看来我们需要重新开始了……”

En: After double- and then triple-checking your maths, you have your final score...
Zh: 在两遍然后是三遍检查过你的数学计算之后，你得到了最后胜点……

En: Thirty-five victory points! The suzerain will be extremely pleased.
Zh: 35胜点！大领主会非常高兴的。

En: The lieutenant looks up from his tabulations. "I've got 26 points," he says, slightly disappointed...
Zh: 警督抬起埋在表格上的头。“我得了26点，”他有些失望地说到。

En: Looks like you won convincingly... well played, detective.
Zh: “看来你赢得让人心服口服……玩的不错，警探。”

En: After double- and then triple-checking your maths, you have your final score...
Zh: 在两遍然后是三遍检查过你的数学计算之后，你得到了最后胜点……

En: Fifteen victory points. The suzerain will not be impressed.
Zh: 15胜点！没怎么给大领主留下深刻印象。

En: The lieutenant looks up from his tabulations. "I've got 26 points," he says, a barely contained smile breaking out across his face...
Zh: 警督抬起埋在表格上的头。“我得了26点，”他一边说着，一边露出了极度隐忍的微笑……

En: Don't be glum, detective. There's always next time. Figuratively, I mean. There's no way we have time to play this game again...
Zh: “别闷闷不乐的，警探。总归还有下次的。我的意思是说，象征意义上的。我们绝对没时间再玩一次这个游戏了……”

En: Fifteen victory points. Whether you win or not, the suzerain is unlikely to be pleased at the loss to productivity caused by his subordinates' shenanigans.
Zh: 15胜点。不管你赢没赢，大领主都不太可能对下属的恶作剧造成的生产力损失感到高兴。

En: After double- and then triple-checking your maths, you have your final score...
Zh: 在两遍然后是三遍检查过你的数学计算之后，你得到了最后胜点……

En: The lieutenant looks up from his tabulations. "I've got 15 points," he says...
Zh: 警督抬起埋在表格上的头。“我得了15点，”他说到……

En: A tie, he says, with evident irritation. "Well, I suppose that's what we deserve for trashing the suzerain's economy. Good game, detective."
Zh: “平局，”他说到，明显有些恼火。“呃，看来这就是我们破坏大领主经济的报应吧。这是一场很精彩的游戏，警探。”

En: Negative five victory points. You'll be lucky if the suzerain doesn't have your whole family executed for such a pitiful performance.
Zh: -5胜点。如果大领主不会因为这种差劲的表现处决你全家的话，那你真是走运了。

En: After double- and then triple-checking your maths, you have your final score...
Zh: 在两遍然后是三遍检查过你的数学计算之后，你得到了最后胜点……

En: The lieutenant looks up from his tabulations. "I've got 15 points," he says with a slight smirk on his face...
Zh: 警督抬起埋在表格上的头。“我得了15点，”他一边说着，一边露出了幸灾乐祸的笑容……

En: Don't look too glum, detective. There's always next time. Figuratively, I mean. There's no way we have time to play this game again...
Zh: “别太闷闷不乐了，警探。总归还有下次的。我的意思是说，象征意义上的。我们绝对没时间再玩一次这个游戏了……”

En: After double- and then triple-checking your maths, you have your final score...
Zh: 在两遍然后是三遍检查过你的数学计算之后，你得到了最后胜点……

En: Two points. Though you're beloved by your well-educated worker tokens, the suzerain is immensely displeased, and recalls you from your post.
Zh: 2点。虽然你被受过良好教育的工人代币所爱戴，大领主还是非常不高兴，把你从岗位上召了回去。

En: Hey, at least you went out playing the game how you wanted. There's something to be said for that.
Zh: “喂，至少你按照自己想要的方式完成了这个游戏。这还是很值得一提的。”

En: Ouch, the lieutenant says, cringing at your spectacular failure.
Zh: “哎哟，”警督说到，他对你惊人的失败感到有些尴尬。

En: This stupid game is rigged.
Zh: “这个愚蠢的游戏被人操纵了。”

En: Huh, maybe I should have pursued a different strategy.
Zh: “嗯，也许我应该采取一个不同的策略。”

En: The lieutenant opens his mouth as if to issue a blistering retort, then hesitates...
Zh: 警督张开嘴，似乎想要发表激烈的反驳，然后他犹豫了……

En: You taught me that points are arbitrary. I decided to play the game how I wanted instead.
Zh: “你教我说胜点是随心所欲的。所以我决定要按照我想要的方式来游戏。”

En: You know, detective, there's... something *admirable* about that, in its own way...
Zh: “你知道吗，警探，这里面……有种*令人钦佩*的东西，难以言喻……”

En: Come on, detective, you knew the rules of the game going in...
Zh: “拜托，警探，你知道游戏规则的……”

En: That might have been a good idea, yes...
Zh: “也许是个好主意吧，没错……”

En: Now, let's tally up the scores, shall we?
Zh: “现在，我们把胜点结算一下，怎么样？”

En: Computing the final scores is almost a game unto itself. You each spend an inordinate amount of time making stacks of coins, consulting tables, and struggling with basic addition and multiplication...
Zh: 计算最终胜点本身几乎就是一场游戏。你们都花了大量的时间堆硬币，查表格，在基础的加减法里挣扎……

En: After double- and then triple-checking your maths, you have your final score...
Zh: 在两遍然后是三遍检查过你的数学计算之后，你得到了最后胜点……

En: Negative five victory points. You'll be lucky if the suzerain doesn't have your whole family executed for such a pitiful performance.
Zh: -5胜点。如果大领主不会因为这种差劲的表现处决你全家的话，那你真是走运了。

En: The lieutenant looks up from his tabulations. "I've got 20 points," he says with measured pride...
Zh: 警督抬起埋在表格上的头。“我得了20点，”他的语气中透露出慎重地骄傲感……

En: Well, there's always next time. Figuratively, I mean. There's no way we have time to play this game again...
Zh: “别闷闷不乐的，警探。总归还有下次的。我的意思是说，象征意义上的。我们绝对没时间再玩一次这个游戏了……”

En: The lieutenant looks up from his tabulations. "I've got 20 points," he says, slightly embarrassed at the point differential...
Zh: 警督抬起埋在表格上的头。“我得了20点，”他说到，对分数差感觉略有一些尴尬……

En: Now, let's clean up and get back to work.
Zh: “现在，我们清理好之后就继续工作吧。”

En: Alright, detective, you win. How do we play this game?
Zh: “好吧，警探，你赢了。我们怎么玩这个游戏？”

En: See? He's doing the hard work himself. All he needed was a little nudge...
Zh: 看见没？他自己干得也是份苦差事。他需要的只是一点刺激……

En: As he completes his own contract card, the lieutenant is rewarded with four coins and a round wooden token that he places in the centre of the board...
Zh: 警督完成了自己的契约卡，奖励是4枚硬币，以及一枚圆形木制代币——被他放在了棋盘中央……

En: In response, the lieutenant spends two of his sugar and one of his apricot tokens to complete his contract card. He is rewarded with four coins and a round wooden token that he places in the centre of the board...
Zh: 作为回应，警督花费了两枚糖币和一枚杏子币，完成了他的契约卡。他的奖励是4枚硬币，以及一枚圆形木制代币——被他放在了棋盘中央……

En: Feels good, doesn't it? But you know what would feel even *better*?
Zh: 感觉挺好的，不是吗？但是你知道什么能让你感觉*更好*吗？

En: Winning the game?
Zh: 赢得游戏？

En: Solving the murder?
Zh: 破获凶杀案？

En: You're going to say 'drugs,' aren't you?
Zh: 你要说‘毒品’，是不是？

En: *Drugs*. Specifically uppers. Speaking of, aren't you supposed to be looking for some right about *now*?
Zh: *毒品*。特别是安非他命。说到这里，难道你不打算*现在*去找点吗？

En: *Drugs*. Specifically uppers. That's an itch... in the back of your neck and elsewhere...
Zh: *毒品*。特别是安非他命。这是一种渴望……在你的后颈和其他地方骚弄着……

En: While you daydream about speed, the lieutenant has built himself a formidable economic engine of his own...
Zh: 在你幻象着思必得的时候，警督为自己建造了一个强大的经济引擎……

En: The endgame is upon you. Do you escalate the *trade war* with the lieutenant in hopes of crushing him with your economic might?
Zh: 游戏尾声已经临近。你会升级跟警督之间的*贸易战*，希望能用你的经济实力碾压他吗？

En: Or do you ignore his aggression and focus on building the mighty *victory column* structure in Revachol herself?
Zh: 或者你会无视他的进攻，集中在瑞瓦肖建造强大的*胜利之柱*？

En: Alternatively, you could throw the whole game away by trying to build a *public education system* for the children of your workers...
Zh: 或者，你可以把整个游戏抛在一旁，试着为工人的孩子们建造一个*公共教育系统*……

En: The endgame is upon you. How will you spend the vast resources you've acquired for the glory of the suzerain?
Zh: 游戏尾声已经临近。你会如何分配自己获取的最大资源，来为大领主赢得荣耀呢？

En: Flipping through the manual, you find the most expensive structure in the game... the Revacholian *victory column*, worth *twelve victory points*. If you can successfully build it, victory will be all-but assured...
Zh: 翻阅手册的时候，你找到了游戏中最昂贵的建筑……瑞瓦肖*胜利之柱*，价值*12胜点*。如果能成功建造它，胜利几乎是必然的……

En: Alternatively, you could try launching a *trade war* to crush the lieutenant's economy, or you could blow all that money on a *public education system* for your worker tokens...
Zh: 或者，你可以试着发动一场*贸易战*，摧毁警督的经济，或者也可以为了你的工人代币，把所有钱全部投入到*公共教育系统*……

En: The endgame is upon you. What will be your crowning achievement as the suzerain's territorial administrator?
Zh: 游戏尾声已经临近。作为大领主一名地方行政官，你将获得怎样的最高成就呢？

En: You could attempt to build the mighty *victory column* structure, or launch a *trade war* against the lieutenant, or spend everything to build a *public education system* for your worker tokens...
Zh: 你可以尝试建造强大的“胜利之柱*，或者发动针对警督的*贸易战*，又或者为了你的工人代币，耗尽所有资源去建设*公共教育系统*……

En: Large letters on the front form a title: "Wirrâl." The colourful box is illustrated with bucolic vistas. The cover art also features odd-looking humanoids, some short, some taller, some with pointy ears, others with ephemeral wings.
Zh: 封面上的大字构成了一个标题：“威勒尔。”彩色的盒子使用的是田园风格。封面图片上印着奇怪模样的类人动物，有些矮小，有些高大，有些长着尖尖的耳朵，还有些生着薄如蝉翼的翅膀。

En: Text underneath the title, in smaller typeface, reads: "Third Edition, Mega-Setting Supplements Module." The side panel adds: "A sword and sorcery adventure board game. With new maps and miniatures."
Zh: 标题下面，用较小的字体写着：“豪华配置增量套装第三版。”。侧面补充着：“一个剑与魔法的冒险桌游。附带新地图和新模型。”

En: Put the box away.
Zh: 把盒子放在一边。

En: Examine the box.
Zh: 检查盒子。

En: Mysterious things rattle inside. What could they be? Dice? Plastic miniatures? A fantastical alternate world full of magic and wonder?
Zh: 里面有些神秘的东西在沙沙作响。会是什么呢？骰子？塑料微缩模型？一个充满魔法和奇迹的幻想交叉世界？

En: Shake the box.
Zh: 摇动盒子。

En: A blurb on the back reads: "Tired of the tedium and toil of modern life? Escape to Wirrâl! Leave behind isolas and nations with their petty squabbles. Discard electricity, magnets, and boring technological widgets..."
Zh: 背后的简介写着：“厌倦了烦闷又劳累的现代生活吗？那就逃往威勒尔的世界吧！把各洲和国家还有它们之间的小纷争抛在脑后。抛弃电力，磁铁，还有无聊的技术工具……”

En: Succumb to a world of high-Pasternal fantastique, unleash your imagination and create an adventure of endless possibilities. Discover the terrible secret threatening Wirrâl -- can your band of adventurers save the world?
Zh: “沉迷于一个异想天开的奇幻世界，尽情释放你的想象力，创造一段充满无尽可能的冒险。发现威胁威勒尔的恐怖秘密——你的冒险团队能否拯救世界？”

En: Look at the back.
Zh: 看看背面。

En: Enough inspecting.
Zh: 检查够了。

En: A colourful box with the title "Wirrâl" in bold letters. It's a board game.
Zh: 一个彩色盒子，上面用黑色粗体字母印着“威勒尔”的标题。这是个桌游。

En: Yes, we're ready to take on this challenge.
Zh: 是的，我们已经准备好接受挑战了。

En: I'm not sure I can handle all this responsibility.
Zh: 我不确定自己能不能承担这些责任。

En: Exactly, that's the spirit! All you have to do is read an intricate rulebook, study an assortment of maps, unfold the illustrated gameboard, and start rolling dice.
Zh: 没错，就是这种精神！你只需要阅读一些复杂的规则手册，研究各种各样的地图，铺开配有插图的棋盘，然后开始丢骰子。

En: Definitely not, it sounds too dangerous.
Zh: 绝对不行，听起来太危险了。

En: Man up, this is about having structured fun! All you have to do is read an intricate rulebook, study an assortment of maps, unfold the illustrated gameboard, and start rolling dice.
Zh: 拿出点男子气概来，这可是有关结构化的乐趣！你只需要阅读一些复杂的规则手册，研究各种各样的地图，展开插图棋盘，然后开始丢骰子。

En: It's not dangerous, it's fun, structured fun! All you have to do is read an intricate rulebook, study an assortment of maps, unfold the illustrated gameboard, and start rolling dice.
Zh: 它不危险，它充满乐趣，结构化的乐趣！你只需要阅读一些复杂的规则手册，研究各种各样的地图，展开插图棋盘，然后开始丢骰子。

En: In no time you could be romping through grasslands with low-level characters, hunted by iyskel riders... or battling unspeakable monsters in endless dungeons fraught with danger and despair, conjuring up forceful maegics to aid your quest.
Zh: 很快你就可以使用低级人物角色在整个大草原上玩闹嬉戏，被艾斯凯尔骑士猎杀……或者在充满危险和绝望的无尽地下城里，跟难以言喻的野兽战斗，召唤强大的魔法来协助你完成任务。

En: Don't forget heated arguments escalating to physical confrontation with your friends.
Zh: 别忘了，还有您和朋友之间激烈的争吵会上升到身体上的对抗。

En: And beer. Lots of beer.
Zh: 还有啤酒。很多啤酒。

En: And most importantly, never forget to rage-quit if the dice don't go your way!
Zh: 最重要的是，如果骰子没有按您的路走，永远也不要忘记怒退游戏！

En: It's not. It's for pansies. Man from Hjelmdall is way more awesome.
Zh: 不。这是给基佬准备的。来自赫姆达尔的男人要比它棒得多。

En: Yes, the Wirrâl setting is known for its complicated system of political alignments. But if you're not into that you can just hack your way through dungeons in search of loot. That's what most people do.
Zh: 是的，威勒尔的设定是因为复杂的政治联盟体系而闻名的。不过如果你不感兴趣的话，可以直接去地下城中寻找战利品。大部分人都会这么做。

En: None of that witless Man from Hjelmdall fascist dross hidden behind faux-realistic allegory. Wirrâl is no cliché-ridden apologia for colonial violence. Wirrâl is pure *imagination*.
Zh: 不像那个愚蠢的*来自赫姆达尔的男人*，将法西斯主义的糟粕隐藏在伪现实的寓言背后。威勒尔完全没有为殖民主义的暴行做出陈词滥调的辩解。威勒尔是纯粹的*想象力*。

En: Open the box.
Zh: 打开盒子。

En: A quick guide to the maegical races of Wirrâl. Create your own hero choosing from any of these completely unique and fantastical backgrounds.
Zh: 威勒尔魔法种族的快速引导。使用这些独一无二的幻想背景来创建属于你自己的英雄。

En: The options are, in order of importance: the welkin, the dweorgr, the humans, the faerie folk, and the pygmies.
Zh: 根据重要程度，选项有：精灵，矮人，人类，仙灵和侏儒。

En: You pry open the box. Inside you find a folded-up map, a small booklet, a 24-sided die, and a little plastic figurine.
Zh: 你撬开盒子，在里面找到一份折叠地图，一本小册子，一个24面的骰子，还有一些小塑料雕像。

En: The welkin -- tall, lithe and graceful, with long flowing hair and pointy ears. They're known for being powerful maegic users, but can also hold their own in a brawn-driven fight.
Zh: 精灵——高大，轻盈又优雅，长着长长的头发和尖尖的耳朵。他们因为能够使用强力魔法而闻名，不过在肉搏战中的也颇为出色。

En: Read about the welkin.
Zh: 了解精灵。

En: Put the booklet away.
Zh: 把小册子放在一边。

En: Read about the dweorgr.
Zh: 了解矮人。

En: A grand race of industrious mountain people. They're short, stout and muscular, and enjoy digging for gold and other precious minerals. They're also well-versed in the art of combat, where they prefer to use axes and hammers.
Zh: 一个由勤勉的山民组成的伟大种族。他们矮小、结实、强壮，喜欢挖掘金子和其他珍贵矿石。他们同样精通战斗艺术，更喜欢使用斧头和锤子。

En: The dweorgr also come in a few different sub-races: hill dweorgr, shield dweorgr, and dark dweorgr.
Zh: 矮人下面又分出一些不同的亚种族：高山矮人，守卫矮人和黑暗矮人。

En: Look at the die.
Zh: 看看骰子。

En: Take the die.
Zh: 拿走骰子。

En: Look at the figurine.
Zh: 看看雕像。

En: Take the figurine.
Zh: 拿走雕像。

En: Close the box.
Zh: 关上盒子。

En: It's made from some sort of wood and has been decorated with peculiar plant motifs.
Zh: 它是由某种木头制成的，上面装饰着特殊的植物图案。

En: You place the die into your pocket. It's always good to have luck on your side.
Zh: 你把骰子放进口袋。把好运带在身边总是好的。

En: You see a man in ragged clothes wearing a lopsided hat and wielding some sort of a firearm.
Zh: 你看见一个衣衫褴褛的男人，戴着一顶歪帽子，正挥舞着某种枪械。

En: You pick the figurine up by the base to meet your gaze. The little plastic man stares back at you, his face contorted into a disturbing shout. Then you pocket it.
Zh: 你托起底座，平视着这个雕像。塑料小人回望着你，他的表情扭曲，像是在不安地呼喊。你把它装进了口袋。

En: With fewer items inside, the box now rattles with less enthusiasm. Only the map and booklet shuffle around, hitting the sides of the box.
Zh: 盒子里的东西越来越少，发出的咔哒声也失去了从前的热情。只有地图和小册子还在四下晃动，撞击着盒子的侧壁。

En: Huh, interesting. A communard.
Zh: “嗯，有意思。一个公社社员。”

En: A leftist revolutionary... a communard. He looks lost, a stranger in a strange land. Most likely, he was just stuck in the wrong box on accident.
Zh: 一个左翼革命分子……一位公社社员。他看起来很迷失的样子，像一个异乡异客。更有可能的是，他只是意外困在了错误的盒子里。

En: Look at the map.
Zh: 看看地图。

En: A reprint of a crude hand-drawn map. The top left corner reads: 'Lands of Wirrâl.' The map features both small villages and mid-sized towns (with odd names), in addition to meadows, forests, hills, lakes and seas (also with odd names).
Zh: 一份粗糙的翻版手绘地图。左上角写着：‘威勒尔地区’。地图上有一些小村庄和中型城镇（名字很奇怪），除此之外，还有草地，森林，山脉，湖泊和海洋（名字也很奇怪）。

En: You don't know much about dice, but this one looks pretty damn fancy.
Zh: 你对骰子不太了解，不过这个看起来该死的神奇。

En: It doesn't seem to correspond with anything you've seen thus far. It's not a very helpful map.
Zh: 它跟你目前见过的任何东西都不相符。这可不是份有用的地图。

En: You hear the die rattling inside the box, bumping against the sides.
Zh: 你听见骰子在盒子里撞击着侧壁，发出咔嗒咔哒的声响。

En: You hear the figurine rattling inside the box, bumping against the sides.
Zh: 你听见小雕像在盒子里撞击着侧壁，发出咔嗒咔哒的声响。

En: What's so interesting about that?
Zh: “那有什么有意思的？”

En: That doesn't sound very Wirrâl-like.
Zh: “看起来跟威勒尔不搭啊。”

En: An odd-looking fellow... he doesn't seem to fit the rest of the setting. He has more of a... *revolutionary* tinge.
Zh: 一个模样很古怪的家伙……看起来跟其他背景都不太符合。他有一股更加……*革命*的味道。

En: The figurine is not a part of the Wirrâl game setting... I guess someone misplaced it during the packaging process.
Zh: “这个小雕像不是威勒尔游戏背景的一部分……我猜是有人在包装的时候放错了。”

En: It is not. The communards are not a part of the game setting... I guess someone misplaced it during the packaging process.
Zh: “它不是的。公社社员不是游戏背景的一部分……我猜是有人在包装的时候放错了。”

En: You stuff it back inside the box.
Zh: 你把它塞回盒子里。

En: Look at the booklet.
Zh: 看看小册子。

En: The welkin come with a variety of exciting sub-races: high welkin, forest welkin, lake welkin, and snow welkin. But if you're not feeling experimental -- a basic welkin will always do.
Zh: 精灵有各种各样有趣的亚种族：高精灵，森林精灵，湖水精灵，还有雪精灵。但是如果你不想太实验性的话——基础的精灵就足够了。

En: Read about the faerie folk.
Zh: 了解仙灵。

En: A very small race of flying people, known for being mischievous, full of trickery. They often lure people into their maegical traps. There are no sub-races for the faeries.
Zh: 一个能够飞行的小种族，以喜好恶作剧和诡计而闻名。他们经常会把别人引入自己的魔法陷阱。仙灵没有其他亚种族。

En: Read about the pygmies.
Zh: 了解侏儒。

En: The least popular of the Wirrâl races, the pygmies are short, rotund and dim-witted. Pygmies live in small villages made of shoddy wooden dwellings. They spend most their days tilling the earth and smoking their pipes. There are no sub-races for the pygmies.
Zh: 威勒尔中人口最少的种族，侏儒矮小、圆胖而愚蠢。侏儒生活在一个由劣质木屋组成的小村庄里。每天大部分时间都在耕作和抽烟。侏儒没有其他亚种族。

En: Read about the humans.
Zh: 了解人类。

En: They're just humans... what else is there to tell? They're average in all stats and jacks-of-all-trades.
Zh: 他们就只是人类而已……还有什么好说的？他们是能力均衡的万事通。

En: A what?
Zh: “一个什么？”

En: A communard. One of the leftist revolutionaries in the Antecentennial Revolution.
Zh: “公社社员。就是世纪前大革命中的左翼革命分子。”

En: Does this mean we can't play?
Zh: “是不是说我们就没办法玩游戏了？”

En: The lieutenant shrugs. "I have a feeling the figurines are more there to set the scene than anything else."
Zh: 警督耸耸肩。“我有种感觉，这些雕像比其他任何东西更能起到布景作用。”

En: Maybe someone should make a role-playing game set during the Revolution.
Zh: “也许有人应该做一个以大革命为背景的角色扮演游戏。”

En: Hmm. Good luck finding people who'd want to play as communards.
Zh: “嗯。祝你能成功找到愿意扮演公社社员的人。”
